26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 1/28

--- Page 68 ---

How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
SCANLAB scan system s im plem ent im age ﬁeld correction with the help of correction ﬁles. The correction ﬁle contains all
inform ation required for scanning within a plane or - in the case of 3D correction ﬁles - within a working volum e. W hen calculating
control values, the RTC board takes the correction ﬁle content into account to com pensate the im age ﬁeld distortion inherent in
two-m irror system s (including optics).
By default, SCANLAB correction ﬁles are calculated from  general system  data (for exam ple, m irror geom etry, calibration factor,
objective speciﬁcations). Individual system  properties and alignm ent errors are not taken into account.
If your application needs correction ﬁles precisely reﬂecting your particular system 's properties, then the 3D-Calibration W izard
serves to calibrate your 2- or 3-axis scan-system . W ith this calibration you can create system  speciﬁc correction ﬁles.
Note This topic gives you an overview of the 3D-Calibration W izard, to let you understand the com plete calibration.
The 3D-Calibration W izard guides you through the calibration in several steps.
Note Further detailed inform ation on calibration can be found in the application note 'Calibrating 3-Axis Laser Scan
System s'. Contact Scanlab to obtain the docum ent.
Note The 3D-Calibration W izard leads you through a com plex and precise calibration.
Alternatively - if you do not need the highest accuracy - the Calibration Factor W izard, whose calibration factors are the
result of linear scaling, can be used (see Determ ining the Scan System  Calibration Factors).
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 2/28

--- Page 69 ---

Execution M ode
Calibration can be executed in laserDESK  (alternatively):
Full Function M ode
‒ laserDESK  ≥ V1.4 is installed on the PC controlling the laser scan system  (production PC),
‒ a valid laserDESK  U SB dongle is connected to the production PC,
‒ a SCANLAB RTC5 board | RTC6 board is properly installed or a SCANLAB RTC6 Ethernet board is connected to the production
PC.
This way you can use the full functionality of laserDESK  what gives you full access to the entire laser scan system  and allows
you to execute laser processes. The m arking on a substrate required as part of the calibration is perform ed with laserDESK
using predeﬁned m arking patterns.
Demo M ode
‒ laserDESK  software ≥ V1.6.8.0 is installed on a PC,
‒ no laserDESK  U SB dongle is required,
‒ no RTC5 board | RTC6 board is required.
However, without the RTC board and U SB dongle, laserDESK  only runs in Dem o M ode. W ith the consequence that laserDESK
cannot control the laser scan system . Therefore, all m arking patterns required within the calibration m ust be created and
executed externally (using your laser processing software). As a further lim itation, one step of the calibration, the focus
adjustm ent (see Calibration Steps), cannot be perform ed and has to be done externally by a diﬀerent procedure.
Note W ith a dongle conﬁgured for the Oﬃce Edition, calibration can be executed in Dem o M ode.
W hen entering the m easurem ent results and calculating the new correction ﬁle, Full Function M ode and Dem o M ode do not diﬀer.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 3/28

--- Page 70 ---

Applicable System  Conﬁgurations
The 3D-Calibration W izard (under M enu Hardware > 3D Calibration W izard) can be applied with the following system
conﬁgurations:
System  for 2D processing with F-Theta lens
System  for 2D processing in a plane with varioSCAN but without F-Theta lens (using pre-focusing)
System  for 3D processing with varioSCAN but without F-Theta lens
System  for 3D processing with varioSCAN and F-Theta lens
The system  conﬁguration is autom atically captured when the conﬁguration ﬁle is read (see Step 2: System  Adjustm ent).
Note If using the 3D-Calibration W izard in Full Function M ode and using a system  conﬁguration with a varioSCAN, the option
"3D" on the RTC board m ust be enabled.
If using the 3D-Calibration W izard in Full Function M ode and using a SCANahead system , the option "SCANa" m ust be
enabled on the RTC6 board.
Note U nless otherwise indicated, varioSCAN stands for all types of varioSCAN and varioSCANFLEX.

ﬁle is calculated from  general system  data. Both ﬁles are provided by SCANLAB. A 1-to-1 correction ﬁle m ay also be used. If already
selected in the Hardware Conﬁguration, the correction ﬁle is displayed, but another one can be selected here. At each further step
a new correction ﬁle is created and the step is appended to the ﬁle nam e.
After the conﬁguration ﬁle has been read, the wizard recognizes the conﬁguration of your system  to be calibrated autom atically.
Several param eters are displayed (depending on execution m ode, see footnote in the table below). The speeds are calculated
using the param eters of the conﬁguration ﬁle. These param eters reﬂect the best values of the optical system s but m ay not give a
good m arking result. Only the power is taken from  the current m arking param eter set (from  the job node), because the power is
independent of the optical system . You m ay change the param eters for som e reason. Especially you should adapt the m arking
param eters to get a good result on the used m aterial. All further m arking param eters which are not shown here, are taken from  the
Hardware Conﬁguration job node (see G eneral Inform ation below).
Note U sing a varioSCAN FLEX in Full Function M ode, the stepper m otor m ust be initialized and referenced before you can
use it here inside the wizard (refer to Testing the M otor Controllers). The dialog "M otor Control" m ust then rem ain

Step 2: System Adjustm ent
Note This step is necessary only with system  conﬁgurations including a varioSCAN or varioSCAN FLEX.
Full Function M ode
Dem o M ode - Exam ple: System  conﬁguration for 3D processing with varioSCAN
The 'System  Adjustm ent' page shows a schem atic diagram  of the selected system  conﬁguration. A precise system  adjustm ent is
the prerequisite for a successful calibration process. Thus, the adjustm ent of the com plete scan system  m ust be perform ed
accurately. The higher the accuracy during adjustm ent, the better is the calibration result.
The working plane m ust be parallel in x and y direction to the scan system 's bottom  side. Otherwise, the z-axis is not
perpendicular to the working plane.
Note Any tilt of the working plane cannot be corrected by the calibration procedure.
A beam  expander m ust provide the correct expansion factor in accordance to the varioSCAN's entrance aperture and the xy scan
system  aperture. Take into account the internal expansion factor of the varioSCAN given in the corresponding varioSCAN m anual.
Two consecutive m irrors serve to align the laser beam  in position and angle to the optical axis of the xy scan system .
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 11/28

--- Page 78 ---

The working distance A is the m echanical distance between the center of the xy scan system 's beam  entrance (= laser input axis)
and the z=0 working plane. The distance B is the m echanical distance between the exit of the varioSCAN (at delivery status) and
the entrance of the xy scan system . The distances A and B is read out the delivered conﬁguration ﬁle and are shown below the
schem atic diagram .
Note Do not m ix up the working distance A and the distance B with the A and B coeﬃcients of the focus length correction.

Step 3: Adjust the Focus - Only with Full Function M ode
Only with Full Function M ode
3 options are available for adjusting the focus distance:
(1) M anual
Possible with all system s that have a focus ring for m echanical adjustm ent.
(2) By m eans of a stepper m otor
Possible only, if a focusing unit with stepper m otor (for exam ple, varioSCAN FLEX or powerSCAN II) is present.
(3) Custom  A value in the correction ﬁle
Basically possible with all system s, however, can cause a reduction of the usable z-range and an asym m etry in the system .
Therefore, should only be used with system s that either do not have a m echanical option for adjusting the focus position (for
exam ple, ﬁberSYS) or system s that do have such an option but the adjustm ent is too tim e-consum ing from  the user's point of
view.
Adjusting the focus distance is an iterative process. W ith each of the options, at the reference point of the system , a test pattern (7
parallel lines on diﬀerent z-planes) is m arked on a m arking substrate. The central line is m arked without defocus, the other lines
are sym m etrically arranged around the central line. The diﬀerent z-values (± i * Δd) are set by a defocus (RTC com m and
set_defocus), not by varying the z-position.
To get a larger z-range of the line pattern, either 'nonlinear z-spacing' can be selected or the z-spacing in the line pattern can be
increased. This m akes the diﬀerences between the lines m ore visible. It is recom m ended to use this for rough adjustm ent. In order
to evaluate the laser spot quality with suﬃcient accuracy, SCANLAB recom m ends the use of a m icroscope. The adjustm ent is
com plete when the center line is best focused. Otherwise, user actions m ay be necessary depending on the chosen option.
Evaluation with option (1): After the result has been entered by the 'Result' check boxes, you receive the m essage "Please turn the
focus ring clockwise / counter-clockwise (for laserbeam  direction)". Adjust the focus ring m anually.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 13/28

--- Page 80 ---

Evaluation with option (2): After the result has been entered by the 'Result' check boxes, you receive the m essage "Please m ove
the varioSCAN FLEX stepper m otor in negative/positive direction". M ove the varioSCAN FLEX stepper m otor by the displayed M ove
Stepper button.
Evaluation with option (3): Enter the num ber of the best focused line. If the m iddle line (num ber 0) is best focused, the focus
distance is already correct and no new correction ﬁle is created. This calibration step is com pleted. Otherwise, click the button
Adjust correction ﬁle. Then, a new correction ﬁle (*_A.ct5) is written having the recalculated A-value.
Repeat the procedure (m arking - evaluating - adjusting) until the center line is optim ally focused.
Note After this step the focus ring or stepper m otor position should not be changed anym ore. The resulting calibration is
only valid for this very position.
Note If you use a focusing unit (for exam ple, varioSCAN FLEX) with stepper m otor you should note down this axis position.
The absolute position is displayed in the 'M otor Control' dialog. This position is strongly related to the correction ﬁle. A
diﬀerent position needs a diﬀerent correction ﬁle!

Step 4: Tilt Correction
Note This step is not applicable with system  conﬁgurations for 2D processing with F-Theta lens.
Note Tilt correction is not suitable for use with telecentric lenses.
If a conﬁguration with telecentric lens is used nevertheless, the m essage that no correction ﬁle has been created
opens.
Full Function M ode
Dem o M ode
W hen the laser is coupled into the scan system , even tiny angular errors can introduce rem arkable position deviations, depending
on the working distance. W ith this step, the beam  direction is corrected to be perpendicular to the m arking surface in the origin.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 15/28

--- Page 82 ---

Legend: 1. Angular Error. 2. Non-Telecentric Objective. 3. Oﬀset. 4. Beam  Tilt. 5. Ideal Position.
In two consecutive steps, two concentric circles with cross-hair in the m iddle of each circle are m arked: one bigger circle at or near
z = zm ax and one sm aller circle at or near z = zm in. The circles are m arked in or near the m iddle of the im age ﬁeld. Both circles have
to be m arked on the sam e m arking substrate to be able to m easure the relative shift. J ust adapt the z-position for each m arking
step. Be careful not to m ove the m arking substrate in x,y between the two consecutive starts.
Note ‒ In Full Function M ode, the m arking patterns are predeﬁned and executed by laserDESK .
‒ In Dem o M ode, the m arking patterns m ust be created and executed externally, i. e. the custom er m ust use his own
laser processing software. It is recom m ended to add axis m arkers in the test patterns to indicate the x and y
orientation of the SCANLAB Reference Coordinate System .
Note If for som e reason the z-positions can not be adjusted, they can be changed to values closer to zero. Larger z-ranges
m ay not be available by the varioSCAN.
The center of the circle at z = zm in is the reference point. M easure the distance to the correspondent circle center at z = zm ax. If the
circle at z = zm ax is shifted right and up, the values for Δx and Δy are positive.
To achieve highest precision, SCANLAB recom m ends to use a coordinate m easuring m achine. The m easured values are entered
into the Δx and Δy input ﬁeld.

--- Page 83 ---

Note W hen m easuring/evaluating the test pattern, m ake sure to align the m arking substrate correctly in xy direction.
Note As a secondary eﬀect of the tilt correction, this m ay result in the shift of the origin. Therefore, the scan head origin in
the m achine coordinate system  can be checked after the calibration procedure and adapted in the Hardware
Conﬁguration if necessary.
To calculate a new correction ﬁle, the Calculate Correction button m ust be clicked.
After clicking the Calculate Correction button the new correction ﬁle with the corrected beam  tilt is calculated.
In Full Function M ode, the Calculate Correction button changes to Repeat to allow the user to do this calibration again for
reﬁnem ent. Clicking the Repeat button loads the newly calculated correction ﬁle and reinitializes the process. In addition, the last
correction param eters are retained and used for reﬁnem ent. W hen the page is left, the last param eters are discarded and you start
from  scratch.
In Dem o M ode, the Calculate Correction button rem ains. If you want to repeat this calibration step with the new correction ﬁle,
you have to go ﬁrst to the next page and then go back and repeat the last calibration step with the new correction ﬁle.
The new correction ﬁle is applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next. After going to the next step, the nam e of the
new individual correction ﬁle is then appended by "_OFFSET". If tilt correction was done several tim es, "_OFFSET" is appended
accordingly m any tim es.

Full Function M ode
Dem o M ode
This step serves to elim inate residual deviations in xy position resulting from  for exam ple, m echanical tolerances or scan-system
non-linearities.
The m arking will create a test pattern at z = 0 consistent of 11 x 11 sym bols, which are sym m etrically distributed all over the im age
ﬁeld. If for som e reason a test position is not m arked or cannot be evaluated, leave the ﬁeld in the input table blank. This m ay only
reduce the accuracy in that area. Nevertheless a calibration ﬁle can be created.
In Full Function M ode the sym bol type (cross +, x-cross x or ﬁlled circle •) can be selected for best results of your coordinate
m easuring m achine.
Note ‒ In Full Function M ode, the m arking pattern is predeﬁned and executed by laserDESK .
‒ In Dem o M ode, the m arking pattern m ust be created and executed externally, i. e. the custom er m ust use his own
laser processing software. It is recom m ended to add axis m arkers in the test patterns to indicate the x and y
orientation of the SCANLAB Reference Coordinate System

The pattern size is deﬁned by the scan ﬁeld size deﬁned in the conﬁguration ﬁle. If you want to calibrate a sm aller area, this value
can be reduced in the Step 2: System  Adjustm ent page prior to executing the xy-plane correction.
If your m arking area is very sm all, you m ay increase the im age ﬁeld size to increase the sym bol distances of the m arking pattern.
In that case you m ay only evaluate the inner positions and get a correction ﬁle for that area. M ake sure to shield the laser beam
when it is directed to the outer positions!
To achieve highest precision, SCANLAB recom m ends to use a coordinate m easuring m achine. Enter the m easured values in the
table cells. The m easured values can be im ported by Copy & Paste from  another program  (for exam ple, M S Excel).
Note The ﬁlled circle sym bols (selectable in Full Function M ode only) always have a diam eter of 2 m m  - regardless of the size
of the scan ﬁeld.
W ith a sm all scan ﬁeld size or a narrow test pattern this can lead to overlapping of the ﬁlled circles and thus m ake the
evaluation m ore diﬃcult or even im possible.
Therefore, select the sym bols cross or x-cross for sm all scan ﬁelds.
Note The origin is deﬁned by the center sym bol (preset in Full Function M ode). This sym bol is m arked with (0,0) values for
the galvanom eter scanner and corresponds to the origin of the scan head.
If your m easurem ent system  evaluates non-zero values for the origin, these values serve as an oﬀset for all positions.
Therefore, you do not need to place the test pattern exactly in your m easurem ent system  that the center point m atches
the coordinate origin.
If you need to shift the origin and adapt it to your m achine coordinate system , you can set appropriate values in the
Hardware Conﬁguration after you have ﬁnished the wizard (see Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters) (in Full Function M ode
only).
To calculate a new correction ﬁle, the Calculate Correction button m ust be clicked. The 'Tolerance [µm ]' input ﬁeld allows to
adjust the tolerance of the correction ﬁle calculation. It should correspond to the accuracy of the m easuring m achine.
The new correction ﬁle is applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next, not if you repeat this step. After going to the
next step, the nam e of the new individual correction ﬁle is then appended with "_XY_PLANE".

Step 7: Calibration of the ABC values
Note This step is applicable only to system  conﬁgurations with varioSCAN or varioSCAN FLEX.
Full Function M ode
Dem o M ode
The ABC values are used to calculate the z-output value for the varioSCAN, to get an optim al focused laser spot (where the z-
output = A + B * l + C * l²  with l = focus length value, see RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual). These values are recalculated by a new ﬁt
using m easured values. This step serves to achieve a best focused laser spot at a distinct x,y,z position. Only for m arking in a plane
with F-Theta lens, the lens itself is responsible to keep the focus in the x,y-plane. All other system s need an adaption in z when
changing (x,y).
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 23/28

--- Page 90 ---

A 7-line pattern is m arked at diﬀerent z-positions according to your system  conﬁguration. The central line is m arked without
defocus, the other lines are sym m etrically arranged around the central line. The diﬀerent z-values (± i * Δd) are adjusted by a
defocus (RTC com m and set_defocus), not by a variable z-position.
Deﬁned test pattern (left). To-be-expected m arking result (m iddle). Real m arking result (right).
Note In Full Function M ode, the line pattern is predeﬁned and executed by laserDESK . The diﬀerent defocus, set for each
line, is executed autom atically.
In Dem o M ode, the line pattern m ust be created and executed externally, i. e. the custom er m ust use his own laser
processing software. The diﬀerent defocus for each line m ust also be set by the custom er.
The distance for the z steps in the line pattern ‒ this is the defocus value Δd by which the defocus is changed from  line to line in the
test pattern ‒ can be edited in the corresponding input ﬁeld according to your requirem ents. The value, initial shown in this ﬁeld,
corresponds to half the value shown in the 'Rayleigh Length [m m ]' ﬁeld in the system  settings (see Step 2: System  Adjustm ent),
but only if the z range allows it. If the z-range is too sm all, the distance for the z steps are adjusted autom atically.
The best focused line of each m arked test pattern m ust be evaluated. In order to evaluate the laser spot quality suﬃciently
accurate, SCANLAB recom m ends to use a digital m icroscope.
The m arking and evaluating process can be perform ed in two ways:
As a step by step procedure: The pattern to be m arked is given by the highlighted cell/row in the table. The m arking substrate
is m arked in the position given in the instruction. After m arking, the best focused line is evaluated and its num ber m ust be
entered in the corresponding table cell. Leaving the cell for the best line num ber will force the program  to calculate the z-
output value for this line autom atically. This value is used for the ﬁt. Repeat this sequence for every row (x/y/z coordinate) in
the table.
In two phases: In the ﬁrst phase, the test patterns are m arked in succession. Here, care m ust be taken to m ark each pattern at
the correct position given in the instructions. Subsequently, the test patterns are evaluated and the num bers of the best lines
are entered in the corresponding cells/lines.
Note You m ay jum p between positions of the table. But we recom m end to execute the m arking in the order of the table to
avoid confusion.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 24/28

--- Page 91 ---

Note For the best focused line, you can even enter an interm ediate value. Exam ple: "-2.5", if the -2nd line and the -3th line
look sim ilarly good.
After all best line num bers have been entered and all z-output values have been calculated by the program , the new ABC-values
are ﬁtted by the Calculate ABC button. The corresponding ﬁelds change accordingly and additional the ABC values are written
into the new correction ﬁle.
The new correction ﬁle is applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next, not if you repeat this step. After going to the
next step, the nam e of the new individual correction ﬁle is then appended with "_ABC".
To im prove accuracy or if the m arked lines can not be evaluated well, the calibration of the ABC values is perform ed a second tim e.
The Next button opens a further page for ABC correction and uses the correction ﬁle of the ﬁrst ABC evaluation (if created). A
m odiﬁed value in the 'Distance for z steps in the line pattern [m m ]' ﬁeld is used. This value corresponds then to one quarter of the
value set on the previous page. However, this value can be chosen lower or higher. If you do not want to perform  a second run, this
step can be skipped.
If the procedure has been perform ed a second tim e, the new ABC-values are ﬁtted by the Calculate ABC button again. And so they
are written into a new correction ﬁle again.
The new correction ﬁle is applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next, not if you repeat this step. After going to the
next step, the nam e of the new individual correction ﬁle is then appended with another "_ABC".
Note As a secondary eﬀect of calibrating the ABC values, the z range could have m odiﬁed slightly.
W hen the calibration is ﬁnished, you can check the speciﬁed volum e. For exam ple, m ark the edges of the outerm ost z-
range levels and check the spot quality.

Result
Depending on the used system  conﬁguration and the num ber of perform ed calculations, m ore or less created correction ﬁles are
shown on the 'Calibration Results' page. As well, the nam e of the current correction ﬁle (at the bottom  of the page) has m ore or
less nam e extensions. The correction ﬁles are saved in the sam e directory as the original correction ﬁle.
For each correction also a data ﬁle and a log ﬁle are created: D3_..._ct5_ReadMe.txt and
D3_..._..._correXion_yy_mm_dd_hh_mm_ss.log. These ﬁles are also saved in the sam e directory as the new correction ﬁle.
If you leave the wizard with Finish, the new created calibration ﬁle is applied and written into the Hardware Conﬁguration (see
Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters).
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:57 How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_3D-Wizard.htm 26/28

G eneral Inform ation
Before you start the 3D-Calibration W izard, the following requirem ents m ust be fulﬁlled:
Your scan system  m ust correspond to one system  conﬁguration listed in Applicable System  Conﬁgurations.
In Full Function M ode the Hardware Conﬁguration m ust be open.
In Full Function M ode the correct Hardware Conﬁguration has to be selected. Its settings m ust correspond to your laser scan
system .
Coordinate transform ations (for exam ple, oﬀset or rotation) in the Hardware Conﬁguration is set to zero while the wizard is
running in order to avoid errors in the following calibration process. After the calibration, the previously deﬁned values m ay
not be valid anym ore.
In Full Function M ode adapted m arking param eters have to be selected for the job node of the Hardware Conﬁguration, see
m arking param eters. These param eters are used for the m arking on the test m aterial. Inside the wizard you can only adapt
the laser power and frequency and the m ark and jum p speed. The scanner delays are calculated by the conﬁguration ﬁle and
can not be changed. The laser frequency and the speed is also calculated to get separated laser spots, but you m ay change
these value to get a better m arking quality.
The laser scan system  m ust be adjustable to diﬀerent heights in order to m ove the m arking substrates to the diﬀerent z-levels
(z <0, z=0, z>0). (Not for pure 2D calibration)
The laser scan system  should be warm ed-up for m inim um  30 m inutes prior to the calibration procedure in order to achieve
m axim um  accuracy.
SCANLAB recom m ends to m onitor the tem perature of the galvanom eter scanners and servo boards (using an iDRIVE scan
system , see intelliSCAN Inform ation) and, if accessible, of the scan head housing and m ount.
If you use a varioSCAN FLEX, you have to do a reference run before running the wizard.
In Full Function M ode the stepper m otor is supported inside the wizard. You should keep the dialog "M otor Control" open
when you have executed the reference run to get the position inform ation of the varioSCAN FLEX (see Testing the M otor
Controllers).
In Dem o M ode the stepper m otor m ust be controlled externally.
W hen perform ing the calibration, observe the following:
As test m aterial, suitable ﬂat m arking substrate, for exam ple, anodized alum inum  or steel plate, should be used.
Suitable m easuring tools (digital m icroscope, coordinate m easuring m achine) should be used.
If not especially denoted, m easured values m ust always correspond to the absolute positions in the coordinate system .
Note The orientation of the axes in the coordinate system  corresponds to the orientation used by the RTC board.
See SCANLAB Reference Coordinate System .
For m arking in dem o m ode, it is recom m ended to add axis m arkers when creating the test patterns to indicate the
x and y orientation of the coordinate system .
The new correction ﬁle created in each step is applied only when going forward to the next step with the Next button.
I.e. in Full Function M ode, the new correction ﬁle is loaded to the RTC board and used by it. In Dem o M ode the new correction
ﬁle will just be saved and used for the next calculation; you need to apply this correction ﬁle in your laser processing software
by your own!
If a step is sim ply repeated, the correction ﬁle just created is not applied (exception: tilt correction in Full Function M ode).
Running in Dem o M ode requires that you create your own test patterns and control the laser scan system  using your own
control software.

How to Calibrate an U nknown Optical System
This topic describes what you need to consider when you want to calibrate a system  for which you have neither a SCANLAB
correction ﬁle nor a system -speciﬁc xm l-conﬁguration ﬁle.
Note For system s with unknown optical system  only an xy-plane calibration (2D) is possible.
Note For a better understanding the background of the following description, see How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan
System .
See especially sections 'System  Settings', 'System  Adjustm ent' and 'XY-Plane Correction'.
As no correction ﬁle and no system -speciﬁc xm l-conﬁguration ﬁle is available, default ﬁles are provided by SCANLAB. The default
correction ﬁle is Cor_1to1.ct5  and the default data ﬁle is XML_for_1to1.xml. Both ﬁles are in laserDESK  program  directory (for
exam ple, C:\Program Files\SCANLAB\laserDesk).
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Calibrate an Unknown Optical System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_UnknownSystemCalibration.htm 2/4

--- Page 97 ---

Process
Proceed as follows:
To start 3D-Calibration W izard, press <F2>.
Click the Next button to continue.
The 'System  Settings' page opens.
To load the conﬁguration ﬁle, click the corresponding Button 
  and select the XML_for_1to1.xml conﬁguration ﬁle in the
program  directory.
To load the correction ﬁle, click the corresponding Button 
  and select the Cor_1to1.ct5 correction ﬁle in the program
directory.
To perform  an XY calibration, the following inform ation is required:
Size of the im age ﬁeld:
Deﬁne the border length of the im age ﬁeld. The resulting correction ﬁle will lim it the accessible area to this size (see rem arks
below for m ore inform ation).
For Full-Function M ode only: laser frequency, laser power, jum p and m arking speed as well as the laser spot size (for hatching
calculation).
Deﬁne these values to achieve a good m arking result.
For a successful calibration it is im portant that
‒ the laser beam  runs in the optical axis,
‒ the m arking area is not tilted,
‒ the m arking area is exactly in the focus distance of the scan system ,
‒ accurate m arking is created on the substrate,
‒ the accuracy of the m easurem ent is high enough.
Sm all m isalignm ent can be suﬃciently corrected by the calibration process but will not achieve the best possible result.
W hen the settings and adjustm ent are done, the 'XY-Plane Correction' page is entered to process the only possible calibration
step.
If the wizard is started in Full-Function M ode, the m arking pattern is autom atically calculated for execution.
In Dem o M ode you need to process the m arking pattern using a diﬀerent program  and then enter the m easured values.
After the calculation, a new 2D correction ﬁle is obtained with a well-deﬁned calibration factor that allows laser processes all over
the deﬁned im age ﬁeld.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Calibrate an Unknown Optical System
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_UnknownSystemCalibration.htm 3/4

--- Page 98 ---

Com m ents
The correction ﬁle Cor_1to1.ct5   does not contain a calibration factor. This is autom atically calculated by the wizard
dependent on the im age ﬁeld size:
k = 220 / border length of the im age ﬁeld [bit/m m ].
The im age ﬁeld deﬁnes the m axim um  area which can be accessed by the evaluated correction ﬁle. This calculation assum es,
that the optical system  can be controlled by the full 20 bit range. If your optical system  is m ore lim ited in the bit range, choose
an im age ﬁeld larger than the accessible area of your optical system . Som e sym bols lie outside the accessible ﬁeld and m ay
not be m arked at all. Ignore these points (leave the table blank or set the actual position to the target position).
The test pattern consists of 11 x 11 sym bols spread over the whole deﬁned im age ﬁeld. The positions of these sym bols are
given in the table. If som e sym bols cannot be processed or m easured, leave the values in the table blank. Nevertheless the
evaluation of the correction ﬁle will work.
In Dem o M ode, the used calibration factor is displayed to allow the conversion of the bit and m illim eter values needed for the
m arking pattern.
In Full-Function M ode, the calculated calibration factor is used for the new correction ﬁle.

How to Fill Barcodes
laserDESK  oﬀers a special ﬁlling function for 1D and 2D barcodes that realizes uniform  ﬁlling lines.
Additionally, for 2D codes this function let you execute the individual ﬁlling lines as pixel lines with constant speed.
Barcode Filling
2D Code Filling
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Fill Barcodes
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_BarcodeFilling.htm 2/8

--- Page 101 ---

Barcode Filling
W ithout the special barcode ﬁlling, that is, with the standard ﬁlling, the following ﬁlling can occur perpendicular to the bars:
It can happen that the sam e bars receive a diﬀerent num ber of ﬁlling lines (exam ple above: bar1 has two ﬁlling lines, bar 2 has
three). In this case, the bars are labeled with diﬀerent widths. This also has the consequence that the gaps between the bars
becom e diﬀerently wide. If the bar is very sm all, it becom es unreadable.
To avoid inaccurate or even faulty barcode ﬁllings, laserDESK  oﬀers the barcode ﬁlling with the following general speciﬁcations:
Filling type = hatch.
Num ber of hatches = 1.
Line reduction and beam  com pensation are not available.
Scaling param eters are not used.
Angle start = 0° or ‒90° (the m arking is executed horizontal from  left to right or vertical from  top to bottom ). The ﬁlling is
always calculated relative to the barcode orientation.
The 'Line Distance' param eter is replaced by the param eter 'Hatch Lines per M odule'. This ensures that all bars of the sam e
width receive the sam e num ber of ﬁlling lines.
This produces the following uniform  ﬁlling with bar width w, ﬁlling line distance d and edge distance e (with e = d/2):

--- Page 102 ---

If the barcode has a textual com ponent, this text is calculated with the standard Fill Param eters and can therefore be ﬁlled
diﬀerently from  the bars:
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Fill Barcodes
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_BarcodeFilling.htm 4/8

To apply the barcode ﬁlling, the barcode properties include the following param eters, which can be edited in the G raphic
Param eters - Barcode, category '[4] M arking' (or category '[5] M arking' in the variable barcodes):
Barcode Filling
If "True" is selected, the barcode ﬁlling is applied for m arking. Also, the two other param eters (see below) are displayed.
Note ‒ If "True" is selected, the standard ﬁlling is deactivated for the bars but applied to the textual com ponent (if any).
‒ If "False" is selected, the standard ﬁlling is applied to the bars (and textual com ponent).
‒ To be able to ﬁll the barcode, whether with or without special barcode ﬁlling, the 'Filling Lines' param eter (see
G raphic Param eters - Barcode\[4] M arking) m ust always be set to 'True.
Hatch Lines per M odule
This param eter deﬁnes the num ber of ﬁlling lines per basic m odule (= sm allest bar; see 'M odule W idth' param eter in G raphic
Param eters - Barcode\[3] Barcode) and thus the ﬁlling line distance. This ensures that each bar always has the sam e num ber
of ﬁlling lines and thus has the sam e thickness during m arking. The ﬁlling lines are always centered and arranged
sym m etrically to the bar's center.
Vertical Filling
If "True" is selected, the ﬁlling lines are aligned vertically; if "False" is selected, the ﬁlling lines are aligned horizontally.
For 1D barcodes, this param eter should be set to "True". It is m ainly used for 2D barcodes.
Note The barcode ﬁlling is always object-speciﬁc (therefore also object param eters) and does not participate in the
inheritance m echanism  of the param eter sets.
The ﬁlling is sorted so that you get a bidirectional execution. This reduces the process tim e.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Fill Barcodes
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_BarcodeFilling.htm 6/8

2D Code Filling
W ithout the barcode ﬁlling, the ﬁlling lines for the entire 2D code are calculated together, but each m odule (rectangle) has its own
contour line. This results in single ﬁlling lines that end at the m odule boundaries. Therefore, m any jum ps between the individual
ﬁlling lines are executed during execution:
In order to avoid that jum p and m ark delays are executed in the m odule gaps, the ﬁlling lines of the single m odules are com bined
to one pixel line, which can be executed with constant speed. The pixel distance then corresponds to the m arking speed / laser
frequency and can therefore be speciﬁed by the user. The pixel lines have the speciﬁed ﬁlling line distance, speciﬁed as the
num ber of hatch lines per m odule. The pixel lines are m arked bidirectionally to avoid long jum ps.
The laser frequency and the m arking speed result in the pixel distance and thus the contrast of the m arking. Higher frequency (at
the sam e pulse power) will result in a stronger m arking. Because the barcode elem ents have no bitm ap param eters, the following
settings are predeﬁned by laserDESK :
The grayscale table consists of 2 values:
‒ Pixel value 0 (white)      => power 100%, pulse width 0 µs (no m arking),
‒ Pixel value 255 (black) => power 100%, pulse width 1/(2*frequency).
For the ﬁlling lines black pixels are created, for the gaps white pixels.
The Black Value is 255 => for m arking black pixels.
The pixel lines are m arked bidirectionally.
Dithering is not used.
Synchronization of the im age lines can be set.
This produces the following uniform  bitm ap ﬁlling:
Note In laserDESK  graphic view, the calculated ﬁlling lines are not displayed as bitm ap but as line elem ents.

file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_BarcodeFilling.htm 7/8

--- Page 106 ---

'[4] M arking' (or category '[5] M arking' in the variable barcodes)):
Barcode Filling
Hatch Lines per M odule
Vertical Filling (all three, see section 'Barcode Filling' above)
Bitm ap Filling
If "True" is selected, pixel lines are calculated from  the ﬁlling lines and are executed as bitm aps. Also the param eter below is
displayed.
Row Synchronization [m m ]
Serves to synchronize the pixel lines. A positive value shifts the pixel lines opposite to the m arking direction, a negative value
in m arking direction.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the Ramping Function
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Ramping.htm 1/5

--- Page 108 ---

How to U se the Ram ping Function
The "Ram ping" function (synonym : ram ping) allows to vary a param eter along the edges of a graphic object (polygonal line). This
allows the laser intensity to gradually increase or decrease, which is especially used for welding applications.
The following graphic shows exem plarily ram ping of the laser intensity in a welding process.
Ram ping of the laser intensity can be set by following param eters:
Laser power
Pulse length
Frequency
To activate the ram ping, a control elem ent "Ram ping" m ust be inserted into the J ob Explorer and placed in the tree in front of the
graphic object(s) that is (are) to be processed accordingly. The ram ping function is applied on all following graphic objects in the
tree.
To use the ram ping function on consecutive graphic objects but with diﬀerent ram ping properties , one or m ore further control
elem ent "Ram ping" m ust be inserted.
To deactivate the ram ping (and thus to use the ram ping function only for the intended graphic objects), another control elem ent
"Ram ping" m ust be inserted, placed behind the object(s), and set to "Ram ping oﬀ".
The control elem ent "Ram ping" can be inserted by: Toolbar "Control Elem ents", M enu Tools > Control Elem ents, W indow
"Toolbox" or J ob ExplorerContext M enu (Right M ouse Button) (see Inserting a control elem ent "Ram ping").
The control elem ent "Ram ping" has the following param eters, which can be edited in the W indow "Properties":
Signal Type:
Selection of the variation param eter:
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the Ramping Function
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Ramping.htm 2/5

--- Page 109 ---

‒ Analog Out 1: lets deﬁne the laser power (by the connector LASER)
‒ Digital Out : lets deﬁne the laser power (by the connector EXTENSION 2)
‒ Pulse length (equal to the power for CO2 lasers)
‒ Frequency
‒ Ram ping oﬀ: is only required to term inate the ram ping function if further graphic objects in the job execution need no
ram ping (see above).
Note For the 'Signal Type' param eter you need to select an output which controls the laser in som e way.
For exam ple: If you select the 'Analog Out 1' type and the laser system  does not use the RTC Analog Out 1 (like a
CO2 laser), this selection has no eﬀect.
Start value [%]:
The ram ping starts at this value and is linearly changed to 100 % (see schem atic diagram  above; observe notes below).
End value [%]:
The ram ping ends at this value and is linearly changed from  100 % (see schem atic diagram  above).
Note The 100 % correspond to the value deﬁned for the graphic object (see 'M arking Param eters ‒ Laser Param eters').
Exam ple:
The graphic object's 'Laser Power P' param eter has been set to 80 %, which corresponds to 8 V analog output on
Analog Out 1. This is the 100% value of the object.
The ram ping start value is deﬁned by 50 % (which is 50 % of the laser power deﬁned for the graphic object).
The resulting output voltage at the start point is then 50 % of 8 V = 4.0 V
Note It is possible to deﬁne values above 100 %. In that case the start/end value is higher than the value deﬁned for the
graphic object's 'Laser Power P' param eter.
Rising Edge Tim e:
Tim e duration in [m s] for the ram ping at the beginning of the object (observe note below).
Falling Edge Tim e:
Tim e duration in [m s] for the ram ping at the end of the object.
Note If the rising and falling edge tim e are longer than the whole execution tim e, the 100% value of the object will never
be reached.
In this case the slope is not changed, but the tim e duration is decreased.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the Ramping Function
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Ramping.htm 3/5

--- Page 110 ---

Properties and Restrictions
The num ber of control elem ents "Ram ping" is not lim ited.
The value ranges of the param eters are checked after the ram ping calculation and are restricted if need be (for exam ple, laser
power never exceeds the absolute 100% value, frequency is not outside the laser frequency range).
If the laser m ode is "0" for CO 2 Lasers (see Creating a Laser Deﬁnition File for a G eneral Laser Type), the power deﬁnes the
pulse length. Then, the pulse length param eter is not available in the m arking param eter set. In that case the power property
deﬁnes the 100 % value for the pulse length param eter of the ram ping.
To calculate and execute the ram ping, all spline or arc objects are internally expanded and converted into polygonal lines. Be
aware that very sm all edges m ay be created which slow down the m arking speed. To avoid this, you m ay use the 'Expand'
function (see Expanding M arking Objects) to check the created edge length. The m arking speed is reduced, if the edge length
divided by 0.01 m s is lower than it. Increase the 'G raphic Resolution' value (see 'G raphic Param eters\M arking' in the W indow
"Properties"), if need be to get longer edges.
If ram ping is switched on inside a variant, it is not switched oﬀ autom atically for the objects behind the variant.
The ram ping function works for ﬁlling lines and 3D objects, too.
For variable objects the ram ping function is not applied.
Ram ping can even be applied to m arking objects of type "Point", see also Inserting Points (laserDESK  ≥ V1.6.24.0). Here, laser
power is varied over tim e while the point is m arked (and not varied locally along a vector as usual). Note that the 100% value
is not reached for [(Rising Edge Tim e [m s] + Falling Edge Tim e [m s]) ≥ m arking duration of the point].
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the Ramping Function
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Ramping.htm 4/5

--- Page 111 ---

Visualization
For the Ram ping function a special graphical illustration is included: In the W indow "Views" the ram ping lines can be set visible.
Then a gradient colored line is displayed where the ram ping occurs. The higher the start or end value, the brighter the color.
The 100 % color can be deﬁned in the window "Options" (see 'G raphics\Ram ping Lines' in Deﬁning the 'View' Param eters).
Note The display of the ram ping lines is not actualized during an edit action. Therefore, it m ay happen that som e ram ping
lines are not valid anym ore and you m ay refresh the view m anually (for exam ple, scroll or zoom ).
Changing the properties of the ram ping control node, the ram ping lines are actualized im m ediately.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 1/12

--- Page 113 ---

How to Use the General M otor Control Interface
laserDESK  oﬀers the possibility to integrate a custom er deﬁned m otor controller plug-in to be applied by laserDESK . This enables the
custom er to use any m otor controller unit he likes and is no m ore restricted to the full supported types laserDESK  has integrated. A
generic m otor type is linked to a general interface class, so that all m otor functions can and are executed by this interface.
Note Inside laserDESK  there is no diﬀerence between a full supported or a generic m otor.
The usage of the m anual m otor control or m otor control nodes in a job is exactly the sam e.
U sing this plug-in feature the custom er has to write a DLL by its own. The DLL has to im plem ent a speciﬁc interface deﬁned by
laserDESK . This interface enables laserDESK  to call the necessary functions for the initialization and m otions of an axis.
The laserDESK  installation is delivered with a 'dum m y' DLL (GenericMotorControl.dll). If the custom er wants to use the Plug-In
feature he just needs to exchange this 'Dum m y' DLL by his own.
Note A custom er-speciﬁc GenericMotorControl.dll with a version num ber other than 1.0.0.0 is not overwritten during a new
installation or an upgrade!
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 2/12

--- Page 114 ---

Deﬁnition of the Interface
Note The nam es, variable types and m ethods of this interface need to m atch exactly the deﬁnition below.
namespace GenericMotor
{
   public interface ISLGenericMotorController
   {
      bool InitMotor(int UID, string Name);
      void DeInitMotor(int UID);
      bool DoReferenceRun(int UID, int RefType);
      bool MoveAbsPosition(int UID, double pPosition);
      bool MoveRelPosition(int UID, double pPosition);
      bool EmergencyStop(int UID);
      bool Reset(int UID);
      string[] GetReferenceRunTypes(int UID);
      uint GetState(int UID);
      double GetPosition(int UID);
      public void SetConfiguration(int UID, string ComInterface, double cal, int refType, double speed, double
refSpeed, double acc, double refOffset);
The parameters are supplied by the Hardware Configuration of laserDESK
      bool SetSpeed(int UID, double Speed);
      string GetErrorMessage(int UID);
      int PollingTime { get; set; }
   }
}
The DLL nam e is "G enericM otor.dll" and the nam espace "G enericM otor". The class nam e of the m otor control is
"G enericM otorControl".
You m ay use any .NET language (for exam ple, C#, VB) to create the DLL. You m ay even use a wrapper DLL in C# im plem enting this
interface which calls a native C++ DLL. The native C++ DLL in turn has the real code for execution.
The DLL has the param eter public intPollingTime { get; set; }. This param eter deﬁnes the tim e span between adjacent state queries
during a m otion. It should be set in the InitMotor m ethod or the constructor. This param eter is acquired directly after the call of
InitMotor().
The DLL m ethods should have following functions:
   public bool InitMotor(int UID, string Name);
The param eters of this m ethod deﬁne the U ID (U nique Interface) and the nam e of the axis. The nam e will only be used in laserDESK  to
identify the axis. It is deﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration. The U ID is used in all function calls to select the axis. For each axis deﬁned
in the Hardware Conﬁguration a new instance of the DLL class are created. The U ID and/or nam e can be used inside the DLL to
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 3/12

--- Page 115 ---

distinguish between diﬀerent axes. This allows to use m ore than one axis of the sam e type. The U ID is set by laserDESK  and
corresponds to the position in the m otor list in the Hardware Conﬁguration. The ﬁrst appearance of a generic m otor gets num ber 1, the
next num ber 2 and so on.
Furtherm ore the InitMotor function has to
‒ conﬁgure and initialize the interface to the axis (RS232, Ethernet … ),
‒ conﬁgure and initialize the axis by sending the appropriate com m ands,
‒ activate (enable) the m otor.
After this com m and the state M OT_INIT should be set (for the state ﬂags see below).
   public void DeInitMotor(int UID);
Function deactivates the axis and releases the com m unication channel (hardware interface). After this com m and the axis state
M OT_INIT should be reset.
   public string[] GetReferenceRunTypes(int UID);
Query for all possible reference run m odes. This function will return a list of all reference run types as strings. These strings are
displayed in the Hardware Conﬁguration for the selection of the reference run. laserDESK  assigns a num ber to each list entry starting
with 1. This num ber is used as a param eter of the DoReferenceRun function (param eter RefType) to select the kind of the reference run
. The default reference run "actual position is reference, no reference run is executed", should always be the ﬁrst in the list getting
num ber 1.
   public bool DoReferenceRun(int UID, int RefType);
Starts the reference run of type RefType. This function should set the state M OT_RU N_ACTIVE while the reference run is executed. After
a successfull reference run the state ﬂags M OT_REF_FOU ND and M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E should be set and M OT_RU N_ACTIVE
should be reset.
After a reference run the axis m ay stay on an end switch or m ay already be m oved apart. The actual position should then be deﬁned by
the refOffset param eter of the SetConﬁguration com m and.
The function should only trigger the reference run and return im m ediately. It should not wait until the reference run has ﬁnished. Else
laserDESK  is unable to evaluate the actual state.
   public bool MoveAbsPosition(int UID, double Position);
This m ethod starts an absolute positioning. Position is the new position in [m m ] or [°] for a rotary axis. The DLL has to calculate the
m otor units using the gear and calibration factor. The used velocity is set by the SetSpeed m ethod.
This function should set the state M OT_RU N_ACTIVE and reset M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E while the m otion is executed. After a
successfull positioning the state ﬂag M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E should be set and M OT_RU N_ACTIVE should be reset.
The function should only trigger the m otion and return im m ediately. It should not wait until the m otion has ﬁnished. Else laserDESK  is
unable to evaluate the actual state.
   public bool MoveRelPosition(int UID, double Position);
This m ethod starts a relative positioning. Position is the distance to the new position in [m m ] or [°] for a rotary axis. The DLL has to
calculate the m otor units using the gear and calibration factor. The used velocity is set by the SetSpeed m ethod.
This function should set the state M OT_RU N_ACTIVE and reset M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E while the m otion is executed. After a
successfull positioning the state ﬂag M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E should be set and M OT_RU N_ACTIVE should be reset.
The function should only trigger the m otion and return im m ediately. It should not wait until the m otion has ﬁnished. Else laserDESK  is
unable to evaluate the actual state.
   public bool EmergencyStop(int UID);
This function should stop any m otion im m ediately. If no m otion is active the function does nothing. This function resets the state ﬂag
M OT_RU N_ACTIVE.
   public bool Reset(int UID);
This m ethod resets the error ﬂag M OT_ERROR. Any action to enable the next m otion com m and has to be executed. A fatal error which
needs a reinitialisation (state M OT_INIT is reset) is not reset by this com m and.
   public uint GetState(int UID);
This m ethod returns the actual state (indicated by the state ﬂags) of the axis. The m ethod is called several tim es during any m otion to
check the execution process. Therefore, during any m otion the state M OT_RU N_ACTIVE has to be set and M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 4/12

--- Page 116 ---

has to be reset. If an error occurs the ﬂag M OT_ERROR should be set, M OT_RU N_ACTIVE should be reset and
M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E should rem ain unset. If a fatal error occurs which needs a reinitialisation the ﬂag M OT_INIT should be reset,
too.
In laserDESK  you can check the m otor axis state in the tab 'Axes Controller' of the Dialog "Runtim e Inform ation", see Tab 'Axes
Controller'.
   public double GetPosition(int UID);
This function returns the actual position (in [m m ] or [°]). While a positioning is executed this function is called several tim es to display
the progress.
   public void SetConfiguration(int UID, string ComInterface, double cal, int refType, double speed, double
refSpeed, double acc, double refOffset);
This function transfers the param eter settings of the axis deﬁned in the hardware conﬁguration of laserDESK  (see Hardware Devices) to
the DLL. It is called prior to the InitM otor m ethod to deﬁne the necessary param eters for the initialisation. The reference run type is
transferred using the num ber in the reference run list. If this type does not exist, the default reference run (num ber 1 = actual position is
reference, no reference run is executed) is selected.
The sequence and param eter types are:
ComInterface- communication interface (COM1, COM2 ...)- string
cal - calibration factor - double
refType - selected reference run typet - int
speed - default speed - double
refSpeed - reference speed - double
acc - acceleration - double
refOffset - reference point offset - double
The Com Interface param eter is only im portant if the axis controller needs a serial interface of the PC. The DLL need not use all
param eters but can set them  diﬀerent.
   public bool SetSpeed(int UID, double Speed);
This function sets the actual speed in [m m /s] or [°/s] which is used for all following m otions. It refreshes the default speed set by the
speed param eter of the SetConfiguration m ethod.
   public string GetErrorMessage(int);
This function is called when GetStatereturns an error (ﬂag M OT_ERROR is set). It should supply a speciﬁc error inform ation as a string.
This inform ation is displayed in laserDESK  to give precise inform ation for error handling.
Note, GetErrorMessage is available with ≥ V1.6.15.0 only. If GetErrorMessage is not present in the dll, only a standard error text is
displayed in laserDESK . This allows downward com patibility to older GenericMotorControl.dll ﬁles which do not have
GetErrorMessage included.
The constructor public GenericMotorControl(int UID) gets the axis num ber (U ID) for identiﬁcation from  laserDESK . This U ID is
present as the ﬁrst param eter of all m ethods to sim plify the assignm ent of the com m and to the axis.
The axis num ber (U ID) is set by laserDESK  correspondent to the appearance of the axis in the m otor list in the Hardware Conﬁguration,
starting with 1.
In the exam ple below "M yM otor1" gets the U ID = 1 and "M yM otor2" the U ID = 2.

Note Because "FLEX" is no generic but a full supported m otor, it will not use the plug-in DLL and is therefore not taken into
account when assigning the num bers.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface

--- Page 118 ---

Properties and Requirements
All m otion com m and functions should return im m ediately, even if they are not executed yet. The (successful) execution is checked by
status queries. That requires that status (and position) queries have to be com m itted during a possibly running m otion.
The return value of a com m and should always return true if the com m and is accepted and is executed. Only if a requirem ent is not
fulﬁlled, the return value should be false.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 7/12

--- Page 119 ---

M achine Status Description
The status of an axis is described by state ﬂags:
Flag (Bit) M eaning Status
0 (0000 0001) Axis control active The DLL will always return this ﬂag (DLL 'is running')
1 (0000 0002)
M OT_INIT
Axis initialized Set, when InitMotor has been executed.
Reset, when InitMotor has to be called (in the beginning,
after DeInitMotor or after a fatal error).
2 (0000 0004)
M OT_REF_FOU ND
Reference run executed Set, when the axis knows the reference point.
If this ﬂag is not set, only the reference run can be executed
but no other m otion.
A successful reference run has to set this ﬂag.
3 (0000 0008)
M OT_ERROR
Error Axis is in an error state.
This ﬂag has to be reset by InitMotor or Reset prior to any
further m otion.
4 (0000 0010) Axis at the lower end switch Axis can only m ove in positive direction.
5 (0000 0020) Axis at the upper end switchAxis can only m ove in negative direction.
17 (0002 0000)
M OT_RU N_ACTIVE
Axis in m otion Only set when the axis is currently m oving (absolute, relative
or reference).
18 (0004 0000)
M OT_POS_ACK NOWLEDG E
Position reached Set, when the axis is at the com m anded position.
M ay not be set, if the 'axis in m otion' ﬂag is set.
These state ﬂags are returned by the G etStatus query.
Example: While a reference run is executed the state should be "0x00020003".
The state changes during a standard procedure are:
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 8/12

--- Page 120 ---

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 9/12

--- Page 121 ---

M otor Error Handling
If an error occurs, the state ﬂag 3 (0x08) has to be set. laserDESK  will abort any execution and displays an error m essage. To get speciﬁc
error inform ation, laserDESK  calls GetErrorMessage() and shows the returned error string. Therefore, the DLL is able to supply
detailed inform ation about the error. In the event of an error, the Autom atic M ode is switched oﬀ to allow the user to check for errors.
However, no further action is executed.
In this state you can try to com m and the axis again. If the status query no longer results in an error, you can continue without further
action. If the error condition persists, you m ust reinitialize the m otor controller. Then, laserDESK  will call DeInitM otor() and Reset()
explicitly to reset the error state and starts the initialization sequence again.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the General Motor Control Interface
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_GeneralMotorControlInterface.htm 10/12

--- Page 122 ---

Description of the Template Project
Note, contact SCANLAB for a Dem o Visual Studio Project which can be used to create a G eneric M otor Control DLL.
The tem plate project 'G enericM otorControl.sln' consists of the interface deﬁnition (G enericM otorInterface.cs), a base class with a
default im plem entation of the interface (G enericM otorControl.cs) and a class for the database of the m otor (Conﬁguration.cs).
You m ay add and change the code directly in the G enericM otorControl class or you m ay renam e it (for exam ple,
G enericM otorBaseControl) and use it as a base class for your own m otor control class and override the m ethods.
For a real m otor control you need to add a speciﬁc com m unication channel class (for exam ple, for a serial interface) and adapt the
m ethods to the speciﬁc controller.
The SetConﬁguration() and SetSpeed() m ethods usually do not need to be overridden.
You should use your own com m unication thread for the controller to be able to com m and the m otions and return status queries to
laserDESK  at the sam e tim e.

--- Page 123 ---

Deﬁning the M otor Control Axis
In order to use a generic m otor type with laserDESK , the user m ust not only write the DLL.
Additional, the hardware param eters (serial connection / com m unication interface, calibration factor, ...) m ust be deﬁned in the
Hardware Conﬁguration. Then these param eter settings can be transform ed to the DLL (by    public void SetConﬁguration(int U ID,
string Com Interface, double cal, int refType, double speed, double refSpeed, double acc, double refOﬀset);, see above).
Note For deﬁning the m otor control axis' hardware param eters, refer to 'M otor Control Axes' in Hardware Devices.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Logging Possibilities
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_LoggingPossibilities.htm 1/8

--- Page 125 ---

How to U se Logging Possibilities
Note "Logging of RTC Com m ands" functionality is covered in How to U se the Logging of RTC Com m ands.
Note For Data Stream ing (see Data Stream ing), you need to have protocol nodes in the job (= control node 'Protocol start'
and optionally control node 'Protocol end') (described in the following).
laserDESK  lets you log a large am ount of diﬀerent data. In principle 2 diﬀerent sources of the logged data are present ‒ the
connected scan head and the RTC board.
01. The scan head is the data source (only possible with iDRIVE axes)
To get these values you need to deﬁne the kind of value by the control_com m and and to set the signal source by the
set_trigger com m and.
02. The RTC board is the data source (always possible)
To get these values you need to deﬁne the signal source directly by the set_trigger com m and. The control_com m and is not
needed.
Logging is activated by the protocol start and stop nodes. M ost of the logging possibilities of the RTC are available.
Because the logging of positions is the m ost use case, this selection is sim pliﬁed in the '[2] Control' category by a drop-down
m enu under 'Protocol Type'.
Available entries are:
'Actual Positions'
'Target Positions'
'custom '
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Logging Possibilities
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_LoggingPossibilities.htm 2/8

--- Page 126 ---

'Actual Positions' and 'Target Positions'
'Actual Positions' require connected iDRIVE axes.
'Target Positions' are RTC output data and can be logged without connected axis.
For actual positions and target positions all necessary param eters are well deﬁned (see below) and no further user input is
necessary. Therefore, the param eter ﬁelds for individual settings are not visible. During execution laserDESK  detects the available
scan head(s) and sets the corresponding param eters.
For 'Actual Positions' and 'Target Positions' it is possible to log the values with or without transform ation. This can be deﬁned by
additional param eters
- 'Apply Back Transform ation' (for actual positions, see below)
- and 'Include Transform ation' (for Target Positions).
It is also possible to distinguish between m arks and jum ps, adjustable with the param eter Log J um ps (see below).
Note xy positions logged with z ≠ 0 in the 3D volum e are backwards transform ed into the (z = 0) plane![*]
W hen laserDESK  visualizes these positions, they are shifted (m ainly due to the stretch factors) with respect to their
original position (that is, away from  the center for z > 0).
[*]Source RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual, com m and get_transform  | get_transform_oﬀset:
"For backward transform ation of xy position values (Code Bit #0 = 0), only the z = 0 plane are transform ed. xy stretching and Z defocus due to z
deviations (particularly with non-F-Theta system s) are not taken into account."
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Logging Possibilities
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_LoggingPossibilities.htm 3/8

--- Page 127 ---

'custom '
If 'custom ' is selected, param eter values for each channel are present. U p to 4 diﬀerent signals using 8 channels can be logged and
adjusted individually. There, the 3 properties
‒ 'Source',
‒ 'G alvo Selection',
‒ and 'Signal'
allow to adjust the logging of each channel separately:
The value in the 'Source' param eter allows to select the data source:
‒ Head,
‒ RTC,
‒ Position or
‒ no source.
Norm ally, 'Head State' and 'RTC Param eter' are enough but to allow an easy logging of positions this selector is added. Dependent
on the source selection further param eters are available:
Position
This selection logs the actual positions of the connected iDRIVE axes. All param eters are deﬁned and no further param eter is
needed. Channel 1 reads back the x-position of scan head 1, channel 2 the y-position. If a 3D system  is used, channel 3 reads the z-
position, if 2 scan heads are used channel 3 and 4 read back the x,y position of the second scan head.
It is also possible to read back just one coordinate for a single axis m odule (SAM ).
The actual positions can be back transform ed. This backward transform ation can be controlled by the 'Apply Backward
Transform ation' param eter (displayed in the '[4] Back Transform ation' category) and is not channel selective.
Note It is only possible to use the last applied transform ation for backward transform ation.
If the transform ation has been changed during execution (by transform ation control nodes) all positions before the
last one are wrong back transform ed. Therefore, avoid transform ation control nodes when logging positions.
If channel 1 and 2 log positions, an additional general param eter 'Log J um ps' is available. If activated, the positions of jum ps and
m arks can be distinguished (see rem arks). This param eter needs an own logging channel. Therefore, channel 4 is blocked and

only one scan head (and additional the z-coordinate or one other signal) can be logged.
RTC Param eter
The logged values are param eters of the RTC which are always available (even without a connected iDRIVE system ). The kind of
param eter can be deﬁned by the 'Signal' property of the elem ent, especially it is possible to log the target positions to check the
calculated job data. For this selection the Signal property corresponds to the signal param eter of the com m and set_trigger (see
RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual, Delayed Short List Com m and set_trigger). The source is always the RTC, m aking the 'G alvo Selection'
param eter redundant.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Logging Possibilities
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_LoggingPossibilities.htm 6/8

--- Page 130 ---

Remarks for logging the Head State / Positions
If no iDRIVE system  is present, the Head State (including 'Actual Positions') can not be logged. Therefore, the logging signal of
channel 1 and 2 are set internally to the output position and channel 3 and 4 are not used (the sam e relation as for Positions
selection is used).
If Positions are logged, a back transform ation (deﬁned by the 'Apply Back Transform ation' param eter) m ay be executed. If a
3D system  is connected and used (job param eter U se 3D = "True"), the z-value is logged in channel3 (connected to head 2, x-
axis). This is autom atically detected by laserDESK .
To get real scan head position values without back transform ation you m ay set 'Apply Back Transform ations' to false (or
individually set for the corresponding channel the source to Head State and Signal to 1).
If channel 1 and 2 log Positions, the 'Log J um ps' param eter is available. If true, the LaserOn signal is logged in channel 4 to
distinguish between jum ps and m arks. Therefore, channel 4 is blocked and no other signal can be deﬁned there. Vice versa if
a signal is deﬁned for channel 4, 'Log J um ps' cannot be activated. The jum ps are displayed in the graphic view with
com plem entary color of the m arks.
If Positions are logged, a graphic ﬁle (plt) can be created. There the m ark vectors in 2D are saved. The ﬁle nam e can be
deﬁned in the 'G raphic File' property. This ﬁle can later on be reim ported into laserDESK  to com pare the deﬁned vectors with
the real scanner positions. The unit of the graphic ﬁle values are [µm ].
Inside the plt-ﬁle scaling param eters are deﬁned to adjust the ﬁle values to the default unit of the plt-ﬁle [0.025m m ]. Thus,
you can reim port the ﬁle without any scale or size.
All data can be saved into a data ﬁle (.csv) for further evaluation. Each channel is separated by a sem icolon. The ﬁle nam e can
be deﬁned in the 'Protocol File' property. For scan head status values raw values are logged (except [m m ] for positions). For
the unit of these values refer to the RTC and scan head speciﬁc m anuals. They m ay depend on the used hardware. If actual
positions or target positions are logged, the saved values are in [m m ]. If positions are logged by the selection 'U ser-deﬁned',
the saved values are in [bit]. To prevent overwriting the 'U nique Protocol Filenam e' param eter can be used. If it is true, a tim e
stam p is added to the csv-ﬁlenam e to m ake it unique.
It is not possible to log m ore than one speciﬁc data type from  one axis (= galvanom eter scanner).
Note If the speed dependent laser control is activated, the protocol node is ignored and a warning m essage is displayed in
the output window.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Logging Possibilities
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_LoggingPossibilities.htm 7/8

--- Page 131 ---

Extended Logging Options for RTC6 Boards | RTC6 Ethernet Boards
RTC5 boards can log 4 channels. In contrast, RTC6 boards | RTC6 Ethernet boards can log up to 8 channels. If you have selected an
RTC6 board | RTC6 Ethernet board, therefore, there are 4 additional protocol channels (com pared to an RTC5 board). Since only 4
channels can be used to log scan head data (one for each galvanom eter scanner), channel 1...channel 4 are suﬃcient. Accordingly,
channel 5...channel 8 can only log RTC board param eters. Therefore, they only need property 'Source' and property 'Signal'. If 'Log
J um ps' is enabled, channel 8 is used to log the LaserOn signal. Then you can record the y position of the 2nd scan head in channel
4, that is, the total positions of both scan heads as well as the jum ps. This is not possible with the RTC5 board.
If channel 5...channel 8 are not used, even with an RTC6 board | RTC6 Ethernet board, set_trigger4 is called instead of
set_trigger8. This increases the m easurem ent tim e and data count for each channel.
If you want to log jum ps in this case:
01. Deselect 'Log J um ps' (since channel 8 is used for this).
02. Set channel 4 to 'LaserOn (RTC param eter 0)'.
03. Set channel 8 to 'do not use'.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Vectorize Pixel Images
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Microvectors.htm 1/2

--- Page 133 ---

How to Vectorize Pixel Im ages
W ith an RTC6 board, laserDESK can convert a pixel im age into m icrovectors.The bitm ap will not use the RTC pixel output m ode,
but instead the bitm ap is converted into m icrovectors. Each pixel is executed as a burst of laser pulses (black) or no laser pulse
(white).
To vectorize a pixel im age, the bitm ap m ode 'Vectorized (up to 1 M Hz)' m ust be selected in the 'Pixel Im age Param eters' (see
param eter Bitm ap M ode):
Note The bitm ap m ode 'Vectorized' is m ainly intended for free running lasers where pulses on dem and are not available but
the laser pulse burst is activated by a gate signal (LaserOn).
It is not suited for lasers with q-switch delay or lasers using the ﬁrst pulse suppression.
Nevertheless, this m ode can be used for all laser types.
Note The bitm ap m ode 'Vectorized' can only be used with an RTC6 board (not with an RTC5 board).
Note for free running laser types (using laser m ode 4 or 6): Because the gate signal is not synchronized to the pulses of a free
running laser, at worst a pulse burst m ay vary by ±1. Therefore, we recom m end to use high frequencies and deﬁne som e pulses
for a pixel to m inim ize the eﬀect of one pulse m ore or less.
Note For YAG  m odes 1-3 and 5 the Laser1 signal is synchronized with the LaserOn signal and is shifted by the deﬁned Q-
switch delay value. The Q-Switch Delay will reduce the active LaserOn tim e and thus the deﬁned num ber of pulses. It
depends on your laser system  which Q-switch value is suited. If possible, use no delay.
Additionally to and only with the bitm ap m ode param eter 'Vectorized', the 'Pulses per Pixel' param eter is available instead of a
calibration table. It deﬁnes the am ount of laser pulses given out for a m arked pixel (one burst).
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK V1.6.25
© SCANLAB G m bH 2025

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use the Logging of RTC Commands
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_LoggingRTCCommands.htm 1/2

--- Page 135 ---

How to U se the Logging of RTC Com m ands
"Logging of RTC Com m ands" functionality...
Is only available with laserDESK  Prem ium  Edition ≥V1.6.15.0
G ives you insight to the RTC com m and sequences which are exactly used and thus:
Enables you to reuse them  in your own user program s
Facilitates the veriﬁcation of actually applied settings
Speeds up analyzing problem s
Is enabled (disabled) by selecting (clearing) the checkbox 'Log RTC Com m ands'
(under Edit > Options > G eneral, see Deﬁning the 'G eneral' Param eters).
There are 2 separate logging types:
RTC List Com m and Logging
Logs RTC list com m ands that have been uploaded to the RTC board for executing the current job
Occurs as soon as a job is executed in M anual M ode (not: Autom atic M ode)
Target ﬁle is RTCExecutionlist.txt (target path see Note on RTCExecutionlist.txt and DirectCm dListLog.txt)
RTC Control Com m and Logging
Logs RTC control com m ands that have been called for initializing and system  setup
However, not all RTC control com m ands are logged. Especially those for error handling and runtim e inform ation
(such as get_state or get_error) are called very often in general and would generate a large am ount of data
As soon as the checkbox 'Log RTC Com m ands' is selected, laserDESK  logs the RTC control com m and calls to an
internal list
Note, if checkbox 'Log RTC Com m ands' is selected when laserDESK  shuts down RTC Control Com m and Logging
even begins upon the next start.
To save this current internal list as DirectCmdListLog.txt (target path see Note on RTCExecutionlist.txt and
DirectCm dListLog.txt),
choose Tools > Control Com m and Logging (Save).
The internal list rem ains as is, that is, is not cleared and is continued to be used
To clear this current internal list, choose Tools > Control Com m and Logging (Start)
Note, som e RTC control com m ands return values. M ost of them  are also logged which is useful for detecting errors
in the system .
Sam ple line: "6/23/2022 12:22:22.817 PM : init_rtc_dll();"
[= tim estam p according to laserDESK  G U I language [m s]; com m and nam e; plus optionally: return value in "<= 0"
notation]
Note on RTCExecutionlist.txt and DirectCmdListLog.txt
If you want to keep an already existing ﬁle, you have to renam e or copy it.
This is because each of the 2 ﬁles exists only once and these are overwritten at any save operation
The path to them  are speciﬁed in the ﬁeld 'Log Files Path'
(under Edit > Options > Directories, see Deﬁning the Directories)
(original text - this is not a translation)
