How to Control a Laser System  with Laser W izard
Note For procedural inform ation see Laser W izard.
W ith Laser W izard you can:
Conﬁgure the control system  (RTC and PC) to control and m onitor any laser system
In addition, deﬁne som e special functions of laserDESK
Laser W izard can be opened by:
01. Dialog "Hardware Settings" > Processing Laser > button Laser W izard
02. M enu Hardware > Laser Control > Laser W izard
For a speciﬁc laser system , the following settings are possible with Laser W izard:
To specify control signals
To assign control signals to output ports
To specify m onitoring lines
To specify states associated with m onitoring lines
To specify laser system  switch-on sequence and switch-oﬀ sequence
To specify RTC settings for the laser system
Deﬁning the range lim its of the laser system
These settings are stored in a laser deﬁnition ﬁle *.sll and read back in with it.
Laser deﬁnition ﬁles *.sll are located in C:\Program Data\Scanlab\SLlaserDESK \Laserdeﬁnitions.
In "Dialog "Hardware Settings", the processing laser (and thus the laser deﬁnition ﬁle) is selected.
laserDESK  com es with som e predeﬁned laser deﬁnition ﬁles, see table below:
wiring diagram s in laserDESK  Help only ﬁt these and are only a SCANLAB suggestion.
For each m odiﬁed or newly created laser deﬁnition ﬁle *.sll, users m ust create the wiring diagram  them selves.
Notes on "Previous" Laser W izard Versions (laserDESK ≤ V1.6.18.0)
The code base of the laser control has been com pletely revised with laserDESK  V1.6.19.0.
W ith laserDESK  ≥ V1.6.19.0, there are no longer any functional diﬀerences between "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" and "Laser
of Type 'G eneral'"
Nevertheless, the established term inology is still used in this laserDESK  Help:
"Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" "Laser of Type 'G eneral'"
• Laser with status m onitoring • Laser without status m onitoring and without on sequence/oﬀ
sequence
• Laser with on/oﬀ sequence
• Laser with status m onitoring and on sequence/oﬀ sequence
(z.B. Trum pf_TruPulse_nano) Synonym : G eneralType, general laser type
See also "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" vs. "Laser of Type 'G eneral'".
The following applies:
Laser W izard ≥ V1.6.19.0 replaces the previous Laser W izard and Laser Param eter W izard in a fully backwards com patible
m anner.

How to Highlight the Currently Executed Elem ent
For long jobs in particular, it is interesting to see where laserDESK  is currently at in the job execution.
If required, laserDESK  can be set to highlight the currently executed elem ent in color.
The setting, see step 01 and step 02 below,
m ust have been m ade before creating the execution list
applies to each proﬁle (design proﬁle, production proﬁle etc.) separately, that is, not globally in laserDESK
Note The m axim um  num ber of highlighted elem ents is ≤ 2^24 - 1 (= 16,777,215).
In rare cases, elem ents - seem  - to be not highlighted in color.
The reason for this is a very short highlighting tim e of the elem ent (relative to the screen refresh rate).
Select (in step 01 below) a color that is not already used elsewhere (e.g. as a layer color).
This will m ake it easier to recognize the active elem ent.
Setting can be carried out independently at 2 locations:
01. U nder Edit > Options > Dialog "Options" > 'View' > select checkbox 'Active processed elem ent'.
Furtherm ore, you can specify a color.
Dialog "Options" > 'View' > checkbox 'Active processed elem ent'.
02. In W indow "Views" > 'G raphics' > click 'Active processed elem ent'.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Work with Remote Interface Mode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Remote_Interface_Mode.htm 1/5

--- Page 144 ---

How to W ork with Rem ote Interface M ode
Switching on Rem ote Interface M ode
Switching oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode Tem porarily
Switching oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode Perm anently
Note Rem ote Interface M ode serves to control RTC6 Ethernet boards without RTC6 DLL.
W hen Rem ote Interface M ode is switched on, the RTC6 Ethernet board no longer responds to RTC6 com m ands from  user
program s which includes laserDESK .
The Rem ote Interface M ode is switched on at every boot after a power cycle in standalone operating m ode. For this, the
RTC6 Ethernet board m ust be conﬁgured in "standalone full state" and the RTC6 com m and eth_set_remote_
tgm _form at is called in boot phase 1.
To exit this endless loop, either a. the boot sequence m ust be deleted from  the RTC6 Ethernet board or b. another boot
sequence m ust be written to the RTC6 Ethernet board NAND without calling the RTC6 com m and
eth_set_remote_tgm _form at.
For m ore inform ation, see the chapter on Rem ote Interface M ode in RTC6 M anual.
Switching on Rem ote Interface M ode
Prerequisites
The RTC6 Ethernet board is selected (M enu Hardware > RTC Board Selection), see Selecting an RTC Board.
The desired job is open (M enu File > Open > J ob > *.sld).
01. Choose M enu File > Open > Hardware Conﬁguration.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
02. Click 'RTC Boards'.
03. In group 'Ethernet Param eter', select Checkbox 'Activate Rem ote Interface' (see also Setting the Param eters 'RTC Board').
Checkbox 'Activate Rem ote Interface'
04. Click the button Apply.
05. Choose M enu View > Standalone Control.
The W indow "Standalone Control" opens.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Work with Remote Interface Mode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Remote_Interface_Mode.htm 2/5

--- Page 145 ---

W indow "Standalone Control"
06. Click the button Download Data.
A m essage opens:
"Warning: You will activate the remote interface. The board will not be accessible via the RTC6DLL and laserDESK
anymore. Do you really want to activate?"
07. Click the button OK.
08. Disconnect the RTC6 Ethernet board from  the power supply for a short tim e and reconnect it (="power cycle").
The RTC6 Ethernet board boots. Boot tim e depends on job size and can take up to 2 m inutes.
After that, Rem ote Interface M ode is active. As a result, per design, the RTC6 Ethernet board no longer responds to laserDESK .
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Work with Remote Interface Mode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Remote_Interface_Mode.htm 3/5

--- Page 146 ---

Switching oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode Tem porarily
You can disable the rem ote interface m ode in laserDESK  in the board selection. You can then access the RTC6 Ethernet board again
until a reboot takes place (power cycle).
The button Deactivate Remote Interface has no eﬀect on an RTC6 Ethernet board that has been acquired by another user
program  and does not have an active Rem ote Interface M ode.
01. Choose M enu Hardware > RTC Board Selection.
The RTC6 Ethernet board is shown in the Dialog "Select Board" (if not: click button Search ﬁrst).
Note its status in RED in colum n 'Select'.
Dialog "Select Board"
02. Select the RTC6 Ethernet board (click row in the table).
The button Deactivate Remote Interface is now shown.
03. Click button Deactivate Remote Interface.
Rem ote Interface M odus is now tem porarily deactivated.
IM PORTANT: At this point, the RTC6 com m and eth_set_remote_tgm _form at still is in the boot sequence of the RTC6
Ethernet board.
Correspondingly, after a power cycle, Rem ote Interface M ode would be switched back on.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Work with Remote Interface Mode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Remote_Interface_Mode.htm 4/5

--- Page 147 ---

Switching oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode Perm anently
G oal: no Remote Interface M ode anym ore, but Standalone M ode active.
Here, the existing boot sequence is overwritten with another boot sequence, but it no longer contains a
eth_set_remote_tgm _form at call.
01. Clear Checkbox 'Activate Rem ote Interface'.
02. Switch oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode tem porarily, see Switching oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode Tem porarily.
03. M ake sure the desired J ob for Standalone M ode is open in laserDESK .
04. Click button Download Data.
05. Disconnect the RTC6 Ethernet board from  the power supply for a short tim e and reconnect it (="power cycle").
The RTC6 Ethernet board boots. After that it is in "standalone full state" (no Rem ote Interface M ode).
G oal: no Remote Interface M ode anym ore, no Standalone M ode anym ore.
Here, the existing boot sequence is deleted.
01. Clear Checkbox 'Activate Rem ote Interface'.
02. Switch oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode tem porarily, see Switching oﬀ Rem ote Interface M ode Tem porarily.
03. In W indow "Standalone Control", click button Clear Data.
Now, the RTC6 Ethernet board can be used as usual in laserDESK  (no Rem ote Interface M ode anym ore, no Standalone M ode
anym ore, even after a power cycle).
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Mark on 3D Surfaces
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Mark_on_3D_Surfaces.htm 1/7

--- Page 149 ---

How to M ark on 3D Surfaces
Compare to: How to Use 3D Functions
Prerequisites
laserDESK  Premium
RTC5 board | RTC6 board with enabled option "3D"
Functionality
A 2D marking pattern is projected onto a 3D surface, and later the workpiece is marked exactly there.
When marking, the z-focus is adjusted at each position by projecting onto the workpiece surface.
For this, z-values are taken from a 3D correction ﬁle that has been previously created speciﬁcally for the workpiece ("free-form surface
calibration", see About G enerating the New 3D Correction File ).
Example: Imported 3D surface (freeform surface in its own layer 'Surface', brown) which is not marked. A positioned 2D marker element (text "Head", blue) isprojected onto it. The 2 reference stamps (lower edge of the picture) have been imported as well. They have a known distance in x and are outside (!) of the actual3D object.
Basic Workﬂow
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Mark on 3D Surfaces
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Mark_on_3D_Surfaces.htm 2/7

--- Page 150 ---

Basic Workﬂow: Freeform Surface Import
Step 01 ‒ Provide Workpiece as an stl File
Create an stl ﬁle of your workpiece using a suitable CAD program.
If your workpiece does not have deﬁned external dimensions, store reference points in the stl ﬁle with your CAD program. These
must be outside the actual object. Note their distance (for the Dialog "Import STL-File").
Step 02 ‒ Import stl File to laserDESK
laserDESK  extracts a 3D point cloud from the stl ﬁle, in which only the points relevant to laserDESK  are on the freeform surface.
The new 3D correction ﬁle (POST *.ct5) is generated from the 3D point cloud and the output correction ﬁle (PRE *.ct5). See also
About G enerating the New 3D Correction File .
IM PORTANT: This new 3D correction ﬁle is speciﬁc to this 3D interface and can only be used for it.
In addition, the 'Surface' layer is created in laserDESK  and the 3D surface is displayed in it.
IM PORTANT: As soon as positions change in the real work area (that is, the workpiece itself or its orientation), then you must
create a 3D correction ﬁle again.
Note: After importing the stl ﬁle, save the job with the 'Surface' layer. This job can serve as the starting point for all marking jobs
on the deﬁned 3D surface.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Mark on 3D Surfaces
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Mark_on_3D_Surfaces.htm 3/7

--- Page 151 ---

Step 03 ‒ Deﬁne and place any 2D marking patterns in laserDESK
2D labeling patterns can even be: characters, barcodes and ﬁllings.
laserDESK  projects the 2D marking patterns (orthogonally) onto the 3D surface.
2D marking patterns can be easily exchanged in laserDESK .
All laserDESK  functions for ﬁlling can be used.
Any laserDESK  2D function can be used.
Step 04 ‒ Use laserDESK to mark the workpiece
When marking, laserDESK  replaces all positions according to: Position(x ,y, 0) = > position(x, y, z-value from the new 3D correction
ﬁle).
The workpiece in the working ﬁeld and the imported 3D surface in laserDESK  must match exactly: positioning equal, alignment
equal.
During a job execution, all positions and all associated microsteps are transformed. They receive their z-values from the 3D
correction ﬁle (see RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual, chapter on breaking it down into microsteps).
Therefore, there is no restriction for long vectors. Even these are projected and marked exactly onto the 3D surface, as each
microstep receives its z-coordinate from the 3D correction ﬁle.
IM PORTANT: Distortion of the marking is only to be expected at more inclined workpiece areas.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Mark on 3D Surfaces
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Mark_on_3D_Surfaces.htm 4/7

--- Page 152 ---

About Generating the New 3D Correction File
During "free-form surface calibration" (see Step 02 ‒ Import stl File to laserDESK ) a new 3D correction ﬁle is created that is speciﬁc to a
workpiece.
The correction ﬁle currently used in laserDESK  ("initial correction ﬁle") serves as the basis.
Therefore, the following applies:
The initial correction ﬁle must match the hardware used (and could have been generated previously by 3D-Calibration Wizard)
The initial correction ﬁle and its corresponding ReadM e ﬁle must be in the same directory
The new 3D correction ﬁle is created in the directory of the initial correction ﬁle.
naming convention: [initial correction ﬁle name]_[stl ﬁle name]
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Mark on 3D Surfaces
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Mark_on_3D_Surfaces.htm 5/7

--- Page 153 ---

Procedure in laserDESK
Prerequisites
See About G enerating the New 3D Correction File .
Steps
See also Comments.
Dialog "Import STL-File"
01. Choose M enu Hardware > Adjustment  > 3D Surface Deﬁnition.
The Dialog "Import STL-File" opens.
02. In the ﬁeld 'STL - Filename', specify the ﬁle name.
03. Case a. Reference points with a known distance are stored in the stl ﬁle (as explained above).
In the group 'Size', select the "Fixed" entry in the list 'Aspect ratio' and enter the values in the corresponding ﬁeld (here: 'Length x',
see example Basic Workﬂow: Freeform Surface Import). The other two values (here: for y and z) are later calculated automatically.
Case b. There are no reference points with a known distance in the stl ﬁle.
Enter the 3 values for 'Length x', 'Width x', 'Height z'.
04. With the 'Position' group, you specify the position of the base point (= center point in the lowest z-plane) of the 3D object in the
scan head coordinate system.
The aim is to match the position and alignment of the imported free-form surface and the real workpiece as precisely as possible
at this step (necessary for the subsequent laser process). Reference points on the workpiece and in the stl ﬁle can also support
this.
05. Click the button OK.
The new correction ﬁle is generated and loaded, but only used until exit of laserDESK  though.
The 3D surface is displayed in a separate 'Surface' layer.
06. For permanent use, enter the new correction ﬁle in the hardware conﬁguration and save it: CTRL + H > Dialog "Hardware Settings"
> Hardware Settings > Scan Head [1] > ﬁeld 'Correction ﬁle'.
If hardware conﬁguration has been open prior to step 05, the new correction ﬁle has been automatically entered in step 05 (and
can be permanently saved with closing the hardware conﬁguration).
07. IM PORTANT: stl ﬁle, imported 3D surface and new correction ﬁle form a unit in terms of content. However, laserDESK  users must
take this dependency into account themselves.
Example: If you were to change the new correction ﬁle, the 'Surface' object would also become invalid, that is, laserDESK  would
not automatically adjust the correction ﬁle if you were to reload the job.
08. Place the desired 2D marking elements. The 'Surface' layer facilitates precise placement.
Furthermore, you can use all 2D functions of laserDESK .
09. Execute the selection.
As with the display of the work surface, the 'Surface' layer is not executed.
Comments
Regarding step 04: Deﬁne the z = 0 position of the 3D surface in the Dialog "Import STL-File" and not only after the import:
Do not use a z-focus control node and no z-oﬀset in the hardware conﬁguration to position the 3D surface in z.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Mark on 3D Surfaces
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Mark_on_3D_Surfaces.htm 6/7

--- Page 154 ---

Regarding step 03 and step 04: After the import, the 3D surface cannot be rotated in laserDESK . Therefore, make sure that the 3D
object (in the stl ﬁle) is rotated to the same position as the workpiece during import: Dialog "Import STL-File", ﬁeld 'Rotation'.
Regarding step 06: If you want to use a 3D surface marking in a production with a deﬁned workpiece position, you should
permanently save the new 3D correction ﬁle in the hardware conﬁguration. Then no adjustment is necessary after a laserDESK
restart. Please note that all jobs used can only mark correctly on this 3D surface and only on this speciﬁc workpiece.
Regarding step 08: Do not use any 3D marking elements.
Regarding step 08: Avoid deﬁning 2D marking elements on steeply inclined surfaces. The marking will be distorted there. You may
have to repeat the entire process with the rotated stl ﬁle 3D object and workpiece in order to achieve a more vertical marking.
Regarding step 08: Always set the job parameter Use 3D = "True", even if you are only deﬁning 2D objects.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Activate Data Streaming by laserDESK
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Activate_Data_Streaming.htm 1/3

--- Page 156 ---

How to Activate Data Stream ing by laserDESK
Note Data stream ing is only supported by RTC6 Ethernet boards.
Note In addition to laserDESK , you will also need another software that has the data stream ing counterpart im plem ented.
StreamParser.dll (https://www.scanlab.de/en/stream parser) provides the necessary API to integrate the functions
into your counterpart.
For m ore inform ation, refer to:
Chapter on data stream ing in RTC6 M anual
M anual "Stream Parser-DLL ‒ Application Program m ing Interface" (part of Stream Parser Software package)
Note For Data Stream ing, the job needs to have protocol nodes deﬁned (= control node 'Protocol start' and optionally
control node 'Protocol end'), see How to U se Logging Possibilities.
In laserDESK , data stream ing is activated in the Dialog "Hardware Settings" > page 'M iscellaneous' > group 'Data Stream ing ' >
checkbox 'Activate Data Stream ing'. Optionally, the data packet size can be set by the list box 'Package Size'.
In order to start data transm itting a control node 'Protocol Start' m ust also be integrated into the job, see How to U se Logging
Possibilities. This can be used to specify which data is transferred.
The data is sent the ﬁrst tim e the job is executed (after reaching the protocol node). If there is no control node 'Protocol End'
in the job, the sending of the data continues after execution has ended. This m eans that status inform ation can be obtained
even without job execution.
A control node 'Protocol End' in the job execution stops data stream ing. Data stream ing can also be stopped by reinitializing
the hardware.
Running another control node 'Protocol Start' restarts data stream ing, with changed data if applicable.
Notes on laserDESK protocol function in connection with data stream ing
If the control node 'Protocol Start' and the control node 'Protocol End' are present in the job: the log is recorded and
evaluated independently of data stream ing.
If the control node 'Protocol End' is m issing: saving and sending of the data continues. At the end of the job, laserDESK  reads
out the data and uses it as usual.
However, when reading out the data, som e values have already been saved after the end of the job. laserDESK  needs about
70 m s to read the data from  the RTC6 Ethernet board m em ory.
These are about 7000 data records (at a sam pling rate of 10 μs) that continue to be logged after the job has ended and data
stream ing is active. These data records can be ignored during an evaluation.
In the case of long recordings, values can also be overwritten. The evaluation of this data is not easy in this case and it is not
recom m ended to use data stream ing and laserDESK  data recording at the sam e tim e.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Activate Data Streaming by laserDESK
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Activate_Data_Streaming.htm 2/3

--- Page 157 ---

Procedure in laserDESK
Prerequisites
The RTC6 Ethernet board is selected (M enu Hardware > RTC Board Selection), see Selecting an RTC Board.
The desired job is open (M enu File > Open > J ob > *.sld) and contains at least 1 control node 'Protocol Start'.
Steps
01. Choose M enu File > Open > Hardware Conﬁguration.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
02. Click 'M iscellaneous'.
03. In group 'Data Stream ing', select checkbox Activate Data Stream ing.
Checkbox 'Activate Data Stream ing'
04. Click the button Save and Exit.
05. Execute the job to start data stream ing.
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Analog Input
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Analog_Input.htm 1/7

--- Page 159 ---

How to U se Analog Input
The Control Elem ent "Analog Input" can be used to read in a voltage that is applied to an analog input ("analog voltage"), see
Prerequisites.
Control Elem ent "Analog Input"
This analog voltage can be used:
To set the m arking plane (z position), see 'Adapt the focus distance'
To m ove m arking pattern (x position, y position), see 'x,y-Oﬀset'
To rotate the m arking pattern around the origin, see 'Apply Rotation'
To change the laser power, see 'Adapt Power'
Note For inform ation on deﬁning the Control Elem ent "Analog Input" (in W indow "Properties"), see G raphic Param eters ‒
Analog Input.
Prerequisites
The Control Elem ent "Analog Input" is available for:
RTC6 PCI Express Boards
RTC6 Ethernet Boards
The Control Elem ent "Analog Input" is not available for:
RTC5 Boards
Control Elem ent "Analog Input" uses (alternatively):
RTC6 PCI Express Board > socket connector M cBSP/ANALOG  > pin (01) = input port ANALOG  IN0
RTC6 PCI Express Board > socket connector M cBSP/ANALOG  > pin (02) = input port ANALOG  IN1
RTC6 Ethernet Board > socket connector M cBSP/ANALOG  > pin (01) = input port ANALOG  IN0
RTC6 Ethernet Board > socket connector M cBSP/ANALOG  > pin (02) = input port ANALOG  IN1
The resolution of the analog voltage is:
1/409.5 V = 2.44 m V (12 bit)
A Control Elem ent "Analog Input" can be inserted like all other control elem ents (for exam ple, see also Inserting a Control Elem ent
"Ram ping") by:
Toolbar Control Elem ents
M enu Tools > Control Elem ents
W indow "Toolbox"
Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) of J ob Explorer
Events when Reaching an Control Elem ent "Analog Input" During J ob Execution
01. List execution is interrupted.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Analog Input
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Analog_Input.htm 2/7

--- Page 160 ---

02. The analog voltage is read in.
03. The function set by the Param eter "Applied Function" is executed.
04. List execution is continued.
Param eter "Applied Function"
'Adapt the focus distance' sets the m arking plane (z position)
'x,y-Oﬀset' m oves the m arking pattern (x position, y position)
'Apply Rotation' rotates the m arking pattern around the origin
'Adapt Power' changes the laser power
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Analog Input
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Analog_Input.htm 3/7

--- Page 161 ---

'Adapt the focus distance'
Param eter "Applied Function" > 'Adapt the focus distance'
The analog voltage deﬁnes the z position of the m arking. This allows the working distance to be set during job execution, for
exam ple, by a distance sensor.
The read in analog voltage m ust:
In the desired z range, be always in the 0 V... 10 V range
Be linearly dependent on the z oﬀset,
according to z = k * (U  ‒ U 0 with
z [m m ],
k = Calibration factor [m m /V],
U  = m easured voltage [V],
U 0 = Z-Oﬀset [V] = voltage [V] at z = 0
Calibration factor [m m /V] deﬁnes the z oﬀset in [m m ] to be perform ed per volt.
Z-Oﬀset [V] deﬁnes the analog voltage in the z = 0 plane. It can even be speciﬁed outside the 0 V... 10 V range. Then you can
not get to the z = 0 plane anym ore, but you can set very ﬁne relative position changes within the available range.
Higher analog voltages m oves the focus in the positive z direction (opposite to the laser beam ), thus shortening the focus
distance. A negative Calibration factor [m m /V] reverses this behavior.
W ith 2D jobs, the analog voltage deﬁnes a new z-plane. All subsequent laser processes are carried out in this z plane. This
corresponds to a control elem ent "z-Focus Control" with dynam ic z value. Therefore, after executing a sim ple 3D object (for
exam ple, spiral/helix) in a standard 2D job, the current z position can no longer be set. U se a 3D job instead.
Control elem ent "z-Focus Control" and Control Elem ent "Analog Input" override each other's current z value.
W ith 3D jobs, the analog voltage deﬁnes a new z oﬀset to the z coordinates of all objects in the job. A z oﬀset speciﬁed in the
Hardware Conﬁguration is thus tem porarily overwritten. A z value deﬁned by a control elem ent "z-Focus Control" or a level
causes an additional z oﬀset and is not overridden by the Control Elem ent "Analog Input".
In M anual M ode, the z value is reset when a new m arker is started.
In Autom atic M ode, the z value rem ains valid until another Control Elem ent "Analog Input" is executed or Autom atic M ode
exits.
Deﬁne focus distance adjustm ent using the analog voltage only. That is, do not use z oﬀset in the Hardware Conﬁguration to
do this. Set Z-Oﬀset [V]without z-oﬀset.
Do not use a control elem ent "z-Focus Control" and Control Elem ent "Analog Input" together in a job.
The Control Elem ent "Analog Input" can be used in conjunction with the Transform  control when it is ﬁrst executed.
Do not use 'Adapt the focus distance' together with a 3D surface m arking.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Analog Input
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Analog_Input.htm 4/7

--- Page 162 ---

'x,y-Oﬀset'
Param eter "Applied Function" > 'x,y-Oﬀset'
The analog voltage at input ANALOG  IN0 deﬁnes an oﬀset in x.
The analog voltage at input ANALOG  IN1 deﬁnes an oﬀset in y.
Calibration factor [m m /V] deﬁnes the oﬀset in [m m ] to be carried out per volt (equal for x axis and y axis).
X-Oﬀset [V] deﬁnes the analog voltage that is applied, if no oﬀset in the x direction is to occur. It can be speciﬁed even outside
the 0 V... 10 V range. Then you have a constant oﬀset around this value, by which you can vary over the applied analog
voltage.
Y-Oﬀset [V] is like X-Oﬀset [V].
The set 'x,y-Oﬀset' corresponds to the function of a control elem ent "Transform ation" and acts in addition to the value
speciﬁed in the Hardware Conﬁguration. Oﬀsets caused by a control elem ent "Transform ation" and Control Elem ent "Analog
Input" override each other. Therefore, they should not be used together.
NOTICE! Possible dam age to the scan head. Specify the range so that laserDESK  cannot get outside the work area, as this is
not m onitored.
To apply 'x,y-Oﬀset' and 'Apply Rotation', use 2 × Control Elem ent "Analog Input" for to read in 1 analog voltage each.
laserDESK  retains the other value. After the job has been executed (in Autom atic M ode or M anual M ode), the oﬀset and
rotation are reset again.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Analog Input
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Analog_Input.htm 5/7

--- Page 163 ---

'Apply Rotation'
Param eter "Applied Function" > 'Apply Rotation''
The analog voltage at input ANALOG  IN0 rotates the m arking by the speciﬁed value.
Calibration factor [°/V] deﬁnes the rotation in [°] to be carried out per volt.
Zero Rotation [V] deﬁnes the analog voltage that is applied when no rotation is to be carried out (rotation angle 0°). It can be
speciﬁed even outside the 0 V... 10 V range. Then a rotation deﬁnitely occurs and furtherm ore, you can set very ﬁne rotations
within the available range.
To apply 'x,y-Oﬀset' and 'Apply Rotation', use 2 × Control Elem ent "Analog Input" to read in 1 analog voltage each. laserDESK
retains the other value. After the job has been executed (in Autom atic M ode or M anual M ode), the oﬀset and rotation are
reset. Rotation is perform ed before oﬀset.
This rotation is in addition to the value speciﬁed in the Hardware Conﬁguration.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 How to Use Analog Input
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/C_How_to_Topics/How_to_Analog_Input.htm 6/7

--- Page 164 ---

'Adapt Power'
Param eter "Applied Function" > 'Adapt Power'
The analog voltage at input ANALOG  IN0 varies the currently set laser power by the speciﬁed value.
Power Adaption U nit [%/V] deﬁnes the change in [%] to be perform ed per volt. The 100% value is deﬁned by the currently set
power value (perform ance in the m arking param eters of the previous control elem ent).
Original Power Level [V] deﬁnes the analog voltage at which no power change should occur. It can be speciﬁed even outside
the 0 V... 10 V range. Then a change of power deﬁnitely occurs and furtherm ore, you can set very ﬁne power adjustm ents
within the available range.
The power adjustm ent is done with the current perform ance, that is, the perform ance of the currently executed control
elem ent or the job node. Therefore, subsequent control elem ents m ust use the sam e power. Otherwise, this power
adjustm ent is overwritten im m ediately. Therefore, use 'Adapt Power' only for jobs with constant power.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 Installing laserDESK
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/B_Start_Topics/Installing_laserDESK.htm 2/4

--- Page 167 ---

Requirem ents
Before installing laserDESK , m ake sure that the PC on which laserDESK  is installed m eets the following requirem ents:
The PC m ust run M icrosoft W indows 10 or newer 64-bit (with M icrosoft .NET Fram ework 4.5).
Required hard disk space is approx. 350 M B (incl. Help ﬁle)
To run a laserDESK  job, it is essential that the PC controlling the laser scan system  (production PC) either contains a SCANLAB
RTC5 board | RTC6 board or is connected to an a SCANLAB RTC6 Ethernet board.
Note The RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual detail how to install the RTC5 board | RTC6 board and its associated software.
Note It is also possible to install both an RTC5 and an RTC6 board together in the PC.
At program  start, the required RTC board can be selected (see Starting laserDESK ).
Note For creating jobs, an RTC5 board | RTC6 board is not necessary. J obs can be created on any PC without an RTC
board and run later on the production PC (with RTC board, laserDESK  software and dongle).
The RTC5 board | RTC6 board can be conﬁgured for optional functionality not available in the standard version. This optional
functionality m ust be enabled by SCANLAB or installed by the custom er.
The following RTC options are available:
‒ Processing-on-the-ﬂy (POF),
‒ 3D (for controlling a 3-axis scan system ),
‒ Second Scan Head Control (for controlling two XY scan system s sim ultaneously),
‒ Optoelectronic Couplers (for optodecoupling the laser control signals),
‒ SCANahead (only RTC6) (if using an SCANahead system ).
For details, see the 'Optional Functionality' chapter of the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Note The 3D display is based on W PF (W indows Presentation Foundation) functions. These functions are executed
properly only with 32 or 16 Bit color depth setting (color quality) in the desktop properties. U sing the laserDESK
3D option or if 3D display is required, your m onitor m ust use one of these settings.
Note For the RTC6 board the control com m and set_rtc5_m ode has been rem oved and all z values and laser delays of
the RTC6 are used with higher resolution (20 bit).
Ensure that the laser is connected correctly to the RTC5 board | RTC6 board's connectors (see Connecting Lasers to RTC
Boards).
After laserDESK  is installed, laserDESK  users m ust get speciﬁc write and/or read perm issions. Because the setup routine cannot
assign write and read perm issions, the custom er's system  adm inistrator m ust ensure that the laserDESK  users get the required
write and/or read perm issions depending on their level of laserDESK  access authorization (see below).
Before starting the program , the appropriate dongle (software protection device) m ust be inserted to run laserDESK  in full m ode,
that is, unrestricted. W ithout dongle, laserDESK  can only used in dem o m ode. In dem o m ode, no saving or m arking is allowed,
thus no laser jobs can be executed.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 Installing laserDESK
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/B_Start_Topics/Installing_laserDESK.htm 3/4

--- Page 168 ---

Installing laserDESK
01. Insert the laserDESK  data CD into the PC's CD/DVD drive.
An Installation W izard starts autom atically.
02. Otherwise, run "laserDESK x64_installer.exe" from  the software package directory.
03. Installation then proceeds autom atically by the W indows Installer.
Follow the W izard's instructions until the installation is com pleted.
Assigning Read and Write Perm issions
W rite and read perm issions m ust be assigned by the custom er's system  adm inistrator:
01. Assign write and read perm issions for the laserDESK  data directory - typically C:\ProgramData\Scanlab\SLLaserDESK - to
each user with laserDESK  "Adm inistrator", "Supervisor" or "Designer" access authorization.
02. Assign read perm issions only to each other user ("Production" and "Viewer" access authorization) for the aforem entioned
directory.
Inserting the Dongle
Insert the appropriate dongle in any U SB port of the PC that is running laserDESK  in full m ode (PC with or without RTC5 board
| RTC6 board) latest before starting the program .
Note To start laserDESK , see Starting laserDESK .

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 Starting laserDESK
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/B_Start_Topics/Starting_laserDESK.htm 1/5

--- Page 170 ---

Starting laserDESK
Prerequisites
Starting laserDESK  the First Tim e after Installation
Starting laserDESK
Prerequisites
To create a laserDESK  job on any PC, the following requirem ents m ust be m et:
laserDESK  m ust be properly installed (for details, see Installing the laserDESK  software).
Full m ode program  usage requires that the legal dongle is inserted in any U SB port of the PC.
W ithout dongle, laserDESK  can only be used in dem o m ode without allowing saving or m arking (a conﬁrm ation m essage
opens upon laserDESK  start).
Note If the dongle has been disconnected from  the PC while running laserDESK  in full m ode, the program  will switch to
dem o m ode autom atically.
To run laserDESK  in full m ode again, the program  m ust be restarted with the dongle inserted in any U SB port.
The user m ust have speciﬁc write and/or read perm issions appropriate for their level of laserDESK  access authorization (for
details, see Installing the laserDESK  software).
In addition to the above requirem ents, the production PC m ust fulﬁll the following prerequisites for executing a laserDESK  job:
To control the laser scan system , a SCANLAB RTC5 board | RTC6 board m ust be properly installed or a SCANLAB RTC6 Ethernet
board m ust be connected (for details, see Installing the laserDESK  Software).
Note W hen laserDESK  is started and several RTC boards (RTC5 and/or RTC6) are installed in the PC, the dialog "Select
Board" opens and the board can be selected.
Because laserDESK  will rem em ber the last selected board and tries to acquire it again, the selection dialog is no
m ore displayed.
If this board is not found, no board is acquired and you need to open the dialog "Select Board" m anually to select
another board.
If no board was selected the last tim e, this dialog will appear again.
If necessary, RTC5 board or RTC6 board options (POF, 3D, Second Scan Head Control, Optoelectronic Couplers, SCANahead)
m ust be enabled (for details, see Installing the laserDESK  software).
Ensure that the laser is connected correctly to the RTC board connectors (see Connecting Lasers and RTC Boards).

Starting laserDESK the First Tim e after Installation
After laserDESK  is installed, it is started as follows:
01. Run SLLaserDesk.exe directly from  the laserDESK  program  directory (typically C:\Programs\Scanlab\laserDESK).
The Dialog "SCANLAB laserDESK " opens.
02. Enter „Adm in" in the 'U sernam e' input ﬁeld but no password. This is the default user.
Dialog "SCANLAB laserDESK "
03. Click the button OK.
‒ If only one RTC board (RTC5 or RTC6) is installed in the PC, the program  window opens im m ediately. Continue with step 6.
‒ If both an RTC5 board and an RTC6 board are installed in the PC, the dialog "Select Board" opens. Continue with step 4.
04. Select the required RTC board by activating the corresponding check box in the 'Select' colum n.
How to search an RTC6 Ethernet board see Selecting an RTC Board.
Dialog "Select Board"
05. Click the button OK.
06. W hen the program  window opens, a new job is autom atically created.
The user interface does not yet contain m arking objects.
Depending on the used RTC board (see above), the laserDESK  output window shows (i.a.) "U sed DLL: .\RTC5DLL.DLL" or
"U sed DLL: .\RTC6DLL.DLL".
Note Following the ﬁrst tim e start the following steps to conﬁgure the laser scan system  should be perform ed:
- Assigning the RTC correction ﬁle (see Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters)
- Deﬁning the used laser type (see Setting 'Processing Laser' Param eters)

Starting laserDESK
laserDESK  can be started as follows:
Note If the last user, who has closed the program , has a password (see Deﬁning U sers' Access Authorization), the "SCANLAB
laserDESK " dialog opens. Autom atically, the respective nam e is shown in the ﬁeld 'U sernam e'. All users can log in by
entering the own nam e and ‒ if required ‒ the own password.
If the last user, who has closed the program , has no password, the program  window opens im m ediately. All individual
settings of the last user is inherited. If required, you have to log in with your user nam e (see New U ser Log-in).
Note If an additional RTC board (RTC5 and/or RTC6) is installed in the m eantim e, the dialog "Select Board" (see Starting
laserDESK  the First Tim e after Installation) is displayed autom atically when starting laserDESK  subsequently.
01. Run SLLaserDesk.exe
‒ directly from  the laserDESK  program  directory (typically C:\Programs\Scanlab\laserDESK)
‒ or by a link/icon.
If the last user has no password, the program  window opens im m ediately. Continue with step 5.
If the last user has a password, the Dialog "SCANLAB laserDESK " opens.
02. Enter your user nam e in the 'U sernam e' input ﬁeld.
Dialog "SCANLAB laserDESK "
03. If required, enter your password in the 'Password' input ﬁeld.
04. Click the button OK.
05. W hen the program  window opens, a new job is autom atically created.
The user interface does not yet contain m arking objects.
The output window shows (i.a.) the following inform ation:
‒ "U sed DLL: .\RTC5DLL.DLL" or "U sed DLL: .\RTC6DLL.DLL", depending on the used RTC board (see Starting laserDESK  the
First Tim e after Installation).
‒ "SCANahead system  detected.", if an RTC6 board is used and a SCANahead system  is connected and 'SCANahead Control
Support' has been selected (see Setting the Param eters 'Optics') or
‒ "No SCANahead system  connected.", if an RTC6 board is used and 'SCANahead Control Support' has been activated, but no
SCANahead system  is connected.
Additionally, a window with tips opens autom atically. W ith Next you can scroll forward, with the button OK you can close the
window. If you do not want the window to be displayed at each start, clear the checkbox 'Show Tips at Startup'.
06. If you want to switch between the RTC5 board and RTC6 board, select it by RTC Board Selection in the M enu Hardware.
07. To open an existing job ([J obNam e].sld),
‒ select a recent ﬁle listed in the M enu File (if applicable)
‒ or choose M enu File > Open > J ob and then the desired ﬁle in the M S W indows-standard dialog "Open".
The program  window shows any already-created job.

Logging-on as a New U ser
The access to laserDESK is assigned to each user by the adm inistrator (see Deﬁning U sers' Access Authorization). If a new user
takes over laserDESK from  an previous user without exiting laserDESK, he m ust log in with his user nam e and, if required, his
password.
Note W hen you have ﬁnished using laserDESK, you should log in as 'Viewer' for locking laserDESK functions or to exit
laserDESK.
For log-in the corresponding dialog m ust be opened:
To log in:
01. Choose M enu Edit > Log In As.
The dialog "SCANLAB laserDESK" opens.
02. Enter your user nam e into the corresponding input ﬁeld.
03. If required, enter your password into the corresponding input ﬁeld.
04. Click the button OK.
The dialog closes and you are logged in.
Note If you cannot log in (for exam ple, if you have forgotten your password), contact your adm inistrator.
In the worst case, you need to reinstall laserDESK to get the default login back.

Changing the Password
Access to laserDESK can be perm itted by password only. Passwords can be assigned by the adm inistrator individually to each user
or for one user group (see Section 'U ser and G roups'). The assigned password can be changed later by the users them selves in the
Dialog "Change Password - <user nam e>".
Dialog "Change Password - <user nam e>"
Note U sers can change the password only when they are logged in with their user nam e (see Starting laserDESK or Logging-
on as a New U ser ). But all passwords can be changed by the adm inistrator!
To change the password:
01. Choose M enu Edit > Change Password.
The Dialog "Change Password - <user nam e>" opens.
02. Enter your old Password into the corresponding text ﬁeld.
03. Enter your new Password into the corresponding text ﬁeld.
04. Repeat the new Password into the corresponding text ﬁeld.
05. Click the button OK.
The Dialog "Change Password - <user nam e>" closes and the new password is valid.

G U I Elem ents
laserDESK  graphical user interface (G U I) is optim ized for fast user access to needed functions. It oﬀers all functions for fast and
easy creation of a job and consists of several elem ents.
# Element Nam e Description
[1] W ork Area The m ain G U I elem ent. Serves to create and m anually edit m arking objects. The
content shown in the W ork Area is a "picture" to be executed.
[2] Title Bar M akes laserDESK  m ain window identiﬁable.
[3] M enu Bar Com prises all com m ands of laserDESK . It is subdivided into m enus and sub-m enus.
[4] Toolbars Provide fast access to m ost laserDESK  functions.
[5] J ob Explorer Shows the structure of the job and lets you create m arking objects or control
elem ents and deﬁne their sequence of execution.
[6] Library Explorer Lets you m anage param eter sets for m arking, ﬁlling and sym bols.
[7] Laser Control W indow Lets you quickly execute a m anual m arking or switch to the Autom atic M ode. The
laser control can be individually adjusted. Appropriate to the current work step,
diﬀerent proﬁles (design, test or production) are selectable.
[8] Dialog "M otor Control" Lets you test axis controllers or m otion controllers.
[9] W indow "Properties" Lets you deﬁne the settings (properties) for graphic, m arking and ﬁll param eters.
[10] W indow "Toolbox" Provides an easy way to create all m arking objects and control elem ents for a job in
addition to the M enu Bar and Toolbars.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:58 laserDESK GUI
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/GUI.htm 3/6

--- Page 184 ---

# Element Nam e Description
[11] W indow "Views" Serves to set elem ents in the work area to visible or invisible quickly.
[12] W indow "Layers" Lets you create layers and deﬁne their properties.
[13] W indow "Output" Shows the system  status during job execution.
[14] Overview W indow Displays the W ork Area in sm all form at to give an overview of the current shown
section of the W ork Area.
[15] Status Bar Indicates the status of the RTC connection, the m ode (current selected function), the
cursor position, and the deﬁned units.
W hen m arking a job, a progress bar is displayed, if the expected execution tim e will
last for longer than 10 seconds (waiting periods due to long_delay and set_wait
com m ands or waiting for digital input signals are not taken into account). W hen a
m arking job is ﬁnished, the actual m arking tim e is indicated.
[16] Button Reference Run Lets you execute a reference run of all deﬁned axes by a single click.
Note In addition to the M enu Bar, Toolbars, and W indow "Toolbox", laserDESK  oﬀers an additional way to access functions: a
Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) presents a num ber of com m ands optim ized for the task of creating and editing
m arking objects.

Arranging laserDESK Windows
The m enu bar and work area are always displayed. Other windows and the Toolbars can be shown or hidden by the M enu View.
Apart from  the status bar, all other windows are dockable so that user can arrange them  according to individual preferences. If
desired, the windows can appear or disappear autom atically. To arrange a window:
01. Open a desired window by the M enu View.
That window appears in the program  window.
02. Click the 
  icon in the title bar.
It will change to 
 .
03. Click the window title bar and keep the m ouse button pressed.
The window is highlighted blue.
04. Drag the window in the desired direction.
One or several buttons appear:
05. Arrange the window by the buttons as follows:
Icon Description
Inserts the window above the subjacent window.
Inserts the window below the subjacent window.
Inserts the window to the left of the subjacent window.
Inserts the window to the right of the subjacent window.
Inserts the window inside the subjacent window. In this case the windows share one
fram e and the desired window m ust be selected with the appropriate tab (as an exam ple,
see J ob Explorer / Library Explorer in the topm ost ﬁgure).
06. Deﬁne the window behavior by selecting its pin in the title bar:
Icon Description
Pins the open window perm anently.
Autom atically shows the window as soon as the cursor is placed onto the respective tab.
Autom atically hides the window as soon as the cursor leaves the respective tab.

M enu Edit
Com m and Description
U ndo Cancels the last executed action.
This m ay not be possible for few actions, especially if U ID
creation is suppressed. See 'Suppress autom atic U ID
Creation' param eter in Section 'Create'.
Redo Executes the last canceled action (U ndo).
Select
M ode Switches the program  to select m ode for selecting objects
and elem ents, see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing.
Select m ode is the standard m ode for creating m arking
objects, inserting control elem ents and editing them .
All All m arking objects on the work area is selected
sim ultaneously.
Scroll Lets you scroll through several selected m arking objects, if
you have selected several m arking objects at once, see 'Area
Select' in Selecting M arking Objects for Editing or Select >
All (Scroll) in Section 'Edit'.
Scroll serves, for exam ple, to deﬁne the m arking object on
which other objects are adjusted when arranging m ultiple
m arking objects by the align or spacing buttons, see Toolbar
Arrange.
Select Edit > Select > Scroll until the desired m arking object
is highlighted in a diﬀerent color.
M arked Lets you select one of m any selected m arking objects, if you
have selected several m arking objects at once, see 'Area
Select' in Selecting M arking Objects for Editing or Select >
All (Scroll) in Section 'Edit'.
‒ U se Scroll (see above) until the desired m arking object is
highlighted.
‒ Select Edit > Select > M arked. Now, only the desired
m arking object is selected.
Select Lets you select one of m any selected m arking objects, if you
have selected several m arking objects at once, see 'Area
Select' in Selecting M arking Objects for Editing or Select >
All (Scroll) in Section 'Edit'.
Opens the Dialog "Select", see Select > All (Select) in
Section 'Edit'.
Clear Cancels selection of an object or elem ent.
Edit For editing points or lines of a selected m arking object, see
Editing Objects ‒ Overview.
Position
Place Opens the Dialog "Place" for positioning the selected object
by num erical entry, see Arranging M arking Objects ‒
Overview.
Center to Origin Positions the selected m arking object directly to the origin
of the work area, see Positioning M arking Objects to the
Origin.
M ove Opens the Dialog "M ove" for translating the selected object
by num erical entry, see Arranging M arking Objects ‒
Overview.
M ove in z Opens the Dialog "M ove in Z" for shifting the selected object
in z direction by num erical entry, see M oving M arking

Fill Fills selected m arking objects such as rectangle, circle,

Copy Copies a selected m arking object or control elem ent.
Paste Pastes a selected m arking object or control elem ent.
Delete Deletes a selected m arking object or control elem ent.
Oﬀset Enlarges a m arking object by a deﬁned dim ension. Its
corners are rounded sim ultaneously by the deﬁned radius,
see Altering the M arking Object Shape.
Round Corners Rounds the m arking object's corners by a deﬁned radius.
The m arking object can be closed or open, see Altering the
M arking Object Shape)
Com bine See Com bining M arking Objects and G roups.
U nion Com bines two overlapping m arking objects by four diﬀerent
ways and thus create new objects.Intersect
Exclusive Or (Xor)
Exclude

polygon,graphic path - but not spiral and arc).
M erge Com bines two or m ore groups (groups, graphic sets, tile sets
and variants) into one single group containing all m arking
objects.
Separate
Split Oﬀers several m odes to cut one or several m arking objects,
see Splitting M arking Objects.
Tile Divides a m arking object exceeding the work area into
several tiles to m ark it in several steps, see Deﬁning a Tile
Set.
The tile set function requires, that the work piece to be
m arked can be m oved with an appropriate device (for
exam ple, positioning table) coordinated to the laser scan
system .
Cutout Cuts intersecting lines by to avoid poor m arking quality at
intersections, see Cutting Out M arking Objects.
 Extract Filling Separates the ﬁlling from  the selected ﬁlled elem ent(s).
The result is the non-ﬁlled elem ent and the ﬁlling lines
grouped inside a G raphics Paths. The two elem ents are
displayed accordingly in the J ob Explorer:

Menu Hardware
Com m and Description
Laser Control
Laser System The Dialog "M anual Laser Control" lets you test the laser or
scan head, see Direct M anual Control of the Laser System
and Scan Head.
Laser W izard Serves to create a laser deﬁnition ﬁle, see Laser W izard.
Calibration Factor W izard Serves to determ ine exact calibration factors for the work
area, see Determ ining the Scan System  Calibration Factor.
Restart Initializes the laser, if a m alfunction occurs, see M anually
Restarting the Laser.
M anual Control
RTC IO-Control The 'RTC IO Desk' serves to test signal exchange from  the
RTC5 board | RTC6 board, see Testing the Signal Transfer by
the RTC IOs.
M otor Control Serves to control m otors or axes by the dialog "M otor
Control", see Testing the M otor Controllers.
Available only, if a m otor and its serial connection is deﬁned
in the hardware settings, see Setting the Param eters
'Hardware Devices'.
intelliW ELD II The Dialog "Scanlab intelliW ELD II Control" serves to set the
position of the intelliW ELD II zoom  axis, see Setting the
Position of the intelliW ELD II Zoom  Axis.
Available only, if the intelliW ELD II support is activated in the
Hardware Conﬁguration, see Setting the Param eters 'Optics'
and an additional zoom  correction ﬁle is available.
Adjustm ent 
POF Calibration W izard Serves to determ ine exact calibration factors for the
processing-on-the-ﬂy (POF) m echanism , see Determ ining
the Scan System  Calibration Factor.
Polygon Delay The variable polygon calibration
wizard serves to create a custom ized calibration curve, see
Creating the Variable Polygon Delay Calibration Curve.
Executable only with iDRIVE scan system s.
Tracking Error The tracking error wizard serves to evaluate the tracking
error of the scan head, see Evaluating the Scan Head
Tracking Error. The evaluated value can then be used to
calculate the Laser Delays and Laser Delays in the W indow
"Properties".
Executable only with iDRIVE scan system s.
Param eter W izard The param eter wizard serves to ﬁnd the m ost suited
param eter set for your application. This routine com prises
the selection of a param eter set, the setting of the param eter
values, the execution of the test pattern m arking, and ﬁnally
the selection of the m ost suited param eters, see Evaluating
the Best Param eter Set.
3D Surface Deﬁnition Enables the creation of a special correction ﬁle for a 3D
surface m arking, see How to M ark on 3D Surfaces. The 3D
surface m ust be deﬁned by an stl ﬁle.
System  Inform ation
Scan System See Viewing System  Inform ation - Dialog "Inform ation".
Runtim e Inform ation See Viewing System  Inform ation - Dialog "Runtim e
Inform ation".

Com m and Description
3D Calibration W izard If your application needs correction ﬁles precisely reﬂecting
your particular system 's properties, then the 3D-Calibration
W izard serves to calibrate your 2- or 3-axis scan-system . W ith
this calibration you can create system  speciﬁc correction
ﬁles, see Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System .
Edit Hardware Settings/
View Hardware Settings
Opens the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
If Hardware Conﬁguration is open, the dialog serves to
deﬁne the hardware param eters appropriate for the current
system  conﬁguration.
If a job or the M arking Library is open, the dialog serves to
Deﬁning the Active Hardware Param eter Set. All other
current settings can only be viewed but not changed.
RTC Connection Release and regain the RTC connection.
Serves to disconnect laserDESK  from  the RTC5 board | RTC6
board without closing the program , see Releasing/Regaining
the RTC Connection.
Initialize Rem ote Control Resets the rem ote interface of laserDESK . This is necessary
to m ake the rem ote interface available again if a client
program  has shut down, see Initializing the Rem ote Control.
Reference Run for Axes Opens the Dialog "M otor Control" to start a reference run,
see Testing the M otor Controllers.
RTC Board Selection Lets you select one certain RTC board, if m ore than one RTC
board is connected to the PC on which laserDESK  is
installed, see Selecting an RTC Board.

Menu Arrange
Com m and Description
G rouping Serves to group or ungroup m arking objects:
G roup Com bines several m arking objects, selected together in the
work area or In J ob Explorer, to one group.
G raphics Paths Com bines several independent m arking objects, selected
together in the work area or In J ob Explorer, to one graphics
paths (set). Their individual param eter sets are discarded.
G raphics Set Com bines several m arking objects, selected together in the
work area or In J ob Explorer, to one graphic set.
Tile Set Com bines several m arking objects, selected together in the
work area or In J ob Explorer, to one tile set.
Variant Com bines several m arking objects, selected together in the
work area or In J ob Explorer, to one variant.
U ngroup Dissolves a selected group, graphic set, tile set or variable in
single objects.
Sequencing For deﬁning the sequence of the objects/elem ents in the
J ob Explorer tree:
First Positions the selected object to the top of the tree.
M ove U p M oves the selected object one position up.
M ove Down M oves the selected object one position down.
Last Positions the selected object to the bottom  of the tree.
By Position Sorts several selected objects in the tree depending on their
position in the work area:
‒ Left To Right
‒ Top To Bottom
‒ Right To Left
‒ Bottom  To Top
‒ Distance
By Layer Sorts several selected objects in the tree depending on their
assignm ent to diﬀerent layers.
Aligning Serves to align several selected m arking objects in the work
area:
Lefts Aligns the m arking objects to the left.
Centers Centers the m arking objects horizontally.
Rights Aligns the m arking objects to the right.
Tops Aligns the m arking objects to the top.
M iddles Centers the m arking objects vertically.
Bottom s Aligns the m arking objects to the bottom .
Put Text On Path Aligns a selected text to a sim ultaneously selected m arking
object (here generally called path).
Rem ove Text From  Path Rem oves the text from  a m arking object, if aligned by 'Put
Text On Path' (see above).
Distributing For distributing several selected m arking objects in the work
area:
Left Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced left borders.
Horizontal Center Spacing Distributes horizontally with evenly spaced centers.
Right Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced right borders.
Horizontal Distance Distributes horizontally with even distance between objects.
Top Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced top borders.
Vertical Center Spacing Distributes vertically with evenly spaced centers.

Com m and Description
Bottom  Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced bottom  borders.
Vertical Distance Distributes vertically with even distance between the
objects.
Across Area Opens a dialog for distribution across the work area.
4 diﬀerent layout types are available: "G rid", "Box", "Circle",
"Arc".
Repeat Opens the 'Repeat' dialog to distribute a selected m arking
object as often as deﬁned across the work area, see Copying
and Aligning M arking Objects.
4 diﬀerent layout types are available: "G rid", "Box", "Circle",
"Arc".

Toolbar View
The buttons of the Toolbar "View" serve to change the work area's view and switch between several m odes:
Select m ode lets you select, create and edit m arking objects and insert control elem ents
Zoom  m ode and pan m ode serve to change the work area's view
M easuring m ode assists in m easuring distances
The "m ode buttons" are m arked by a point (see table below) Once a m ode button is selected, this m ode rem ains in eﬀect until you
switch to another m ode. In zoom  m ode, pan m ode and m easuring m ode you can alternatively select 'Finish' by the right m ouse
button. In this case, laserDESK  always returns to select m ode.
Button Description
Select M ode Serves to select m arking objects (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
Note
Only in 'Select M ode' m arking objects can be edited.
Select Clear Serves to deselect selected m arking objects.
Pan M ode For m oving the work area (see M oving the W ork Area).
Zoom  M ode Serves to enable zoom  m ode and directly apply a zoom  function (see U sing the Zoom
Functions).
Zoom  In See above.
Zoom  Out See above.
Zoom  To All G raphics See above.
Zoom  To W ork Area See above.
Zoom  To Selection See above.
M easure Distance Assists in m easuring the distance between points (see M easuring Distances).
Scan Head Positioning Switches the program  to the scan head m ode. Serves to place the scan head m irrors
to a desired position in the work area (see Positioning the Scan Head).

selected m arking objects in the work area.

Right Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced right borders.

Top Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced top borders.
Vertical Center Spacing Distributes vertically with evenly spaced centers.
Bottom  Border Spacing Distributes with evenly spaced bottom  borders.
Vertical Distance Distributes vertically with even distance between the objects.

Button Description
Across Area Opens the Dialog "Distribute" for distributing across the work area.
Four diﬀerent layout types are available: G rid, Box, Circle, Arc, see also Copying and
Aligning M arking Objects.
Rotate Opens a dialog for rotating the selected object by num erical entry (see Arranging
M arking Objects in the W ork Area).
M irror Horizontal M irrors one or several m arking objects with respect to a horizontal axis or to a vertical
axis.
Note
‒ The m irror action for groups m ay need to transfer the transform ation to em bedded
objects. In special cases (for exam ple, if the group has a rotation) this can prevent the
undo function to work correctly and m ay change the size of the bounding box.
‒ Because the barcode is an im port object with no m irror function, the m irroring by
the toolbar buttons has no eﬀect. If you need to m irror a barcode, put it in a group and
use the group properties 'Flip X' or 'Flip Y' instead (see '[4] Transform ation'
param eters 'Flip X' and 'Flip Y' in the 'G roup' properties).
M irror Vertical
Center to Origin Positions the selected m arking object directly to the origin of the work area (see
Positioning M arking Objects to the Origin).

--- Page 230 ---

W ork Area
The work area is the m ain elem ent of laserDESK . It is the drawing plane for creating m arking objects (see Inserting Objects) and
editing them  m anually (see M anually Editing M arking Objects). The work area ('G raphic View [1]' tab) is always displayed. To use
diﬀerent views of the m arking objects (Top, Front/Side, 3D View, see Views W indow), additional graphic views can be opened (in
Design Proﬁle only).
The work area's appearance can be custom ized by each user:
Additional graphic views can be opened by the M enu View (see New G raphic View).
Change between the several views by selecting the respective tabs (G raphic View [...]).
To close a graphic view, select it and click the X button.
The work area's window dim ensions can be changed (indirectly) by shifting the fram es of the adjacent dockable windows
(J ob Explorer, Property W indow, ...):
01. M ove the cursor above the fram e.
The cursor will change to a bar with arrows:
02. Press the m ouse button and ‒ while keeping it pressed ‒ shift the fram e up or down or to the left or right.
Note The absolute dim ensions of the work area should correspond to the dim ensions of the m arking area of your laser
scan system .
These dim ensions m ust be deﬁned within "Hardware Conﬁguration" (see 'M arking Area W idth/Height [m m ]' in
Setting 'Optics' Param eters).
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Work Area
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/WorkArea.htm 2/3

--- Page 231 ---

G rid, axes, ruler, work area's background, graphic elem ents (selected, unselected, edited), m arking vectors, m arking protocol,
and m arking sorting can be displayed or hidden as follows:
Select or clear the respective elem ents' checkboxes in the G U I settings (see Deﬁning the View Param eters).
Set the respective elem ents (if available) visible or invisible in the Views W indow.
Choose "Ruler" or "G rid" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
G rid style and units of length to be indicated on the ruler can be deﬁned in the G U I settings (see Deﬁning the View
Param eters).
Colors of work area's background, graphic elem ents (selected, unselected, edited), m arking vectors, m arking protocol, and
m arking sorting can be deﬁned in the G U I settings (see Deﬁning the View Param eters).
W hen you insert or edit m arking objects in the work area, you'll appreciate the enhanced convenience oﬀered by the zoom
functions and pan m ode (see U sing the Zoom  Functions and M oving the W ork Area).
Additional, the m ouse wheel serves to m ove the work area:
01. U se the m ouse wheel to scroll the work area up or down
02. U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Shift> key to scroll the work area up or down very fast.
03. U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Alt> key to scroll the work area left or right.
04. U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Ctrl> key to zoom  in or out.

control elem ents. At any tim e, you can set or change the executable order of m arking objects, control elem ents and variants by
their positions in the tree. The J ob-Explorer Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) serves for creating, editing and sequencing
m arking objects and control elem ents.

If you insert one or several variants in a job, these is listed below the 'Variants' node (see ﬁgure above). W hen a variant is inserted,
this node gets created autom atically. All variants of a job are always be listed below one node - it is not possible to distribute
several variants across the tree.
W hen laserDESK  starts, it autom atically creates a new job. As long as the job is not saved, no job nam e is indicated. W hen you save
a job or open an already existing job, the job nam e is indicated in parentheses , for exam ple, "J ob (Test_1.sld)", at the top of the
tree. Likewise in a typical job, m arking objects can be created under "Hardware Conﬁguration" and 'M arking Library', for exam ple,
for checking hardware param eter settings or creating sym bols. If "Hardware Conﬁguration" is open, then "J ob
(HardwareConﬁguration.sld)" is displayed at the top of the tree, if 'M arking Library' is open, then "J ob (M arkingLibrary.sld)" is
displayed.

Library Explorer / M arking Library
By assigning processing param eters (m arking, pixel im age and ﬁll param eters), you tell the laser scan system  how to handle individual
m arking objects for optim al m arking and m aterials processing. M arking Parameters that aﬀect laser and scanner behavior are always
required. Filled m arking objects require additional Fill Parameters and (rastered) pixel im ages require additional Pixel Image
Parameters.
The process param eters can be assigned to each m arking object in a job (see Window "Properties"). But deﬁning the process
param eters for each m arking object of a job is a extensive work. To facilitate easier reuse at diﬀerent positions within a job, you can
deﬁne m arking, pixel im age and ﬁll param eter sets, which can be assigned to several or to all m arking objects
Additionally, frequently used m arking objects need not be created again and again. Theses can be created by the user and saved as
"Symbols" (See also Creating Sym bols).
If you want to use Fonts with characters created/edited by yourself or centerline fonts (converted from  standard fonts, see Creating
Centerline Fonts), these fonts can be created in Library Explorer and used again.
Param eter sets, fonts and sym bols created in a job are autom atically saved to the corresponding (local) job library. Provided they were
not subsequently deleted there, they are then saved in the job ﬁle ([JobNam e].sld). A job's param eter sets and sym bols in the job
library can not be (directly) used by another job. Instead, they can be exported to the (job-superordinate) M arking Library
([M arkingLibraryNam e].sld) and im ported from  there into the job library of another job. Typically, process param eter sets are created
for a job, but can also be created in the m arking Library, independent of a job.
The advantage of exporting param eter sets, fonts and sym bols to the M arking Library and to im port them  into job libraries can be
increased by expanding the easy data exchange to a network. Param eter sets evaluated as suitable and frequently used sym bols and
centerline fonts can be exported to a M arking Library on the network server and thus are available for all clients.

The Library Explorer is available for m anaging process param eter sets, fonts and sym bols ‒ both in the job and in the M arking Library.
Like Job Explorer, the Library Explorer has a tree structure for creating, copying, renam ing, deleting, im porting or exporting data sets,
fonts and sym bols.
Library Explorer
The (job-superordinate) M arking Library m ust be opened by the 'File\Open > M arking Library' m enu.
The Library Explorer is shown autom atically.
The title bar shows "M arkingLibrary.sld" and the work area's background colors pale blue.

The (local) job library m ust be displayed by choosing M enu View > Library Explorer and, if com bined with another window (for
exam ple, Job Explorer), by selecting the corresponding tab 'Library Explorer'.
The title bar shows "[JobNam e].sld" and the work area's background colors gray.
The program 's M arking Library already includes a default m arking param eter set, a default ﬁll param eter set, and a default pixel im age
param eter set. Designated as "<default>", these sets are (as long as no others were deﬁned as default) autom atically used as default
param eter sets in the job library when a new job is opened. The default m arking param eter set from  the job library is assigned initially
to all newly created m arking objects, and the default pixel im age param eter set to all pixel im ages. And for ﬁll assignm ents, the default
ﬁll param eter set from  the job library is initially used.
Both in the M arking Library and in the job library new param eter sets can be created with arbitrary nam es. These is listed below the
<default> param eter set (as an exam ple, see "M ark1" and "M ark2" in the ﬁgure above). Saving param eter settings in the Window
"Properties" without applying a nam e, these is indicated as "Auto-..." autom atically in the Library Explorer. How to edit the process
param eters in the Window "Properties" and how m anage them  is described in Window "Properties".
U se the dialog "G U I Settings" (see 'U ser Interface (G U I)' above) to specify the directory in which the used M arking Library
([M arkingLibraryNam e].sld) should be m anaged. You can freely select the path and ﬁlenam e. Firstly, this enables use of network paths
and thus autom atic backups, etc. Secondly, a directory can be chosen and conﬁgured to speciﬁcally provide laserDESK  users with
appropriate access rights.
To m anage the param eter sets, sym bols and fonts, the Library Explorer Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) m ust be used. Depending
on the function, the cursor m ust be placed in the Library Explorer or a param eter set or sym bol m ust selected in the tree.

GUI Proﬁles / Window "Laser Control" / Window "Standalone Control"
laserDESK  jobs are im plem ented in 2 phases: creating the job and executing the job. Creating the job is divided again into creating
the m arking objects and testing the param eter settings. Additionally, if your laser scan system  is equipped with a pilot laser, this
can be used for aligning the job m arking objects to the m arking surface.
For each of these 4 steps, users beneﬁt from  well-arranged program  windows and quick access to the functions to be perform ed.
To execute a job either m anually (for exam ple, for testing the process param eter settings), or autom atically (by external input
signals in a production environm ent), or to use the pilot laser alignm ent, you'll need an appropriate software tool. Therefore,
laserDESK  provides its Laser Control window with a start button and diﬀerent G U I proﬁles suited to the current step. The required
G U I proﬁle can be selected from  the M enu View or the Toolbar File:
W hen you create m arking objects for a job, the work area is your m ost im portant program  window. On the one hand,
inserting and editing the m arking objects requires screen space and on the other hand, no m arking procedure needs to be
started. So for this step, laserDESK  lets you select the Design Proﬁle. By default, no window "Laser Control" is displayed.
W hen all m arking objects, variants and control elem ents have been created, you m ay iteratively determ ine optim al m arking
param eters by executing test m arkings. To provide quick and convenient job execution for this step, laserDESK  lets you select
the Test Proﬁle, which displays the window "Laser Control" as shown below:
"Laser of Type 'G eneral'" and "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" when switched on
"Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" when switched oﬀ
‒ W ith a "Laser of Type 'G eneral'", the M anual M arkingbutton lets you directly execute the job m arking. In contrast, a "Laser
of Type 'Fully Supported'" m ust be switched on before.
‒ U sing s, the Sim ulate Executionbutton lets you execute the job without activated laser to im prove param eter settings.
U sing the protocol nodes with an iDRIVE scan system  allows to check the real positions and to adapt the scanner delays.
‒ The 'Start' input ﬁeld lets you enter the num ber of m ultiple executions if required.
‒ The 'Sel.' listbox lets you select a desired variant or desired m arking object (if present).
‒ The 'Head' listbox is active only, if scan head [2] has been activated in the Hardware Conﬁguration (a desired variant, if the
job com prises two or m ore variants, see Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters). In this case you can select scan head [1], [2] or [1]+
[2].
‒ If required, you can click the STOP M arkingbutton as an em ergency stop.
How to execute a job m anually is described in Start a job m anually.
If your laser scan system  is equipped with a pilot laser, you can check whether the objects are aligned correct to the laser scan
system  after the job has been created and adjust it if necessary. To do so, select the 'Pilot Laser Proﬁle' (which can only be
selected in M anual M ode). The corresponding window "Laser Control" (see ﬁgure below) provides listboxes and buttons for
pilot laser transform ation.

W hile the pilot laser, started with the Pilot Laser button, scans the job's objects continually (num ber of scans are set in the
input ﬁeld 'Starts'), the m arking objects can be aligned in x/y directions and in rotation by using the respective pilot laser
transform ation listboxes or buttons. (Additionally, keys on the keyboard can be used.) W hen the correct position is found, the
transform ation values can be applied to the job with one click on the Apply To J ob Data button.
The com plete procedure is described in U sing the Pilot Laser for Alignm ent.
W hen you are ﬁnished with creating the job and testing the process param eters, and where necessary and applicable,
adjusting the job, the ﬁnal step is the job execution. For actual production, laserDESK  lets you select the Production Proﬁle. In
production jobs are typically started and controlled exclusively by external input signals (for exam ple, by signals from  a
control PC or PLC). To do so, laserDESK  m ust be switched to Autom atic M ode where the start input of the RTC is enabled. The
production proﬁle displays the corresponding window "Laser Control" - diﬀerent for a "Laser of Type 'G eneral'"and "Laser of
Type 'Fully Supported'" - as shown below:
