file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/LayersWindow.htm 1/4

--- Page 260 ---

Window "Layers"
The layer function serves to assign m arking objects or groups to various layers. It is well suited for creating m arking objects to be
m arked in diﬀerent z positions (see Inserting Z-Focus Control Elem ents).
Because each layer can be enabled or disabled for viewing, editing and m arking, the corresponding m arking objects can be set to
visible or invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable. To diﬀerentiate between the single layers, each one can be
assigned a diﬀerent color. Furtherm ore, for all objects of one layer a new m arking param eter set can be assigned.
The layer settings are m ade in the Layers window (see below), the assignm ent of m arking objects to the layers is perform ed in the
object's G raphic Param eters (see param eter 'Layer').
The Layers window can be displayed or hidden and arranged according to your individual preferences, see Arranging
laserDESK  W indows. It is structured as follows:
Colum n Description
Nam e Lets you enter an arbitrary nam e.
Visible
If the checkbox is selected, the m arking objects assigned to this layer are visible.
If deactivated, invisible.
(See also 'Layers' in the View Param eters.)
Locked
If the checkbox is selected, the m arking objects assigned to this layer are not editable.
If deactivated, editable.
M arkable
If the checkbox is selected, the m arking objects assigned to this layer are m arkable.
If deactivated, not m arkable.
Color
Click in the colored ﬁeld to open the M S W indows-standard dialog 'Color'.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
(See also 'Layers' in the View Param eters.)
Note During "Oﬄine" tiling and the tiling set for "Online" tiling, the following applies:
‒ 'Visible' is only applied for the display in the graphic view.
‒ 'M arkable' is only used for the laser processing.
‒ If 'M arking Vectors' is activated in the Views W indow, all m arking vectors are displayed even if 'Visible' is unchecked.
Additionally, the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) opens when selecting a row (layer) in the table and clicking the right m ouse
button:
Com m and Description
Select > Clear Lets you unselect the selected layer (row).
New Lets you create a new layer.
Edit Lets you open the 'Edit Layer' dialog (see Editing a Layer).
Renam e Lets you renam e the selected layer in the 'Renam e Layer' dialog.
Visibility > On/Oﬀ Lets you switch the visibility of the selected layer on or oﬀ.
Locking > On/Oﬀ Lets you switch the locking of the selected layer on or oﬀ.
M arking > On/Oﬀ Lets you switch the m arking of the selected layer on or oﬀ.
Color... Opens the M S W indows-standard dialog 'Color'.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Window "Layers"
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/LayersWindow.htm 2/4

--- Page 261 ---

Com m and Description
Set M arking Param eters Opens the 'Tem plate' dialog to let you assign a m arking param eter set for all
objects of the selected layer.
Select the desired param eter set of the M arking Library (identiﬁed by the
extension [lib]) or job and click the button OK.
If the selected param eter set is from  the M arking Library, it is adopted
autom atically into the job's Library Explorer.
Delete Lets you delete an existing layer.
Delete U nused Lets you delete all unused layers.
Delete with Elem ents The selected layer is deleted together with all elem ents belonging to that layer.
If a group belongs to that layer, the whole group is deleted.
Elem ents in the Sym bol Library (see Library Explorer) which belong to that
layer are not deleted. Only the layer inform ation is rem oved.
Above all, objects can be set to an inclined plane and then processed in the z-direction. Thus, m arking objects created in the xy-
plane can then, for exam ple, be m arked on the inclined side of a workpiece as a correct 2D shape. To do so, double click a row in
the table or select 'Edit' from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) (see above). The 'Edit Layer' dialog opens.
To deﬁne a layer:
01. Enter the layer's nam e in a blank row of the table.
   or
Choose 'New' from  the context m enu.
The 'New Layer' dialog opens.
Enter the layer's nam e and click the button OK.
02. To enable or disable the layer's properties (visibility, locking, m arking):
‒ activate or deactivate the corresponding checkboxes in the table
‒ or select 'On' or 'Oﬀ' in the respective functions of the context m enu.
03. To deﬁne the layer's color:
‒ click in the table's color ﬁeld
‒ or select 'Color...' from  the context m enu.
Then select the color in the 'Color' dialog.
04. If you want to set an object to an inclined plane, double click a row/layer or select 'Edit' from  the context m enu.
The 'Edit Layer' dialog opens (for a description, refer to Editing a Layer).
05. Renam e a layer directly in the colum n 'Nam e'.
   or
Choose 'Renam e' from  the context m enu.
The 'Renam e Layer' dialog opens.
Enter the layer's nam e and click the button OK.
06. To assign a m arking param eter set, select 'Set M arking Param eter' from  the context m enu.
The 'Tem plate' dialog opens.
Select the desired param eter set and click the button OK.
07. Delete a layer by selecting 'Delete' from  the context m enu.
The selected layer is deleted im m ediately.
08. Delete all unused layers by selecting 'Delete U nused' from  the context m enu.
All unused layers are deleted im m ediately.
Note A layer's visibility and color can also be deﬁned in the window "G U I Settings" (see the 'Layers' param eter in View
Param eters).

Explorer).

m arking object is selected.

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Configuring laserDESK GUI - Dialog "Options"
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/DLG_Options_0_GUIConfiguration.htm 1/3

Conﬁguring laserDESK GU I - Dialog "Options"
The appearance of the graphical user interface (G U I) and the behavior of certain G U I com ponents can be individually conﬁgured
by each laserDESK  user according to personal preference. G U I conﬁguration options encom pass:
Snap functions
Behavior of objects while scaling
Pre-settings of m arking and graphics properties
Display of grid and ruler
Deﬁnition of colors
Deﬁnition of units
Directory assignm ent for hardware and m arking param eter ﬁles
Language
Shortcut key deﬁnition
Password setting
The laserDESK  G U I can be conﬁgured by each user at any tim e by the Dialog "Options". These settings are saved separately for
each user in a user data directory.
Dialog "Options"
To open the Dialog "Options":
Do one of the following:
Choose M enu Edit > Options.
Click Toolbar Edit > Options.
The Dialog "Options" opens. The controls displayed depend on the entry selected in the tree, see:
Section 'Edit'
Section 'Create'
Section 'View'
Section 'Directories'

Section 'Edit'
Param eters under Dialog "Options" > Section 'Edit' deﬁne the behavior of the cursor and the m arking objects when creating and
editing them . The user can conﬁgure these settings according to his personal preference and thus m akes his job easier.
Param eter Description
Edit - M easure
   Snap To G rid If activated, the cursor snaps to the grid (snap distance, see below).
A reticle indicates the respective grid point.
Note
If created in the work area with this function activated, a m arking object's location is then
constrained to the grid points.
   Snap Distance [U nits] Deﬁnes the snap distance for 'Snap To G rid'.
The units correspond to the settings of the 'View' Param eters ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]).
   Snap To Cursor If activated, the reference point of an object is snapped by the cursor during m otion.
   Snap To G raphic If activated, the cursor snaps to the graphics when M easuring Distances.
A sm all circle indicates snapping of the cursor to the graphic's edge.
   Recalculate Filling If activated, changes of the ﬁll param eters are recalculated after editing.
If deactivated, the m arking object will lose its ﬁlling when changing the param eters; the
m arking object m ust be ﬁlled with the new param eter settings again.
Resize
   K eep Shape If activated, m arking objects such as circles do not lose their shape.
   K eep Aspect Ratio If activated, the aspect ratio of m arking objects persist when scaled.
M ove - Rotate
   X/Y Increm ent [U nits] Deﬁnes the step size for (pilot laser) transform ation in x/y direction and rotation in [units].
   Rotation Increm ent [U nits]
   Do not Shift the Copy Norm ally, the copy of a graphic elem ent is shifted a little when pasting it with <Ctrl>+<v>, to
see the copy in the graphic window and to be able to select it.
If this checkbox is selected, the copy is inserted at exactly the sam e place as the original.
Note
Because the copy covers the original exactly, you can see it in the J ob Explorer only but not in
the graphic window.
Select Position Lets you deﬁne how m any overlapping m arking objects are selectable by the cursor.
   Single From  all m arking objects captured by the cursor, only the m arking object next to the cursor is
selected.
   All (Scroll) All m arking objects captured by the cursor are selected.
Subsequent, you can scroll through or select one of the m arking objects (see "Select >" in
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Section 'Edit'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/DLG_Options_1_Edit.htm 2/3

--- Page 279 ---

Param eter Description
Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)).
   All (Select) All m arking objects captured by the cursor are selected (see ﬁgure above).
Autom atically, the dialog "Select" opens to let you select one of these m arking objects.
To set param eters in Section 'Edit':
01. Click Dialog "Options" > Section 'Edit'.
02. M ake your settings according to your needs.
03. Click the button OK.
The window "G U I Settings" is closed.
The settings are saved perm anently.

Section 'Create'
Param eters under Dialog "Options" > Section 'Create' lets you deﬁne default properties of som e speciﬁc param eters of the
m arking objects as default properties. I.e. each new m arking object (if featuring these param eters) gets these settings
autom atically when created or im ported. Nevertheless, these settings can be m odiﬁed later for each m arking object in the
W indow "Properties". Assum ing that the settings are applied m ainly to all m arking objects featuring these param eters, the pre-
settings help you to save tim e.
Param eter Description
M arking Properties (also see group 'M arking' in the G raphic Param eters)
   M ark Lets you enable or disable the m arking process for this object. W hen enabling the m arking
process, the order of hatchlines and outlines are deﬁned. One of the following item s can be
selected:
‒ Hatchlines BEFORE Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked before the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AFTER Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked after the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AND Outlines: m ust be selected, if the whole object shall be sorted.
‒ Hatchlines only: only the hatchlines are m arked.
‒ None: the m arking object is not m arked.
   G raphic Resolution [U nits]: Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution. This factor is used for the calculation of the curve's
polygon adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) is the curve ﬁtting.
   Point
      Tim e Duration [µs]:
Lets you deﬁne the m arking tim e duration (in [µs]) especially for m arking points.
   Z-Focus Control
      J um p Speed [m /s]: Speed (in [m /s]) of the 'virtual' laser beam  while positioning to the next m ark in z-direction.
      J um p Delay [µs]: The jum p delay is needed to equalize the tracking error at the end of the jum p. Because the z-
focusing optics have larger tracking errors, this value can be adapted here.
G raphic Properties
   Layer: A m arking object can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set the m arking object visible or
invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable (see Layers W indow).
By default none layer is assigned, the listbox is blank.
Open the dialog "Select" by the Button 
 , to select a listed layer or "<None>".
   Suppress autom atic U ID Creation Suppressing the autom atic U ID creation is particularly helpful when m any elem ents are
created. W ith (for exam ple,) m ore than 10,000 elem ents, U ID handling requires considerable
execution tim e.
If the checkbox is selected, no U ID is created for new job elem ents. Despite this setting it is
always possible to assign a U ID m anually to avoid the drawbacks.
Drawbacks for objects without U ID are:
‒ U IDs are used for rem ote control com m ands to assign elem ent speciﬁc values. Therefore,
these com m ands cannot be used.
‒ The execution of selected objects is not possible.
‒ Som e undo functions that use U IDs then no longer work.
   Text Content: Lets you deﬁne the text content of static font and vector text (also see param eter 'Text' in
G raphic Param eters ‒ Font Text and G raphic Param eters ‒ Vector Text).
The default setting is "<Text>".
   Text J ustiﬁcation: Lets you deﬁne the reference point of static font text:
‒ Left Bottom
‒ Left Baseline
‒ Left Center
...
‒ Right Top
The default setting is 'Left Bottom '.
(See also param eter 'J ustiﬁcation' in G raphic Param eters ‒ Font Text.)

Param eter Description
   Barcode Type: laserDESK  oﬀers a large num ber of linear and m atrix barcode types, which can be selected
here from  a drop-down m enu.
This param eter lets you pre-set the code type for the 'Barcode' objects.
(See also param eter 'Code Type' in G raphic Param eters ‒ Barcode.)
To set the param eters in Section 'Create':
01. Click Dialog "Options" > Section 'Create'.
02. M ake your settings according to your needs.
03. Click the button OK.
The Dialog "Options" is closed.
The settings are saved perm anently.

Section 'View'
Param eters under Dialog "Options" > Section 'View' decisively determ ine the appearance of the G raphical U ser Interface (G U I), in
particular the display of the work area and the m arking objects.
Param eter Description
W ork Area
G rid If activated, the grid is shown in the work area.
(The grid can also be set to visible or invisible by 'G rid' in the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
and W indow "Views".)
Axes If activated, x and y axes are shown in the work area.
(The axes can also be set to visible or invisible by 'Axes' in the W indow "Views".)
Ruler If activated, two rulers (x and y directions) is shown in the work area.
(The rulers can also be set to visible or invisible by "Ruler" in the Context M enu (Right M ouse
Button) and W indow "Views".)
G rid Style
Autom atic If activated, a grid is set autom atically.
PointsDistance If activated, points are shown. The points distance m ust be deﬁned.
The distance corresponds to the settings of the 'U nits' ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in], see below).
Tab G raphics Note
The 'Visible' checkbox is not program  but proﬁle dependent. Therefore, the checkbox displays the
speciﬁc proﬁle setting and allows to adapt the current proﬁle (see G U I Proﬁles / Control W indows).
It m ay change when a proﬁle is changed.
M arking Area If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, the work area is displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.Deﬁnes the color of the work area.
The work area can also be set to visible or invisible by 'M arking Area' in the W indow "Views".)
M arking Objects If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, all m arking objects are displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
(The m arking objects can also be set to visible or invisible by 'M arking Objects' in the W indow
"Views".)
Selected Objects If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, the selected m arking object(s) is (are) displayed in
the deﬁned color.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
Edited Objects If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, m arking objects selected with 'Edit' is displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
M arking Vectors If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, the m arking vectors are displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
(The m arking vectors can also be set to visible or invisible by "M arking" in the Context M enu (Right
M ouse Button) and by 'M arking Vectors' in the W indow "Views".)
M arking Sorting If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated and the 'M arking Vectors' are activated (see above),
the m arking sorting is displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
(The m arking sorting can also be set to visible or invisible by 'M arking Sorting ' in the W indow
"Views".)
M arking M otion If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, the position of the graphic objects with sim ulated
axes m otions, deﬁned by the m otor control nodes, is shown. The axes m otion is shown as a shift of
the objects.
Note

Param eter Description
To get a correct sim ulation, the param eter 'M otion Direction' of the m otor control node (see
G raphic Param eters ‒ M otor Control) has to be set according to the build in position of this axis.
M arking Protocol If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, the m arking protocol is displayed in the deﬁned
color.
The RTC protocol data is shown, if the protocol nodes are deﬁned in the job and the job was
executed once m inim um .the m arking sorting is displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
(The protocol data can also be set to visible or invisible by "M arking Protocol" in the Context M enu
(Right M ouse Button) and W indow "Views".)
Ram ping Lines If the checkbox in colum n 'Visible' is activated, the ram ping lines are displayed.
Click on a colored ﬁeld in the colum n 'Color' to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color for the ram ping line's 100 % value and click the button OK.
Note that ram ping lines can also be set to visible or invisible by 'Ram ping Lines ' in the W indow
"Views".
Active processed elem ent See How to Highlight the Currently Executed Elem ent.
Standalone Error This color is used to display elem ents inside the job tree which can not be used for standalone RTC
boards. This colouring is only and always applied in the Standalone Proﬁle when a job is checked
(see RTC6 Ethernet Boards with Standalone Option).
The 'Visible' checkbox setting is not used (that is, it can be activated or not).
Tab Variants For creating and editing a variant, it can be useful to hide already created variants.
If the 'Visible' checkbox is selected, the corresponding variant is visible in the work area.
The colum ns 'Identiﬁer' and 'Bit Pattern' are not editable.
(Variants can also be set to visible or invisible by 'Variant #' in the W indow "Views".)
Tab Layers If the 'Visible' checkbox is selected, the corresponding layer is visible in the work area.
Click on a colored listbox to open the 'Color' dialog.
Select or deﬁne a color and click the button OK.
The colum n 'Identiﬁer' is not editable.
To deﬁne the com plete setting of the layers, see Layers W indow.
M arking
Vectors: If param eter 'M arking Vectors' (see above) has been selected, the display of the m arking vectors'
can be adjusted like follows:
‒ Arc
‒ Ellipse
‒ Polygon
Resolve Filling Lines This checkbox controls the display of the ﬁlling.
‒ If activated, each of the entire ﬁlling lines is displayed (if the screen resolution will allow it).
‒ If deactivated, a ﬁlled area is displayed instead of the ﬁlling lines. In this case, drawing the ﬁll
requires less CPU  load. This is especially useful when there are m any ﬁlling lines and they are
subject to change.
Button Reset Protocol Deletes the protocol data m em ory.
 Fill Distance [px] Filling lines within the speciﬁed distance (in [px]) is displayed as a ﬁlled area and not as separate
lines to save drawing tim e.
U nits
Length Available entries are:
‒ "m m " (m illim eter)
‒ "cm " (centim eter)
‒ "m il" (a thousandth of an inch)
‒ "in" (inch)
Angle Applied unit: [deg] (not editable).
Proportion Applied unit: [%] (not editable).
Decim al Places To deﬁne the num ber of decim al places
‒ enter the desired value into the input ﬁeld

‒ or click the arrow buttons (up/down).
Range: "0" (m in.) to "10" (m ax.).
Explorer
Num ber of Shown Nodes Deﬁnes the m axim um  num ber of nodes to be shown in J ob Explorer.
Input ﬁeld range : "0" (m in.) to "9999" (m ax.).
  3D
Activate 3D Views If this checkbox is selected, the 3D View is applicable (see listbox 3D View (XYZ) in Views W indow).
Display Layers in 3D View If this checkbox is selected, and the 'Activate 3D Views' checkbox (see above) is selected, layers are
displayed in the 3D view.
On the other hand, deactivating this checkbox allows you to keep track of m any layers. Especially
when slicing, a 'block' of parallel layers would otherwise m ask the real cut 3D body.
To set param eters in Section 'View' :

02. M ake your settings according to your needs.

Section 'Directories'
[Topic applies to Dialog "Options" > Section 'Directories']. The settings of the "Hardware Conﬁguration" and the 'M arking Library
as well as the user adm inistration are always be saved in predeﬁned ﬁles and directories as long as these are not changed. If
laserDESK  is started the ﬁrst tim e, default ﬁles for HardwareConfiguration.sld and MarkingLibrary.sld are created. The sam e
happens, if one ﬁle is m issing.
Log ﬁles are created, if logging is activated (see 'Log Level' in Deﬁning the G eneralParam eters). Protocol ﬁles are created, if
protocol nodes are inside the job and no log ﬁle nam e is deﬁned or a relative path is used (only for iDRIVE scan system s).
The default directory (including subdirectories) for all theses ﬁles is C:\ProgramData\Scanlab\SLLaserDesk. If not yet present, it
is created.
All ﬁles m entioned above can be renam ed and collected in an own directory. It is recom m ended not to change the ﬁle nam es.
Note for exam ple, for data exchange between several laser scan system s or jobs (see Library Explorer / M arking Library), we
recom m end to copy the m arking library ﬁle onto a network server.
Observe the read and write perm issions for each user when changing the directory (see Installing laserDESK )!
In the ﬁrst step, the ﬁles m ust be copied into a desired directory, in the second step, the directories can be deﬁned under Dialog
"Options" > Section 'Directories':
Param eter Description
Hardware Conﬁguration The button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open".
Deﬁne the directory for the required [HardwareConfigurationName].sld ﬁle.
M arking Library The button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open".
Deﬁne the directory for the required [MarkingLibraryName].sld ﬁle.
U ser Adm inistration The button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open".
Deﬁne the directory for the required [LaserDeskName].pwd ﬁle.
Log Files Path The button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open".
Deﬁne the directory for both the [LaserDesk.log].txt ﬁle and the
[protocol<timestamp>].csv ﬁle.
To deﬁne the directories:
01. Click Dialog "Options" > Section 'Directories'.
02. Deﬁne the directory of the "Hardware Conﬁguration":
‒ Click button 
  to open the M S W indows-standard dialog "Open".
‒ Select the desired path/directory.
‒ Click Open.
The deﬁned directory is shown in the ﬁeld 'Hardware Conﬁguration:'.
03. Repeat step 2 for "M arking Library", "U ser Adm inistration", and "Log Files".
04. Click the button OK.
The Dialog "Options" is closed.
The settings are saved perm anently.

Section 'Shortcut Keys'
[Topic applies to Dialog "Options" > Section 'Shortcut K eys']. Shortcut keys let you execute functions with keyboard interaction in
a very easy and quick way.
By default, a whole string of shortcut keys are already applied by laserDESK . These are listed in How to U se Shortcut K eys.
laserDESK  lets you deﬁne individually keyboard shortcuts in the Dialog "Options" and, if desired, lets you change m ost of the
applied shortcut keys.
Dialog "Options" > Section 'Shortcut K eys' serves to deﬁne shortcut keys for m ost of the actions of laserDESK .
Item Description
Shows actions containing: This input ﬁeld serves to ﬁnd a desired action quickly.
Enter a word or only a few characters.
The table below will list the corresponding action(s).
Action Lists of the actions of laserDESK .
To select a desired action, just click in the corresponding line.
The shortcut keys of actions signed with the lock icon cannot be changed.
These are M S W indows-standard shortcut keys.
K eys Shows the deﬁned shortcut key of the corresponding action.
Press new shortcut keys: This input ﬁeld lets you enter the desired shortcut key.
Assign Click this button to assign the entered shortcut key to the selected action.
Clear Click this button to delete the assigned shortcut key of the selected action.
To deﬁne the shortcut keys:
01. Click Dialog "Options" > Section 'Shortcut K eys'.
02. Deﬁne the shortcut keys for the desired action(s).
‒ Select an action in the table by clicking the corresponding line.
‒ Place the cursor in the 'Press new shortcut keys:' input ﬁeld.
‒ Enter new shortcut key.
‒ Click the Assign button.
The shortcut key is displayed in the 'K eys' colum n.
   or
If the key is already used, an appropriate m essage is displayed.
In this case, enter another shortcut key.
03. Select another param eter inside the desired G U I settings.
   or
Click the button OK.
The Dialog "Options" is closed.
The settings are saved perm anently.
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Section 'User and Groups'

--- Page 292 ---

Section 'U ser and Groups'
[Topic applies to Dialog "Options" > Section 'U ser and G roups']. laserDESK  enables assignm ent of access rights to users. Five user
groups are available, each with diﬀerent access rights (the classiﬁcations and the access rights associated with them  cannot be
changed). The access rights are hierarchized that is, a superordinate group always has the access rights of subordinate groups.
Role Deﬁnition W hat
Viewer U sers assigned to the Viewer groupCan only view existing jobs. Therefore, job ﬁles can be opened and
printed, but not edited or saved. The view functions (Select, Zoom , Pan,
M easure) can be used for closer inspection of the m arking objects in the
work area. All param eter and hardware settings and som e G U I settings
can only be viewed. Exceptions are the 'View' and 'G eneral' G U I
settings, which can be edited (for exam ple, for setting colors, selecting
the language, etc). The 'Hardware Control' can only be opened for
additional 'System  Inform ation'.
Production U sers assigned to the Production
group
Are perm itted to start existing jobs. Furtherm ore, these users can
restart the laser system , switch between Autom atic M ode and M anual
M ode, and stop a running job (executable by the Laser Control
W indow).
Designer U sers assigned to the Designer
group
Have all rights to create and execute jobs. All functions for creating,
inserting, and editing objects or elem ents are available, including
setting of graphic and process param eters. A Designer can save created
jobs and renam e existing jobs.
Supervisor U sers assigned to the Supervisor
group
Are perm itted to perform  all functions oﬀered by 'Hardware Control'
with the exception of the 'Calibration Factor W izard' (see Determ ining
the Scan System  Calibration Factor). Thus, a Supervisor can deﬁne
nearly all settings for hardware control and perform  m arking tests.
Adm inistrator U sers assigned to the
Adm inistrator group
Are perm itted to perform  all functions of laserDESK . This includes
"Hardware Conﬁguration", deﬁnition of library directories and
deﬁnition of access authorization.
Only an Adm inistrator can assign users to a group, by deﬁning a usernam e. A usernam e can be assigned both individually to each

sam e group. In this case, also the password m ust be entered for log in (see dialog above). If passwords are not used, then this line
m ust be left blank.
Note Individual passwords can be changed by the users them selves, see Changing the Password.
Dialog "Options" > Section 'U ser and G roups' serves to deﬁne the user's access authorization.

Note

m ay result in a huge log ﬁle.
The m axim um  log ﬁle size is 50 M B; if this value is exceeded, the log ﬁle is labeled
with a num ber (for exam ple, laserDESK.log1.txt) and a new ﬁle is created. If m ore
than 20 log ﬁles exist, the oldest one is deleted to restrict the needed hard disk
space.

ﬁle laserDESK.log.txt. Contact SCANLAB Support. They will explain to you exactly
what you should m ake available and how. SCANLAB will carry out an error analysis
and then show you how to avoid this type of problem .

26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Using the Zoom Functions
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/ZoomFunctions.htm 1/3

--- Page 299 ---

U sing the Zoom  Functions
The laserDESK  G U I provides several zoom  functions for selecting desired display sizes of the work area and m arking objects.
Seven diﬀerent zoom  functions can be selected by the M enu View bar, the Toolbar View buttons or the Context M enu (Right M ouse
Button) opened by the right m ouse button.
Icon Com m and Description
Zoom  M ode W hen selected you can draw a fram e in desired size and position in the work area.
Depending on the cursor direction, the section is
‒ enlarged (cursor direction from  top left to bottom  right)
‒ or shrink (cursor direction from  bottom  right to top left).
Zoom  In Each click scales up the work area.
Zoom  m ode need not be activated.
Zoom  Out Each click scales down the work area.
Zoom  m ode need not be activated.
Position Only Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
W hen selected, the work area aligns with the cursor position at the center of the work
area window ‒ but without scaling up or down.
Zoom  m ode need not be activated.
Zoom  To All G raphics W hen selected, the work area scales up or down to show all m arking objects with the
largest size possible.
Zoom  m ode need not be activated.
Zoom  To W ork Area W hen selected, the work area scales up or down to ﬁt the work area window.
Zoom  m ode need not be activated.
Zoom  To Selection W hen selected , the work area scales up or down to show selected m arking objects at the
largest size possible.
Zoom  m ode need not be activated.
To enlarge a section:
01. Choose Zoom  > M ode or Zoom  M ode.
The cursor changes to a square.
02. Click a desired position in the work area and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ draw a fram e from  top left to
bottom  right.
W hen the fram e reaches the desired dim ensions, release the m ouse button.
The section scales up to the size of the fram e.
To shrink a section - Zoom  M ode:
01. Choose Zoom  > M ode or Zoom  M ode.
The cursor changes to a square.
02. Click a desired position in the work area and - while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ draw a fram e from  bottom  right to
top left.
W hen the fram e reaches the desired dim ensions, release the m ouse button.
The section scales down.
To zoom  in and zoom  out:
01. Choose
‒ Zoom  > In / Zoom  In
‒ or Zoom  > Out / Zoom  Out.
The work area scales up or down.
02. Repeat until the work area reaches the desired size.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Using the Zoom Functions
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/ZoomFunctions.htm 2/3

--- Page 300 ---

To use Zoom  Position:
01. Place the cursor on a desired position in the work area and click the right m ouse button.
02. Choose Zoom  > Position from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The work area centers itself in the work area window corresponding to the cursor position.
The work area will not scale up or down.
To use Zoom  To All G raphics:
Choose Zoom  > To All G raphics or Zoom  To All Graphics.
The work area scales up or down to show all m arking objects at the largest size possible.
To use Zoom  To W ork Area:
Choose Zoom  > To W ork Area or Zoom  To Work Area.
The work area is displayed com pletely.
To use Zoom  To Selection:
01. Select one or several m arking objects (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
02. Choose Zoom  > To Selection or Zoom  To Selection.
The work area scales up or down to show the selected m arking objects at the largest size possible.
To use Zoom  in or out independently from  zoom  functions:
U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Ctrl> key.

M oving the W ork Area
Depending on the work area's extent of enlargem ent (zoom  factor), it m ight occur that a m arking object is undisplayed or only
partially visible in the work area window. In this case, use pan m ode to m ove the work area to the desired position.
Independent from  pan m ode, the work area can be m oved by the m ouse wheel in the select, zoom  or m easure m odes.
M ove the work area by using the pan m ode as follows:
01. Select 'Pan M ode' from  the
‒ M enu View,
‒ or Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
‒ or in the Toolbar View.
The cursor's form  changes to a hand.
02. Click a desired position in the work area and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag the work area to the desired
position.
03. To exit pan m ode, choose "Finish" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The program  switches to select m ode.
   or
Choose 'Select M ode', 'Zoom  M ode' or 'M easure M ode'.
The chosen m ode then becom es active.
To m ove the work area independently of pan m ode:
Do one of the following:
Choose M enu View > Select Pan > U p /Down / Left / Right
The work area then shifts correspondingly.
Choose Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) > Select Pan > U p /Down / Left / Right
The work area then shifts correspondingly.
U se the m ouse wheel to scroll the work area up or down.
U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Alt> key to scroll the work area left or right.
U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Shift> key to scroll the work area up or down very fast.
U se the m ouse wheel while pressing the <Alt>+<Shift> keys to scroll the work area left or right very fast.

Measuring Distances
M easuring m ode assists in m easuring distances, for exam ple, between m arking objects or characteristic points of m arking objects.
M easured distances are shown as a colored line and the m easured result is displayed in a rectangle fram e:
Note The indicated units of length ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]) depend on deﬁnitions in 'G U I Settings' (see Deﬁning the 'View'
Param eters).
To m easure distances, we recom m end selecting 'Snap To G raphics' in 'G U I Settings' (see Deﬁning the 'Edit'
Param eters). If the cursor nears a speciﬁc point, then a green circle appears and the cursor will snap to the speciﬁc
point.
To m easure distances:
01. Choose 'M easure Distance' in the
‒ M enu Tools or
‒ Toolbar View.
A cruciform  cursor appears:
02. Place the cursor above the starting point and press the m ouse button.
A rectangle fram e appears (all values are "0").
03. Drag the cursor ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ to the ending point.
If set, the cursor will snap to a speciﬁc point.
The m easured result displays in the rectangle fram e.
04. To term inate the m easurem ent, just release the m ouse button.
05. To exit m easuring m ode choose
‒ "Finish" by the right m ouse button (and return to 'Select M ode') or
‒ another m ode.
Back to laserDESK  G U I

For special applications, the scan head m irrors can be positioned to a desired position in the work area with one m ouse click. This
function is m ainly applied when using a cam era adapter (for positioning the m irrors to a new cam era im age) or for testing the scan
head.
To position the scan head once:

02. Choose "Scan Head" > "Position" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The scan head m irrors are positioned directly to the cursor's position.

‒ M enu Tools or
‒ in the Toolbar View
   or

The scan head m ode is indicated by a cruciform  cursor.
02. J ust click on desired positions in the work area.
The scan head m irrors are m oved to these positions.
03. To exit the scan head m ode, choose "Finish" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The program  switches to select m ode autom atically.
   or

Inserting Objects ‒ Overview
W hen creating a new job, you m ust insert m arking objects and m ay insert control elem ents and protocol nodes.
Note To create a new job, we recom m end inserting and editing the m arking objects ﬁrst.
Deﬁne a m arking object's layout ﬁrst before specifying the production param eters (m arking, hatching, and pixel im age
param eters).
The procedure for inserting a new m arking object into the work area is not always identical for all m arking objects:
Inserting a new line, rectangle, circle, ellipse, arc, spiral or barcode directly into the work area is perform ed by a com m on
procedure.
Inserting a new point, text, or variable object (date/tim e, serial num ber or im port text) is sim ilar.
Inserting a new polygon or graphics path is sim ilar too, but diﬀers som ewhat a little due to additional steps (depending on
the num ber of points).
Creation of a group, graphic set or variant is divided into several steps: ﬁrst a group, graphic set or variant m ust be created,
then the m arking objects m ust be inserted.
In contrast, pixel im ages and vector graphics are im ported by a dialog that allows picture-speciﬁc settings.
The procedure for inserting a control element or protocol/tim er node into the J ob Explorer tree only requires one m ouse click.
laserDESK  oﬀers several options for inserting new objects:
M enu Tools
Toolbar M arking Objects and Toolbar Control Elem ents
W indow "Toolbox"
Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
Note The J ob Explorer m ust be selected.
W hen the Library Explorer is selected, you cannot insert new objects.
To insert new m arking objects, see:
Inserting M arking Objects
Inserting Arcs
Inserting Points
Inserting Lines
Inserting Polygons or G raphics Paths
Inserting Text
Creating a Centerline Font
Inserting Variable Objects
Creating G roups, Sets, Variants
Im porting Pixel Im ages, Vector G raphics and STL Files
Inserting Reference to Sym bol
To insert control elem ents or protocol/tim er nodes, see:
Inserting Control Elem ents
Inserting a Control Elem ent "Serial Com m unication"
Inserting a Control Elem ent "M otor Control"

Inserting M arking Objects
The procedure for inserting new m arking objects which applies for rectangles, circles, ellipses, spirals and barcodes is very easy.
The procedure described here uses the exam ple of a rectangle:
01. Choose M enu Tools > M arking Objects > Rectangle or Rectangle from  Toolbar M arking Objects.
A cruciform  cursor appears:
02. Click a desired position in the work area and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ draw a rectangle.
The rectangle is enlarged in drawing direction to its reference point (norm ally the lower left corner).
Pressing the <Ctrl> key sim ultaneously, the reference point of the rectangle is changed to the center.
W hen the rectangle reaches the desired dim ensions, release the m ouse button..
or
01. Place the cursor in the work area and click the right m ouse button.
The Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) opens.
02. Choose "New" > "Rectangle".
A rectangle gets inserted, which can be stretched to the desired dim ensions with the cruciform  cursor.
03. Click the left m ouse button to ﬁnish the action.
or
01. Select 'Rectangle' from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag it into the work area.
A rectangle of predeﬁned dim ensions are inserted.
The inserted m arking object can be edited afterward m anually or by the W indow "Properties".

Inserting Arcs
For inserting new arcs, laserDESK  oﬀers several creation m odes:
Three point arc m ode: Deﬁne the arc by (1) the starting point, (2) the end point and (3) an arc point.
Arc quadrant m odes (cw and ccw): Deﬁne the arc by specifying a rectangle in which the arc ﬁts in ‒ clockwise or
counterclockwise.
This always results in a 90° arc.
Arc center m ode: Deﬁne the arc by (1) center point, (2) diam eter, and (3) end point of arc (=> angle extent).
To insert a new arc:
01. Choose
‒ M enu Tools > M arking Objects > Arc
‒ or Arc from  Toolbar M arking Objects.
A cruciform  cursor appears:
02. Select the required arc m ode (three point, quadrant (cw/ccw) or center m ode)
‒ in the Toolbar G raphics Path or
‒ by the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
03. Create the arc by the three points m ode:
‒ Click two desired positions in the work area to deﬁne the arc's endpoints.
‒ Draw the arc to the desired radius/diam eter.
‒ Select a third point, which lies on the arc, to deﬁne the radius.
or
Create the arc by quadrant m ode (clockw ise/counterclockw ise):
‒ Click a desired position in the work area to deﬁne the arc's start point,
‒ Draw the arc by deﬁning a bounding rectangle in which the arc ﬁts as a 90° arc.
‒ Click a desired position in the work area to deﬁne the arc's end point.
or
Create the arc by center m ode:
‒ Click a desired position in the work area to deﬁne the arc's center point.
‒ Draw the circle to the desired diam eter.
‒ Place the cursor on a desired position of the circle and click the left m ouse button to deﬁne the angle start and to deﬁne the
diam eter.
26. 3. 27. 오전  11:59 Inserting Arcs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/NewObject_New_Arc.htm 2/3

--- Page 315 ---

‒ M ove the cursor to deﬁne the angle extent (by setting the end point).
‒ Click the left m ouse button to ﬁnish the arc.
or
01. Place the cursor in the work area and click the right m ouse button.
The Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) opens.

cursor.
03. Click the left m ouse button to ﬁnish the action.
or

The inserted m arking object can be edited afterward m anually or by the W indow "Properties".

Inserting Polygons or Graphics Paths
The procedure of inserting a new polygon (consisting of lines) or graphics path (consisting of curves and/or lines) is sim ilar to the
procedure for other m arking objects, but diﬀers som ewhat due to additional steps - depending on the num bers of points.
M oreover, polygonal or graphics paths can be created as unclosed or closed m arking object (you can subsequently hatch closed
m arking objects).
The procedure described here uses the exam ple of a polygon:
01. Choose
‒ M enu Tools > M arking Objects > "Polygon"
‒ or "Polygon" from  Toolbar M arking Objects
‒ or W indow "Toolbox".
A cruciform  cursor appears.
02. For each point of the polygon, click a desired position in the work area.
Each new point gets connected by a line to the previous point.
03. To edit or change the polygon during creation, several com m ands are available by the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button):
Com m and Description
New Point Adds another point
Append To Polygon Resum es successive creation of a polygon, if this had been interrupted (see 'Release
Polygon' below)
Open Polygon Opens a closed polygon
Rem ove Point Deletes the last ﬁxed point
Close Polygon Closes the open polygon (required for ﬁlling)
Reverse Points Lets you change the point to continue creating the polygon or graphics path: from  the
last point to the start point or reverse.
Release Polygon Interrupts successive creation of a polygon; points can be edited and new points can be
inserted
Zoom  > Lets you execute zoom  functions (description, see U sing the Zoom  Functions).
   In
   Out
   Position
   To All G raphics
   To W ork Area
   To Selection
Options [View] > Lets you show or hide the work area's ruler an grid.
   Ruler
   G rid
Finish Finishes the creation procedure
04. To com plete the polygon, choose 'Finish (Edit)' from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
or
01. Place the cursor in the work area and click the right m ouse button.
The Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) opens.
02. Choose 'New > Polygon'.
A line is inserted, with one point ﬁxed and one point freely positionable.
03. Continue as described in steps 2 to 4 in the section above.
The procedure for creating a graphics path is sim ilar to that for creating a polygon. To edit or change a graphics path during
creation, additional functions are available by he Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) and (partly) by the Toolbar G raphics Path:
Com m and Description
New Point Adds another point

Inserting Text
To create static text, a placeholder or fram e is inserted ﬁrst, then the placeholder gets overwritten / the fram e gets ﬁlled with the
desired text. Static text, which is displayed in the work area, can be selected in two diﬀerent form ats:
Single-line Font Text uses properties of a font ‒ the proportions are ﬁxed. Therefore, the font text always retains its shape and
aspect ratio when m oved, rotated or scaled m anually. M ore extensive editing ‒ for exam ple, resolving the shape or
m anipulating single points ‒ is not possible.
M ultiline font text can be created by using the Text Box. The text-enclosing rectangle in the work area can be edited m anually
(position, rotation, size) ‒ but without aﬀecting the font text's size.
Vector Text (always single-line) is fully editable. Each character can be edited separately by m anipulating single points.
Note The textual content is deﬁned during creation (see below) or can be edited later in the graphics param eters (see Font
Text , Text Box and Vector Text).
Font text and text box content can be m odiﬁed later very easily:
- double click on the text (in the work area or J ob Explorer) to open a dialog.
- M odify the text content and click the button OK.
Note Non regular fonts (which does not support the 'regular' attribute) can be used. If such a font is chosen, laserDESK
checks the available attributes and selects an available one autom atically for creation. If later a non supported
attribute is activated, the font is switched to G enericSansSerif. Undo will roll back this action and restores the original
font.
Note In contrast to static text, text can be im ported from  external text ﬁles (see Inserting Variable Objects).
Note This topic describes how to create text in standard font form at. Additionally, laserDESK  lets you create text in
centerline fonts form at (see Creating Centerline Font).
To insert single-line text:
01. Select 'Font Text' or 'Vector Text' from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag it into the
work area.
A text elem ent of predeﬁned dim ensions gets inserted and the placeholder "<Text>" (default setting) appears.
02. Continue with step 3 in the section below.
or
01. Choose
‒ M enu Tools > M arking Objects > "Font Text" or "Vector Text"
‒ or 'Text 6 Font Text' or 'Text 6 Vector Text' from  the Toolbar M arking Objects.
A cruciform  cursor appears.
02. Click on a desired position in the work area:
A text elem ent in predeﬁned dim ensions gets inserted, the "<Text>" placeholder appears.
03. Enter the desired text.
04. W hen you have com pleted text input, choose "Finish" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
or
01. Place the cursor in the work area and click the right m ouse button.
The Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) opens.
02. Select 'New > Font Text' or 'New > Vector Text'.
A placeholder "<Text>" gets inserted, which can be stretched directly to the desired dim ensions by the m ouse.
03. Click the left m ouse button.
The placeholder changes color.
04. Continue with step 3 in the section above.
To insert m ulti-line font text:

Inserting Variable Objects
Date/tim e as well as serial num ber and im port text are special m arking objects em bedded with a function that can change the
inform ation:
The variable object 'Date,Tim e' always updates the tim e. laserDESK  lets you deﬁne the date/tim e form at (for exam ple, "J uly
4, 2010 - 2:53 pm " or "14.12.2010 - 14:53") by using several form at identiﬁers in the graphic param eters.
The variable object 'Serial Num ber' counts upwards or downwards with each m arked object. A start and end value as well as
an increm ent value can be set in the graphics param eters.
Additional to static text, laserDESK  allows to im port an external text ﬁle. The ﬁle's text content is assigned to the Im port Text
variable object by the deﬁnition of lines and colum ns to be done in the graphics param eters.
Note The ﬁle form at for the im ported text m ust be a U nicode text ﬁle.
Only the U nicode form at ensure that all characters are displayed correctly.
The variable objects can be selected in diﬀerent form ats:
Font Text Date/Tim e, Font Text Serial Num ber, and Im port Font Text behave like font text. The variable object always
retains its shape and aspect ratio when m oved, rotated or scaled m anually. Furtherm ore, the variable object cannot be edited
m anually.
Vector Text Date/Tim e, Vector Text Serial Num ber, and Im port Vector Text behave sim ilarly, but unlike the font text
objects, they do not retain their aspect ratio when edited m anually.
Barcode Date/Tim e, Barcode Serial Num ber, and Im port Barcode display the date/tim e, serial num bers or im port text in
barcode form at. Apart from  that, their behavior is sim ilar to the vector text objects.
The procedure for inserting a new variable object is very easy, followed by deﬁning the functions in the W indow "Properties".
To insert a new variable object:
01. Select the required variable object ('Date/Tim e\Font Text' ... 'Im port Text\Barcode') from  the W indow "Toolbox".
Continue with step 3 below.
or
01. Choose
‒ 'M arking Objects > Date/Tim e >'
‒ or 'M arking Objects > Serial Num ber >'
‒ or 'M arking Objects > Im port Text >'
from  the M enu Tools or from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
A list box opens.
02. Select
‒ 'Font Text' or 'Vector Text' or 'Barcode'.
03. Continue with step 3 below.
or
01. Select 'Date/Tim e 6', 'Serial Num ber 6' or 'Im port Text 6' from  the Toolbar M arking Objects.
A list box opens.
02. Select the corresponding '...Font Text...' or '...Vector Text...' or '...Barcode...'.
03. Click on a desired position in the work area.
The variable object is inserted and rem ains selected.
04. If desired, edit the settings of the em bedded function by the G raphic Param eters.
   or
Click next to the m arking object in the work area to ﬁnish the action.
The inserted variable object can be edited afterward m anually or by the W indow "Properties".

Inserting Reference to Sym bol
M arking objects or groups with m arking objects, which is used periodically (for exam ple, com pany logo, speciﬁc shape), can be
saved as a m aster to the local library labeled as 'Sym bol' (see Creating Sym bols). New m arking objects can then be inserted by
'Reference To Sym bol'.
Note The m arking object inserted in the work area by 'Reference To Sym bol' is not a copy of the sym bol in the library, it is a
real reference. M odifying the library's sym bol will change the referenced m arking object in the work area!
To insert a m arking object with reference to a sym bol:
01. Choose M enu Tools > M arking Objects > Reference To Sym bol or Reference To Sym bol from  Toolbar M arking Objects.
   or
Select Reference To Sym bol from  the W indow "Toolbox" and - keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag it in the work area.
   or
Choose New > Reference To Sym bol from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The dialog "Sym bols" opens:
Sym bols in the M arking Library are signed by the extension [lib].
02. Select a sym bol in the colum n 'Nam e'.
The shape of the sym bol is shown in the ﬁeld beside.
03. If necessary, select further sym bols until you ﬁnd the desired one.
04. Click the button OK.
The reference to sym bol is inserted in the work area and in the J ob Explorer tree.

Inserting Control Elem ents
Analog or digital control elem ents serve to control the m arking process. The m arking process is interrupted until the deﬁned
condition for the digital input signals is m et.
Output control elem ents serve to regulate the program  ﬂow between a job's m arking objects and an external system  by signal
outputs (for exam ple, for triggering a conveyor system ).
The param eters or signal m ode depend on the control elem ent. The values or settings of the respective control m ode are deﬁned
by the W indow "Properties" (graphic param eters).
Digital Input: digital input signal [bit pattern]
Digital Output: digital output signal [bit pattern]
Pulse Output: pulse length [m s]
Analog Input: analog input signal [V]
Analog Output: analog output signal [V]
Tim er Delay: waiting tim e [m s]
Control elem ents get inserted into the J ob Explorer tree. Depending on their position in the tree, control elem ents can control a
com plete job or a job sequence. You can change their position in the tree arbitrarily.
Note To insert a variant control elem ent, see Creating G roups, Sets or Variants.
To insert a serial com m unication control elem ent, see Inserting a Serial Com m unication Control Elem ent.
To insert a m otor control elem ent, see Inserting a M otor Control Elem ent.
To insert a z-focus control elem ent, see Inserting a Z-Focus Control Elem ent.
To insert a laser control elem ent, see Inserting a Laser Control Elem ent.
To insert a wait for encoder control elem ent, see Inserting a W ait For Encoder Control Elem ent.
To insert protocol and tim er nodes, see Inserting Protocol and Tim er Nodes.
To insert a control elem ent "Ram ping", see Inserting a control elem ent "Ram ping".
To insert a transform ation control elem ent, see Inserting a Transform ation Control Elem ent.
If the Processing-on-the-ﬂy (POF) option is enabled on the on the RTC5 board | RTC6 board enabled, the RTC supports
processing of parts in m otion (for exam ple, parts on a conveyor belt or rotating plate), as well as stationary parts with a
m oving scan system  (for exam ple, by a robot arm ). To adjust laser scan processes to the current workpiece position,
the position of the workpiece relative to the scan system  can be detected with the RTC by counting encoder signals (for
detailed description, refer to the 'Processing-on-the-ﬂy' chapter in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).
To insert a control elem ents:
01. Choose
‒ M enu Tools > Control Elem ents > (desired control elem ent)
‒ or (desired control elem ent) from  Toolbar Control Elem ents.
   or
Select the desired control elem ent from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag the
cursor into the work area.
The control elem ent is inserted autom atically into the J ob Explorer tree.
To change the control elem ent's position in the tree (and thus the program  ﬂow):
01. Select the desired control elem ent in the tree.
It is highlighted.
02. To m ove the control elem ent to the desired position
‒ sim ply m ove the control elem ent up or down by the cursor
‒ or choose "Sequencing" > "..." from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
‒ or use the Sequence ... buttons in the Toolbar Arrange.
Note To deﬁne the control elem ent input m ode or output m ode, see G raphic Param eters ‒ Overview.
W hen deﬁning the control elem ents' signal input or output observe the pin assignm ent of the RTC board (see

Inserting a Control Elem ent "M otor Control"
laserDESK  can com m and m otor (axis) controllers for diﬀerent purposes, especially for m oving or rotating parts. This m otion can
be included into the job execution to be executed between diﬀerent m arking processes without interaction of another control
device like a PLC. The m otor control elem ent is used to execute this task.

Inserting a Control Elem ent "Z-Focus Control"
A 3-axis scan system  with a dynam ic focusing unit allows you to focus the laser beam  in the z direction. The z-focus control
elem ent serves to control a varioSCAN and thus to focus the laser beam  in a deﬁned z position. W ith the z-focus control elem ent,
you can im plem ent 2.5D applications, for exam ple, deep engraving.
Note Z-focus control is only possible, if the Option "3D" is enabled on the RTC5 board | RTC6 board (see 'Controlling a 3-Axis
Scan System  (optional)' in RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual) and a 3D correction ﬁle (D3_nnn.ct5) is used (see 'Correction
File' param eter in Setting 'Optics' Param eters).
The z position set with this control elem ent rem ains active until changed by another z-focus control elem ent. If a m arking object
(or several m arking objects) should be m arked in diﬀerent levels (z positions), you m ust deﬁne the z position for each level:
Create and edit the m arking object(s).
Copy and paste thee desired num ber of m arking object(s).
Align the m arking object(s) to each other.
Insert the corresponding num ber of z-focus control elem ents.
Align one z-focus control elem ent in front of each object / group of objects (see ﬁgure below).
Deﬁne the z position for each z-focus control elem ent in the Properties W indow.
Note After a job term inates, the dynam ic focusing unit's diverging optic does not return to the neutral/zero position by itself.
The m ost-recently-set z position rem ains active and thus in eﬀect across-jobs. This can cause an unintended z-focus
position when executing the next job.
W hen using a 3-axis scan system , we recom m end always inserting a z-focus control elem ent at the top of the J ob
Explorer and deﬁning the z position (norm ally "0").
Note If you want to m ark one or several m arking objects in diﬀerent levels as described above, it can be helpful to assign
diﬀerent layers to the m arking objects to distinguish m ore easily between the objects (see param eter 'Layer' in the
graphic param eters).
To insert a z-focus control elem ent:
01. Choose
‒ M enu Tools > Control Elem ents > "Z-Focus Control"
‒ or "Z-Focus Control" from  Toolbar Control Elem ents.
   or
Select 'Z-Focus Control' from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag the cursor into the
work area.
The control elem ent is inserted autom atically into the J ob Explorer tree.
To change a z-focus control elem ent's position in the tree:

Inserting a Control Elem ent "W ait For Encoder"
W ith enabled Processing-on-the-ﬂy (POF) option, the RTC5 board | RTC6 board supports processing of workpieces in m otion (for
exam ple, workpieces on a conveyor belt or rotating plate), as well as stationary workpieces with a m oving scan system  (for
exam ple, by a robot arm ). To adjust laser scan processes to the current workpiece position, the position of the workpiece relative
to the scan system  can be detected with the RTC by counting encoder signals. For further inform ation, refer to chapter
"Processing-on-the-ﬂy" in RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
To start part of an execution at an exactly deﬁned position, the control elem ent "W ait for Encoder" is available. Its intended use is
to separate the overall m arking into execution sequences (m arking parts) and deﬁne a control elem ent "W ait for Encoder" before
its appropriate m arking part to elim inate the problem s caused by m arking tim e diﬀerences between them . This ensures that the
m arking starts only when its position is inside the m arking area of the scan head.
Note Prerequisites for control elem ent "W ait for Encoder" are: a. RTC5 board | RTC6 board with enabled Processing-on-the-
ﬂy (POF) option (refer to chapter "Processing-on-the-ﬂy" in RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual) and b. Hardware
Conﬁguration POF param eters are set correctly, see Setting the Param eters 'Processing-On-The-Fly'.
Further execution of a list can be postponed by the control elem ent "W ait for Encoder" until the selected encoder counter (that is,
the workpiece position) has exceeded or fallen below a predeﬁned value. The control elem ent "W ait for Encoder" can be inserted
several tim es. It is im portant to ensure that their param eters are adapted to the overall m arking process.
Param eter 'M ode' can have the values 'Exceed', 'Fall Below' or 'Pass By'. Only with 'Pass By' does the process wait until the encoder
count actually passes the deﬁned distance. For both other both values, the process does not wait if the condition is already
fulﬁlled (see com m and wait_for_encoder_m ode in RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).
To insert a control elem ent "W ait for Encoder":
01. Select
‒ M enu Tools > Control Elem ents > W ait For Encoder
‒ or "W ait For Encoder" from  Toolbar Control Elem ents
   or
Select W ait For Encoder from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag the cursor into the
work area.
The control elem ent "W ait For Encoder" is inserted autom atically into the J ob Explorer tree.
To change the position of a control elem ent "W ait For Encoder" in the tree:
01. Select the desired control elem ent in the tree.
It is highlighted.
02. To m ove the control elem ent to the desired position:
‒ sim ply m ove the control elem ent up or down by the cursor
‒ or choose "Sequencing" > "..." from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
‒ or use the Sequence ... buttons in the Toolbar Arrange.
Note To deﬁne the wait for encoder param eters, see G raphic Param eters ‒ W ait For Encoder.
(original text - this is not a translation)

Inserting Control Elem ents for Protocol and Tim er
To record signals of the scan head or RTC5 board | RTC6 board, you m ust additionally insert one pair of protocol nodes
(Protocol Start/Stop) into the J ob Explorer tree. The sam pling rate can be set from  10 µs.
Additionally, the Protocol Start node supports m ost logging options. In order to take full advantage of the extensive logging
capabilities it is recom m ended to read How to U se Logging Possibilities.
The tim er (Tim er Start/Stop) serves to m easure the m arking execution tim e, using the very precise tim er of the RTC board. If
the tim er is set, the tim e m easured by the tim er is indicated in the status bar and not the tim e of the com plete job (exception:
the tim er fram es the com plete job). There is only one m easuring possible.
Both protocol and tim er nodes can be positioned arbitrarily in the tree, but m ust always be set with 'Protocol/Tim er Start' above
'Protocol/Tim er Stop'. Only program  ﬂow (this can be the com plete job or a job sequence) that is fram ed by the protocol nodes or
the tim er is recorded or m easured.
The value of the protocol node's sam pling rate is deﬁned in the window "Elem ent properties". For the tim er no deﬁnitions are
required.
G raphic Param eters ‒ Protocol Start: sam pling rate [µs]
G raphic Param eters ‒ Tim er Start
G raphic Param eters ‒ Tim er Stop
To insert protocol or tim er nodes:
01. Select
‒ M enu Tools > Control Elem ents > "Protocol Start" or "Tim er Start" from
‒ or "Protocol Start" or "Tim er Start" from  Toolbar "Control Elem ents".
   or
Select 'Protocol Start' or 'Tim er Start' from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag the
cursor into the work area.
'Protocol Start' or 'Tim er Start' is inserted autom atically in the J ob Explorer tree.
02. Proceed sim ilarly to insert 'Protocol Stop' or 'Tim er Stop'.
'Protocol Stop' or 'Tim er Stop' is inserted autom atically in the J ob Explorer tree.
To position the protocol or tim er nodes in the J ob Explorer tree (and thus in the program  ﬂow):
01. Select 'Protocol Start' or 'Tim er Start' in the tree.
It is highlighted.
02. Sim ply m ove 'Protocol Start' or 'Tim er Start' by the cursor.
   or
Choose "Sequencing" > "..." from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
   or
U se the Sequence ... buttons in the Toolbar Arrange
03. Proceed sim ilarly to m ove 'Protocol Stop' or 'Tim er Stop'.
Note 'Protocol Stop' or 'Tim er Stop' m ust always be positioned below 'Protocol Start' or 'Tim er Start'!

Inserting a Control Elem ent "Transform ation"
The transform ation control elem ent is used to set a transform ation global to the working area, that is, the rotation is around the
origin. It is additional to the transform ation deﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration (see param eters 'Oﬀset' and 'Rotation' in
Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters). This allows both to adapt the scanner coordinate system  to the m achine coordinate system  by
the Hardware Conﬁguration and to transform  the elem ents in the m achine coordinate system  by the transform ation control
elem ents.
The control elem ent includes the 'Scan Head' param eter, which allows to assign the transform ation head speciﬁc. A
transform ation for head 1 will not change the transform ation of head 2 (and vice versa).
The deﬁned transform ation is applied for all following elem ents until a new control node will change it again. At job end the
transform ation is reset, else the next execution would start with the transform ation of the last control node. Therefore, it is
recom m ended to use either transform ation control elem ents with rem ote com m and #55 or using rem ote com m ands #16, #20, #43
and #44 for global transform ations, refer to Rem ote Control M anual.
The transform ation param eters can be changed by the rem ote com m and #55 using the Transform ation ID value.
To insert a transform ation control elem ent:
01. Select M enu Tools > Control Elem ents > Transform ation or Transform ation from  Toolbar Control Elem ents.
   or
Select 'Transform ation' from  the W indow "Toolbox" and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ drag the cursor into the
work area.
The control elem ent is inserted autom atically into the J ob Explorer tree.
To change a transform ation control elem ent's position in the tree:
01. Select the desired control elem ent in the tree.
It is highlighted.
02. To m ove the control elem ent to the desired position:
‒ sim ply m ove the control elem ent up or down by the cursor
‒ or choose "Sequencing" > "..." from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
‒ or use the Sequence ... buttons in the Toolbar Arrange.
Note To deﬁne the transform ation param eters, see G raphic Param eters ‒ Transform ation.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25

Im porting Pixel Im ages, Vector Graphics and STL Files

Bitm ap (*.bm p)

from  a scanner (*.twain)

Form at).

DXF (*.dxf)

Scalable Vector G raphics (*.svg)

Sym bol Im port (vector graphics only!)
New Sym bols If the vector graphics contains new sym bols, you can deﬁne how to proceed with the
sym bols during im port:
‒ If "Add" is selected, then the new sym bols are inserted in the m arking library
autom atically.
‒ If "Expand" is selected, then all sym bol data is inserted and resolved. This m ode is required
for vector graphics with nested sym bols.
Existing Sym bols If the vector graphics contains existing sym bols, you can deﬁne how to proceed with the
sym bols during im port:
‒ If "Skip" is selected, the vector graphics' sym bol is skipped and the existent sym bol is kept.
‒ If "U pdate" is selected, the existent sym bol is updated with the data of the vector graphics'
sym bol.
‒ If "Copy" is selected, the vector graphics' sym bol is saved with a new nam e.

is connected autom atically.
This corresponds to the 'Connect' function (see Com bining Two M arking Objects).

'Connect Lines' (see above) is activated. The ﬁrst line which satisﬁes the condition is
connected.
Enter the value directly in the input ﬁeld or use the arrow buttons.

objects ‒ not as full m arking objects. The graphics objects have no individual param eters
and cannot be m odiﬁed individually (with the exception of point lists). This results in a faster
im port and reduces m em ory capacity problem s.

deﬁned as 'Outline' inside the vector ﬁle. Thus, these objects can be ﬁlled im m ediately.

Im porting File Sets
The im porting of ﬁle sets allows you to arrange associated ﬁles (vector graphics or pixel im ages) one upon the other in the z-
direction. This function is very beneﬁcial when ﬁles are to be arranged for 2.5D applications (for exam ple, deep engraving) and
every ﬁle deﬁnes one layer. The required Z-Focus Control Elem ents between the several ﬁles/objects are inserted autom atically in
J ob Explorer.
The ﬁle nam es need a special syntax. They consist of a constant string and an increasing num ber (for exam ple, xxxxxx001.dxf ‒
xxxxxx100.dxf)
The Dialog "Im port File Set" lets you select the desired ﬁles, deﬁne the z-oﬀset and m ake size and position adjustm ents:
Dialog "Im port File Set"
Field Description

[Field] The Button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog.
Available entries:
Select the ﬁrst ﬁle of the desired ﬁle set.
Start Deﬁnes the ﬁrst ﬁle to be im ported.
Note
‒ The num ber m ust be a com ponent of an available ﬁle nam e.
‒ All ﬁle nam es of one ﬁle set m ust have the sam e syntax
(for exam ple, "Nam e_1", "Nam e_2", "Nam e_3", ...
or for exam ple, "01_nam e", "02_nam e", "03_nam e", ...).
‒ Only ascending order is possible.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Importing and Slicing STL Files
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Import_ImportSTLFile.htm 1/6

--- Page 366 ---

Im porting and Slicing STL Files
An STL ﬁle (Standard Tessellation Language) deﬁnes the surface of a 3D object by triangles. It cannot be used directly for laser
processes, because the data are surface objects (triangles in space), but the scan heads are controlled by vector data.
During the STL ﬁle im port the triangles are sliced by equidistant levels parallel to the x,y-level. The result are closed ﬂat polygons
laying in diﬀerent levels. Each point of one polygon has the sam e z-value.
This function is prim arily required for volum e processing, that is, the rem oval of m aterial (deep engraving) and the fusion of
m aterial (rapid prototyping / 3D printing).
Original STL ﬁle with triangles STL ﬁle after im port with equidistant
polygons
Note The slices are called levels, the layers are features of laserDESK . Here levels m ay correspond to layers if selected by the
user.
The STL ﬁle im port of laserDESK  supports both ﬁle types, ASCII and binary STL ﬁles.
The im port and slicing of the STL ﬁle is carried out by the Dialog "Im port STL-File". It lets you select the desired ﬁle and deﬁne the
size, position and the am ount of levels.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Importing and Slicing STL Files
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Import_ImportSTLFile.htm 2/6

--- Page 367 ---

Dialog "Im port STL-File"
Field Description
STL-Filenam e
[Field] The Button 
  serves to select the desired ﬁle.
A M S W indows-standard dialog opens.
The selectable form at is: 'STL (*.stl)'.
Size
Length x The size can be deﬁned by the input ﬁelds 'Length x', 'W idth y' or 'Height z' (absolute in
[m m , cm . m il or in]; the units are set in View Param eter by the window "Options").
If 'Aspect Ratio' (see below) is ﬁxed, only one value needs to be deﬁned under 'Length',
'W idth' or 'Height'. But if m ultiple values are inserted, the ﬁrst value given deﬁnes the size.
If 'Aspect Ratio' is variable, all values m ust be deﬁned. Or, if all three values are "0", a 1:1
im port is executed.
Note
The height and the num ber of levels deﬁne the distance of the levels (see 'Num ber of
Levels' below).
W idth y
Height z
Aspect Ratio If set to "Fixed", only one size param eter ('Length', 'W idth' or 'Height', see above) m ust be
set. In this case, the aspect ratio of the original ﬁle are retained.
If set to "Variable", all size param eters m ust be deﬁned. In this case, the aspect ratio of the
original ﬁle m ay change.
Slicing
Num ber of Levels If the 'U se Level Distance' checkbox (see below) is unchecked, then the 'Height z' (see
'Size' param eters above) and the num ber of levels deﬁne the distance of the levels:
(height / [num ber ‒ 1]).
U se Level Distance If this checkbox is selected, then the level distance (absolute in [m m , cm . m il or in]) can be
entered directly into the text ﬁeld.
If this checkbox is cleared, then the distance m ust be deﬁned with the param eters 'Height'
and 'Num ber of Levels' (see above).
Arrange Levels descending If this checkbox is selected, the sequence of the calculated levels is reversed to upside
down (starting from  the top).
This should be the standard sequence for deep engraving.
Create Z-Focus Control Elem ents If this checkbox is selected, then Z-Focus Control elem ents is used and all objects have z =
0. The Z-Focus Control elem ents adjust the z-position for each level by the varioSCAN
dynam ic focusing unit.
If a varioSCAN is not connected, you can use an external axis to adjust z instead. The z-
values for the axis system  are read from  the distinct Z-Focus Control elem ents. The
execution of these nodes do not change the scan system .
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Importing and Slicing STL Files
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Import_ImportSTLFile.htm 3/6

--- Page 368 ---

Field Description
Because the ﬁlling lines can only be calculated in 2D, they will always be displayed in the
x,y,z = 0‒level (but executed in the actual one), unless they are transform ed by a layer (see
param eter below).
If this checkbox is cleared, then real 3D polygons are used.
Note
‒ The visualization of the polygons in the 3D view is not possible if the check box is
activated and thus Z-Focus Control elem ents are used.
‒ The im port autom atically sets the job param eter U se 3D = "True" unless this checkbox is
selected.
Create Layer for each Level If this checkbox is selected, then an individual layer for each level with the nam e 'Layer
[num ber]' is created. This allows to rem otely assign diﬀerent param eter sets to each layer
using the rem ote com m and #38, refer to Rem ote Control M anual.
The z-values of the graphics are all zeros (0) and the real z-position is a z-transform ation in
the layer properties (see Editing a Layer). This allows the shift and correct visualization of
the ﬁlling, too.
Create J obs If this checkbox is selected, then each layer is inserted in an own job. Each job is labeled
with the extension '_Level [num ber]' (for exam ple, 'M yJ ob_Level 4.sld'). This allows to
execute each level separately and avoids m em ory problem s.
This feature is m ainly intended to be used with the rem ote control functions. Each layer
job can be loaded, positioned and executed by rem ote com m ands and a m arking
param eter set can be assigned individually.
In between the job executions an external handling (for exam ple, m oving a z-axis) can be
executed.
Create Signal Exchange If this checkbox is selected, a signal exchange consisting of three control nodes are
inserted before each layer.
The control nodes are:
‒ 'Digital Out' to signal "A new layer is started";
‒ 'Digital In' to wait for the preparation of the new layer;
‒ 'Digital Out' to reset the signal for the next cycle.
The digital lines can be deﬁned by the 'Digital Out' ﬁeld and 'Digital In' ﬁeld.
Available are the digital IOs 0-7 of Connector EXTENSION 1.
Hatching
Apply Hatching If this checkbox is selected, then laserDESK  calculates the hatching autom atically.
If this checkbox is cleared, hatching m ust be perform ed later (see Filling M arking Objects).
Angle-Oﬀset The oﬀset (in [°]) entered here, rotates each hatching of a level relative to the previous
one.
Hatching with an oﬀset is used to avoid furrows or unevenness.
U se Fill Param eters Set The listbox lets you select a Fill Param eters set used for the hatch calculation (assum ing
that besides <default> further Fill Param eter sets have been created; see Library Explorer /
M arking Library).
Position
Center X Deﬁnes the reference point (in [m m , cm . m il or in]) of the 3D object position.
Center Y
Z-Position (1.Slice) Deﬁnes the
‒ bottom  position for ascending slices or
‒ upper position for descending slices (in [m m , cm . m il or in]),
dependent on the checkbox 'Arrange Levels descending '.
Rotation All slices are rotated by the angle entered in the text ﬁeld.

01. Choose M enu File > Im port > STL File Slicing.
The Dialog "Im port STL-File" opens.

Proposed Workﬂow
SCANLAB proposes a workﬂow for the execution sequence using the rem ote control:
01. Create a new job and save it with a m eaningful nam e.
02. Im port the STL ﬁle and set the im port param eters as you like (see description above).
SCANLAB proposes that you save each level in an own ﬁle and create layers.
03. U se the rem ote control for the execution sequence:
‒ Load the ﬁrst level job (com m and #7, refer to Rem ote Control M anual)
‒ Set the appropriate ﬁlling (com m and #38), optional
‒ If necessary position the job (com m and #20), optional
‒ Execute the job (com m and #12)
‒ Repeat these steps for each level
Note Before and after each com m and you can process additional handling outside of laserDESK , for exam ple, preparing
the next level for rapid prototyping or m oving an external z-axis to adjust the focus.
(original text - this is not a translation)

Inserting Pixel Im ages or Vector Graphics
Pixel im age or vector graphics insertion by the toolbox or he Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) is diﬀerent from  im porting a pixel
im age or vector graphics. You have to ﬁrst deﬁne the position and size of the im age/graphics in the work area, before selecting the
im age/graphics by a M S W indows-standard dialog. Pixel im ages with bm p, jpg, gif, pcx, png, tag, tif or twain form at and vector
graphics with ai, dxf, plt or svg form at can be inserted.
Note laserDESK  supports only the pure 'ai' form at up to Adobe Illustrator Version 8.0.
In higher versions, Adobe has extended the ai-Form at to a pdf-Form at without a need to change the extension 'ai' to
'pdf'. This form at is not supported by laserDESK .
SCANLAB recom m ends to use ai-Form at.
To insert a pixel im age or vector graphics:
01. Click "Pixel Im age" or 'Vector G raphics' in the W indow "Toolbox".
A cruciform  cursor appears.
02. Click a desired position in the work area and ‒ while keeping the m ouse button pressed ‒ draw a rectangle.
W hen the rectangle reaches the desired dim ensions, release the m ouse button.
The M S W indows standard dialog "Open" opens (see ﬁgure above).
03. Continue with step 3 in the section below.
or
01. Choose "New > Pixel Im age" or "New > Vector G raphics" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
A fram e is inserted, which can be stretched to the desired size with the cruciform  cursor.
02. W hen the rectangle reaches the desired dim ensions, click the left m ouse button.
The M S W indows standard dialog "Open" opens.
03. Select the desired pixel im age or vector graphics.
If necessary, choose the desired ﬁle form at in the 'File Type' listbox.
04. Click the Open button.
The selected pixel im age or vector graphics are inserted into the work area.
Note The G U I settings (see 'Edit' Param eters) determ ine whether the inserted im age/graphics keep its aspect ratio.
Back to Im porting Pixel Im ages and Vector G raphics
