26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Creating Groups, Sets or Variants
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/NewObject_New_GroupVariant.htm 1/4

--- Page 374 ---

Creating Groups, Sets, Variants
Som e objects of laserDESK  let you collect m ultiple objects:
A group can collect m ultiple m arking objects and control elem ents. The m arking objects keep their individual param eters.
A graphics set is sim ilar to a group, but has no individual param eters. Therefore, it is possible to sort all the objects inside a
graphics set. If a single object needs individual param eters, it has to be ungrouped from  the graphics set. Characters of a text
object are special graphics sets.
A graphics paths (set) is sim ilar to a graphics set, but the m arking objects include only the graphic inform ation without any
param eter set, that is, the objects inside a graphics paths (set) can not be individually param eterized. Filling paths are special
graphics paths (sets).
(The diﬀerence between a graphics paths (set) and a graphics path is that the graphics paths (set) can consist of several
independent individual graphic elem ents whereas a graphics path is a collection of connected curves or lines which represent
one path.)
A tile set lets you m ark a workpiece in several steps if (for exam ple,) the size of a workpiece exceeds the dim ensions of the
laser scan system 's m arking area. Therefore, the m arking object(s) is divided into several tiles. The m arking objects lose their
individual param eters and get the tile set param eters.
A variant is a partial job (subroutine) within a (m ain) job. It can include m arking objects and control elem ents, which keep
their individual param eters. A variant requires a control node which deﬁnes a starting condition for this partial job. Therefore,
each variant needs to be assigned a bit pattern.
Note To handle variants optim ally, it is recom m ended to read the topic How to U se Variants.
The procedure to create a collecting object (group, graphics paths (set), graphics set, tile set, variant) is the sam e for all. You ﬁrst
select the collecting object and then insert the m arking objects (and, if applicable, the control elem ents). Alternatively, it is
possible to create a collecting object by grouping several already created m arking objects (and control elem ents).
Note W hen creating a collecting object, the following restrictions apply for inserting objects:
‒ G enerally, variants cannot be inserted in any other object.
‒ A graphics set cannot com prise groups, graphics sets, tile sets, variable objects ('Date, Tim e', 'Serial Num ber', 'Im port
Text') or control elem ents.
‒ A graphics paths (set) cannot com prise groups, graphics paths (sets), graphics sets, and control elem ents.
‒ A tile set cannot com prise tile sets, variable objects ('Date, Tim e', 'Serial Num ber', 'Im port Text') or control elem ents.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Creating Groups, Sets or Variants
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/NewObject_New_GroupVariant.htm 2/4

--- Page 375 ---

Creating a Group, Set or Variant
To insert a collecting object:
01. Do one of the following:
‒ Choose M enu Tools > M arking Objects > "G roup", "G raphics Paths", "G raphics Set", "Tile Set"
‒ Choose M enu Tools > Control Elem ents > Variant
‒ Choose 'G roup', 'G raphics Paths', 'G raphics Set' or 'Tile Set' from  the Toolbar M arking Objects
‒ Choose 'Add Variant' in the Toolbar Control Elem ents
‒ Choose "New" > "G raphics Paths", "G raphics Set", "G roup", "Tile Set", "Variant" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse
Button).
The collecting object is listed in the J ob Explorer and below it '<em pty>'.
Inserted m arking objects still in the work area are shown in light gray.
02. Insert one or m ultiple m arking objects and (if applicable) control elem ents as described in Inserting new Objects.
In J ob Explorer, each m arking object and control elem ent is listed below the collecting object.
03. After inserting all m arking objects and control elem ents, choose 'Finish (Edit)' from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
In J ob Explorer, the collecting object is preceded with a '+':
Note If one or several variants are inserted into a job, these is listed below the 'Variants' node:
This node gets created autom atically when a variant is inserted. All variants are always listed below this node, because
laserDESK  does not allow distribution of several variants across the tree .
Note After you have inserted variants into a job, you m ust deﬁne the 'Variant Bit M ask' in the G raphics Param eters ‒ J ob and
the 'Bit Pattern' in the G raphics Param eters ‒ Variant. Otherwise, the variants cannot be distinguished and selected
(see How to U se Variants for a detailed description).

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Creating a Centerline Font
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/CenterLineFont.htm 1/3

--- Page 378 ---

Creating a Centerline Font
TrueType fonts are deﬁned by a closed outline (type 'Area'). For m any applications, a centerline font is advantageous (e.g it
shortens the m arking tim e). laserDESK  lets you easily reduce TrueType fonts to centerline fonts ‒ the single line is placed in the
center of the source font:
Centerline fonts are handled as an attribute like bold or italic. J ust click the Centerline button to assign this attribute to the text
object. Centerline fonts are created autom atically when creating or editing any kind of text object in font or vector form at: single
line text, m ulti line text (text box), variable objects (date/tim e, serial num ber, im port text). The text objects can be converted
directly by clicking a respective button or by param eter setting in the W indow "Properties". Additionally, a centerline font can be
created in the Library Explorer by the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The default for autom atically created centerline fonts includes all characters A-Z, a-z and num bers 0-9. If other characters are used
in the text they are autom atically created and added. If the centerline font gets created in the Library Explorer, the range of created
characters can be deﬁned. Thus, it is possible to create centerline characters for all available U nicode characters including Chinese
or J apanese glyphs.
laserDESK  saves the centerline font in the local job library. Later, the font can be exported to the global M arking Library (using and
exchanging fonts is described in Library Explorer / M arking Library). The centerline font can be edited in the Library Explorer, for
exam ple, if you want to im prove the conversion result to your requirem ents. The centerline characters are edited like any other
m arking object (see Editing Fonts).
If a character of a centerline font is created and stored in the job library, it can be used in all job objects. During execution of the
job, the stored characters are used and no further calculation is necessary.
To create a centerline font in the Library Explorer, see Creating a new Font.
To create a centerline font while creating or editing text:
01. Before creating a text object, select the desired font and click (activate) the Centerline button in the Toolbar Text.
The dialog "Font" opens, asking for conﬁrm ation to create a center line font.
02. Click Yes.
03. Create the text as described in Inserting Text.
The text is converted to center line font.
The new font is saved in the job library.
or
01. Create the text as described in Inserting Text.
02. Select the text object (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
03. Click (activate) the Centerline button in the Toolbar Text.
The 'Font' dialog opens, asking for conﬁrm ation to create a center line font.
04. Click Yes.
The text is converted to a center line font.
The new font is saved in the job library.
or
01. Create the text as described in Inserting Text.
02. Select the text object (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
03. Select the graphic param eter 'Centerline' in the W indow "Properties".
04. Select "True" in the corresponding listbox.
The text is converted directly to a center line font.
The new font is saved in the job library.
If desired, edit the individual characters of the new font (see Editing Fonts).

Creating a New Font
laserDESK  not only oﬀers a huge num ber of fonts, it also lets you edit fonts to m odify characters based on your ideas or
requirem ents. Because standard TrueType fonts cannot be m odiﬁed, new fonts m ust be created. First, these fonts are
autom atically saved in the local job library and can be exported later to the global M arking Library. In a second step, each
character of the saved fonts can be edited.
laserDESK  provides two options to create new fonts:
Outline and centerline fonts can be created inside the local job library or the global M arking Library as described below.
Centerline fonts only can be created when creating or editing text (see Creating a Centerline Font).
Inside the library, a new font is created by using the Dialog "New Laser Font".
Dialog "New Laser Font"
Com m and Description
Font Fam ily:
   Font Style Font.
   Font Size Font size. 8pt...78pt.
Alternatively, the font size can be entered directly. In this case, sizes sm aller than 8pt or greater than
78pt are possible.
Note
Larger font sizes are m ore accurate, but need a longer processing tim e for creating centerline fonts.
B Boldface can be set or reset.
I Italic can be set or reset.
T Centerline can be set or reset.
G lyphs
   Count Lim it Lets you lim it the num ber of characters to be created.
By default, the total num ber of characters are created.
Enter the desired value in the input ﬁeld or use the arrow buttons.
Centerline
   G raphic Resolution Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution of the centerline font. This factor is used for calculating the
curve's polygon adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) the curve ﬁtting.
Only available if Centerline (see above) is activated.
To create a new font:
01. Select the window "Library Explorer" inside a job or choose M enu File > Open > M arking Library.
The Library Explorer tree shows "Library" at the top.
02. Place the cursor in the Library Explorer .

The Dialog "New Laser Font" opens.
04. Select the font fam ily and font size; if desired activate Bold, Italic or T (Centerline).

06. If available (with centerline only), deﬁne the graphic resolution.

Editing Objects ‒ Overview
After you have inserted new m arking objects, they can be edited in the work area or by the W indow "Properties". For editing, the
m arking objects m ust be selected in the work area or in the J ob Explorer tree. G enerally, you can choose between two m ethods,
depending on individual preferences or the dem ands on precision:
To to edit m arking objects (as well as groups and variants) m anually with the m ouse in the work area is the m ost sim ple and
fastest m ethod. This com prises hatching, m oving, scaling, rotating and changing the m arking object's shape.
M odifying the m arking object's graphic param eters is the m ost accurate m ethod.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Manually Editing Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_EditObject.htm 1/4

M anually Editing M arking Objects
M arking objects (including groups and variants) can be edited m anually in the work area as follows:
M ove the m arking object
Rotate the m arking object
Scale the m arking object
M ove single points of the m arking object (and thereby change its shape)
Add single points to the m arking object (and thereby change its shape)
Change an open m arking object to a closed polygon by connecting points
Note The scaling behavior of m arking objects can be set globally in Dialog "Options", see Section 'Edit'.
‒ Select the checkbox 'K eep Shape', m arking objects such as circle will not lose their shape.
‒ Select the checkbox 'K eep Aspect Ratio', the aspect ratio of m arking objects persist when scaled.
For each m arking object, this behavior can be individually speciﬁed in the W indow "Properties", see G raphic
Param eters ‒ Overview):
‒ <default>: the shape inherits the settings of the edit param eters (see above).
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio deﬁnitely.
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio deﬁnitely.
Note If a circle rem ains a circle after all applied changes, the circle then corresponds to one arc of 360°. In this case, the
m arking of the entire circum ference is executed in one go. The rotation deﬁnes the starting point on the circum ference
of the circle. The circle will always be m arked clockwise.
In case the editing of the circle results in stretching or transform ation or if sym bol references are used, the circle is
m arked as four 90° arcs or as polygon.
Path ﬁlling of a circle will create polygons as ﬁlling lines, not arcs.
Note Font text, barcode and variable objects (Date/Tim e, Serial Num ber and Im port Text in any form at) can only be m oved,
rotated, scaled, and ﬁlled.
M ore extensive editing ‒ for exam ple, resolving the shape or m anipulating single points ‒ is not possible.
The content of single- and m ulti-line font text (see Inserting Text) can be m odiﬁed in a dialog, the content of variable
objects can only be m odiﬁed in the graphic param eters.
Proceed as follows:
01. Select the desired m arking object (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shape changes color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e with support points for editing:
02. For editing, m ove the cursor above a desired support point.
The cursor changes appearance, for exam ple,:
03. Press the m ouse button and m anipulate the m arking object:
‒ U se the rectangle support points on the fram e to change the shape's dim ensions (if 'K eep Aspect Ratio' is not activated,
then sim ultaneously press the <Shift> key for proportional scaling).
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Manually Editing Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_EditObject.htm 2/4

--- Page 392 ---

‒ U se the round center support point to m ove the shape in the work area.
‒ U se the round upper support point to rotate the shape.
04. Choose "Edit" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The shape will change color and m anipulation points are shown:
Note Depending on the m arking object, Edit can be chosen one or several tim es from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse
Button).
For exam ple, vector text can be edited in several steps/levels: text, character or outline:
Note If (single- or m ulti-line) font text has been selected, a dialog opens. Change or overwrite the text content and click
the button OK . Further editing is not possible.
05. Open the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) again.
Following com m ands are available:
Com m and Description
New Point Adds another point.
Append To Path/Polygon Resum es successive creation of the path/polygon, if this has been interrupted (see 'Release
Path/Polygon' below).
Release Path/Polygon Interrupts successive creation of the path/polygon; points can be edited and new points can
be inserted.

   Three Point Arc M ode Deﬁnes the arc by three points.
   Arc Quadrant M ode (CW ) Deﬁnes the arc by a quadratic function (clockwise).
   Arc Quadrant M ode (CCW ) Deﬁnes the arc by a quadratic function (counterclockwise).
   Center M ode Deﬁnes an arc by the center point.
Bezier Curve M odes > (only path)
   Bezier Points M ode Deﬁnes a param etric points curve.
   Bezier Curves M ode Deﬁnes a param etric curves curve.
Reserve Points Inverts the points' order from  the start point to the end point (or reverse).
U nround Corner Restores the round corner of the selected edge to angular shape, if it has been rounded
before with 'Round Corner' (see Altering the M arking Object's Shape).
(only path)
Expand (only path)
Zoom  > see U sing the Zoom  Functions
   In
   Out
   Position

   To W ork Area
   To Selection
Finish (Edit) Finishes the editing procedure

M anually Editing Groups, Sets, Variants and Vector G raphics
Note W hen you edit a graphics paths (set), graphics set, tile set, variant or vector graphics, these m arking objects have the
sam e properties as a group.
Therefore; the following description uses the term  "group" only.
G roups can be edited m anually in the work area as follows:
Editing a Com plete G roup:
‒ m ove the group
‒ scale up the group (up or down)
‒ rotate the group
Editing a Single M arking Object of the G roup
Dissolving a G roup
Note The scaling behavior of the group can be set globally in Dialog "Options".
‒ If the 'K eep Shape' checkbox is selected, then the shape of the m arking object will persist when scaled.
‒ If the 'K eep Aspect Ratio' checkbox is selected, then the aspect ratio of the m arking object will persist when scaled.
For each m arking object or group, this behavior can be individually speciﬁed in G raphic Param eters (window "Elem ent
Properties"):
‒ <default>: the shape inherits the settings of the edit param eters (see above).
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio deﬁnitely.
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio deﬁnitely.

02. M ove the cursor above a desired support point.
The cursor changes appearance, for exam ple,:
03. Click m ouse button and m anipulate the group:
‒ U se the rectangle's support points on the fram e to change the shape's dim ensions (if 'K eep Aspect Ratio' is not activated
(see note above), then sim ultaneously press the <Shift> key for proportional scaling).
‒ U se the round center support point to m ove the shape in the work area.
‒ U se the round upper support point to rotate the shape.
04. After m anipulating the group, choose 'Finish' from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).

Editing Fonts
After a new font has been created (see Creating a New Font and Creating a Centerline Font), each character of the font can be
edited. The new font, which is initially saved in the local job library and m ay be exported later to the global M arking Library, is
listed in the Library Explorer below 'Fonts'.
W hen you click the plus sign on the new font's left side, all characters are shown in the work area (see ﬁgure below).The individual
character to be edited can then be selected in the J ob Explorer or work area.
After you select an individual character, it is shown em bedded in horizontal lines (see ﬁgure below). These lines deﬁne 'Baseline
Descent', 'x Height', 'Cap Height' and 'Ascent' to facilitate the character's alignm ent inside the font. The positions of these lines are
deﬁned by the original TrueType Font. The further editing procedure corresponds to the editing procedure of all other m arking
objects.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Editing Fonts
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_EditFont.htm 2/4

--- Page 401 ---

To edit the font characters:
01. To edit a font in the local job library, select the window "Library Explorer".
   or
To edit a font in the global M arking Library, choose M enu File > "Open" > "M arking Library".
The Library Explorer tree shows "Library" at the top.
02. Click the plus sign on the left side of 'Fonts'.
All present fonts are listed below.
03. Click the plus sign on the left side of the desired font.
All characters of this font is listed below and shown in the work area.
04. Select the character to be edited by double clicking in the J ob Explorer or work area.
The character is im m ediately shown em bedded in horizontal lines.
   or
First, select the character to be edited in the Library Explorer or work area, then select 'Edit'
‒ from  the M enu Edit
‒ or from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The character is shown em bedded in horizontal lines.
05. Select the character (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Filling Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_Fill.htm 1/5

--- Page 404 ---

Filling M arking Objects
Fill
Rem ove Filling
M arking objects (for exam ple, rectangles, circles, ellipses, polygons, ...) can be ﬁlled if their shapes are closed. You can ﬁll a
selected m arking object sim ply by clicking the appropriate button in the Toolbar "Edit" or by selecting the function in the M enu
Edit. Alternatively, the m arking object can be ﬁlled by setting the corresponding param eter in G raphics Param eters (shown in the
W indow "Properties"), where additional ﬁll settings can be m odiﬁed.
Note For barcodes laserDESK  provides a special barcode ﬁlling (see How to Fill Barcodes).
Likewise single m arking objects, groups, that is, the group's m arking objects, can be ﬁlled. Depending on their num ber and
aligning to each other, the overlapping areas are ﬁlled or not (see table below). If a group com prises open m arking objects also,
these are not provided for ﬁlling.
Note A m arking object (stand-alone or inside a group) can only be ﬁlled, if its 'Type' param eter is set to "Area" (see G raphics
Param eters). If this param eter is set to "Outline", objects cannot be ﬁlled.
If a m arking object cannot be ﬁlled, check the 'Type' param eter's setting.
The ﬁlling behavior of several overlapping m arking objects inside a group, like described in the table below, assum es
that all m arking objects are closed.
Filling Description
Single m arking object:
W ill always be ﬁlled com pletely.
G roup of two ore m ore m arking objects without overlapping:
Each m arking object is ﬁlled com pletely.
G roup of two m arking objects partially overlapped:
The not overlapped areas of each m arking object is ﬁlled, the overlapped area is not ﬁlled.
G roup of two m arking objects totally overlapped:
The outer, overlapping m arking object is ﬁlled, the inner, sm aller m arking object is not ﬁlled.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Filling Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_Fill.htm 2/5

--- Page 405 ---

Filling Description
G roup of three or m ore m arking objects partially overlapped:
The not overlapped areas of each m arking object is ﬁlled, the areas with overlapping of two m arking
objects are not ﬁlled, the areas with overlapping of three m arking objects are ﬁlled, the areas with
overlapping of four m arking objects are not ﬁlled, ... .
G roup of three or m ore m arking objects totally overlapped:
The m arking objects are ﬁlled and not ﬁlled alternately from  the outside inwards. The outer/largest
m arking object will always be ﬁlled.
The ﬁlling properties correspond to the default settings in the Fill Param eters (shown in the W indow "Properties"). Here, you can
m odify the settings regarding the ﬁlling type (hatch or path), the ﬁlling alignm ent and other properties.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Filling Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_Fill.htm 3/5

--- Page 406 ---

Fill
To ﬁll a closed m arking object:
01. Select the desired m arking object or group (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shape changes color and gets enclosed by a rectangle fram e with support points for editing.
02. Do one of the following:
‒ Choose M enu Edit > Fill
‒ Choose "Fill" from  the Toolbar Edit
‒ Choose "Fill" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
‒ Select "True" in the 'Filling Lines' param eter inside the G raphics Param eters.
The m arking object or group is then ﬁlled.
The ﬁlling lines properties correspond to the predeﬁned settings for the job.
03. To m odify the ﬁlling lines properties, select the G raphics Param eters and Fill Param eters in the W indow "Properties".
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Filling Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_Fill.htm 4/5

Rem ove Filling
To rem ove ﬁlling:
01. Select the desired m arking object or group (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
02. Do one of the following:
‒ Choose M enu Edit > Rem ove Filling
‒ Choose "Rem ove Filling" in the Toolbar Edit
‒ Choose "Rem ove Filling" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button)
‒ Select "False" in the 'Filling Lines' param eter inside the G raphics Param eters
The m arking object ﬁlling or group ﬁlling is then rem oved.

--- Page 409 ---

Altering the M arking Object Shape
laserDESK  oﬀers two functions to alter the m arking objects' shape easily: 'Round Corners' and 'Oﬀset'.
Com m and Shape original Shape edited Description
Round
Corners
Lets you round the m arking object's corners by a deﬁned radius. The m arking
object can be closed or open.
The radius to be deﬁned is lim ited according to the m arking object's dim ension.
for exam ple, the radius for a square can be m axim um  its half side length - with
the result of a circle.
For m arking objects with round corners (for exam ple, graphics path with
arcs/curves), these round corners can be excepted from  rounding to the deﬁned
radius.
Note
The com m and 'Round Corners' can be executed once only for a m arking object.
Oﬀset
 Lets you scale up or down a m arking object by a deﬁned dim ension.
‒ Scale Up: the m arking object's corners are rounded sim ultaneously. The
radius corresponds to the oﬀset value.
‒Scale Down: the m arking object's corners are not rounded.
Note
The com m and 'Oﬀset' can be applied only to closed m arking objects.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Altering the Marking Object's Shape

--- Page 410 ---

Round Corners
To alter the m arking object shape:
01. Select the desired m arking object (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shape changes color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e with support points for editing.
02. Choose M enu Edit > Round Corners or Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) > Round Corners .
The Dialog "Round Corners" opens.
03. Enter the desired radius in the text ﬁeld.
04. If enabled, select the checkbox 'Skip round Corners', if you want to avoid rounding existent round corners.
05. Click the button OK .
The m arking object corners are rounded as deﬁned.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Altering the Marking Object's Shape
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Offset_RoundCorners.htm 3/4

To alter the m arking object shape:
01. Select the desired m arking object (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shape changes color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e with support points for editing.
02. Choose M enu Edit > Oﬀset Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) > Oﬀset.
The Dialog "Oﬀset" opens.
03. Enter the desired oﬀset in the 'Oﬀset' input ﬁeld.
04. Select 'Scale U p' or 'Scale Down' from  the listbox.
05. Click the button OK .
The m arking object is scaled up with round corners or scaled down as deﬁned.

Splitting M arking Objects
M arking objects can be split in two ways:
One or m ultiple selected m arking objects can be split horizontally or vertically by deﬁning a cutting line relative to the x axis
or y axis of the working area coordinate system .
Two or m ore overlapping objects can be split by assigning one of them  as the cutter. Here, the outline of the cutter object
deﬁnes the cutter contour and cuts all other selected objects at their intersections.
Both open and closed objects can be split. If splitting closed m arking objects (ﬁlled or not ﬁlled), the param eter 'Cut Edge' deﬁnes
whether the cut object keeps its type (outline or area) or always becom es an outline. To keep the type 'Area' (Cut Edge = Enclosed),
the object needs a closed outline which is created using the cutter's shape. The split m arking objects change their identiﬁers in J ob
Explorer.
Note It is not possible to split pixel im ages and groups.
In the Dialog "Split" you can deﬁne several split settings (see exam ples below).
Dialog "Split"

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:00 Cutting Out Marking Objects
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Cutout.htm 1/4

--- Page 420 ---

Cutting Out M arking Objects
During a m arking process, the intersections of overlapping lines / m arking objects are m arked twice. To avoid poor surface quality
at these points, intersecting lines can be cut (interrupted) with a deﬁned clearance.
Note The cutout function can be applied only with the following m arking objects:
‒ line, rectangle, polygon, arc, graphics path
‒ font text and vector text
The cutout function is not available with all other m arking objects ( including group/set/variant, all variable
objects,im age/graphics, barcode, text box ) or with ﬁlling lines.
One of two or m ore m arking objects with intersecting lines always cuts the other m arking object(s).
Note W hich m arking object cuts the other m arking object(s) depends on the selection procedure:
‒ W hen selecting each m arking object by m ouse click, the m arking object selected ﬁrst is the cutter.
‒ W hen selecting the m arking objects using the area select or select all function, the m arking object with the highest
position in the J ob Explorer tree is the cutter.
W hen you select cutout function, the Dialog "Cutout" serves to deﬁne the clearance of the intersecting lines.
Dialog "Cutout"
Note Cutout can be executed only, if in the Dialog "Cutout" 'Clearance' is set > 0.
Cutout Exam ples
Initial Object(s) /
Identiﬁers
Cut out Objects / Identiﬁers Description
'Line'
'Line'
'Line'
'Line'
'Polygon (Line)'
'Polygon (Line)'
'Polygon (Line)'
'Polygon (Line)'
'Polygon (Line)'
Depending on the sequence of
selection or the position in the J ob
Explorer tree, the higher lines
always cut the lower lines.
In this exam ple with 3 intersecting
lines, the result is a unchanged line
and 5 polygons with clearance n.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Combining Marking Objects and Groups
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_Combine.htm 1/4

--- Page 424 ---

Com bining M arking Objects and Groups
To sim plify the creation of graphics paths, laserDESK  oﬀers four com bine functions. These functions let you com bine two
overlapping m arking objects and thus create new objects. The table below describes how to use the com bine functions.
Note W ith the functions 'U nion', 'Intersect', Exclusive Or' or 'Exclude', only closed m arking objects can be com bined. The
'Type' param eter of both objects m ust be set to "Area" (see G raphics Param eters). If this param eter is set to "Outline",
objects cannot be com bined. If m arking objects cannot be com bined, check the 'Type' param eter's setting.
The function 'Connect' is executable only with the open m arking objects Line, Polygon and G raphics Path.
Original
Objects
Com bine > Com bined
Objects
Description
U nion
 The two m arking objects are com bined into one m arking
object.
The new m arking object is a 'G raphics Path - Com bined'.
Intersect
 The intersecting area of the two m arking objects are
cropped.
The new m arking object is a 'G raphics Path - Com bined'.
Exclusive Or (Xor)
 The overlapping area of the two m arking objects are cut out.
Each of the rem aining two m arking objects changes to
'G raphics Path', both form ing a 'G roup'.
Exclude
 One m arking object and the overlapping area is rem oved.
The rem aining m arking object changes to 'G raphics Path -
Com bined'.
W hich m arking object is rem oved depends on the selection
procedure:
‒ W hen selecting each m arking object by m ouse click, the
m arking object selected last is rem oved.
‒ W hen selecting both m arking objects using area select, the
m arking object positioned lower in the J ob Explorer is
rem oved.
Connect
 The function com bines 2 open paths into one if their
endpoints are closer than a user deﬁned lim it. A new graphic
path is created where the end point of one open m arking
object is shifted to the end point of the other open m arking
object. This function is (for exam ple,) very useful for post-
editing vector graphics to create a closed path for ﬁlling.
W hich m arking object's end point is shifted, depends on the
selection procedure:
‒ W hen selecting each m arking object by m ouse click, the
end point of the m arking object selected last is shifted.
‒ W hen selecting both m arking objects using area select, the
end point of the m arking object positioned lower in the J ob
Explorer is shifted.
Note
This function is executable only with open paths, not closed
polygons or not closed graphic paths.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Combining Marking Objects and Groups
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_Combine.htm 2/4

--- Page 425 ---

Original
Objects
Com bine > Com bined
Objects
Description
M erge
 This function lets you com bine two or m ore groups (groups,
graphic sets, tile sets and variants) into one single group
containing all m arking objects. Additionally, all closed
m arking objects are ﬁlled, overlapping areas are not ﬁlled.
W hich kind of group will result depends on the following
requirem ents:
‒ If the selection to be m erged com prises a group, the result
is always a group.
‒ If the selection to be m erged com prises a variant but no
group, the result is always a variant.
‒ If the selection to be m erged com prises diﬀerent sets only,
the result is the set selected ﬁrst.
Note
There is only one restriction for the selection of groups: the
selection exclusive of variants is not possible.
To com bine two closed m arking objects:
01. Select two overlapping m arking objects (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shape changes color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e.
02. Choose
‒ 'Com bine > U nion'
‒ or 'Com bine > Intersect'
‒ or 'Com bine > Exclusive Or (Xor)'
‒ or 'Com bine > Exclude'
from  the M enu Edit or from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The m arking objects are directly com bined corresponding to the selected function (see table above).
To connect two open m arking objects:
01. Select two neighboring open objects (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The m arking object to be m isaligned m ust be selected last or have the lower position in the J ob Explorer tree.
The shape changes color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e.
02. Choose M enu Edit > "Com bine" > "Connect" or "Connect" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The 'Connect' dialog opens.
03. Deﬁne the distance within the m arking objects' pass points that is connected by entering the value in the input ﬁeld or using
the arrow buttons.
04. Click OK  to connect the two objects.
If the distance of the pass points is within the entered value, the m arking objects are directly connected.
If the distance of the pass points is outside the entered value, the m arking objects are not connected.
To m erge two or m ore groups:
01. Select two or m ore groups (groups, sets, variants) (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shapes change color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e.
02. Choose M enu Edit > "Com bine" > "M erge" or "M erge" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The groups are directly m erged into one group (group, set or variant, depending on the selection).

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Creating Symbols
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_CreateNewSymbol.htm 1/2

--- Page 434 ---

Creating Sym bols
M arking objects or groups with m arking objects, which is used periodically (for exam ple, com pany logo, speciﬁc shape), can be
saved as a m aster to the local library labeled as 'Sym bol'. New m arking objects can then be inserted by Reference To Sym bol. In a
second step, the sym bol can be exported to the global M arking Library and im ported again to another job.
Note Non-m arking objects (control node, tile set) are not allowed inside sym bols.
If such an object is included in a group and the group is to be saved as a sym bol, these objects are rem oved and a note
is displayed.
Note Sym bols cannot include variable objects like serial num bers or tim e objects.
If a variable text is included it is set to constant.
Note The m arking object inserted in the work area by 'Reference To Sym bol' is not a copy of the sym bol in the library, it is a
real reference. M odifying the library's sym bol will change the referenced m arking object in the work area!
 To create a sym bol:
01. Insert a m arking object like described in Inserting a new M arking Object or create a group like described in Creating G roups,
Sets or Variants.
02. Select the desired m arking object (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
The shape will change the color and is enclosed by a rectangle fram e with supporting points for editing.
03. Do one of the following:
‒ Choose M enu Edit > Save as Sym bol
‒ Choose Save as Sym bol from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The dialog "Create New Sym bol" opens.
04. Enter the desired nam e in the 'Nam e' input ﬁeld and click the button OK.
The reference object is added to the job Library Explorer in the directory 'Sym bols'.
05. If desired, export the sym bol to the M arking Library.
(For detailed description how to use the library, see Library Explorer / M arking Library).
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25
©  SCANLAB G m bH 2025

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Converting Text Objects, Groups and Graphic Sets
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/EditObject_ConvertTo.htm 1/2

--- Page 436 ---

Converting Text Objects, Groups and Graphic Sets
In laserDESK  for static text and variable objects (date/tim e, serial num ber, im port text) diﬀerent form ats are available: font text,
vector text, and barcode. If required, a num ber of these form ats can be converted into another form at:
Font text to vector text and barcode
Vector text to font text and barcode
Barcode to font text and vector text
Note For variable objects (date/tim e, serial num ber, im port text) each form at can be converted as shown above.
Single-line static text can only be converted from  font text to vector text and vice versa. It is not available for barcode.
The text box always uses font text.
Note W hen converting vector text to font text, the font text always regains the regular shape and aspect ratio of the
respective font.
Furtherm ore, groups and graphic sets can be converted:
G roups or graphic sets to graphics paths
G roups or graphics paths to graphic sets
G raphics paths or graphic sets to groups
This can be advantageous to assign for exam ple, speciﬁc properties to the group (content of control elem ents or variable objects)
or the graphic set (m arking objects can be sorted to decrease the m arking tim e).
To convert text objects, groups or graphic sets:
01. Select the m arking object(s) (font text, vector text, date/tim e, serial num ber, im port text, group or graphic set) in the work
area or J ob Explorer.
02. Choose M enu Edit > "Convert To" or "Convert To" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The available text form at(s) or the available grouping is displayed black colored in a pop-up m enu.
03. Depending on the selected object, select (if provided)
‒ 'Font Text', 'Vector Text' or 'Barcode'
‒ or 'G roup' or 'G raphic Set' .
The current form at/grouping is converted im m ediately to the selected form at/grouping.

If required (for exam ple, for editing), m arking objects consisting of curves (circle, ellipse, arc, spiral, graphics path) can be ﬁtted by
a polygon into single vectors. The ﬁgure below shows an ellipse in edit m ode, on top in initial state and below after ﬁtted by a

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Defining a Tile Set
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Tile.htm 1/9

--- Page 440 ---

Deﬁning a Tile Set
If the size of the m arking exceeds the dim ensions of your laser scan system 's m arking area or if the overall m arking can not be
executed at one tim e for som e other reason (for exam ple, m arking on a roll), then the tile function lets you m ark the workpiece in
several steps. Therefore, the m arking objects are divided into several tiles. U sing positioning devices with m otors, the workpiece
can then be m oved to several positions to m ark it stepwise. M ainly intended for processing-on-the-ﬂy, a stripe and cylinder m ode
are available.
Note A tile set / tiles cannot com prise tile sets, variable objects ('Date, Tim e', 'Serial Num ber', 'Im port Text') or control
elem ents.
Note M arking tile sets require positioning devices to m ove the workpieces to the deﬁned position.
To control the m otor(s), laserDESK  control elem ents can be used. These have to be created in the laserDESK  job (see
Inserting a M otor Control Elem ent). M otor control elem ents can be inserted directly in the 'Tile' dialog (see below).
The axis can also be controlled with an independent system  ‒ then a signal handshake with laserDESK  m ust be used.
Note If you need a very precise calculation of the cutting edges, the value for the graphic resolution should be < 0.01 (see for
exam ple, G raphic Param eters ‒ G raphics Paths).
The tile function can be applied in two ways:
U sing the "Online M ode", ﬁrst a tile set m ust be created with one or several m arking objects (see Creating G roups, Sets or
Variants). Then the partition of the m arking object(s) m ust be deﬁned and saved. The m arking object(s) will still rem ain as
whole object(s), thus the J ob Explorer still indicates 'Tile Set' The tile set is a special group and has an own object type. It has
its own creation buttons and m enu com m ands and is inserted into the job tree as an individual node.
The separation of the tile set is not carried out until m arking. The advantage of "Online M ode" is that the tile set / m arking
object(s) can be edited even directly before m arking. The disadvantage is a longer executing tim e caused by the calculation
during m arking. Post processing after the separation is not possible. The tile set is sim ilar to a graphics set with an additional
tiling action at execution tim e.
U sing "Oﬄine M ode", existing m arking object(s) is divided directly into as m any tiles as deﬁned. The tiles are arranged
properly in the work area and thus the J ob Explorer lists the several 'Tile's. The Oﬄine tiling is an user action (see below).
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Defining a Tile Set
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Tile.htm 2/9

--- Page 441 ---

The advantage of "Oﬄine M ode" is faster execution when m arking the separated m arking object(s), because a new
calculation is not required. After tiling, post processing is possible. The new created 'Tiles' are of the type 'G raphics Set'.
To deﬁne the tile set's partitions or the single tiles, the Dialog "Tiles" m ust be opened.
Dialog "Tiles"
The Dialog "Tiles" lets you deﬁne the tile param eters (tab 'Tile Param eters') and create and deﬁne control elem ents (tab 'Control
Elem ents').
The 'Tiles' dialog is opened as follows:
If a tile set should be edited ("Online M ode"):
‒ If not yet existing, create a tile set (see Creating G roups, Sets or Variants).
‒ Select the tile set (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing)
‒ Select the Button 
  for the 'Tile Param eter' in the W indow "Properties" (see G raphic Param eters ‒ Tile Set).
If tiles should be created ("Oﬄine M ode"):
‒ Create one or m ore m arking objects (see Inserting Objects).
‒ Select the m arking object(s) (see Selecting M arking Objects for Editing).
‒ Choose "Separate > Tile" from  the M enu Edit or Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Converting Vectors to Point Lists
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/VectorToPointLines.htm 1/3

--- Page 449 ---

Converting Vectors to Point Lists
This action converts the vectors of a selected m arking object into point lines. The vectors are substituted by a graphics paths
(group) which contains the point elem ents.
Convertible m arking objects are single graphic elem ents (rectangle, circle, ...), groups, graphics paths, graphics sets, tile sets,
variable objects (date / tim e, serial num ber, ...) and vector graphics.
Note The conversion can be executed only, if one m arking object is selected. If several m arking objects are selected
sim ultaneous, the conversion cannot be executed.
In general, variants and bitm aps cannot be converted into point lines.
Note If you convert variable objects, these objects lose their variable property and gets static. That m eans a date or serial
num ber is not actualized anym ore and a tile set deﬁnition is rem oved.
Therefore, this conversion should not be applied to variable objects unless you want to keep that object constant.
The conversion is done using the Dialog "Vector to Points Conversion".
Dialog "Vector to Points Conversion"
The Dialog "Vector to Points Conversion" lets you deﬁne the distance between the points and the tim e duration (in [µs]) of the
laser process when m arking each point.
By activating or deactivating the 'Equal Spacing' checkbox, you can select two diﬀerent calculation m ethods for the point
alignm ent:
Deactivated 'Equal Spacing' checkbox:
The deﬁned point distance is exactly used. If the contour length of the vector graphic divided by the deﬁned point distance is
not an integer, this results in a diﬀerent/wrong distance for the last point.
Activated 'Equal Spacing' checkbox:
This function adjusts the distance so that all points get equal distances. The equal distance is applied to each edge, so that
the corner points are m aintained. This are done only for edges larger than 5 tim es of the point distance, else the diﬀerence to
the user deﬁned distance is too large.
Note If the contour is a curve, it has to be interpolated by a polyline and rounding errors m ay occur. This m ay result in a
non-equal distance between the points, especially for the connecting point of closed curves where the rounding
errors sum  up.
Note The U ndo and Redo functions are working. After the conversion, you m ay adapt the created point lists.
The U ndo function m ay not recover the correct position, because in nested groups the individual transform ations are
lost.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Converting Vectors to Point Lists
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/VectorToPointLines.htm 2/3

--- Page 450 ---

To convert the vectors of a m arking object to point lines:
01. Select a m arking object (only one is possible).
02. Choose M enu Edit > Convert to Point Lines or Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) > Convert to Point Lines.
The Dialog "Vector to Points Conversion" opens.
03. Enter the desired values in the 'Point Distance' and 'M arking Tim e' input ﬁelds.
04. Select or clear the 'Equal Spacing' checkbox.
05. Click the button OK.
The vectors are converted to point lines.

‒ or "Position" from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
   or

03. Click the button OK .
The m arking object m oves in z by the deﬁned value.
The z coordinates of the outline points (see 'Edit Points' dialog) change accordingly.

exam ple,) be m arked on the inclined side of a workpiece as a correct 2D shape:

Dialog "Edit Layer"

num ber), group and variant ‒ every m arking object type has speciﬁc graphic param eters.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Unique IDentifier (UID)
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_UIDHandling.htm 1/3

--- Page 471 ---

U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID)
Each elem ent within a job can have a U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
This U ID is m ainly used by the rem ote control com m ands to assign an action to a speciﬁc elem ent.
The U IDs are autom atically created by laserDESK  according to the schem e Preﬁx + '#' + Num ber, where the preﬁx is a character
corresponding to the elem ent. For exam ple, 'G ' is used for all graphic elem ents and 'C' for all control elem ents (for exam ple,
G #123, C#456).
In the graphic param eters of each elem ent, it is possible to change the U ID m anually to any string you like (see param eter 'U ID' -
for exam ple in G raphic Param eters - Circle). laserDESK  will check this U ID for uniqueness and in case m ay add a num ber.
Since handling U IDs for a large num ber of elem ents are tim e consum ing, you can prevent autom atic creation by enabling the
Suppress autom atic U ID Creation option on the 'Create' page of the 'Option' dialog. You m ay deﬁne a U ID m anually later on.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Unique IDentifier (UID)
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_UIDHandling.htm 2/3

--- Page 472 ---

Repeat Function
Som e U IDs belonging to library elem ents or sym bols m ay not be visible, but prevent them  to be assigned to another elem ent.
Therefore, a Repeat  action uses a special U ID creation. This action creates consecutive U IDs for the cloned elem ents. To allow
easy rem ote handling of the repeatedly created elem ents, the U ID creation has the following behavior:
All U IDs inside the job is checked and the highest U ID num ber is evaluated. The start elem ent gets the next num ber rounded
to hundred and all further elem ents get consecutive U IDs. As a result, all repeatedly created elem ents have consecutive
num bers starting from  the U ID of the start elem ent. Be aware that the U ID of the start elem ent is adapted.
Note All U IDs that do not correspond to the laserDESK  schem e (Preﬁx + '#' + Num ber) are
ignored when evaluating the highest num ber. The U IDs are created according to this
schem e, so it is ensured that the U IDs are really unique. The preﬁx is deﬁned by the type
of the elem ent.
If the U ID of the ﬁrst elem ent is the highest num ber, it is used and not changed. Therefore, it is possible to exactly assign a
start U ID. It only has to be higher than any present U ID.
If a U ID is em pty it is ignored by the U ID assignm ent in the repeat action.
If a group is repeated, all children will also receive consecutive num bers and the group num bers are increased by the num ber
of children inside (+ 1 each).
Exam ple:
A group with 2 children is repeated. The group gets the U ID G #100, the ﬁrst child G #101, the second child G #102 and the next
group elem ent G #103 and so on.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25
© SCANLAB G m bH 2025
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Unique IDentifier (UID)
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_UIDHandling.htm 3/3

--- Page 473 ---

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Graphic Parameters - Arc
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_ArcProperties.htm 1/3

--- Page 478 ---

Graphic Param eters ‒ Arc
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne the geom etric properties of arcs.
Note If units of length ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]) are not explicitly indicated, then the
respective values correspond to the units deﬁned in the 'View' Param eters (see
'U nits\Length').
Param eter Description
[1] Arc
Label Default nam e: "Arc". You can change the nam e.
Center M arking object's center position in the x and y directions in the work area's coordinate
system .
Both values can be entered/m odiﬁed in 'Center' or each value in 'X' and 'Y'.
  X
  Y
W idth Lets you deﬁne the width of the base object in the x direction (with param eter
'Rotation'= "0", see below).
Height Lets you deﬁne the height of the base object in the y direction (with param eter
'Rotation'= "0", see below).
Angle Start Lets you deﬁne the angle start position:
"0" or "360" equates to the x axis in the positive direction.
"90" or "-270" = equates to the y axis in the positive direction.
Angle Extent Lets you deﬁne the angular degree relative to the angle start (see above).
Positive value (unsigned): counterclockwise direction of rotation.
Negative value (with m inus "-"): clockwise direction of rotation.
Aspect Ratio Scaling behavior of the shape:
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters (window
"G U I Settings")
Layer A m arking object can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set the m arking object
visible or invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable and assign a
new m arking param eter set for all objects of one layer (see Layers W indow).
By default none layer is assigned, the listbox is blank.
Open the dialog "Select" by the Button 
 , to select a listed layer or "<None>".
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer.
You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Boundary
Left Left, right, top and bottom  position of the bounding box of the arc in the work area's
coordinate system  (not editable).Right
Top
Bottom
Z Bottom Only if U se 3D = "True" is set in the job node, for all objects these z-dim ensions are
evaluated and displayed.
Z Bottom  and Z-Top are 'read only'. They are evaluated from  the z values of the point
list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.
The param eter 'Z Height' can be changed to rescale the object(s) in the z-dim ension.
Thus, this param eter is particularly suitable for spirals.
U sing this param eter in the job node, all 3D objects inside the job is rescaled
correspondingly.
Note
Z Top
Z Height
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Graphic Parameters - Arc
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_ArcProperties.htm 2/3

--- Page 479 ---

Param eter Description
This feature has no eﬀect on 2D objects which are transform ed on an inclined plane.
This feature scales the z-dim ension of 3D objects previous to the transform ation on an
inclined plane.
Param eter Description
[3] Marking
M ark Lets you enable or disable the m arking process for this object.
M arking Count If an application requires repeated m arking, you can deﬁne the num ber of m arking
iterations here.
G raphic Resolution Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution. This factor is used for the calculation of the
curve's polygon adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) is the
curve ﬁtting.
Param eter Description
[4] Transform ation
Rotation Rotation (in [deg]) in m athem atical positive direction.
Flip X The arc can be m irrored with respect its orientation in the x direction.
"True": m irroring, "False": no m irroring.
Flip Y The arc can be m irrored with respect to its orientation in the y direction.
"True": m irroring, "False": no m irroring.
Inform ation on editing param eters is described in W indow "Properties".

Graphic Param eters ‒ Barcode
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne properties of barcodes. These com prise the geom etric properties (Appearance,
Boundary, Transform ation), the barcode properties (Barcode) and the m arking properties (M arking).
Note If units of length ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]) are not explicitly indicated, then the
respective values correspond to the units deﬁned in the 'View' Param eters (see
'U nits\Length').
Param eter Description
[1] Barcode
Label Default nam e: "Barcode". You can change the nam e.
Type Closed m arking objects are set to "Area" by default. W ith this setting an object can be
ﬁlled.
If "Outline" is selected, an object cannot be ﬁlled even if it has a closed outline.
In this case, the 'Filling Lines' param eter (see below) cannot be set to "True''.
Center M arking object's center position in the x and y directions in the work area's coordinate
system .
Both values can be entered/m odiﬁed in 'Center' or each value in 'X' and 'Y'.
  X
  Y
W idth Lets you deﬁne the width in the x direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see
below).
Height Lets you deﬁne the height in the y direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see
below).
Aspect Ratio Scaling behavior of the shape:
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters (window
"G U I Settings")
Layer A m arking object can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set the m arking object
visible or invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable and assign a
new m arking param eter set for all objects of one layer (see Layers W indow).
By default none layer is assigned, the listbox is blank.
Open the dialog "Select" by the Button 
 , to select a listed layer or "<None>".
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer.
You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Boundary
Left Left, right, top and bottom  position of the bounding box of the barcode in the work
area's coordinate system  (not editable).Right
Top
Bottom
Z Bottom Only if U se 3D = "True" is set in the job node, for all objects these z-dim ensions are
evaluated and displayed.
Z Bottom  and Z-Top are 'read only'. They are evaluated from  the z values of the point
list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.
The param eter 'Z Height' can be changed to rescale the object(s) in the z-dim ension.
Thus, this param eter is particularly suitable for spirals.
U sing this param eter in the job node, all 3D objects inside the job is rescaled
correspondingly.
Note
This feature has no eﬀect on 2D objects which are transform ed on an inclined plane.
Z Top
Z Height
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Graphic Parameters - Barcode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_BarcodeProperties.htm 2/5

--- Page 482 ---

Param eter Description
This feature scales the z-dim ension of 3D objects previous to the transform ation on an
inclined plane.
Param eter Description
[3] Barcode
Text Shows the value of the displayed barcode (not editable).
Readable Text Lets you deﬁne text that is displayed below the barcode.
Note
Inserting readable text will disable the display of barcode text/values in the form  of
num bers.
Code Type laserDESK  oﬀers linear and m atrix barcode types:
Code_11, .Code_2OF5, Code_2OF5IL, Code_2OF5IATA, Code_2OF5M , Code_2OF5DL,
Code_2OF5IND, Code_3OF9, Code_3OF9A, EAN8, EAN8P2, EAN8P5, EAN13, EAN13P2,
EAN13P5, EAN128, U PC12, CodaBar2, Code128, DPLeit, DPIdent, Code_9OF3,
Flatterm arken, G S1DataBar, G S1DataBarLtd, G S1DataBarExp, TelepenAlpha, U CC 128,
U PCA, U PCAP2, U PCAP5, U PCE, U PCEP2, U PCEP5, PZN, PDF417, PDF417Trunc,
M AXICODE, QRCode, Code128A, Code128B, Code128C, Code_9OF3A, AusPostCustom ,
AusPostCustom 2,ISBN, DataM atrix, EAN_14, CODABLOCK _F, M icroPDF417, Telepen,
Aztec, M icroQRCode.
Check M ethod M ost barcodes have a deﬁned checksum  calculation. U sing this calculation, select
"Standard".
For som e barcodes it is possible to leave out the checksum . Then select "None". In this
case your barcode reader has to support the reading without checksum .
Available entries: "Standard", "None".
Ratio Ratio between sm all and wide bars.
The availability depends on the barcode type (not editable).
M axim um  Length Allowed num ber of digits, depending on the code type (not editable).
Bar Line Reduction [%] Lets you deﬁne the reduction of the bar lines.
Corresponds to outline reduction (see Fill Param eters), but in % and not absolute.
M odule W idth Lets you deﬁne the width of the m odule (sm allest bar). The width of the wider bars are
set respectively.
Quiet Zone Lets you deﬁne the dim ensions of the m argin quiet zone.
All values can be entered/m odiﬁed in 'Quiet Zone' or each value in 'Left', 'Right', 'Top'
and 'Bottom '.
  Left
  Right
  Top
  Bottom
Enable Text Lets you display the barcode's text/value (see 'Text' above) below the barcode.
Available entries: "True" for enabling text, "False" for disabling text.
Text Distance Lets you deﬁne the distance between the barcode and the text below (if enabled).
W ith increasing distance the height of the barcode will decrease.
Font Nam e laserDESK  oﬀers a large num ber of font types, which can be selected here from  a drop-
down m enu.
Size Lets you deﬁne the desired font size (in [Points]).
Font Style Lets you deﬁne the font style: "Regular", "Bold" or "Italic".
G raphic Resolution Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution. This factor is used for the calculation of the
curve's polygon adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) is the
curve ﬁtting.
Code Page Lets you select a code page (each with an own interpretation of the character display).
Necessary, because barcodes are not always unicode capable and the barcode library
does not support unicode.
Inverse The barcode can be m arked inverse. The barcode is fram ed to m ark the area outside
the bars and inside the fram e.
Available entries: "True" for inverse m arking, "False" for norm al m arking.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Graphic Parameters - Barcode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_BarcodeProperties.htm 3/5

--- Page 483 ---

Param eter Description
Escape Sequences If the 'Escape Sequences' param eter is set to "True", the possible escape sequences are
evaluated and exchanged by their correspondent function. Else, all characters are
taken as they are. Available entries: "True" for evaluating and exchanging the possible
escape sequences, "False". The com plete list of available escape sequences can be
found here:
https://www.tec-it.com /download/PDF/Barcode_Reference_EN.pdf  (see page 16 f).
Exam ple:
If you want to use the G S1 standard in a data m atrix code, you need to set the data
m atrix property 'Form at' to U CCEAN. Then you can insert the escape sequence '\F' to
separate the diﬀerent tags.
During creation of the barcode laserDESK  will exchange the separator by the FNC1
character deﬁned by the G S1 standard.
Param eter Description
[4] M arking
Filling Lines Lets you ﬁll the barcode with ﬁlling lines.
If the 'Type' param eter (see above) is "Outline", this param eter cannot be set to "True"
and thus cannot enable ﬁlling.
Available entries: "True" for ﬁlling the barcode, "False" for not ﬁlling.
M ark This param eter let you deﬁne the kind of execution of this object.
One of the following item s can be selected:
‒ Hatchlines BEFORE Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked before the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AFTER Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked after the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AND Outlines: m ust be selected, if the whole object shall be sorted.
‒ Hatchlines only: only the hatchlines are m arked.
‒ None: the m arking object is not m arked.
Variable Lets you allow to change the text content in rem ote m ode.
Available entries: "True" for changeable/variable text, "False" for not
changeable/variable text.
For further inform ation, refer to Rem ote Control M anual.
Note
If you do not use a rem ote control or if you do not want to change text inside rem ote
m ode, you should set this param eter to "False", because execution will then proceed
faster.
M arking Count If an application requires repeated m arking, you can deﬁne the num ber of m arking
iterations here.
Barcode Filling The characteristics and advantages of the barcode ﬁlling are described in How to Fill
Barcodes.
If set to "True", the barcode ﬁlling is applied. (M ake sure, that the 'Filling Lines'
param eter' - see above - is also set to "True".)
If set to "False", the standard ﬁlling is applied.
Hatch Lines per M odule Only visible, if the 'Barcode Filling' param eter (see above) is set to "True".
Deﬁnes the num ber of hatch lines per basic m odule.
Vertical Filling Only visible, if the 'Barcode Filling' param eter (see above) is set to "True".
If set to "True", the ﬁlling lines are aligned vertically; if set to "False" horizontally
(always relative to the barcode orientation).
For 1D barcodes only vertical m arking (= "True") should be used.
Horizontal m arking (= "False") should only be used with 2D barcodes.
Bitm ap Filling Only visible, if the 'Barcode Filling' param eter (see above) is set to "True" and a 2D
code is selected.
If set to "True", the ﬁlling lines are executed as pixel lines, if set to "False" as vectors.
Row Synchronization [m m ] Only visible, if the 'Bitm ap Filling' param eter (see above) is set to "True".
Lets you synchronize the pixel lines.

Param eter Description
A positive value shifts the pixel lines opposite to the m arking direction, a negative
value in m arking direction.
w
Param eter Description
[5] Transform ation
Rotation Rotation (in [deg]) relative to the original position
Translation Oﬀset in the x and y directions to the value set in the 'Center' param eter (see above).
Both values can be entered/m odiﬁed in 'Translation' or each value in 'X' and 'Y'.  X
  Y

Graphic Param eters ‒ Date/Tim e Barcode
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne the geom etric properties of this variable object. The following table describes each
param eter.
Note If units of length ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]) are not explicitly indicated, then the
respective values correspond to the units deﬁned in the 'View' Param eters (see
'U nits\Length').
Param eter Description
[1] Date/Tim e
View "Barcode". Not editable.
Label Default nam e: "Date/Tim e". You can change the nam e.
Type Closed m arking objects are set to "Area" by default. W ith this setting an object can be
ﬁlled.
If "Outline" is selected, an object cannot be ﬁlled even if it has a closed outline.
In this case, the 'Filling Lines' param eter (see below) cannot be set to "True''.

system .

  X
  Y

below).

below).

‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters ('G U I
Settings window').

 , to select a listed layer or "<None>".

[2] Boundary

Z Bottom Only if U se 3D = "True" is set in the job node, for all objects these z-dim ensions are
evaluated and displayed.
Z Bottom  and Z-Top are 'read only'. They are evaluated from  the z values of the point
list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.
The param eter 'Z Height' can be changed to rescale the object(s) in the z-dim ension.
Thus, this param eter is particularly suitable for spirals.

correspondingly.
Note

Param eter Description
[4] Text Alignm ent
Alignm ent Text can be aligned on a (virtual) circle or a m arking object (here called graphics path).
In this case, further param eter settings (see below) are required. Available entries: "
<None>", "On Circle", "On Path".
Text Rotate Radius Lets you deﬁne the text's radius, if "Alignm ent On Circle" (see above) has been
selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Text Rotate Start Angle Lets you deﬁne the text's start angle.
Only available when "Alignm ent On Circle" (see above) has been selected.
Counter Clock Lets you deﬁne the text's rotation direction, if "Alignm ent On Circle" (see above) has
been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "False" for clockwise, "True" for counterclockwise.
Inside Lets you deﬁne the text's position as inside or outside position, if "Alignm ent On
Circle" (see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "True" for inside, "False" for outside.

(see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Click the Button 
  to open the dialog "Select" for selecting the required path.

adapted when values are changed.

above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
The entered value deﬁnes the oﬀset from  the path start point to the text start point.
Positive and negative values are possible.
Direction Lets you align text to the m arking object direction or opposite to it, if "Alignm ent On
Path" (see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "Path Direction", "Opposite Path Direction".
Alignm ent Lets you align text to the right or left side of the m arking object, if "Alignm ent On Path"
(see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "Right Hand Side of Path", "Left Hand Side of Path".
Vertical Lets you align the text in vertically direction (perpendicular to the y axis).
Available entries: "False", "True".
Spacing Lets you deﬁne the space between the characters (in [%]).
‒ 0 % spacing: totally overlapping of all characters.
‒ 100 % spacing: norm al space between the characters.
M ono Spaced Lets you deﬁne a non-proportional text (like for exam ple, Courier). Each character has
the sam e width. Available entries: "False" for proportional text, "True" for m ono
spaced text.
Character M irror X The characters can be m irrored with respect to their orientation in the x direction.
Available entries: "True" for m irrored characters, "False" for norm al characters.
Character M irror Y The characters can be m irrored with respect to their orientation in the y direction.
Available entries: "True" for m irrored characters, "False" for norm al characters.
Right To Left The sequence of the characters can be changed from  left-to-right ("text") to right-to-
left ("text"). Available entries: "True" for right-to-left, "False" for left-to-right.

Each letter of an identiﬁer equates to a num erical digit, except for the nam es
(identiﬁers "e, E, g, G , p"). Date and tim e can be split into individual digits. The digits
are then written in correct order.
The following delim iters can be used inside the form at string: / ( ) \ + * # < > | , ; . : -
All characters apart from  these delim iters and the identiﬁers listed in the table above is
ignored!
Exam ples:
m m /dd/yy 6/7/09 DD.M M .YYYY07.06.2009
G  m m , yyyyJ une 7, 2009E, G  dd Sunday, J une 7
HH:NN:SS 08:36:07 kk:nn p 8:36 am
DM YYM D 000967 M Y.YM 00.96
Date/Tim e Shows the system  date/tim e of the PC.
Oﬀset Entering an unsigned value (> 0), will increase the displayed tim e, entering a value (> 0)
with m inus sign, will decrease the displayed tim e.
Text Shows the displayed date/tim e (not editable).
Preﬁx Lets you insert a preﬁx or suﬃx.
A static text is displayed in front of or after the date/tim e, for exam ple, "P [date/tim e]
S".Suﬃx
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:01 Graphic Parameters - Date/Time Vector Text
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_DateTimeVectorTextProperties.htm 3/5

--- Page 504 ---

Param eter Description
[4] Text Alignm ent
Alignm ent Text can be aligned on a (virtual) circle or a m arking object (here called graphics path).
In this case, further param eter settings (see below) are required.
Available entries: "<None>", "On Circle", "On Path".
Text Rotate Radius Lets you deﬁne the text's radius, if "Alignm ent On Circle" (see above) has been
selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Text Rotate Start Angle Lets you deﬁne the text's start angle.
Only available when "Alignm ent On Circle" (see above) has been selected.
Counter Clock Lets you deﬁne the text's rotation direction, if "Alignm ent On Circle" (see above) has
been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "False" for clockwise, "True" for counterclockwise.
Inside Lets you deﬁne the text's position as inside or outside position, if "Alignm ent On
Circle" (see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "True" for inside, "False" for outside.
Path Lets you select an already (in this job) created m arking object, if "Alignm ent On Path"
(see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Click the Button 
  to open the dialog "Select" for selecting the required path.
If a path has been selected, the param eters 'J ustiﬁcation' and 'Origin' (see above) are
adapted when values are changed.
Oﬀset Lets you deﬁne an oﬀset between text and m arking object, if "Alignm ent On Path" (see
above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
The entered value deﬁnes the oﬀset from  the path start point to the text start point.
Positive and negative values are possible.
Direction Lets you align text to the m arking object direction or opposite to it, if "Alignm ent On
Path" (see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "Path Direction", "Opposite Path Direction".
Alignm ent Lets you align text to the right or left side of the m arking object, if "Alignm ent On Path"
(see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: ""Right Hand Side of Path", "Left Hand Side of Path".
Vertical Lets you align the text in vertically direction (perpendicular to the y axis).
Available entries: "False", "True".
Spacing Lets you deﬁne the space between the characters (in [%]).
‒ 0 % spacing: totally overlapping of all characters.
‒ 100 % spacing: norm al space between the characters.
M ono Spaced Lets you deﬁne a non-proportional text. (like for exam ple, Courier). Each character
has the sam e width.
Available entries: "False" for proportional text, "True" for m ono spaced text.
Character M irror X The characters can be m irrored with respect to their orientation in the x direction.
Available entries: "True" for m irrored characters, "False" for norm al characters.
Character M irror Y The characters can be m irrored with respect to their orientation in the y direction.
Available entries: "True" for m irrored characters, "False" for norm al characters.
Right To Left The order of the characters can be changed from  left-to-right ("text") to right-to-left
("text").
Available entries: "True" for right-to-left, "False"' for left-to-right.
Param eter Description
[5] M arking
Filling Lines Lets you ﬁll the text with ﬁlling lines.
If the 'Type' param eter (see above) is 'Outline', this param eter cannot be set to "True"
and thus cannot be ﬁlled.
Available entries: "True" for ﬁlling, "False" for not ﬁlling.

  to open the Dialog "Bit Pattern".

  for each bit:

| RTC6 board (DIG ITAL IN 0-7, see Connecting Lasers and RTC Boards).

ﬁlled.

system .

  X
  Y

‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters ('G U I
Settings window').

Top

list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.
The param eter 'Z Height' can be changed to rescale the object(s) in the z-dim ension.
Thus, this param eter is particularly suitable for spirals.
U sing this param eter in the job node, all 3D objects inside the job is rescaled
correspondingly.
Note

This feature scales the z-dim ension of 3D objects previous to the transform ation on an
inclined plane.
Z Top
Z Height

Path" (see above) has been selected. Only then is the param eter displayed.
Available entries: "Path Direction", "Opposite Path Direction".

Filling Lines Lets you ﬁll the text with ﬁlling lines.
If the 'Type' param eter (see above) is "Outline", this param eter cannot be set to "True"
and thus cannot enable ﬁlling.
Available entries: "True" for ﬁlling the text, "False" for not ﬁlling the text.
M ark This param eter let you deﬁne the kind of execution of this object.
One of the following item s can be selected:
‒ Hatchlines BEFORE Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked before the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AFTER Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked after the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AND Outlines: m ust be selected, if the whole object shall be sorted.
‒ Hatchlines only: only the hatchlines are m arked.
‒ None: the m arking object is not m arked.
Variable Lets you allow to change the text content in rem ote m ode.
Available entries: "True" for changeable/variable text, "False" for not
changeable/variable text.
For further inform ation, refer to Rem ote Control M anual.
Note
If you do not use a rem ote control or if you do not want to change text inside rem ote
m ode, you should set this param eter to "False", because execution will then proceed
faster.
M arking Count If an application requires repeated m arking, you can deﬁne the num ber of m arking
iterations here.
G raphic Resolution Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution. This factor is used for the calculation of the
curve's polygon adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) is the
curve ﬁtting.
Param eter Description
[5] Transform ation
Rotation Rotation (in [deg]) relative to the original position
How to edit the param eters is described in W indow "Properties".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:02 Graphic Parameters - Graphics Path

Graphic Param eters ‒ Graphics Path
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne the geom etric properties of graphics paths.
Note If units of length ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]) are not explicitly indicated, then the values
correspond to the units deﬁned under 'View' Param eters > 'U nits' > 'Length'.
Param eter Description
[1] G raphics Path
Label Default nam e: "G raphics Path". You can change the nam e.
Type G raphics paths can be closed or unclosed.
‒ If closed, the type is "Area" and the object can be ﬁlled.
‒ If unclosed, the type is "Outline" and the object cannot be ﬁlled. In this case, the
param eter 'Filling Lines' (see below) cannot be set to "True".
‒ If the param eter 'Closed' (see below) is set to "False", the type changes autom atically to
"Outline".
Points The button 
  opens the Dialog "Edit Points".
‒ For each point of the graphics path, the position in the x-, y-, and z-directions can be
deﬁned individually by entering the value into the input ﬁeld (open and closed graphics
path).
‒ For each point of the graphics path, the point type can be deﬁned individually. Available
entries: "Corner", "Three Points Arc", "Quadrant Arc (CW )", "Quadrant Arc (CCW )", "Center
Arc (CW )", "Center Arc (CCW )", "Bezier", "Sm ooth Bezier". If you change the point type, it
m ay be necessary to insert or change neighboring points, too, due to the curve deﬁnition.
‒ To create a new point, click the button New Point. The dialog "Edit Point" opens to let
you deﬁne the x-, y-, z-value and select the point type.
‒ To delete one or m ore points, ﬁrst select the desired row(s), then click the button Delete
Points. The point(s) is deleted im m ediately.
‒ To change a point's ('Corner' point type only) position in the table, ﬁrst select the
desired row, then click the button M ove Point U p or button M ove Point Down.
‒ To specify a point ('Corner' point type only) as start point (closed graphics path only),
ﬁrst select the desired row, then click the button Specify Start Point.
‒ To set all points in reverse order, click the button Reverse Point List.
‒ To shift one or m ore point(s), ﬁrst select the desired row(s), then click the button Shift
Points. The dialog "Shift Points" opens to let you specify the desired x, y, z oﬀset value.
Num ber of Points Displays the num ber of the object's points (not editable).
Closed Lets you deﬁne whether the object is closed or unclosed and thus can be ﬁlled or not.
W hen set to unclosed, one segm ent between two points are rem oved and the param eter
'Type' (see above) changes to "Outline".
"True" for closed or "False" for unclosed.
W idth Lets you deﬁne the width in the x direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see below).
Height Lets you deﬁne the height in the y direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see below).
Aspect Ratio Scaling behavior of the shape:
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters ('G U I Settings
window').
Center The m arking object center position in the x and y directions in the work area's coordinate
system  (not editable).
Layer A m arking object can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set the m arking object to
visible or invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable and assign a new
m arking param eter set for all objects of one layer (see Layers W indow).
By default, no layer is assigned, the listbox is blank.
Open the Dialog "Select" by the Button 
  to select a listed layer or "<None>".
