26. 3. 27. 오후  12:02 Graphic Parameters - Graphics Paths
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_GraphicsPathsProperties.htm 3/5

--- Page 529 ---

Param eter Description
Sort M ode Lets you deﬁne the kind of m arking order sorting algorithm  for the m arking
objects/vectors:
‒ None: Sort m ode is not applied.
‒ Position: Intended to be used with POF applications; deﬁnes the m arking order
depending on the m oving direction.
‒ Distance: Sorts the m arking objects / vectors by shortest distance to reduce m arking
tim e.
Sort Direction If sort m ode 'Position' has been selected (see above), the this param eter lets you
deﬁne which direction the m arking objects / vectors should be sorted:
‒ Left To Right
‒ Top To Bottom
‒ Right To Left
‒ Bottom  To Top
For sort m ode 'Distance' (see above) this param eter deﬁnes the starting point.
Search Radius If sort m ode 'Position' or 'Distance' has been selected (see above), the program
searches for optim um  jum ps by calculating alternatives between the m arking objects /
vectors.
This param eter lets you deﬁne the radius / distance for the calculation.
If a vector is closer than this param eter, this vector is used, but there m ay be another
vector which is even closer.
A larger value reduces calculation tim e, a sm aller value norm ally reduces execution
tim e.
Point Tim e Duration [µs] Lets you deﬁne the tim e duration (in [µs]) for m arking points. This is the laser's power-
on tim e.
Because no individual param eters are allowed in a G raphics Paths set, this value is
used for all included points.
G raphic Resolution Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution. This factor is used for calculating the curve's
polygon adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) the curve ﬁtting.
Short Vector Calculation Allows to m ark dashed lines or lines with very sm all interruptions with constant speed
and therefore avoids jum p and m ark delays.
The lines inside the group have to be already sorted and aligned. Then they are
executed with constant speed and without delays using m icro vector com m ands until
a m isaligned or direction changing elem ent is next.
Available entries: "True" to enable the short vector calculation, "False" to not enable
short vector calculation.
If short vector calculation is enabled, further param eters (see below) is displayed to let
you m ake detailed settings:
‒ if "standard" system s and settings are used, these are the 'Tim elag' and 'Forerun'
param eters,
‒ if a scan system  with SCANahead control is used and 'SCANahead Control Support'
(see Setting 'Optics' Param eters) is activated, it is the 'Laser Signal Shift' param eter.
Note
‒ Short Vector Calculation is only applied to contours in a G raphic Paths group. That is,
ﬁllings are executed as norm al vectors. If you want Short Vector Calculation applied to
them  as well you need to activate it in the ﬁll param eters.
Tim elag [µs] Only visible, if 'Short Vector Calculation' (see above) is set to "True" and 'SCANahead
Control Support' is not activated.
Serves for exact positioning by com pensating the tracking error of the galvanom eter
scanners; depends on the used scan head.
The tim e period to be entered here (in [µs]) can be taken from  the scan head m anual
(see 'Technical Speciﬁcations\Tuning Speciﬁcations\Tracking error') or is evaluated
with the help of the 'Tracking Error W izard' dialog (see 'Evaluating the Scan Head
Tracking Error').
Forerun [µs] Only visible, if 'Short Vector Calculation' (see above) is set to "True" and 'SCANahead
Control Support' is not activated.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:02 Graphic Parameters - Graphics Paths
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_GraphicsPathsProperties.htm 4/5

--- Page 530 ---

Param eter Description
The forerun (tim e) is required to achieve constant speed. It increases approxim ately
linear with the m arking speed and depends on the used tuning.
The required tim e (in [µs]) m ust be determ ined iteratively.
Laser Signal Shift [µs] Only visible, if 'Short Vector Calculation' (see above) is set to "True" and 'SCANahead
Control Support' (see SCANahead Control Support) is activated.
Due to signal running tim es the position of the m arking m ay be oﬀset. To com pensate
such an oﬀset, this param eter acts as a pure tim e shift on the laser active tim e. The
adjustm ent range is between ‒20 µs and +20µs.
If the laser switches on too late (the m arked line is shifted in scan direction), reduce (‒)
the signal shift.
If the laser switches on too early (the m arked line is shifted against scan direction),
increase (+) the signal shift.
Param eter Description
[4] Transform ation
Rotation Rotation (in [deg]) relative to the original position
Translation Oﬀset in the x and y directions to the value set in the 'Center' param eter (see above).
Both values can be entered/m odiﬁed in 'Translation' or each value in 'X' and 'Y'.  X
  Y
Flip X The graphic set can be m irrored with respect to its orientation in the x direction.
Available entries: "True" for m irroring, "False" for not m irroring.
Flip Y The graphic set can be m irrored with respect to its orientation in the y direction.
Available entries: "True" for m irroring, "False" for not m irroring.
Inform ation on editing param eters is described in W indow "Properties".

Available entries: "True" for m irroring, "False" for not m irroring.

Graphic Param eters ‒ Group
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne the geom etrical properties of groups (including vector graphics).
Note If units of length ([m m ], [cm ], [m il] or [in]) are not explicitly indicated, then the
respective values correspond to the units deﬁned in the 'View' Param eters (see
'U nits\Length').
Param eter Description
[1] G roup
Label Default nam e: "G roup". You can change the nam e.
Type Closed m arking objects are set to "Area" by default. W ith this setting an object can be
ﬁlled.
If "Outline" is selected, an object cannot be ﬁlled even if it has a closed outline.
In this case, the 'Filling Lines' param eter (see below) cannot be set to "True''.
W idth Lets you deﬁne the width in the x direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see
below).
Height Lets you deﬁne the height in the y direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see
below).
Aspect Ratio Scaling behavior of the shape:
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters ('G U I
Settings window').
Center The group's center position in the x and y directions in the work area's coordinate
system . Not editable.
Layer A m arking object can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set the m arking object
visible or invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable and assign a
new m arking param eter set for all objects of one layer (see Layers W indow).
By default none layer is assigned, the listbox is blank.
Open the dialog "Select" by the Button 
 , to select a listed layer or "<None>".
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer. You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Boundary
Left Left, right, top and bottom  position of the bounding box of the group in the work
area's coordinate system  (not editable).Right
Top
Bottom
Z Bottom Only if U se 3D = "True" is set in the job node, for all objects these z-dim ensions are
evaluated and displayed.
Z Bottom  and Z-Top are 'read only'. They are evaluated from  the z values of the point
list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.
The param eter 'Z Height' can be changed to rescale the object(s) in the z-dim ension.
Thus, this param eter is particularly suitable for spirals.
U sing this param eter in the job node, all 3D objects inside the job is rescaled
correspondingly.
Note
This feature has no eﬀect on 2D objects which are transform ed on an inclined plane.
This feature scales the z-dim ension of 3D objects previous to the transform ation on an
inclined plane.
Z Top
Z Height

Param eter Description
[3] Im port Param eter
Text Shows the im ported text (not editable).
Start Index Row num ber in the text ﬁle to be im ported as start line.
The value to be entered is restricted by the num ber of lines in the text ﬁle.
End Index Row num ber in the text ﬁle to be im ported as end line.
The value to be entered is restricted by the num ber of lines in the text ﬁle.
After the end index is executed in Autom atic M ode, this are switched oﬀ autom atically.
In this case no further external starts are executable.
Current Index Row num ber in the text ﬁle to be im ported as current line.
The value to be entered is restricted by the num ber of lines in the text ﬁle.
Colum n Index If the text ﬁle is divided into colum ns (with a respective delim iter, see below), you can
deﬁne the colum n num ber in the text ﬁle to be im ported.
If the value "0" is set, the com plete line with all colum ns are im ported.
Colum n Delim iter To read out colum ns in the text ﬁle, the colum n delim iter used in the text ﬁle (for
exam ple, com m a or sem icolon) m ust be deﬁned here.
Text Qualiﬁer Indicates a text, if a delim iter sym bol (for exam ple, com m a or sem icolon) is part of the
text.
If a text qualiﬁer (typically " ") is set, the delim iter sym bol is displayed with the text.
If not, the delim iter sym bol is interpreted as colum n delim iter (see above) and the text
is divided into text blocks.
If an em pty string should be set to skip this text ﬁeld use "" (text qualiﬁer‒text
qualiﬁer).
Note In this case, do not use a space in between! It m ay create a barcode with an
encoded space character.
Preﬁx Lets you insert a preﬁx or suﬃx.
A static text is displayed in front of or after the date/tim e, for exam ple, "P [date/tim e]
S".Suﬃx
Repetitions Num ber of text repetitions.

changing to Autom atic M ode. "True" for reset or "False".

  to open a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open" to select the

font.

Lets you activate the job for 3D execution. In this case, the laserDESK  applies the z-
coordinate of 3D objects. W hen the execution is ﬁnished, the z-coordinate is reset to
the previous value.
"True" for activating the 3D execution, "False" for deactivating.
NOTES:
‒ To execute any 3D function, Option "3D" on the RTC5 board | RTC6 board m ust be
enabled (see RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).
‒ laserDESK  3D option is available in laserDESK  Prem ium  Edition only.
‒ Z-focus control elem ents and 3D spirals can generally be executed in laserDESK
Standard Edition, that is, without laserDESK  3D option. 'U se 3D' m ust not be set to
"True".
Speed dependent Laser Control Note
‒ The speed dependent laser control param eters can only be applied when using an
SCANahead system  or an iDRIVE scan system . The usage of an iDRIVE scan system
additionally requires a special ﬁrm ware version (contact SCANLAB).
‒ For restrictions of this feature, refer to the RTC5 M anual. Especially the execution of
bitm aps does not work with activated speed dependent laser control. To activate the

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:02 Graphic Parameters - Laser Control
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_GraphicParameters_LaserControlProperties.htm 1/3

--- Page 562 ---

Graphic Param eters ‒ Laser Control
This control elem ent allows special settings of a laser for the m arking process.
However, whether these can be executed depends, for exam ple, on basic settings and hardware.
Param eter Description
[1] Laser Control
Label Default nam e: "Laser Control". You can change the nam e.
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer.
You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Control
Laser Feature Select the adjustable function of the laser. Your laser system  needs to support this
function. Furtherm ore, you m ay need to deﬁne and establish an additional connection
(refer to the corresponding laser m anual). Available entries:
- 'Set Sim m er current'
- 'Set W aveform '
- 'Set Divider (for pulse picking)'
- 'Send Serial com m and'
Note
This elem ent is skipped, if the selected laser does not support this feature (that is, is
not deﬁned in the laser deﬁnition ﬁle).
Sim m er Current [0-100%] Lets you deﬁne the sim m er current (in [%]).
Prerequisite and basic setting: U sage of sim m er current m ust have been activated in
Laser W izard (Laser W izard Page 03-10 Laser Value Ranges > checkbox U se Sim m er
Current).
For sim m er current, ANALOG  OU T2 on Connector LASER is used.
Serial Connection Requires a serial connection to the laser.
For this, the serial connection m ust have been deﬁned in Hardware Conﬁguration (see
Setting 'PC Interfaces' Param eters) and assigned to the laser (see Setting the
Param eters 'Processing Laser'). This allows any com m and to be sent to change a laser
setting. .
Click the button 
  to open the dialog "Select", select the serial connection and click
the button OK.
Pulse Form The pulse form  selection value is an integer deﬁned by the laser m anufacturer. The bit
pattern of this num ber is outputted by the Connector EXTENSION 2 output ports.
The pulse form  rem ains set when the job has ﬁnished until the laser system  is reset or
a laser control node will change it again.
Enter the desired value (for available values, refer to the corresponding laser m anual).
Divider Serves to set a pulse-picking frequency by applying a new divider value to the laser
base frequency, thus changing the current laser frequency. This frequency is used
until the end of the job or until another laser control node changes it again.
Prerequisite and basic setting: Pulse picking has to be activated in the Laser W izard
(Laser W izard Page 02-10 Laser Control > Listbox Pulse-Picking-Laser M ode).
Note
For laser types where the base frequency can be speciﬁed, the following applies: a
frequency change can sim ply be set by the m arking param eters (change frequency
only but leave the globally eﬀective divider value unchanged). Thus, all frequencies
are adjustable.
For laser types with internal base frequency, discrete frequencies can only be set by
adjusting the Divider value.
The control node is intended for exactly this purpose.

Graphic Param eters ‒ Polygon

‒ To change a point's position in the table, ﬁrst select the desired row, then click the
button M ove Point U p or button M ove Point Down.
‒ To specify a point as start point (closed polygon only), ﬁrst select the desired row,
then click the button Specify Start Point.
‒ To specify a point as start point, ﬁrst select the desired row, then click the button
Specify Start Point.
‒ To set all points in reverse order, click the button Reverse Point List.
‒ To shift one or m ore point(s), ﬁrst select the desired row(s), then click the button
Shift Points. The dialog "Shift Points" opens to let you specify the desired x, y, z oﬀset
value.
Num ber of Points Displays the object's num ber of points (not editable).
Closed Lets you deﬁne whether the object is closed or unclosed and thus can be ﬁlled or not.
W hen set to unclosed, one segm ent between two points are rem oved and the 'Type'
param eter (see above) changes to "Outline".
Available entries: "True" for closed, "False" for unclosed.
W idth Lets you deﬁne the width in the x direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see
below).
Height Lets you deﬁne the height in the y direction (with param eter 'Rotation'= "0", see
below).
Aspect Ratio Scaling behavior of the shape:
‒ Fixed: shape keeps aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters ('G U I
Settings window').
Center The m arking object's center position in the x and y directions in the work area's
coordinate system  (not editable).
Layer A m arking object can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set the m arking object
visible or invisible, editable or not editable, m arkable or not m arkable and assign a
new m arking param eter set for all objects of one layer (see Layers W indow).

Graphic Param eters ‒ Ram ping
This control elem ent is used to apply the ram ping function to one or several graphic objects. It allows to vary a param eter along
the edges of a polygonal line.
Note The ram ping function is described in How to U se the Ram ping Function. It describes
what ram ping is all about.
Param eter Description
[1] Ram ping
Label Default nam e: "Ram ping". You can change the nam e.
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer.
You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Control
Signal Type Lets you select the signal type:
‒ Analog Out 1: deﬁnes the ram ping by the laser power.
‒ Digital Out (8Bit, Ext.2): deﬁnes the ram ping by the laser power.
‒ Pulse Length: deﬁnes the ram ping by the laser's pulse length.
‒ Frequency: deﬁnes the ram ping by the laser's frequency.
‒ Ram ping Oﬀ: serves to term inate the ram ping function.
Note
‒ The signal type m ust correspond to an output which controls the laser in som e way.
‒ To term inate the ram ping function (so that the ram ping function is only used for the
intended graphic objects), a second 'Ram ping' control elem ent m ust be inserted
behind the graphic object in the tree. Here, the signal type m ust be set to 'Ram ping
Oﬀ'.
Start Value [%] The ram ping starts at the value entered here and is linearly changed to 100 %. Here,
100 % corresponds to the value deﬁned for the graphic object's param eter (see
'M arking Param eters ‒ Laser Param eters').
End Value [%] The ram ping ends at the value entered here and is linearly changed from  100 %. Here,
100 % corresponds to the value deﬁned for the graphic object's param eter (see
'M arking Param eters ‒ Laser Param eters').
Rising Edge Tim e [m s] Deﬁnes the tim e duration in [m s] for the ram ping at the beginning of the object.
Falling Edge Tim e [m s] Deﬁnes the tim e duration in [m s] for the ram ping at the end of the object.
Inform ation on editing param eters is described in W indow "Properties".
(original text - this is not a translation)

One of the following item s can be selected:
‒ Hatchlines BEFORE Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked before the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AFTER Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked after the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AND Outlines: m ust be selected, if the whole object shall be sorted.
‒ Hatchlines only: only the hatchlines are m arked.

‒ None: Sort m ode is not applied.
‒ Position: Intended to be used with POF applications; deﬁnes the m arking order
depending on the m oving direction.
‒ Distance: Sorts the m arking objects / vectors by shortest distance to reduce m arking
tim e.

direction the m arking objects / vectors should be sorted:
‒ Left ToRight
‒ Top To Bottom
‒ Right To Left
‒ Bottom  To Top
For sort m ode 'Distance' (see above) this param eter deﬁnes the starting point.
Search Radius If sort m ode 'Position' or 'Distance' has been selected (see above), the program
searches for optim um  jum ps by calculating alternatives between the m arking objects
/ vectors.
This param eter lets you deﬁne the radius / distance for the calculation.
If a vector is closer than this param eter, this vector is used, but there m ay be another
vector which is m ore close.
A larger value reduces calculation tim e, a sm aller value norm ally reduces execution
tim e.
Param eter Description

Graphic Param eters ‒ Serial Com m unication
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne the properties of the 'Serial Com m unication' control elem ent.
It serves as a ﬂexible tool to interrupt the RTC list execution for im plem enting actions like axes control com m ands, PLC com m ands
or changing laser param eters.
Param eter Description
[1] Serial Com m unication
Label Default nam e: "Serial Com m unication". You can change the nam e.
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer. You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Control
Serial Connection Serves to deﬁne the serial connection.
Only PC interfaces (COM ), which have been deﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration
(see Setting 'PC Interfaces' Param eters), are shown and can be selected.
Open the dialog "Select" by the Button 
 , to select a listed serial connection.
Data Lets you enter the com m and or m essage to be send to the respective receiver.
Therefore, the receiver m ust be able to identify this com m and/data.
Hexadecim al Values If "True" is selected, data are interpreted as hexadecim al values. Each value then
corresponds to 1 byte. Allowed separators for the individual byte values: space,
com m a, sem icolon.
Exam ple: 0x1F 0xD2 is sent as 2 bytes with the values 31 and 210. The data are only
checked during execution. If they do not have the correct form at, the job is aborted.
Block Start Serve to deﬁne the block start and end of the m essage.
Only binary num bers are allowed.
Exam ple: "0x13" (carriage return) as the m essage end character.Block End
W ait For Reply Lets you deﬁne, whether laserDESK  will wait for reply or not after the com m and (see
above) has been executed.
If it will wait, the execution of a laserDESK  job (m arking) is interrupted until the reply
will return. If not, m arking will continue im m ediately.
"True" for waiting. "False" for not waiting.
Response Tim e [m s] Deﬁnes the tim e (in [m s]), laserDESK  will wait for a reply. (Input is only necessary if the
param eter 'W ait for Reply', see above, has been set to "True").
If the reply will not return within this period, the further process execution is canceled.
'Continue M arking' Reply Serves to deﬁne which reply text laserDESK  is waiting for to continue or to cancel
m arking. Only one of the two param eters m ust be deﬁned (condition to continue or
condition to cancel m arking). Only ASCII characters are allowed.
As a prerequisite the reply text m ust be known ‒ at least approxim ately, because it is
suﬃcient to deﬁne a textual part (for exam ple, "OK ").
If the param eter is not deﬁned (em pty input ﬁeld), this condition is ignored.
Exam ple: 'Continue M arking' Reply : " " (em pty)
'Cancel M arking' Reply: "Error"
'Cancel M arking' Reply
Inform ation on editing param eters is described in W indow "Properties".
(original text - this is not a translation)

ﬁlled.

  X
  Y

below).

below).

‒ Variable: shape does not keep aspect ratio indeﬁnitely;
‒ <default>: shape inherits the aspect ratio settings of the Edit Param eters ('G U I
Settings window').

Top
Bottom
Z Bottom Only if U se 3D = "True" is set in the job node, for all objects these z-dim ensions are
evaluated and displayed.
Z Bottom  and Z-Top are 'read only'. They are evaluated from  the z values of the point
list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.
The param eter 'Z Height' can be changed to rescale the object(s) in the z-dim ension.
Thus, this param eter is particularly suitable for spirals.
U sing this param eter in the job node, all 3D objects inside the job is rescaled
correspondingly.
Note
This feature has no eﬀect on 2D objects which are transform ed on an inclined plane.
Z Top
Z Height

Available entries: "True" for ﬁlling the text, "False" for not ﬁlling the text.
M ark This param eter let you deﬁne the kind of execution of this object.
One of the following item s can be selected:
‒ Hatchlines BEFORE Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked before the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AFTER Outlines: the hatchlines are m arked after the outlines.
‒ Hatchlines AND Outlines: m ust be selected, if the whole object shall be sorted.
‒ Hatchlines only: only the hatchlines are m arked.
‒ None: the m arking object is not m arked.
M arking Count If an application requires repeated m arking, you can deﬁne the num ber of m arking
iterations here.

Lead Lets you deﬁne the lead of a 3D spiral.
Because this param eter/function is typically used for drilling, a positive value results in
a negative z-coordinate.
The z-coordinate will always be executed relative to a z-focus control elem ent (if set) or
to the z=0 plane (norm ally the working plane). W hen the execution is ﬁnished, the laser
focus returns to the initial z-position.
Note

board (see RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).

vector which is even closer.

list. To shift the object in z you can use the z-focus control node.

‒ Distance: Sorts the m arking objects / vectors by shortest distance to reduce m arking
tim e.

Param eter Description

‒ Top To Bottom
‒ Right To Left
‒ Bottom  To Top
For sort m ode 'Distance' (see above) this param eter deﬁnes the starting point.

searches for optim um  jum ps by calculating alternatives between the m arking objects
/ vectors.
This param eter lets you deﬁne the radius / distance for the calculation.
If a vector is closer than this param eter, this vector is used, but there m ay be another
vector which is m ore close.
A larger value reduces calculation tim e, a sm aller value norm ally reduces execution
tim e.
Point Tim e Duration [µs] Lets you deﬁne the tim e duration (in [µs]) for m arking points. This is the laser's power-
on tim e.
Because no individual param eters are allowed in a Tile Set, this value is used for all
included points.
Hom e J um ps If set to "True", a check is done during the list calculation and before each control
node sequence (which m ay last som e tim e) a hom e jum p is inserted.
The hom e jum p position is deﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration (refer to Setting
'Optics' Param eters). The hom e jum p feature of the tile set and the hardware
conﬁguration is independent from  each other. Only the sam e position is used.

Bit Pattern Lets you deﬁne the bit pattern, which selects the variant for execution. The bit pattern
is assigned to the pins of the 'Extension 1' connector of the RTC5 board | RTC6 board
(DIG ITAL IN 0-7, see Connecting Lasers and RTC Boards).
Note
‒ Only bits enabled in the job node's 'Variant Bit M ask' param eter (see G raphic
Param eters ‒ J ob) are available.
‒ The bit pattern "0000 0000" m ust not be deﬁned. Because this bit pattern is exclusive
for idle and reset state, its not valid.
- The selected variant is only started by the start signal to the connector LASER of the
RTC board (see Connecting Lasers and RTC Boards), not by applying the selection
signals.
To deﬁne the bit pattern:
‒ Click the Button 
  to open the Dialog "Bit Pattern":
‒ To deﬁne the bit pattern, click the button 
  for each enabled bit and select "0" or
"1".
‒ Click the button OK.
Visible W hen creating two (or m ore) variants overlapping another, it can be advantageous to
set one variant to invisible while creating the other one. "True" for visible or "False"
for invisible.
Dialog "Bit Pattern"
Param eter Description

ﬁlled.
If "Outline" is selected, an object cannot be ﬁlled.
In this case, the 'Filling Lines' param eter (see below) cannot be set to "True''.

‒ 100 % spacing: norm al space between the characters.

Graphic Param eters ‒ Wait For Encoder
These param eters of the control elem ent "W ait For Encoder" precisely deﬁne the distance and wait m ode for the required
encoder.
Param eter Description
[1] Wait For Encoder
Label Default nam e: "W ait For Encoder". You can change the nam e.
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer.
You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Control
Distance Lets you deﬁne the distance in units (see 'Deﬁning the 'View' Param eters\U nits'). The
distance is m easured from  the position at the start of m arking.
Enter the desired value.
Encoder Only available, if POF-Type XY is set in the Hardware Conﬁguration (see 'Setting

‒ X
‒ Y

Available entries:
‒ Exceed: Further execution of a list is postponed until the encoder counter has
overstepped the deﬁned distance (see above).
‒ Fall Below: Further execution of a list is postponed until the encoder counter has
understepped the deﬁned distance.
‒ Pass By: Further execution of a list is postponed until the encoder counter has
overstepped or understepped the deﬁned distance depending on the sign of the
param eter. In contrast to the other values, this passing by has to occur! If the encoder
counts already have passed the distance, then you m ust wait for a full cycling of the
counter.

Graphic Param eters ‒ Z-Focus Control
These graphic param eters precisely deﬁne the z-focus position when using a SCANLAB varioSCAN dynam ic focusing unit within a
3-axis scan system  (see Inserting Z-Focus Control Elem ents).
Note Z-focus control is only possible, if Option "3D" is enabled on the RTC5 board | RTC6
board (see 'Controlling a 3-Axis Scan System  (optional)' in the RTC5 M anual) and a 3D
correction ﬁle (D3_[nnn].ct5) is used (see 'Correction File' param eter in Setting 'Optics'
Param eters).
Param eter Description
[1] Z-Focus Control
Label Default nam e: "Z-Focus Control". You can change the nam e.
U ID U nique IDentiﬁer.
You can change the U ID. See also U nique IDentiﬁer (U ID).
Param eter Description
[2] Control
Z Position Lets you deﬁne the z-focus position.
A positive value (without "+") m oves the m arking level up (direction opposite to the
laser beam ), a negative value (with "-") down (direction of the laser beam ).
Norm ally, the value "0" deﬁnes the working plane.
The z-focus position is handled as a z-oﬀset to all deﬁned z-coordinates of the objects.
To change or reset the z-focus position a further z-focus control elem ent m ust be
inserted and deﬁned.
The deﬁned z-focus position is m aintained if a sim ple 3D object (for exam ple,
spiral/helix) is executed in a standard 2D job (job param eter U se 3D = "False"). A jum p
back to the z position set here is executed!
Note
‒ M anually change of the z-coordinate in the window "M anual Laser Control" or by the
appropriate rem ote com m and changes this z-focus position too.
‒ Setting a z-focus position or defocussing (see 'M otion Type' below) does not aﬀect
each other. The defocus rem ains after changing the z-position but at the new z-level.
M otion Type Lets you deﬁne the m otion type:
‒ Absolute: M otion to an absolute position within the coordinate system .
‒ Relative: M otion relative to the last z-focus position.
Note
M ake sure to stay in the allowed z coordinate range when using relative m otions,
especially if you use variants and do not know the execution sequence in advance.
‒ Defocussing: M otion by using the set_defocus com m and (see RTC m anual). In
laserDESK , the value of the defocussing (z position) is in the sam e direction than the
values of the z m otion. Positive values shift the focus in positive z direction towards
the scan head. That is in contrast to the direction of the set_defocus com m and, which
is opposite to the z direction. Furtherm ore, the defocus are reset after each m arking
process and will not rem ain like the z position.
Note
Setting a z-focus position (see 'Z Position' above) or defocussing does not aﬀect each
other. The defocus rem ains after changing the z-position but at the new z-level.
Note
U sing 3D scan system  with a varioSCAN dynam ic focusing unit, jum ps in z-direction m ust be executed slower than with a 2D scan
system . Thus, the two param eters below let you deﬁne individual settings for the jum p speed and jum p delay which are applied
for this m otion in z-direction.

Shared Properties
The Shared Properties parameters serve to deﬁne speciﬁc parameters for several marking objects (selected in the work area or
Job Explorer) at one go. The corresponding button in the topmost line of the Properties window is active only, if one of the
following is selected:
a group
a graphic set
a variant
several marking objects
a group, graphic set, variant or several marking objects are selected. Click button to display the shared properties of the selected
objects:
[1] Appearance Description
Type Graphic objects can have closed or open outlines.
‒ Objects with open outlines are always of type 'Outline' and can not be ﬁlled.
‒ Objects with closed outline may be of type 'Outline' or of type 'Area'. Only the 'Area' type objects
can be ﬁlled.
This parameter lets you deﬁne the type of all selected objects, but only the closed outline objects can
and is changed.
If on or several objects are 'Area' type and one or several objects are 'Outline' type, no type is
displayed here, but the closed objects can be ﬁlled.
Layer The marking objects combined for shared properties can be assigned to a layer. Then you can set
them visible or invisible, editable or not editable, markable or not markable (see Layers Window).
By default none layer is assigned, the listbox is empty.
Open the dialog "Select" by the Button 
 , to select a listed layer or "<None>".
[2] Marking Description
M ark Lets you enable or disable the marking process for this object. When enabling the marking process,
the order of hatchlines and outlines are deﬁned. One of the following items can be selected:
- Hatchlines BEFORE Outlines: the hatchlines are marked before the outlines
‒ Hatchlines AFTER Outlines: the hatchlines are marked after the outlines
‒ Hatchlines AND Outlines: must be selected, if the whole object shall be sorted
‒ Hatchlines only: only the hatchlines are marked
‒ None: the marking object is not marked
Graphic Resolution Lets you deﬁne the graphic resolution. This factor is used for the calculation of the curve's polygon
adaptation. The bigger the factor, the rougher (less accurate) is the curve ﬁtting.
Point Time Duration [µs]Lets you deﬁne the marking time duration (in [µs]) for points.
Is only editable, if several points are selected exclusively.
If several points are selected together with other objects, the entered value is applied to these points
and deleted afterwards in the shared properties.
To edit the shared properties for several objects:

01. Select a group, graphic set, variant or several marking objects in the work area or Job Explorer.

The shared graphic parameters are listed.

The parameter is highlighted blue.
04. If you need to modify a value press the <Tab> key.
The current value in the right column is highlighted blue.
Input the desired value using integers or ﬂoating point numbers.
Conﬁrm your input by pressing the <Enter> key.
05. If one of several items must be selected, the button 
  is displayed at the side.
Click the button to select the desired item by a drop-down menu.
06. If one or several items must be deﬁned in a dialog, the Button 
  is displayed at the side.
Click the button to deﬁne the item(s) by the dialog.
Note If you subsequently close the job without saving, the changes are not applied!

   or

the query "Do you want to save your changes?" appears in the 'Save changes?' dialog.
Click the button OK.

Marking Param eters ‒ Laser Param eters
The laser param eters (group [2]) let you deﬁne laser perform ance. Output power, frequency and pulse length settings are
fundam ental to the results of the m arking process. Additionally, the operation m ode of the used laser m ust be set.
[2] Laser Param eters Description
Laser Power (P) [%] Lets you deﬁne the output power of the used laser as a [%] of the laser power deﬁned in the
hardware settings (see 'Calibration Factor Of Power' param eter in 'Processing Laser' Param eters).
Operation M ode An operation m ode m ust be selected that corresponds to the used laser type:
‒ Pulsed
‒ Continuous W ave
Note
‒ If "Continuous W ave" is selected, the param eters 'Laser Frequency' and 'Pulse Length' (see
below) are hidden.
‒ For bitm ap m aking, the calibration table is applied and cw-m ode is ignored.
Laser Frequency (f) [kHz] If a pulsed laser is used, the required pulse frequency (in [kHz]) can be entered here.
If "Continuous W ave" is selected (see above), this param eter is hidden.
Pulse Length [µs] If a pulsed laser is used, the required pulse length (in [µs]) can be entered here.
If "Continuous W ave" is selected (see above), this param eter is hidden.
Note
This param eter is not shown, if a G eneralCO2 laser (laser m ode 0) has been selected in the
Hardware Settings (see Setting 'Processing Laser' Param eter\Laser Type). In this case the power is
set to 100% due to the cw setting (duty cycle).
FirstPulseK iller (FPK ) [µs] Som e laser types (for exam ple, YAG ) show pulse-to-pulse energy variation at the start of a pulse
train (that is, m arking vector). A device for appropriately suppressing or com pensating this
behavior com es with som e laser m odels. The FirstPulseK iller signal can be used to trigger or
enable such a device at the start of m ark vectors.
Enter the length of the FirstPulseK iller signal (in [µs]).
For further inform ation about the FirstPulseK iller signal, see "Laser Control - YAG  M odes" in the
RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Spot Distance [µm ] Only visible, if
‒ an RTC6 board is selected,
‒ Speed dependent Laser Control is set to "True" and
‒ Signal to vary is set to "Spot Distance" in the J ob Properties.
Because the spot distance is dependent on the m arking speed and laser frequency, changing this
value (in [µm ]) results in a change of the laser frequency. Therefore, the laser frequency is
displayed, but just for inform ation (read only).
In addition, changing the m ark speed in the Speed Param eters will result in a change of the spot
distance value here.
Inform ation on editing the individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Marking Parameters - Laser Delays

M arking Param eters ‒ Laser Delays
Laser delays aﬀect the accuracy of the m arking process result. But they do not inﬂuence the duration of the m arking process.
Laser delays deﬁne when the "laser active" laser control signals get turned on or oﬀ before or after a m ark or arc com m and or a
series of m ark and arc com m ands.
[4] Laser Delays Description
LaserOn Delay [µs] To avoid burn-in eﬀects (because the m irrors ﬁrst have to be accelerated up to the deﬁned
m arking speed) this delay is autom atically inserted at the start of a m ark or arc
com m and.
Deﬁnes the m om ent when the RTC5 | RTC6 turns on the laser.
Delay (in [µs]) until the laser gets activated.
LaserOﬀ Delay [µs] The laser should not turn oﬀ before the scanner reaches the ﬁnal set position.
Autom atically inserted before the laser is turned oﬀ.
Delay (in [µs]) until the laser gets deactivated.
Note The values for LaserOn Delay [µs] and LaserOﬀ Delay [µs] (and scanner delay) can be calculated by the Context M enu
(Right M ouse Button) of the W indow "Properties" basing on the tracking error of the used scan head. The calculated
values are then entered autom atically, see Calculate Delays.
If an iDRIVE scan system  is used, the tracking error can be evaluated within hardware control, see Evaluating the Scan
Head Tracking Error.
Note The RTC board sets new position data every 10 µs. Therefore, the point m arking duration (see param eter 'Tim e
Duration' in G raphic Param eters ‒ Point) is rounded to 10 µs. To get a m ore precise tim ing, the LaserOn Delay [µs] and
LaserOﬀ Delay [µs] can be used, which are applied during the point m arking with deﬁned laser on tim e. These delays
are independent of the position output and can be adjusted to units of 0.5 µs.
Exam ple:
Point tim e duration = 10 µs
LaserOn Delay [µs] = 6 µs
LaserOﬀ Delay [µs] = 0 µs
The resulting laser on tim e is : Tim e duration ‒ LaserOn Delay [µs] + LaserOﬀ Delay [µs] = 4 µs.
For further inform ation about the laser delays, see "Laser Delays" in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Note If Sky W riting is activated, the laser delays (as well as the scanner delays) are not taken into account in the sky writing
m ode.
They are fully functional again after deactivating of sky writing m ode.
Note The laser and the scanner, and thus the laser delays and the scanner delays are highly interdependent. Changing the
laser delays m ay require changing the scanner delays too.
For inform ation on optim izing delays, see "Notes On Optim izing The Delays" in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Note The control com m and set_rtc5_m ode for the RTC6 board has been rem oved and all laser delays of the RTC6 are used
with higher resolution.
Inform ation on editing the individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25

file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_MarkingParameters_MarkingProperties_4_LaserDelays… 2/2

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Marking Parameters - Scanner Delays
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_MarkingParameters_MarkingProperties_5_ScannerDel… 1/2

--- Page 663 ---

M arking Param eters ‒ Scanner Delays
The scanner delays aﬀect the accuracy of the m arking process result. After each vector or arc com m and, the RTC5 | RTC6 inserts
one of the scanner delays before the next com m and is started.
[5] Scanner Delays Description
J um p Delay [µs] Com pensates the lag caused by the m irror's settling.
Delay (in [µs]) after a jum p com m and, before the next com m and is executed.
M ark Delay [µs] Com pensates an occurring lag during a m ark or arc com m and to ensure that the scanners
reach the set position properly before the next com m and starts.
Delay (in [µs]) after a single m ark or arc com m and or after the last m ark or arc com m and
of a polyline.
For point m arking the m ark delay can be used to wait for a slow reaction of the laser
system . Although for point m arking the scanners stay (and thus there is no tracking error),
but the laser m ay be switched oﬀ too late.
Polygon Delay [µs] Replaces the m ark delay between two successive m ark or arc com m ands to ensure that
the corners of the polyline is m arked accurately.
Applies for the com plete polyline.
Delay (in [µs]) after a m ark com m and.
Note The scanner (and laser) delays can be calculated by the property window's Context M enu (Right M ouse Button) basing
on the tracking error of the used scan head. The calculated values are then entered autom atically (see Calculate Delays
in W indow "Properties").
If an iDRIVE scan system  is used, the tracking error can be evaluated within the hardware control (see Evaluating the
Scan Head Tracking Error).
For further inform ation about the scanner delays, see "Scanner Delays" in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Note If Sky W riting is activated, the scanner delays (as well as the laser delays) are not taken into account in the sky writing
m ode.
They are fully functional again after deactivating of sky writing m ode.
Note The scanner and the laser, and thus scanner delays and laser delays are highly interdependent. Changing the scanner
delays m ay require changing the laser delays too.
For inform ation on optim izing delays, see "Notes On Optim izing The Delays" in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Marking Parameters - Wobble Mode

M arking Param eters ‒ W obble M ode
W obble m ode (wobble function) varies the line width during laser m arking. An ellipse-shaped m otion is added to the regular,
linear m otion, resulting in a spiral m otion of the laser beam . Alternatively, a com bination with a ﬁgure-of-8 (standing or lying to
the direction of m otion) can be activated.
Note For further inform ation about wobble m ode, see "W obbel Function" in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
The following wobble m ode param eters can be edited:
[6] W obble M ode Description
W obble M ode Serves to activate or deactivate wobble m ode and to select the wobble ﬁgure.
Available entries:
‒ Oﬀ,
‒ Standing 8,
‒ Elliptic,
‒ Lying 8.
If ' Standing 8', 'Elliptic' or 'Lying 8' is selected, the param eters below are enabled for
editing.
W obble Frequency [Hz] Only enabled if 'W obble M ode' is not "Oﬀ", see W obble M ode.
Deﬁnes the wobble frequency (in [Hz]).
Num ber of ellipses per second.
W obble Am plitude Longitudinal [m m ] Only enabled if 'W obble M ode' is not "Oﬀ", see W obble M ode.
Height of the longitudinal am plitude (in [m m ]).
Am plitude of the ellipse-shaped wobble m otion parallel to the current m otion direction.
W obble Am plitude Transversal [m m ] Only enabled if 'W obble M ode' is not "Oﬀ", see W obble M ode.
Height of the transversal am plitude (in [m m ]).
Am plitude of the ellipse-shaped wobble m otion perpendicular to the current m otion
direction.
Note A non-circular wobble needs a deﬁned direction which is not present for point objects. In that case the x,y coordinates
axes are used.
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Marking Parameters - Sky Writing
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_MarkingParameters_MarkingProperties_7_SkyWriting.h… 1/3

--- Page 668 ---

Marking Param eters ‒ Sky Writing
The RTC5 board | RTC6 board provides sky writing m odes for applications with elevated accuracy requirem ents. In the sky writing
m odes, every m ark vector is precisely executed at a constant m arking speed over the entire vector length.
For further inform ation, refer to the chapter on "Sky W riting" in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
[7] Sky Writing Description
Sky W riting Available entries:
Oﬀ
Deactivates the sky writing m ode.
M ode 1
In the run-in phase, the vector | arc is preceded by a "forerun" m otion perform ed by the
galvanom eter scanners at m arking speed: the galvanom eter scanners are driven a short
distance parallel to the vector (or along the arc extension), initially from  the start point in the
opposing direction, then back to the start point. After the vector | arc is processed at m arking
speed, it is then appended in the run-out phase with a short deceleration and retrace m otion
of the galvanom eter scanners (at m arking speed).
M ode 2
Like in M ode 1, each to-be-executed vector | arc gets preceded and appended with a run-in
m otion and a run-out m otion in extension of the vector | arc at m ark speed. But within this
tim e-optim ized m arking sequence, however, neither scanner forerun m otions (in the run-in
phase) nor retrace m otions (in the run-out phase) occur. Instead, jum ps are executed (at the
currently deﬁned jum p speed) from  jum p vector start points to run-in start points, from  run-
out endpoints to run-in start points, and from  run-out endpoints to jum p vector endpoints.
M ode 3
To m inim ize execution tim e, sky writing can be deactivated for vectors | arcs having only sm all
directional changes within a polyline. An angle lim it has to be deﬁned. Below that lim it,
standard m arking using the (variable) polygon is executed. Above that lim it, sky writing M ode
2 is executed.
M ode 4
"Im proved" M ode 3, which executes the optim ized path even when param eters are changing
between objects. Sky W riting M ode 4 is an RTC6 functionality for which Sky W riting M ode 3 is
used with the RTC5 board.
Default
Corresponds to M ode 3, but displays only the param eters 'Tim elag [µs]', 'LaserOn Shift [µs]'
and 'Angular Lim it'. The param eters 'Forerun [µs]' and 'Overrun [µs]' are set to their default
values, to sim plify the usage. Thus, Default is the recom m ended entry.
Once a sky writing m ode is selected, the param eters below are enabled for editing.
Tim elag [µs] Serves to com pensate the tracking error of the galvanom eter scanners. Depends on the used scan
head.
Tim e period (in [µs]).
Note
Initially this param eter 'Tim elag [µs]' should be adjusted and only afterwards the param eter
'LaserOn Shift [µs]'.
LaserOn Shift [µs] Allows adjustm ent of the "laser active" laser control signal's switch-on tim e point.
Tim e period (in [µs]).
Is used instead of the LaserOn-delay when sky writing is active.
Forerun [µs] Only available, if M ode 2 or M ode 3 or M ode 4 is selected under 'Sky W riting'.
Deﬁnes the acceleration tim e (in [µs]) for the "forerun" m otion (run-in phase).
Overrun [µs] Only available, if M ode 2 or M ode 3 or M ode 4 is selected under 'Sky W riting'.
Deﬁnes the deceleration tim e (in [µs]) for the "overrun" m otion (run-out phase).
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Marking Parameters - Sky Writing

--- Page 669 ---

[7] Sky Writing Description
Angular Lim it Only available, if M ode 3 or M ode 4 or Default is selected under 'Sky W riting'.
Deﬁnes the angular lim it for vectors or arcs to be executed with Sky W riting.
If consecutive vectors or arcs enclose a lower angle no sky writing is executed.
Scale [%] Only available, if 'SCANahead Control Support' is selected in the Hardware Settings
AND M ode 2 or M ode 3 or M ode 4Default is selected under 'Sky W riting'.
Deﬁnes the scaling of prerun and postrun distances of the sky writing m otions.
Note If a sky writing m ode is activated, previously deﬁned LaserOn Delay [µs] und LaserOﬀ Delay [µs] as well as m ark and
polygon delays (see M arking Param eters ‒ Scanner Delays) are not taken into account.
They are fully functional again after deactivating the sky writing m ode.
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".

Marking Param eters ‒ SCANahead Param eters
W hen using an SCANahead system  together with an RTC6 board, the SCANahead param eters let you take full advantage of the
SCANahead functionality. Laser delays can be set to add an additional tim e shift, scanner delays can be adapted to deﬁne contour
accuracy.
Note The SCANahead-speciﬁc m arking param eters are only shown in the W indow "Properties", if 'SCANahead Control
Support' is selected in the Hardware Settings.
In turn 'SCANahead Control Support' is only applicable, if an RTC6 board is used (see Starting laserDESK).
[8] SCANahead Param eters Description
LaserOn Tim e Shift [µs] Serves to set the laser delay. Allows to add an additional tim e shift to the laser signal (laser system
dependent).
Refer to the description of the LaserOnShift and LaserOffShift param eters in the m anual
'excelliSCAN Scan Heads ‒ Functional Principle of SCANahead Servo Control and Operation by RTC6
Boards'.
LaserOﬀ Tim e Shift [µs]
Acceleration Scale [%] Aﬀects the laser delays and thus deﬁnes the portion the laser will already be activated during
acceleration.
Refer to the description of the AccScale param eter in the m anual m entioned above
Corner Scale [%] Aﬀects the scanner delays and thus deﬁnes the contour accuracy.
Refer to the description of the CornerScale and EndScale param eters in the m anual m entioned
above.End Scale [%]
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".

Fill Param eters ‒ Overview
The ﬁll param eters let you deﬁne properties aﬀecting the m arking result of ﬁlled shapes. All ﬁll param eters are integrated into one
param eter set (see ﬁgure below), which is displayed in the W indow "Properties". Param eter sets can be deﬁned for one or several
m arking objects or for the job. G enerally, each param eter set provides the sam e param eter types:
The individual ﬁll param eters are described in the following topics:
Fill Param eters - Param eter Set
Fill Param eters ‒ Fill Param eters
Fill Param eters ‒ Scaling Param eters
Fill Param eters ‒ Hatching Param eters
Fill Param eters ‒ Sorting Param eters
Fill Param eters ‒ Short Vector Param eter
Note It can happen that the ﬁll calculation fails (for exam ple, with overlapping edges) and an error m essage is issued. It
often helps to change the param eters slightly and run the ﬁll calculation again.
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter sets is described in W indow "Properties".

Fill Param eters - Param eter Set
The group [1] param eters provide inform ation about the displayed param eter set. The param eter set shown in the W indow
"Properties" is either selected in the Library Explorer (job library or m arking library) or is assigned to a m arking object selected in
the work area or J ob Explorer.
[1] Param eter Set Description
Inherited From Shows the source of the object's param eter settings:
‒ If the settings were assigned from  a tem plate in the job library or m arking library, the ﬁeld shows
"Tem plate: [Tem plateNam e]" (see Library Explorer).
‒ As long as the m arking object's param eters were not m odiﬁed and no param eter set was assigned
to the m arking object, the ﬁeld shows "Elem ent: J ob". But note, when a m arking object gets
created, it is autom atically assigned the param eter set that was designated as the job's default
m arking param eter set.
‒ If the settings were m odiﬁed for a selected m arking object, but the settings are not assigned to a
param eter set, the ﬁeld shows "<None>".
Nam e If a m arking object is selected in the work area or J ob Explorer, the object nam e is displayed.
If a param eter set is selected in the job library or m arking library, the param eter set nam e is
displayed.
Description The input ﬁeld lets you enter a description/com m ent.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Fill Parameters – Fill Parameters

Fill Param eters ‒ Fill Param eters
The ﬁll param eters (group [2]) let you deﬁne basic properties for aligning the ﬁlling lines.
[2] Fill Param eters Description
Filling Type Desired ﬁlling type.
Hatch:
The ﬁlling lines are always parallel. Hatch density is deﬁned by the 'Line Distance' (see below).
If "Hatch" is selected, then the param eters 'Outline Reduction' and 'Beam  Com pensation' (see
below) get disabled, as do the com plete [3] Scaling Param eters.
Path:
The ﬁlling lines are decreasing duplicates of the outline and downscale inward. The ﬁlling
lines' concentration is deﬁned by the 'Line Distance' (see below) and the num ber of 'Loops'
(see [3] Scaling Param eters). If "Path" is selected, the param eter 'Line Reduction' (see below)
gets disabled, as do m ost param eters in the [4] Hatching Param eters and [5] Sorting
Param eters.
Path W ith Hatch:
The ﬁlling lines are a com bination of decreasing outline duplicates (Path) and hatching (see
Hatch).
Note
W hich ﬁlling type you need for your m arking process, depends on several factors: the m aterial,
the used laser, the desired m arking result, and the intended m axim um  m arking tim e.
Line Distance [m m ] Lets you deﬁne the distance between the ﬁlling lines (in [m m ]).
Line Reduction [m m ] Lets you deﬁne the distance between the hatch lines and the outline (in [m m ]) in the x and y
directions.
If the distance is "0", the ﬁlling lines contact the outline (see topm ost ﬁgure).
Disabled if "Path" is selected as the ﬁlling type (see above).

[2] Fill Param eters Description
Outline Reduction [m m ] Lets you deﬁne the reduction of the outline's diam eter (in [m m ]) when using the ﬁlling type
"Path" (see above). This function is m ainly required when m arking very sm all objects.
Disabled if type "Hatch" is selected as the ﬁlling type (see above).
Beam  Com pensation [m m ] Serves to com pensate the beam  width, to avoid the ﬁlling lines to exceed the outline.
If this param eter is deﬁned, the value of the 'Outline Reduction' param eter (see above) will
autom atically get set to the half the value deﬁned here.
Disabled if type "Hatch" is selected as the ﬁlling type (see above).

Fill Param eters ‒ Scaling Param eters
The scaling param eters (group [3]) let you deﬁne the properties of the ﬁlling lines / loops for the ﬁlling type 'Path' (or 'Path W ith
Hatch'). If the ﬁlling type 'Hatch' is selected in the [2] Fill Param eters, then the com plete scaling param eters get disabled.
[3] Scaling Param eters Description
Num ber Of Loops Deﬁnes the num ber of ﬁlling lines (loops) for the ﬁlling type "Path". The loops are decreasing duplicates
of the outline and downscale inward. To ﬁll the com plete object, a suﬃcient num ber of loops m ust be
deﬁned.
J oin First Lines Around Sets the ﬁrst/outer ﬁlling lines to 'Line J oin Type' = "Round" (see above). For the further ﬁlling lines the
settings deﬁned by the user rem ain.
Available entries: "True" for rounding oﬀ the ﬁrst contour lines, "False" for not rounding oﬀ the ﬁrst
contour lines.
Line J oin Type Desired line join type.
M iter:
The ﬁll contour is continued until it is cut by the next edge.
Bevel:
The ﬁll contour will result in a m iter cut at the edge.
Round:
Results in a (polygon approxim ation of a) arc. Each point of the inner contour has the sam e distance to
the corner.
Innerm ost ﬁrst Deﬁnes the m arking sequence: from  inside out or from  outside in.
If set to "True", the innerm ost ﬁlling line is m arked ﬁrst, then the m arking sequence is continued to the
outer contour. If set to "False", the outer contour is m arked ﬁrst, then the m arking sequence is continued
to the innerm ost ﬁlling line.
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Fill Parameters – Hatching Parameters

Fill Param eters ‒ Hatching Param eters
The hatching param eters (group [4]) let you deﬁne hatching properties, prim arily for when the ﬁlling type 'Hatch' has been
deﬁned for the ﬁlling lines (see [2] Fill Param eters).
[4] Hatching Param eters Description
Hatch Angle Required reference axis of the hatch angle. Available entries:
Absolute
The hatch lines correspond to the work area's coordinate system , independent of the rotation
angle of the m arking object. The hatching angle is deﬁned by the 'Angle Start' param eter (see
below).
Relative
The hatch lines correspond to the m arking object. The hatching angle is deﬁned by the 'Angle
Start' param eter (see below). The deﬁned rotation angle of the m arking object is also applied for
hatching.
Aligned
The angle of the hatch lines is m easured relative to the x axis of the m arking object and
additional, the x axis of the m arking objects coordinate system  is the longer side of the bounding
box.
Num ber Of Hatch Levels For hatching, several hatch levels can be deﬁned, which is executed consecutively. W hen deﬁning two
or m ore hatch levels, deﬁne the param eters 'Angle Start' and 'Angle Step' (see below) accordingly.
Disabled if "Path" is selected as the ﬁlling type (see [2] Fill Param eters).
Angle Start [deg] Deﬁnes the hatch angle (in [deg]) and the m arking direction.
The value "0" (or "-180") deﬁnes horizontal hatch lines, the m arking is executed from  left to right. In
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:03 Fill Parameters – Hatching Parameters
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/F_Andockfenster/PropertyWindow_FillParameters_FillProperties_04_HatchingParameters.… 2/4

--- Page 684 ---

[4] Hatching Param eters Description
contrast, the value "180" deﬁnes m arking from  right to left.
The value "90" (or "-270") deﬁnes vertical hatch lines, the m arking is executed from  bottom  to top. In
contrast, the value" 270" (or "-90") deﬁnes m arking from  top to bottom .
Positive values rotate the angle counterclockwise, negative values clockwise.
Disabled if "Path" is selected as the ﬁlling type(see [2] Fill Param eters).
Angle Step [deg] If several 'Num bers Of Hatch Levels' (see above) are deﬁned, this param eter deﬁnes the increm ental
increasing hatch angle (in [deg]) for each hatch level on the basis of the 'Angle Start' (see above).
Disabled if ﬁlling type "Path" is selected (see [2] Fill Param eters).
Bidirectional The hatching lines are executed bidirectional to reduce long jum ps.
This works even for hatching with Line J um p.
Line J um p Deﬁnes the next line in the execution sequence of parallel hatching lines:
If Line J um p = 1, the next/neighboring line is executed.
If Line J um p > 1, one or m ore lines are skipped and a line farther away is executed next; the skipped
lines are executed in consecutive loops.
This function is used to avoid the overheating of adjacent lines.
Exam ple: Line J um p = 3. After line k has been executed (1.), 2 lines (line k+1 and line k+2) are skipped.
Then line k+3 is executed (2.). Line k+1 and line k+2 are executed in consecutive loops (4., 7.).
This feature is additional to the other settings and can be applied together with sorting and jum p
reduction.

[6] Short Vector
Param eters
Description

If set to "False", laser processing is carried out only m onodirectionally, possibly with the

Fill Param eters ‒ Short Vector Param eter
The short vector param eters (group [6]) let you deﬁne the execution param eters for certain dashed lines.
[6] Short Vector
Param eters
Description
Short Vector Calculation If Short Vector Calculation = 'Yes', ShortVector-DLL is applied to ﬁllings.
Allows to m ark dashed lines or lines with very sm all interruptions with constant speed and therefore
avoids jum p and m ark delays. Available entries: "True" (enable short vector calculation), "False"
(disable short vector calculation). W ith "true", further param eters (see below) are shown to let you
m ake detailed settings:
‒ if "standard" system s and settings are used, these are the 'Tim elag' and 'Forerun' param eters,
‒ if a scan system  with SCANahead control is used and 'SCANahead Control Support' (see Setting
'Optics' Param eters) is activated, it is the 'Laser Signal Shift' param eter.
Note
‒ Short Vector Calculation is intended for optim al execution of straight line pieces with short
interruptions. Therefore, do not use it with ﬁlls that can be executed with high jum p speed or path
ﬁlls.
Tim elag [µs] Only visible, if 'Short Vector Calculation' (see above) is set to "True" and 'SCANahead Control
Support' is not activated.
Serves for exact positioning by com pensating the tracking error of the galvanom eter scanners;
depends on the used scan head.
The tim e period to be entered here (in [µs]) can be taken from  the scan head m anual (see 'Technical
Speciﬁcations\Tuning Speciﬁcations\Tracking error') or is evaluated with the help of the 'Tracking
Error W izard' dialog (see 'Evaluating the Scan Head Tracking Error').
Forerun [µs] Only visible, if 'Short Vector Calculation' (see above) is set to "True" and 'SCANahead Control
Support' is not activated.
The forerun (tim e) is required to achieve constant speed. It increases approxim ately linear with the
m arking speed and depends on the used tuning.
The required tim e (in [µs]) m ust be determ ined iteratively.
Laser Signal Shift [µs] Only visible, if 'Short Vector Calculation' (see above) is set to "True" and 'SCANahead Control
Support' (see Setting the Param eters 'Optics') is activated.
Due to signal running tim es the position of the m arking m ay be oﬀset. To com pensate such an
oﬀset, this param eter acts as a pure tim e shift on the laser active tim e. The adjustm ent range is
between ‒20 µs and +20µs.
If the laser switches on too late (the m arked line is shifted in scan direction), reduce (‒) the signal
shift.
If the laser switches on too early (the m arked line is shifted against scan direction), increase (+) the
signal shift.
Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".

Graphic Param eters ‒ Pixel Im age
For jobs com prised of pixel im ages (ﬁle form ats bm p, jpg, gif, png, tif, twain, pcx, tga) you can (additionally to the m arking
param eters) deﬁne pixel im age param eters speciﬁc to the characteristics of those im ages. All pixel im age param eters are
integrated into one param eter set (see ﬁgure below), which is displayed in the property window. Param eter sets can be deﬁned for
one or several m arking objects or for the job. G enerally, each param eter set provides the sam e param eter types:
[1] Param eter Set Description
Inherited From Shows the source of the object's param eter settings:
‒ If the settings were assigned from  a tem plate in the job library or m arking library, the
ﬁeld shows "Tem plate: [Tem plateNam e]" (see Library Explorer / M arking Library).
‒ As long as the m arking object's param eters were not m odiﬁed and no param eter set was
assigned to the m arking object, the ﬁeld shows "Elem ent: J ob". But note that when a
m arking object gets created, it is autom atically assigned the param eter set that was
designated as the job's default m arking param eter set.
‒ If the settings were m odiﬁed for a selected m arking object, but the settings are not
assigned to a param eter set, then the ﬁeld shows "<None>".
Nam e If a m arking object is selected in the work area or J ob Explorer, the object's nam e is
displayed.
If a param eter set is selected in the job library or m arking library, the param eter set nam e
is displayed.
Description The input ﬁeld lets you enter a description/com m ent.
[2] Bitm ap Param eters Description
Pixel Distance [µm ] Deﬁnes the pixel distance in x (in [µm ]).
The value deﬁned here is a ﬁxed value.
Pixel Line Distance [µm ] Deﬁnes the pixel line distance in y (in [µm ]).
The pixel distance (see above) and the pixel line distance can be adjusted diﬀerently. This
results in a rectangle m arking area for each laser pulse and the m aterial reaction m ay get
inhom ogeneous. For surface ablation sm all deviations allow ﬁne tuning.
In that case it m ight be better to disable the dithering procedure (see NOTE below).
Dithering Procedure In com puter graphics, dithering is one m ethod to create illusions of color depth, in
particular for color reduction. A special pixel alignm ent avoids hard transitions between
the colors.
There are several algorithm s designed to perform  dithering. laserDESK  lets you chose
between two algorithm s or to disable the dithering:
‒ the Floyd Steinberg algorithm  results in very ﬁne-grained dithering,
‒ the J arvis J udice Ninke algorithm  is coarser, but has fewer visual artifacts.
‒ 'None' lets you disable the dithering procedure.
Note
The dithering procedure is developed for equal distances and the result m ay not be
satisfying when adjusting pixel distance and pixel line distance diﬀerently (see above). In
that case you m ay disable dithering.
Available entries: "FloydSteinberg", "J arvisJ udiceNinke", "None".
Bitm ap M ode Only visible, if an RTC6 board is selected (see Selecting an RTC Board).
Because the RTC6 board allowsU ltra Fast Pixel M ode (U FPM ) bitm ap processing up to 3.2

The following pixel m odes can be selected:

‒ U ltra Fast Pixel M ode (Ext 1): Variation of the 8 lower output pins of the RTC6 EXTENSION
1 socket connector.
‒ U ltra Fast Pixel M ode (Ext 2): Variation of the 8 output pins of the RTC6 EXT.2 socket
connector.
‒ Pulse Length Variation: Variation of the pulse length up to 4 µs.
‒ Vectorized (up to 1 M Hz): The bitm ap is converted into m icrovectors instead of using
the RTC pixel output m ode (refer to How to Vectorize Pixel Im ages).
The selection deﬁnes the gray value dependent output signal(s). The laser system  m ust
be adjustable by these signal changes.
The restrictions are deﬁned by the RTC control com m and set_pixel_line (see RTC6
M anual). Especially the Pulse Length Variation allows only pulse length up to 4 µs. If you
need longer pulses, you can use the Standard M ode and keep the power constant.
The m axim um  frequency is
‒ 0.4 M Hz for the Standard M ode and
‒ 3.2 M Hz for the other m odes.
If you use U FPM  (Ext.1), only the lower 8 output pins are used due to the m axim um  value
of 255 for one pixel. The upper 8 output pins rem ain unchanged.
Note
U sing these pins for U FPM , prevent the usage for variant selection or digital IO control
nodes.
If you use Pulse Length Variation, the m axim um  tim e is 4 µs. It m ay further be restricted
due to the deﬁned frequency.
Pulses per Pixel Only visible, if pixel m ode "vectorized" is selected (see above).
Lets you deﬁne the am ount of laser pulses given out for a m arked pixel (one burst).

values.

(see ﬁgure below).
‒ In U FPM  m ode (Ext.1 and Ext. 2), the colum ns 'G ray Value' and 'Digital Output' are
shown.
‒ In Pulse Length Variation, the colum ns 'G ray Value' and 'Pulse Length' are shown.
Click the Button 
  to open the Dialog "Calibration Table".
Click on a line and enter or m odify ‒ depending on the pixel m ode - the
‒ G ray value [0-255]
‒ Laser Power (P) [%]
‒ Pulse Length (W ) [µs]
‒ Digital Output.
Enter the values for each desired gray value in a new line.

an analog voltage. Digital controlled laser power by the Connector EXTENSION 2 (like IPG
lasers) can only be adjusted using an U FPM  m ode during bitm ap m arking. For these

Note Note the following for im age fractions:
Im age fractions are calculated from  a bitm ap during a tiling operation. Because the im age fraction is calculated with
respect to the actual deﬁned calibration table it is not possible to add or rem ove item s of this table after this
calculation. If an im age fraction is inside a graphic set (for exam ple, after a tiling action) it has no individual param eters
anym ore (as for all elem ents inside a graphic set). The graphic set param eters deﬁne the im age fraction param eters.
There are 2 exceptions:
‒ The pixel distance and dithering procedure will only be applied to im ages but not to im age fractions, because they
were already used for the im age fraction calculation.
‒ It is necessary that the graphic set deﬁnes a bitm ap calibration table with the sam e size the im age fraction has while
it is calculated. Else it cannot be used. It is strongly recom m ended to use exactly the sam e calibration table. If the size
diﬀers, the original im age fraction calibration table is used. But in that case no value can be adapted because the
calibration table is not accessible.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Hardware Configuration
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareConfiguration.htm 1/8

--- Page 705 ---

Hardware Conﬁguration
Opening Dialog "Hardware Settings"
M anaging the Hardware Conﬁguration
Testing the Parameter Settings
Setting Hardware Parameters
Saving the Hardware Settings
"Hardware Conﬁguration" lets you edit and manage parameters that deﬁne the used hardware. These general settings are closely
related to the used hardware and independent of the job. This enables you to transfer and execute a job on a diﬀerent setup
without any changes, because the machine speciﬁc parameters are deﬁned separately in the "Hardware Conﬁguration". You must
deﬁne a parameter set for your laser scan system's hardware after installing laserDESK on your production PC , but before ﬁrst-
time execution of a job by a speciﬁc laser scan system.
The program always has only one active hardware parameter set, which can be managed by the Dialog "Hardware Settings". To
facilitate quick changes to your hardware conﬁguration, laserDESK lets you archive diﬀerent hardware parameter sets (hardware
templates). M anagement of hardware parameter sets is described in M anaging Hardware Parameter Sets.
Dialog "Hardware Settings"

Opening Dialog "Hardware Settings"
To open Dialog "Hardware Settings":
01. Choose M enu File > "Open" > "Hardware Conﬁguration".
If a job was created previously, then the dialog "Save changes?" opens.
Then you need to proceed step 2, else step 3 is executed.
02. Click OK to save the changes.
A M S Windows-standard dialog "Save as" opens.
Save the changes and click the button OK.)
   or
Click No to cancel the changes.
03. The work area background changes to orange.
The hardware conﬁguration job is loaded.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
Note Inside "Hardware Conﬁguration", you can close the Dialog "Hardware Settings" by the Apply, Save and Exit and
Cancel button and the X button in the title bar.
Closing the dialog by Cancel and X, the changes are discarded and the old hardware conﬁguration is maintained.
For closing the dialog and saving the hardware conﬁguration temporary or permanent, refer to Saving the
Hardware Settings.
You can reopen the dialog by the 'Edit Hardware Settings' in the M enu M enu Hardware or the Hardware Settings
button in the Toolbar File.
Furthermore, the dialog can be opened inside a job to view the current settings, but all parameters are read-only
and the settings cannot be changed.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Hardware Configuration
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareConfiguration.htm 3/8

--- Page 707 ---

M anaging the Hardware Conﬁguration
Inside laserDESK, "Hardware Conﬁguration" is managed like a job. Because only one job can be open at a time, a current job must
be closed when opening "Hardware Conﬁguration".
At the ﬁrst start laserDESK creates a default hardware parameter set stored in the "HardwareConﬁguration.sld" ﬁle in the users
directory. Use the dialog "GUI Settings" to specify the directory in which the deﬁned parameter sets
([HardwareConﬁgurationName].sld) should be managed (see Deﬁning the Directories). You can freely select the path and
ﬁlename. Firstly, this enables the use of network paths and thus automatic backups, etc. Secondly, a directory can be chosen and
conﬁgured to speciﬁcally provide laserDESK users with appropriate access authorizations.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Hardware Configuration
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareConfiguration.htm 4/8

--- Page 708 ---

Testing the Param eter Settings
Similarly to a normal job, marking objects can be created inside the "Hardware Conﬁguration" job (after closing the Dialog
"Hardware Settings") and executed with the laser scan system. Thus, after deﬁning parameter settings in the Dialog "Hardware
Settings", you can immediately check those settings by a marking test (and modify them subsequently if necessary). To create
marking objects, see Inserting Objects ‒ Overview.

Setting the Parameters 'RTC Board'
Setting the Parameters 'Hardware Devices'
Setting the Parameters 'Processing Laser'
Setting the Parameters 'Optics'
Setting the Parameters 'Scan Head [1|2]'
Setting the Parameters 'PC Interfaces'

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Hardware Configuration
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareConfiguration.htm 6/8

--- Page 710 ---

Saving the Hardware Settings
After setting the hardware parameters (see section above), these can be saved temporary or permanent.
With the Apply button (see ﬁgure in section Opening Dialog "Hardware Settings"), the hardware settings are applied
temporary. The Dialog "Hardware Settings" is closed, but the Hardware Conﬁguration job keeps open. In this state you can,
for example, check the parameter settings by marking tests (and change the settings if required). To keep the settings
permanent, you need to save the Hardware Conﬁguration job subsequently.
Note If you close "Hardware Conﬁguration" subsequently without saving, the changes are not applied!
When you have found the correct settings, the Save and Exit button lets you save the hardware settings perm anent. The
hardware settings are applied, the Hardware Conﬁguration job is closed and a new job is opened. Thus, the settings are
permanent.
Note Clicking the Cancel button, the changes are discarded and the old hardware conﬁguration settings are maintained.
Closing the Dialog "Hardware Settings" by the X button in the title bar corresponds to the Cancel button.
When the Apply or Save and Exit button is clicked, the RTC board gets initialized (the output window shows "Hardware
Initialisation"). This resets the signal lines. This may cause problems for connected hardware (especially a connected laser
system). Therefore, a warning dialog is shown:
You may proceed with the button OK to save the settings. Or, to switch oﬀ the laser system before, you may go back to the Dialog
"Hardware Settings" by the Back button. But in this case the settings are not saved.
You can skip this dialog by checking the 'Initialize immediately' checkbox in the Hardware Settings dialog (see ﬁgure in section
Opening Dialog "Hardware Settings"). In this case the settings are saved directly and the RTC board gets initialized.
When the Cancel button is clicked no signal line reset will occur.
W ARNING!
When the hardware parameter settings are saved and the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is closed, the RTC is initialized.
This results in a reset of the RTC IOs (see RTC manuals under M enu Help > "Guides") and may cause signal changes to
connected hardware. Due to these connections, actions may be triggered by this initialization.
Therefore, it is strongly recommended to switch oﬀ connected hardware during an initialization procedure and/or
conﬁgure the signal lines in a way that no unwanted action can take place. M ake sure that no laser emission may
occur.
To save modiﬁed hardware parameter settings with the 'Initialize immediately' checkbox unchecked:
01. Click
a) the Save and Exit button or
b) the Apply button.
A warning dialog regarding the RTC initialization opens.
02. Click the Back button to cancel the action (for example, to switch oﬀ the laser system).
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens again.

or
Click the button OK for saving.
a) The settings are saved permanently, the Dialog "Hardware Settings" and the Hardware Conﬁguration are closed and the
RTC board is initialized.
This completes the procedure.
b) The settings are applied temporarily, that is, they are retained as long as "Hardware Conﬁguration" is open. The Dialog
"Hardware Settings" is closed and the RTC board is initialized.
