file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareManagingPSets.htm 2/10

--- Page 714 ---

Delivery Status
After installing laserDESK on the production PC (PC controlling the laser scan system), you must deﬁne the hardware parameters
appropriate for conﬁguring your laser scan system components (including the PC and board). laserDESK always has only one
active hardware parameter set, called 'Hardware Settings', whose parameters must be appropriate for the current system
conﬁguration. The Dialog "Hardware Settings" serves both for setting the hardware parameters and deﬁning the active hardware
parameter set.
As delivered, laserDESK provides one default and thus active hardware parameter set (hardware template) shown in the Dialog
"Hardware Settings". The dialog opens automatically when "Hardware Conﬁguration" is opened. After a restart, laserDESK always
uses the 'Hardware Settings' parameter set as the active one.
Note For more extensive editing of 'Hardware Settings' (and later 'Hardware Templates', see below), we recommend
creating a new system parameters set or copying an existing one ﬁrst and then subsequently modifying the hardware
parameters.
To clearly identify the active system parameter set ('Hardware Settings'), we recommend providing a meaningful
description (see 'Description' input ﬁeld in the ﬁgure above) for each hardware parameter set.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Managing Hardware Parameter Sets
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareManagingPSets.htm 3/10

--- Page 715 ---

Creating a New Hardware Param eter Set
To facilitate quick changes to your hardware conﬁguration, laserDESK lets you archive diﬀerent hardware parameter sets as
templates. These are listed inside the 'Hardware Templates' node. The templates can be renamed, deleted, copied and edited.
To create a new hardware parameter set (template):
01. Place the cursor above the right ﬁeld in the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
02. Choose 'Save Hardware Parameters As' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The New Template' dialog' opens.
Continue with step 3.
or
01. Place the cursor above the left ﬁeld in the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
02. Choose 'New' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The 'New Template' dialog opens (see ﬁgure above).
03. Enter the desired name and click the button OK.
The new hardware parameter set is inserted below 'Hardware Templates' alphabetically.
Parameter settings are inherited from the active 'Hardware Settings' hardware parameter set.
04. Continue with step 4 in the section below.
or
01. If applicable, place the cursor onto an existing.
02. Choose 'Copy' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The 'New Template' dialog opens (see ﬁgure above).
03. Enter the desired name and click the button OK.
The new hardware parameter set is inserted below 'Hardware Templates' alphabetically.
Parameter settings are inherited from the copied hardware parameter set.
04. For clear identiﬁcation, the 'Description' input ﬁeld should be ﬁlled with a meaningful name.
Note The new hardware parameter set is created so far.
To keep the new settings, these must be saved (see Saving the Hardware Settings).
After creating a new hardware parameter set, its individual parameters can now be modiﬁed (see Setting Hardware Parameters).

Renam ing a Hardware Param eter Set
The archived hardware parameter sets listed inside the 'Hardware Templates' node can be renamed regarding to your
requirements. To rename a hardware parameter set:
01. Select the desired hardware parameter set in the left ﬁeld of the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
02. Choose 'Rename' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The 'New Template' dialog opens.
03. Enter the desired name and click the button OK.
The name of the hardware parameter set will change.
Note The parameter set is renamed so far.
To keep the new settings, these must be saved (see Saving the Hardware Settings).

Deleting a Hardware Param eter Set 
If an archived hardware parameter set listed inside the 'Hardware Templates' node is not required any more, this can be deleted.
To delete a hardware parameter set:
01. Select the desired hardware parameter set in the left ﬁeld of the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
02. Choose 'Delete' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The hardware parameter set is deleted immediately without user information!
Note The parameter set is deleted so far.
To keep the new settings, these must be saved (see Saving the Hardware Settings).

Deﬁning the Active Hardware Param eter Set
If the laser processing system's conﬁguration changes, an appropriate hardware parameter set must be created (see above) or an
already existing appropriate hardware parameter set (template) must be applied. The suitable hardware parameter set must be
deﬁned as the active hardware parameter set as follows:
01. Select a desired hardware parameter set in the left ﬁeld of the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
02. Choose 'Use' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The selected hardware parameter set is applied as the active hardware parameter set ('Hardware Settings').
or
01. Place the cursor above the right column of the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
02. Choose 'Use' from the Context M enu (Right M ouse Button).
The 'Template' dialog opens.
03. Select the suitable hardware parameter set in the listbox.
04. Click the button OK.
The selected hardware parameter set is applied as the active hardware parameter set ('Hardware Settings').
Note The active parameter set is overwritten so far.
To keep the new settings, these must be saved (see Saving the Hardware Settings).
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Managing Hardware Parameter Sets

--- Page 719 ---

Selecting the Active Hardware Param eter Set tem porarily
Unlike all other hardware parameter settings (which can only be done in the Hardware Conﬁguration with administrator or
supervisor user rights) the active hardware parameter set can be selected by all users with at least production rights outside the
Hardware Conﬁguration.
Note Setting the active hardware parameter set outside the Hardware Conﬁguration will remain temporarily only. Closing
laserDESK, this setting is aborted. Restarting the program, the active hardware parameter set deﬁned in the Hardware
Conﬁguration is applied.
User switching with new log-in will not reset the temporary setting!
To deﬁne the active hardware parameter set outside the Hardware Conﬁguration:
01. Choose
‒ 'View Hardware Settings' in the M enu Hardware
‒ or Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens and the current activated hardware parameter set is shown.
02. Click the button 
  in the 'Template:' listbox.
The available templates (hardware conﬁguration sets) is shown in a drop-down-list.
03. Select the desired template.
The description of this hardware conﬁguration set is shown below.
04. Click the button OK.
The selected hardware conﬁguration set is applied as the active hardware parameter set temporarily (see note above).
To reset the active hardware parameter set deﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration:
01. Choose
‒ 'View Hardware Settings' in the M enu Hardware
‒ or Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens and the current activated hardware parameter set is shown.
02. Click the button 
  in the 'Template:' listbox.
A drop-down list be shown.
03. Select "[Restore default settings]".
The original default hardware parameter set deﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration is applied.

Setting the Param eters 'RTC Board'
This page is m ainly intended for inform ation.
In case of connection problem s of the controller to the RTC6 Ethernet board - the tim eout settings can be changed here.
Note To search and select an RTC board, choose M enu Hardware > RTC Board Selection.
Note U nder M enu Hardware > System  Inform ation > Scan System  > Ethernet Boards you can get further inform ation about
the RTC6 Ethernet boards. You can search for m ore boards in diﬀerent LAN areas.
This page shows inform ation about the acquired and all found RTC boards (RTC5 PCI, RTC5 PCIe, RTC6 PCIe, and RTC6 Ethernet):
Param eter Description
Selected Board Shows the selected board's designation (RTC5 or RTC6) and its internally assigned num ber.
Not editable.
Detected Boards If m ore than one board is available, the listbox lets you select one of several boards.
Properties shown (read-only) are: Assigned Num ber, Serial num ber.
W ith an RTC6 Ethernet board in addition: Firm ware version, M AC address, IP address(es) as
well as the states 'Standalone Full State' and 'Rem ote Interface active'.
Ethernet Param eter
Ethernet Tim eout Setting In case of connection problem s of the PC controller to an RTC6 Ethernet board you m ay
change the tim eout settings. Available entries:
‒ very short
‒ short (default setting)
‒ m edium
‒ long
‒ very long
Standalone Boot Delay [s] Is only used for RTC6 Ethernet boards in Standalone Full M ode.
Deﬁnes the waiting tim e in between the boot sequence from  boot phase 1 to boot phase 2. If
"0" is deﬁned, a start signal has to be used to proceed to boot phase 2 (see RTC6 M anual).
Activate Rem ote Interface See How to W ork with Rem ote Interface M ode:
If selected, RTC6 com m and eth_set_remote_tgm _form at is in the boot sequence.
If cleared, RTC6 com m and eth_set_remote_tgm _form at is not in the boot sequence.
To retrieve inform ation about the RTC boards:
01. If you have not already done so, choose M enu File > Open > Hardware Conﬁguration.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
02. Select 'RTC Boards' within the desired system  param eter set in the left ﬁeld of the dialog.
The selected board with its assigned num ber is shown on the right side of the dialog.
03. For inform ation about other boards, select a board in the 'Detected Boards' listbox.
The assigned num ber and the serial num ber of the selected board is displayed.
04. If desired, select further boards in the listbox to get inform ation for them .
In case of connection problem s, change the tim eout settings:
01. If you have not already done so, choose M enu File > Open > Hardware Conﬁguration.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
02. Select 'RTC Boards' within the desired system  param eter set in the left ﬁeld of the dialog.
The selected board with its assigned num ber is shown on the right side of the dialog.
03. Select another tim eout setting in the 'Ethernet Tim eout Setting' listbox.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'Hardware Devices'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareHWDevices.htm 2/6

--- Page 727 ---

Remote Control
The remote control is intended to enable a master control to command laserDESK  by the remote control interface. The purpose of thiscontrol is to execute and adjust the processing of laserDESK  jobs. It is not intended to create or modify graphic objects inside a job.
Note The remote control, and especially its parameters, telegram syntax and commands, are described in Remote ControlM anual.
Note When using the remote control, laserDESK  serves the client's queries and executes the desired actions. Thus:‒ the PC running laserDESK  is the server‒ and the customer program is the client.
The remote control of laserDESK  has two possible connections (see ﬁgure below) with the same telegram and data format:
Serial Connection:If you want to use a serial connection (this must be previously deﬁned in the 'PC Interfaces' Parameter), then select thecorresponding serial connection here.
TCP/IP Connection:If you use an Ethernet connection with TCP/IP protocol, the required server port must be deﬁned.
To set the remote control:
01. If you do not want to use a remote control, activate the 
  'None' radio button.  or
If you want to use a serial connection for remote control, activate the 
  'Serial Connection' radio button.The corresponding listbox is enabled.
02. Click the button 
  to select the required serial connection.
Note The serial connection must have been deﬁned before in the 'PC Interfaces' Parameters.
or
01. If you want to use an Ethernet connection with TCP/IP protocol for remote control, activate the 
  'TCP/IP Connection' radiobutton.The 'Server IP-Address' (PC running laserDESK ) is evaluated by the program.The corresponding 'Server Port' input ﬁeld is enabled.
02. Enter the required value of the server port.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'Hardware Devices'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareHWDevices.htm 3/6

--- Page 728 ---

M otor Control Axes
If you use motors for moving or rotating parts, the motor control axes and their parameters have to be deﬁned in the ﬁeld 'M otorControl Axes'.
With laserDESK  two types of motors can be controlled:
Fully supported motors from Aerotech (Ensemble), Faulhaber and ISEL, as well as stepper motors (using step and directionsignals).These motors can be selected directly, the necessary system-speciﬁc settings are already implemented in laserDESK .
A generic motor type associated with a general interface class that uses the command set deﬁned by SCANLAB. All motorfunctions can and are then executed by this interface.laserDESK  sends 'globalized' motor control commands to an interface DLL. This DLL then converts these motor speciﬁc andexecutes the corresponding action with the actually connected motor. This generalized interface can address many diﬀerentmotor types.
Note If you want to use a generic motor type, also refer to the description in How to Use the General M otor Control Interface.
All further parameters have to be adapted to the used axis. Following items have to be taken into account.
If the motor is controlled by a serial interface, the serial connection to be used must be deﬁned in the 'PC Interfaces' Parametersbefore deﬁning the parameters here.
The use of a generic motor type requires an additional DLL that executes the motor-speciﬁc commands and settings. This must beprogrammed by the system builder/user.
The parameters have to be in the valid range of the used axis.
The calibration factor can be deﬁned in any units [motor steps / user unit]. But it is recommended to use the same length unit asfor the graphics in laserDESK . laserDESK  commands the position (deﬁned in the motor control node properties) multiplied by thiscalibration factor to set the position in steps the motor has to move.Examples:1.) Position is 100 [mm], calibration factor is 200 [steps/mm]:       ‒ The position value must be set in the motor control node to 100;        ‒ Result: laserDESK  commands 20000 steps.2.) Position is 100 [mm], calibration factor is 2000 [steps/inch]:        ‒ The position value must be set in the motor control node to 100 / 25.4 = 3.937 [inch];        ‒ Result: laserDESK  commands 7874 steps.laserDESK  will always take the position value in user units of the calibration factor. Therefore, no explicit unit can be displayed in
the property grid. Correspondent to that, the speed has to be deﬁned in [user units/s] and the acceleration in [user units/s2].
A negative 'Calibration Factor' inverts the motion's direction.
When using motor control elements in the job (see 'Using an Axis in laserDESK'), the speed can be deﬁned independent from the'Speed' deﬁned here.
Selecting the checkbox 'Automatic Reference Run' enables the start of the reference run of the axis to be executed by theReference Run button in the status bar, by the menu command 'Hardware\Reference Run for Axis' or by the remote control.A full automatic reference run is not available for security concerns. Otherwise, a motor could move to an unintentional positionwhich can lead to damage.The reference run must be executed once manually before the axes can be moved.
For endless rotary motions the end switch can be disabled for the stepper motors controlled by the RTC by activating 'RotationAxis'.Only during a reference run the end switch input is activated. It is deactivated after the reference run to be able to do endlessrotations.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'Hardware Devices'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareHWDevices.htm 4/6

--- Page 729 ---

For the tiling calculation, an individual inversion of the axis motion can be deﬁned. To do this, the line 'Invert M arking Shift causedby Axis M otion:' below the table provides the two checkboxes 'X-Axis' and 'Y-Axis'.The setting is taken for the calculation of the tile function. Be aware that the "Online" tiling parameters are calculated and savedwith the actual settings and are used when the job is reloaded. A change of the axis inversion is only taken into account for the jobexecution, if the "Online" tiling parameters are recalculated. (To do so, open the 'Tiles' dialog and save it by clicking the buttonOK.)
Note Additional to this universal description, some axes/motors of diﬀerent manufacturers have special features or properties.If you use‒ Aerotech axes,‒ a Faulhaber motor,‒ an Isel controller,‒ a step motor by RTC5 board | RTC6 board or‒ a SCANLAB scan system with step motor,it is essential to read also Special Settings for M otor Control Axes!
To insert a new motor control axis in the table and to deﬁne its parameters, click the New M otor button and use the M otor SetupWizard.
To edit the parameters subsequently, each column/parameter can be selected in the table:
01. Assign a unique name in the column 'Name'.The axis is referenced by this name in laserDESK .
02. Click the button 
  in the column 'M otor Type' to open a list with all supported motor types.Select the desired motor type.
03. Click the button 
  in the Column 'Serial Connection' to select the serial connection of the PC to which the motor controller isconnected.The serial connection must have been deﬁned in the 'PC Interfaces' Parameters.If you use a stepper motor by RTC board, select "RTC5".
04. Deﬁne the calibration factor in the column 'Calibration Factor'.The calibration factor depends on the used axis, gear ratio and pitch. The unit [steps/user unit] is freely selectable, but werecommend using the same user unit as deﬁned in the window "Options" (see parameter 'Length' in Deﬁning the 'View'Parameters).
Note If you use Aerotechaxes , a step motor by RTC5 | RTC6 or a SCANLAB scan system with step motor, refer to SpecialSettings for M otor Control Axes!
05. Click the button 
  in the column 'Reference Run M ode' to open a list of available types of reference runs for the selectedcontroller.This selection is used if 'Reference Run' is selected in the motor control element's 'M otion Type' parameter (see GraphicParameters ‒ M otor Control).Select the desired reference run mode.
06. Enter the speed and reference run speed values in the corresponding columns.These are used when testing the motion controllers by the 'M otor Control' dialog.
07. Enter the acceleration value in the corresponding column.This parameter is only used if it is adjustable by the controller.
08. If desired, deﬁne an oﬀset in the column 'Origin Oﬀset'.The origin oﬀset allows the user to shift the axis reference (zero) to a deﬁned position (away from the reference switch).
09. Select or clear the 'X-Axis' or/and 'Y-Axis' checkbox in the line 'Invert M arking Shift caused by Axis M otion:'.‒ Checkbox 'X-Axis' activated: scan head moves; marking oﬀset is parallel to the x-axis of the scan head coordinate system.‒ Checkbox 'X-Axis' deactivated (default): material/workpiece moves; marking oﬀset is in opposite direction to the x-axis of thescan head coordinate system.‒ Checkbox 'Y-Axis' activated: scan head moves; marking oﬀset is parallel to the y-axis of the scan head coordinate system.‒ Checkbox 'Y-Axis' deactivated (default): material/workpiece moves; marking oﬀset is in opposite to the y-axis of the scan headcoordinate system.
10. If desired, activate the 'Automatic Reference Run'.
11. For endless rotary motions activate 'Rotation Axis'.
Note Normally every axis needs to perform a reference run before it can be positioned.The reference run can be executed by the 'M otor Control' dialog.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Defining a New Motor Control Axis
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareSetting_HardwareMotorWizard.htm 1/2

Deﬁning a New M otor Control Axis
The M otor Setup W izard will guide you through the steps to deﬁne a new m otor control setup.
W hen the 'M otor W izard' dialog opens:
01. Click Next to continue.
The 'M otor Type' page opens.
02. Select the desired m otor type.
‒ If you use a deﬁned m otor type with a serial interface (Faulhaber, Isel, Aerotech) you need to deﬁne this serial interface prior
to the m otor deﬁnition to be able to select it here (see Setting 'PC Interfaces' Param eters).
‒ If 'varioSCAN_ﬂex' is selected, som e settings are already predeﬁned.
03. Assign a unique nam e.
The axis is referenced by this nam e in laserDESK .
04. Click Next to continue.
The 'M otor Param eter' page opens.
05. Deﬁne the speciﬁc m otor settings (calibration factor, speed, reference run speed, and acceleration).
‒ The calibration factor depends on the used axis, gear ratio and pitch. The unit [steps/user unit] is freely selectable, but we
recom m end using the sam e user unit as deﬁned in the window "Options" (see param eter 'Length' in Deﬁning the 'View'
Param eters).
‒ The speed and reference run speed values are used when testing the m otion controllers by the 'M otor Control' dialog.
‒ The param eter Acceleration is only used if it is adjustable by the controller.
06. Click Next to continue.
The 'Initialization Settings' page opens.
07. Select the used connection of the PC to which the m otor controller is connected.
The serial connection m ust have been deﬁned in the 'PC Interfaces' Param eters.
If you use a stepper m otor by RTC board, select "RTC...".
08. Select the reference run type.
This selection is used if 'Reference Run' is selected in the m otor control elem ent's 'M otion Type' param eter (see G raphic
Param eters ‒ M otor Control).
09. Deﬁne a reference point oﬀset if required.
The oﬀset allows the user to shift the axis reference (zero) to a deﬁned position (away from  the reference switch).
10. Enable rem ote reference run start if desired.
11. Activate rotary axis use if endless rotary m otion is required.
12. Click Next to continue.
The 'Sum m ary' page opens.
13. Check the param eters and press Finish to term inate the procedure.
The dialog closes and the setup is written into the Hardware Conﬁguration.
You need to save the Hardware Conﬁguration to keep the settings perm anent (see Setting and Saving the Param eters on the
'Hardware Devices' page).

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Special Settings for Motor Control Axes
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareSpecialSettingsMotorControlAxes.htm 2/8

--- Page 735 ---

Aerotech Axes
W hen deﬁning the calibration factor in the colum n 'Calibration Factor', note:
The calibration factor for the Aerotech axes can also be set in the Aerotech Conﬁguration M anager (setup program
ConﬁgurationM anager.exe) under 'Axis ‒ U nits ‒ CountsPerU nit'.
During initialization, laserDESK sets the calibration factor deﬁned here in the Hardware Settings and overwrites the Aerotech
controller value unless it is 0 (zero).
To avoid m alfunction,
‒ the calibration factor should be set identically in the laserDESK Hardware Settings and the Aerotech Conﬁguration M anager
‒ or the calibration factor should be set to 0 (zero) in the laserDESK Hardware Setting. In this case the calibration factor stored
in the Aerotech controller is used and not changed.
The ASCII interface has to be activated in the Aerotech Conﬁguration M anager under 'System  ‒ Com m unication ‒ ASCII'.
The param eter 'Com m andSetup' has to be set to "0x00010001". This activates the ASCII interface on the prim ary RS232
interface and sets "Always send EOS".
Note The ASCII interface of the Aerotech controllers is not deﬁned consistently. Each controller type has a speciﬁc ASCII
interface, which is not com patible to another controller's ASCII interface.
laserDESK only supports the ASCII interface of the Ensemble controller!
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Special Settings for Motor Control Axes
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareSpecialSettingsMotorControlAxes.htm 3/8

--- Page 736 ---

Faulhaber M otor
laserDESK supports the following Faulhaber m odel types of brushless servo m otors with integrated controller:
Series 3564… B CS
Series 32xx… BX4 CS
Series 22xx… BX4 CSD
Note M aybe the m otors with external controllers (M CDC 300x RS, M CBL 300x RS) can be controlled too, because they use the
sam e com m and set. But that is not tested yet.
The controllers need a serial interface (RS 232). The CAN bus version is not supported.
Every m otor needs an own serial connection to the PC and uses the default axis num ber 0. The network m ode is not supported.
The m otors are controlled using the positioning m ode with 2 end switches. The end switch inputs and logic is deﬁned as
‒ Lower end switch ‒ Analog In (AnIn) input (wire colour brown)
‒ U pper end switch ‒ reconﬁgured Fault Pin (FAU LT, wire colour white)
The end switches are deﬁned as norm ally closed to avoid m otion if a wire is broken. The end switch logic where the m otion is
interrupted is active low. Because laserDESK deﬁnes the settings only tem porary and does not save them  perm anently we
recom m end to save the Fault Pin setting as input perm anently (com m ands REFIN and SAVE, see Faulhaber m anual).
The connection between Analog G ND (grey) and Power Supply G ND (blue) has to exist, else the serial com m unication will not
work!
The voltage level for the inputs should be set to 5 V but it is possible to use 24 V, too. The level can be deﬁned by the Faulhaber
com m ands SETTTL (5 V) or SETPLC (24 V). Refer to the Faulhaber m anual for these com m ands. This setting is not changed by
laserDESK and can therefore be set externally and should be set perm anent using the SAVE com m and.
Sum m ary
To setup the Faulhaber m otor we recom m end to enter and send following com m ands with the Faulhaber M otion M anager:
REFIN
SETTTL
SAVE
Connection drawing
(proposal; refer to the Faulhaber m anual, too)
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Special Settings for Motor Control Axes
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareSpecialSettingsMotorControlAxes.htm 4/8

--- Page 737 ---

The calibration factor has to be positive. Negative or zero values use the default factor of 3000 [steps/rotation] and 1 rotation
corresponds to 1 m m  m otion. An acceleration factor of 0 (or negative) uses the internal saved factor of the controller.
If the m otion is interrupted by an em ergency stop, the m otor m ay be get disabled. In that case you have to initialize the m otor
again in the m anual m otor control dialog and possibly switch oﬀ the Autom atic M ode ﬁrst.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'Processing Laser'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareLaser.htm 1/4

--- Page 742 ---

Setting the Param eters 'Processing Laser'
Note The 'Processing Laser' param eters can be edited only, if the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is open inside the 'Hardware
Conﬁguration'. To do this, the "Hardware Conﬁguration" m ust have been opened by 'Open > Hardware Conﬁguration'
in the M enu File.
If the Dialog "Hardware Settings" has been opened inside a job or the M arking Library by 'Edit Hardware Settings' in
the M enu M enu Hardware or by Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File, all param eters are read-only and the settings
cannot be changed.
The 'Processing Laser' param eters serve to deﬁne the correct conﬁguration of the laser in use. Additionally to the selection of the
used processing laser, it is useful to set param eters such as power lim it, shutter tim ing and standby settings.
laserDESK  supports several speciﬁc laser system s. These lasers can be com pletely controlled by the program . Therefore, no
additional equipm ent is required for switching the laser on or oﬀ or for m alfunction m onitoring.
For these lasers (for exam ple, FiberLaser_IPG , FOBA, G eneralCO2, SpectraPhysics_Hippo), the connection to the PC and the RTC5
board | RTC6 board is predeﬁned. Depending on the laser type, this is done by speciﬁc I/O pins or the serial interface. All
connections and speciﬁed param eter settings are deﬁned in an XM L ﬁle (sll ﬁle) for each laser type. The laserDESK  package
contains laser deﬁnition ﬁles for speciﬁed lasers that include a num ber of com m on laser types. To ensure that laserDESK  uses the
correct param eter settings, you m ust choose your laser type by the laser type listbox.
For unspeciﬁed lasers (so-called "G eneralType" and "G eneralPlusType" lasers), you m ust create an sll ﬁle and save it. (Here, it is
suﬃcient to establish the standard connections to the RTC board, see e RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual). The Laser W izard helps you
deﬁne these settings and create an adapted laser deﬁnition ﬁle GeneralType.sll. The sll ﬁle gets created in the
C:\Programs\Scanlab\LaserDesk\Libraries directory.
Processing Laser
Param eter Description
Laser Type Available entries:
‒ DPSS_Laser
‒ FOBA
‒ G eneralCO2 (active high)
‒ G eneralCO2 (active low)
‒ G eneralCO2 (enable_low)
‒ G eneralType
‒ IPG _FiberLaser_B [Type B and Type B2 interface]
‒ IPG _FiberLaser_D
‒ IPG _FiberLaser_E [Type E and Type EG  interface]
‒ IPG _FiberLaser_E_free_running [Type E interface]
‒ SpectraPhysics_Hippo
‒ SPI_FiberLaser_G 4_HS [HS and EP series]
‒ SPI_FiberLaser_G 4_RM
‒ SPI_FiberLaser_HS [Type G 3 interface]
‒ SPI_FiberLaser_RM  [Type G 3 interface]
‒ Trum pf_TruePulse_nano(EP)
‒ Trum pf_TruePulse_nano(HS)
‒ Trum pf_TruePulse_nano(RM )
Note
‒ If you have selected a laser type controlled by an RS232 connection, you m ust deﬁne and
assign a serial connection (see below).
‒ If you have selected "G eneral Type", you m ust create an adapted sll ﬁle (see inform ation
above).
‒ The 'IPG _FiberLaser_E_free_running' is nearly identical to the 'IPG _FiberLaser_E' but
uses Laser M ode 4 of the RTC instead of Laser M ode 5. This laser type should be selected if
bitm aps with execution m ode 'Vectorized' (see 'Bitm ap M ode' in the Bitm ap Param eters)
have to be executed. In that case frequency jum ps, which the laser can not establish
im m ediately, is avoided.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'Processing Laser'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareLaser.htm 2/4

--- Page 743 ---

Param eter Description
Calibration Factor Of Power [0-1] This value restricts the output power of the laser.
For analog power control, this factor deﬁnes the fraction of 10V.
For digital control, it is the fraction of 8 bits (= 256).
For CO2 lasers: The duty cycle (deﬁned by the pulse length) is restricted by that factor (0.5
is equal to 50% duty cycle as the m axim um  output power).
The value set here will aﬀect all other laser power settings (for exam ple, a value of 100%
deﬁned in the m arking param eters (see 'Laser Power (P) [%]' in the Laser Param eters) will
result in output of the value deﬁned here).
Power Change Delay [m s] Period of power change delay in [m s].
M axim um  value: "4294967295"
Shutter Open Delay [m s] Period of delay until shutter opens in [m s].
Shutter Close Delay [m s] Period of delay until shutter closes in [m s].
In Autom atic M ode:
Switch Shutter For Each M arking
If the checkbox is selected, the shutter is switched for each m arking.
Standby Power [%] Reduced power (in [%] of the m axim um  output power, deﬁned by the calibration factor of
power, see above) during standby.
Not used for CO2 lasers. There the standby power is deﬁned by the standby signal settings
in Laser W izard.
Delay To Standby [m s] Period of delay until laser switches to standby in [m s].
QSwitch Delay [µs] Period of delay between Laser-On and Laser 1 signal (see 'YAG  M odes' in RTC5 M anual |
RTC6 M anual)
M arking Resolution [m m ] Resolution of the calculation of vectors.
Points closer than this resolution are m erged to one point.
Serial Connection Lets you assign a serial connection.
Available entries: serial interfaces of the PC and "<None>".
Note
If the serial connection was not previously deﬁned in the 'PC Interfaces' Param eters, it will
not appear in the selection list. Serial connections used otherwise will also not appear.
Only the interface has to be labeled.
The 'PC Interfaces' param eters are not used for speciﬁed laser types. These values are
taken from  the laser deﬁnition ﬁle.

Setting the Param eters 'Optics'
Note The 'Optics' param eters can be edited only, if the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is open inside the 'Hardware
Conﬁguration'. To do this, the "Hardware Conﬁguration" m ust have been opened by 'Open > Hardware Conﬁguration'
in the M enu File.
If the Dialog "Hardware Settings" has been opened inside a job or the M arking Library by 'Edit Hardware Settings' in
the M enu M enu Hardware or by Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File, all param eters are read-only and the settings
cannot be changed.
The 'Optics' param eters serve to deﬁne the optical settings of the laser scan system .
This includes selecting the scan system  servo control (= "SCANahead System ", "Preprocessing System ", "Tracking Error System ",
refer to the G lossary in RTC6 M anual) with the corresponding RTC board settings, working ﬁeld and hom e position.
Param eter Description
Description/Lens This com m ent line serves for entering the nam e and technical speciﬁcations of the used
lens/objective.
M arking Area Size
X [m m ]:
Y [m m ]
W idth and height of the laser scan system  m arking area in x and y directions (in [m m ]).
laserDESK  work area dim ensions are adjusted with respect to these values.
Note
These values are only a hint for the user. They do not restrict the applied values.
Calculate The Calculate button helps you to calculate the m arking area size from  the values of the
selected correction ﬁle.
Dependent on the selected head (in the window "Laser Control") it calculates for
correction ﬁle of head 1 or head 2 (if only head 2 is selected, see param eter below).
Therefore, it is necessary to load the actual selected correction ﬁle at once, prior to the
closing of the dialog.
Note
If no RTC board is initialized, the calculation cannot be done and the button is invisible.
Hom e Position
X [m m ]:
Y [m m ]
This function is suitable for the following cases:
‒ Laser types, which cannot be com pletely switched oﬀ, m ust ﬁnd a stand-by or hom e
position (for exam ple, a beam  dum p) to avoid dam aging of the products to be m arked or
the production line.
‒ For the POF functionality these settings are used after every m arking to jum p to the next
m arking's start position.
If this checkbox is selected, the associated 'X [m m ]:' and 'Y [m m ]:' input ﬁelds are then
enabled to deﬁne the hom e (or start) position. And thus after every m arking process a
jum p to this position is executed.
Note
U sing the POF functionality without activating this checkbox, the laser spot will jum p to
the coordinate '0|0' autom atically.
Variable Polygon Delay If the checkbox is selected, the variable polygon delay m echanism  is applied. Then a
desired variable polygon delay ﬁle can be applied optional (see Creating the Variable
Polygon Delay Curve).
The 
  select button will launch a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open" for selecting the
variable polygon delay ﬁle.
If no ﬁle is selected and the checkbox is selected, the internal curve is used (see 'Polygon
Delay' in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).
Tim elag Com pensation [µs] This com pensates the various tracking errors of the XY axes and Z axis.
(Also refer to the set_tim elag_com pensation control com m and in the RTC6 M anual).
Enter the tim elag com pensation values into the 'Head 1' and 'Head 2' input ﬁeld. (Z-axis
system s are usually connected to the scan head 2 connector.)
Note
This param eter is only visible and applicable with an RTC6 board.
U se Second Scan Head If you want to use a second scan head, this checkbox m ust be activated.
Note

Param eter Description
Activating or deactivating the 'U se Second Scan Head' checkbox in the 'Scan Head [2]'
param eters, will activate/deactivate this checkbox too.
U se intelliW ELD II Scan Head If you want to use an intelliW ELD II scan system  with zoom  axis, this checkbox m ust be
activated. Then, the position of the zoom  axis can be set by the 'Scanlab intelliW ELD II
Control' dialog (see Setting the Zoom  Axis of the intelliW ELD II)
Note
The intelliW ELD II needs an additional zoom  correction ﬁle besides the standard 3D
correction ﬁle. This ﬁle has the nam e extension "_zoom " added to the ﬁlenam e xxx.ct5
of the corresponding 3D correction ﬁle.
Both ﬁles (xxx.ct5 and xxx_zoom.ct5) m ust be stored in the sam e directory and need to
be present to be loaded to the RTC board.
If the zoom  correction ﬁle is m issing, the intelliW ELD II support cannot be used.
SCANahead Control Support Only visible, if an RTC6 board is used.
SCANahead Control Support and System  with Trajectory Control are m utually exclusive.
This also applies in particular, if you are using 2 scan heads: M ake sure that both are of
type either "Pre-processing control" or "SCANahead control".
If this checkbox is selected, you can take full advantage of the SCANahead functionality.
Note‒ If selected, PoF M ode 2 for SCANahead system s is used autom atically. The W ait for
Encoder control node uses predicted encoder signals to synchronize list execution with
scan head position. This requires a quite constant speed.
‒ After the settings have been saved (see procedure below), laserDESK  checks
autom atically whether a SCANahead system  is connected or not. Accordingly, the output
window shows either "SCANahead system  detected." or "No SCANahead system
connected.".
Select Scan Head Type for
Sim ulation
Only enabled, if the checkbox SCANahead Control Support is selected.
Available entries: excelliSCAN 14, excelliSCAN 20, excelliSCAN 30. These scan heads have
SCANahead control.
Selecting an entry autom atically populates the values of Preview Tim e [µs], Speed Lim it
[bit/10 µs], Acceleration Lim it [bit/10 µs²] with a default value (stored in laserDESK ).
This selection is only eﬀective for sim ulations (that is, no scan head connected), for
exam ple, to obtain realistic sim ulated job execution tim es.
As soon as a SCANahead system  is connected its param eters are always used and the
settings under SCANahead Control Support are not relevant. This is checked prior to a job
execution.
Preview Tim e [µs] Field is read-only. Populated autom atically.
The shown value is either an actual value that has been read out from  a connected
SCANahead system  at job execution or, a default value (stored in laserDESK ) according to
the selected Select Scan Head Type for Sim ulation entry).
Speed Lim it [bit/10 µs] Like Preview Tim e [µs].
Acceleration Lim it [bit/10 µs²] Like Preview Tim e [µs].
System  with Trajectory Control Only visible, if an RTC6 board is used.
SCANahead Control Support and System  with Trajectory Control are m utually exclusive.
This also applies in particular, if you are using 2 scan heads: M ake sure that both are of
type either "Preprocessing System " or "SCANahead System ".
Select this checkbox with a "Preprocessing System " (= SCANLAB scan system  with
"Preprocessing Servo Control" = trajectory servo control = with Tracking Error as well as
with preprocessing).
Then laserDESK  autom atically shifts all laser signal output by the ControlPreview Tim e
value.
As for the delays, you then only need to take into account the tracking error value which is
speciﬁed in the "Preprocessing System " m anual.
ControlPreview Tim e [µs] Default: 70.
To set the 'Optics' param eters:

Setting the Param eters 'Scan Head [1|2]'
Note The 'Scan Head' param eters can be edited only, if the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is open inside the 'Hardware
Conﬁguration'. To do this, the "Hardware Conﬁguration" m ust have been opened by 'Open > Hardware Conﬁguration'
in the M enu File.
If the Dialog "Hardware Settings" has been opened inside a job or the M arking Library by 'Edit Hardware Settings' in
the M enu M enu Hardware or by Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File, all param eters are read-only and the settings
cannot be changed.
The 'Scan Head [1]' and 'Scan Head [2]' param eters let you deﬁne settings for the scan head adjustm ent (oﬀset, rotation angle)
and optical settings (correction ﬁle, calibration factor). If in addition to the processing laser a pilot laser is used, diﬀerent
correction ﬁles and calibration factors can be deﬁned respectively.
Param eter Description
Laser
   Correction File The button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open" to select the required RTC
correction ﬁle ([FileName].ct5).
Note
‒ SCANLAB suggests to copy the RTC correction ﬁle corresponding to your laser scan
system  to C:\Programs\SCANLAB\LaserDesk\RTC5.
‒ Correction ﬁles for older RTC boards ([FileName].ctb) are not supported.
‒ As long as "<default>" is indicated, a user input is expected.
‒ To support an intelliWELD II (see Setting the Position of the intelliW ELD II's Zoom  Axis),
an additional zoom  correction ﬁle besides the (standard) RTC correction ﬁle is necessary.
This zoom  correction ﬁle m ust be stored in the sam e directory as the standard correction
ﬁle selected here.
   Δ Calibration Factor
      X [‰]:
      Y [‰]:
      Z [‰]:
The values for x, y and z directions are the deviation (in [‰]) of the correction ﬁle
calibration factors. W hen you use the Calibration Factor W izard to deﬁne calibration
factors, the determ ined calibration factor values are entered autom atically in these ﬁelds.
If you evaluate the calibration by another m ethod, you m ay enter the values by yourself.
Oﬀset
   X [m m ]:
   Y [m m ]:
The values deﬁne the shift of the scan head coordinate system  in x, y and z directions in
[m m ].
Their intended use is to adapt the scan head coordinate system  to the m achine setup
(equal origin).
Coordinate System  Rotation - M irroring
   α [deg] The value deﬁnes the rotation of the scan head coordinate system  (ccw) (in [deg]).
Its intended use is to align the coordinate axis of the scan head to the m achine setup
(parallel axis).
Note
This setting is not visible in the work area. The deﬁned rotation will only be applied during
job execution.
   Flip X-Coordinate
   Flip Y-Coordinate
By selecting one or both checkboxes the scan head coordinate system  is m irrored to the
y-axis or/and x-axis.
This setting is useful when using an additional deﬂection m irror or when processing
transparent m aterials.
Note
This setting is not visible in the work area. The deﬁned m irroring will only be applied
during job execution.
Pilot Laser
   Correction File If a pilot laser is used, activate the checkbox to enable the selection of a correction ﬁle.
The button 
  opens a M S W indows-standard dialog "Open" to select the required RTC
correction ﬁle ([FileName].ct5).
Note
The RTC correction ﬁle corresponding to your laser scan system  should be copied before

   Tem perature

Lets you enable scan head m onitoring, separate for tem perature and power.

The "Scan-System -Error-Induced Laser-Signal Auto-Suppression" function of the RTC5
board | RTC6 board is activated (see RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual) and laserDESK  will
evaluate these errors. In case of an error, laserDESK  stops the job execution and switches
oﬀ the Autom atic M ode.
Note
Not every hardware (scan system ) supports these functions. Refer to the m anual of your
scan system  to ﬁgure out whether it supports the corresponding m onitoring functions. In

explanation, refer to Chapter "TrAck Signal (Trajectory Acknowledge)" in the associated

oﬀ the Autom atic M ode.
Note

scan system  to ﬁgure out whether it supports one or both m onitoring functions. In case of
doubt, check your laser scan system .
U se Second Scan Head This checkbox is only present for the 'Scan Head [2]' param eters. It m ust be activated to
let you deﬁne the settings.

param eters, will activate/deactivate this checkbox too.
‒ W hen activating this checkbox, the 'Head' listbox in the Laser Control window is set
autom atically to "1+2". If you want to use initially only one scan head, you have to select
that scan head in the 'Head' listbox.
‒ To avoid nonconform ance when processing a list with 2 scan heads, refer to How to U se
a Second Scan Head.
‒ W hen activating this checkbox, the Scan Head M onitoring checkboxes (see above) can
be activated; when deactivating this checkbox, the varioSCAN M onitoring checkboxes
(see above) can be activated.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'PC Interfaces'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwarePCInterfaces.htm 1/3

Setting the Param eters 'PC Interfaces'
Note 'PC Interfaces' param eters can be edited only, if the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is open inside 'Hardware
Conﬁguration'. To do this, the "Hardware Conﬁguration" m ust have been opened by M enu File > Open > Hardware
Conﬁguration.
If the Dialog "Hardware Settings" has been opened inside a job or the M arking Library by M enu Hardware > Edit
Hardware Settings or by Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File, all param eters are read-only and the settings cannot
be changed.
The PC 'Interfaces' param eters serve to deﬁne one or several PC interface connections for data transfer. Each connection is deﬁned
by several param eters (port, baud rate, parity and data bits) in one line of a table (see ﬁgure below). New connections can be
created in an (always available) blank line, existent connections can be edited.
Reference:
Colum n Description
Nam e Input ﬁeld for entering the nam e - m axim um  20 characters.
W hen a serial connection m ust be deﬁned inside the hardware conﬁguration, the nam e serves as reference (as an
exam ple, see "RCtrl" in the 'Serial Connection' listbox within the 'Hardware Devices' Param eters').
Port Lets you select the desired port.
Available entries are the available ports such as COM 1 etc.
Baud Rate Lets you select the desired baud rate.
Available entries: 4800, 9600, 19200, 38400, 57600, 115200, 128000, 256000 [Bd].
Parity Lets you deﬁne the used parity of the serial connection.
Available entries: Even, Odd, None.
Data Bits Lets you deﬁne the data bits of the serial connection.
Available entries: 5, 6, 7, 8.
Stop Bits Lets you deﬁne the stop bits of the serial connection.
Available entries: 1, 1.5, 2.
U sage This ﬁeld is not editable. It is set by laserDESK , when the deﬁned connection is used by a hardware device.
To set the 'PC Interfaces' param eters:
01. If you have not already done so, choose M enu File > Open > Hardware Conﬁguration.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'PC Interfaces'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwarePCInterfaces.htm 2/3

--- Page 755 ---

02. Select 'PC Interfaces' within the desired system  param eter set in the left ﬁeld of the dialog.
The respective param eters are shown in a table on the right side of the dialog.
03. To deﬁne a new connection, click the last line and then
‒ Enter the nam e.
‒ Deﬁne the various param eters in the listboxes.
04. To m odify existing settings, select the param eters and edit the appropriate ﬁelds.
05. Select another param eter inside the desired system  param eter set for m odiﬁcation.
   or
Save the settings as described in Saving the Hardware Settings.

Setting the Param eters 'Processing-On-The-Fly'
Note The 'Processing-On-The-Fly' param eters can be edited only, if the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is open inside
the 'Hardware Conﬁguration'. To do this, it m ust have been opened by M enu File > Open > Hardware
Conﬁguration.
Note All param eters are read-only and the settings cannot be changed, if:
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" has been opened inside a job or the M arking Library by 'Edit Hardware
Settings' in the M enu Hardware or by Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File
If your production line is used with the RTC5 board | RTC6 board's POF option enabled, the 'Processing-On-The-ﬂy' (POF)
param eters serve to deﬁne the encoder (X, Y, XY directions or rotation) used for the POF m echanism .
Note W hen using a transportation system  with the RTC5 board | RTC6 board's POF option enabled, exact calibration factors
for the Processing-On-The-Fly m echanism  m ust be determ ined.
To do so, see Calibrating the Processing-On-the-Fly M echanism .
Param eter Description
POF-Type Type of m otion (directional or rotating).
Available entries: X, Y, XY, Rotation.
Calibration Factors W hen you use the Processing-On-The-Fly Calibration W izard to deﬁne calibration factors, the
determ ined calibration factor value is entered autom atically in one of the ﬁelds below.
X [increm ents/m m ] Deﬁnes the num ber of encoder increm ents
‒ per 1 m m  m aterial ﬂow in x or y or x and y directions or
‒ per 1 deg m aterial ﬂow in rotation.
For a gear ratio of 1 (m m /rotation), this is the encoder resolution in [increm ents / rotation].
The ﬁelds are enabled or disabled depending on the selection of the 'POF-Type' param eter (see
above).
Y [increm ents/m m ]
Rotation [increm ents/deg]
Rotation Center The input ﬁelds below m ust only be edited when using a rotating transportation system .
X [m m ] Deﬁnes the position (x, y coordinates) of the transport system 's rotation center in relation to the
laser scan system .
Enter the values in the input ﬁelds.
The ﬁelds are enabled only if "Rotation" is selected (see 'POF-Type' param eter above).
Y [m m ]
Tracking Error Com pensation
[µs]
In Processing-on-the-Fly applications the encoder value is added to the target position. Because
the scan head will only arrive at that position after his tracking error delay, the encoder value is
not correct.
To com pensate the additional m otion, the encoder value, present when the scan head really
arrives at that position, can be pre-calculated. The tim e set here is used to calculate the
additional encoder signals (see RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).
Note
This feature should only be used for m ainly constant m otions. If this is not the case, the value
should be set to "0".
Deactivate Encoder Prediction Only availble, if checkbox SCANahead Control Support is selected.
Checkbox is cleared: The RTC6-internally pre-calculated workpiece position refers to the
location that the workpiece has reached after PreviewTim e.
Checkbox is selected: The RTC6-internally pre-calculated workpiece position is switched oﬀ
(this functionality corresponds to RTC6 com m and call fly_prediction(0,0)).
M ake sure that the encoder signals sent correspond to the workpiece position that the
workpiece will have after the PreviewTim e.
Sim ulate Encoder If your transportation system  is not equipped with an encoder and runs with constant speed,
you can activate this checkbox to sim ulate an encoder.
The RTC board then provides a periodic 1 M Hz clock signal which feeds the internal encoder

--- Page 758 ---

counter(s). These is increm ented at this clock rate which sim ulates a transportation system  with
constant speed.
Note
If activated, the signals of an attached increm ental encoder is ignored.
Check M arking Area Lim its Lets you check the m arking area lim its. Encoder failures are also be checked when indicated
(m essage in the output window: "Encoder or Out-Of-Range error during POF execution"), the
m arking execution is canceled.
‒ Selected checkbox: the deﬁned m arking area and the encoder (failures) is checked.
‒ Cleared checkbox: the m axim um  ﬁeld lim its of the RTC board is checked, but no encoder
errors.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Setting the Parameters 'Miscellaneous'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareSetting_HardwareMiscellaneous.htm 1/3

--- Page 760 ---

Setting the Param eters 'M iscellaneous'
Note The 'M iscellaneous' param eters can be edited only, if the Dialog "Hardware Settings" is open inside the 'Hardware
Conﬁguration'. To do this, the "Hardware Conﬁguration" m ust have been opened by 'Open > Hardware Conﬁguration'
in the M enu File.
If the Dialog "Hardware Settings" has been opened inside a job or the M arking Library by 'Edit Hardware Settings' in
the M enu M enu Hardware or by Hardware Settings in the Toolbar File, all param eters are read-only and the settings
cannot be changed.
Param eter Description
IO-port initialization
Bit 7 ... 0 Lets you deﬁne a bit pattern for setting the output pins (see control elem ent Digital
Output), which is applied when switching to Autom atic M ode. This enables you to
deﬁne the initial state of a signal exchange.
Apply Initialization If you do not want the output lines/pins to be initialized when switching to Autom atic
M ode, the 'Apply Initialization' checkbox can be cleared.
Autom atic M ode
No break for undetected end of list
execution
If the executed job has no variable data and needs no list reloading and no part count
checking, there is the possibility to realize very short dead tim es between execution
starts.
For this purpose, this checkbox can be selected optionally. In this case, the Autom atic
M ode is not switched oﬀ, if consecutive starts are executed without proper job end
detection.
Note
The problem  here is, that the status update is not executed prior to the next start. This
can cause wrong variable data processing, wrong part count checking wrong list
reloading and other side eﬀects.
Enable activation by IO signals For activation of the Autom atic M ode and switching the laser by a PLC without using
the Rem ote Control interface, two IO lines can be used:
‒ DIGITAL IN 12 for Autom atic M ode,
‒ DIGITAL IN 13 for Laser Switching (only fully supported lasers).
    (Pins 26 and 28 at RTC Connector EXTENSION 1)
For this purpose, this checkbox can be selected optionally.
The IO lines are edge-sensitive, that is, a rising edge will switch on, a falling edge will
switch oﬀ.
Note
If for som e reason the action was not perform ed, you need to reset the line and set it
again to create the appropriate edge.
PC Perform ance
Save CPU  usage To reduce the CPU  usage of the program  and to give other program s m ore access of the
CPU  tim e, the RTC controlling thread can be interrupted brieﬂy during a job execution.
For this purpose, this checkbox can be selected optionally.
Note
For tim e-critical job execution, it should be oﬀ to avoid latency eﬀects.
M easurem ent
M easure total execution tim e If this checkbox is selected, the total execution including external handling such as
m otor m otions is m easured during the execution tim e evaluation.
If this checkbox is not activated, only the list execution tim e of the RTC is m easured
(that is, the real laser processing tim e). If the list execution is interrupted by external
handling, the tim e m easurem ent is also paused. (This m easurem ent is m ore accurate
than the total execution tim e.)
In both cases the m easured tim e is displayed in the center of the status bar.
Data Stream ing

Param eter Description
Activate Data Stream ing See How to Activate Data Stream ing by laserDESK
Im portant: For Data Stream ing you need to have protocol nodes in the job (= control
node 'Protocol start' and optionally control node 'Protocol end'), see How to U se
Logging Possibilities.
Package Size Available entries: 256...32768. Default: 256.
To set the 'M iscellaneous' param eters:
01. If you have not already done so, choose Open > Hardware Conﬁguration' from  the M enu File.
The Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens'.
02. Select 'M iscellaneous' within the desired system  param eter set in the left ﬁeld of the dialog.
The respective param eters are shown on the right side of the dialog.
03. Deﬁne the param eters just by activating or deactivating the individual radio buttons and the checkboxes.
04. Select another param eter within the desired system  param eter set.
   or
Save the settings as described in Saving the Hardware Settings.

Laser Wizard
Note For general inform ation about Laser W izard, see How to Control a Laser System  with Laser W izard.
Note The Laser W izard is used to create and m aintain laser deﬁnition ﬁles *.sll, see also About Laser Deﬁnition Files.
Always run the laser wizard page-by-page (although you can jum p directly to the pages using the navigation tree on
the left). The settings build on each other. That's why a change on one page usually discards the settings on
subsequent pages. For exam ple, as soon as you delete a status signal, its status deﬁnitions no longer m ake sense and
are therefore deleted as well.
Overview
Side Purpose
Laser W izard Page 01-10 W elcom e Page To deﬁne (load) a new (existing) laser deﬁnition ﬁle *.sll
Laser W izard Page 02-10 Laser Control To specify the RTC board settings for the laser system  to be used
Laser W izard Page 03-10 Laser Value Ranges To set the laser range lim its. These are initially set when the laser is
started and m onitored during job execution
Laser W izard Page 04-10 Signal Lines Speciﬁes signal nam es along with the RTC card signal line to be
used. On the following pages, only the signal lines deﬁned here can
be used!
Laser W izard Page 05-10 Signal Assignm ent To assign a function to each signal line (the signal lines deﬁned on
Laser W izard Page 04-10 Signal Lines)
Laser W izard Page 06-10 Initialisation Settings To initialise output lines during hardware initialization
Laser W izard Page 07-10 Laser Status To deﬁne the laser system  states and assign them  to status lines
Laser W izard Page 08-10 Serial Control To set serial com m ands (if the laser system  to be used requires them )
Laser W izard Page 09-10 Laser Switching To specify the switch-on and switch-oﬀ operations of the laser
system
Laser W izard Page 10-10 Result To view the settings you have m ade. Save as laser deﬁnition ﬁle
*.sll
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Laser Wizard
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_LaserWizard.htm 2/15

--- Page 764 ---

laserDESK  uses the RTC interfaces and the serial PC interfaces in the following categories to control peripherals:
01. Program  Status - This is set autom atically and users cannot change the connections
02. Control via laserDESK  G U I functions - users can specify these, such as turning on the laser
03. Functions during job execution - users can set these, such as m aking variant selection
In laserDESK , the jobs are independent of the hardware conﬁguration. For this reason, and to avoid
m ultiple use, the interface areas of (2) and (3) are separated and cannot be m ixed.
The connections of area (2) are deﬁned in the Laser W izard. The connections of area (3) are deﬁned in the individual job elem ents.
For this reason, not all connections are available in the Laser W izard.
The only exception are the serial interfaces, which are deﬁned for speciﬁc devices (for exam ple, laser interface) and can be used in
(2) and (3).

Laser Wizard Page 03-10 Laser Value Ranges
Laser W izard Page 03-10
Purpose: To set the laser range lim its. These are initially set when the laser is started and m onitored during job execution.
Listbox 'Pulse M ode': Control m ode of the laser. Available entries are: 'Pulse and cw', 'Only pulses', 'Only cw'. cw = continuous
wave. This setting is checked at job execution.
In laserDESK , jobs are created independently of hardware. It is only during job execution that laserDESK  checks whether the
current hardware can execute the job and
whether the range lim its deﬁned here can be m et.
If necessary, laserDESK  adjusts the job values to the lim it values and issues a warning.
Furtherm ore, in the laser control node, deviating values van be deﬁned to be applied in m arking process, see G raphic Param eters
‒ Laser Control.

Laser Wizard Page 05-10 Signal Assignm ent
Laser W izard Page 05-10
Prerequisite: The page is only shown, if the 'Checkbox 'U se IO Control Lines' is selected.
Purpose: To assign a function to each signal line (the signal lines deﬁned on Laser W izard Page 04-10 Signal Lines).
Depending on the assignm ent, the line of laserDESK  is used for special functions. Som e of these features are not laser type-
speciﬁc.
The following applies to the input signals:
U se Function
Laser State All signal lines with this function are com bined to create a state of
the laser system . The 'Laser Status' page deﬁnes these states.
Autom atic m ode on/oﬀ This input can be used to switch the autom atic m ode. High = on. The
function corresponds to the autom atic m ode button in the
laserDESK  G U I.
Laser on/oﬀ The laser can be switched via this input. High = on. The function
corresponds to the Laser button in the laserDESK  G U I.
AU X This input line can be used for a power-on/oﬀ-operation (Laser
W izard Page 09-10 Laser Switching).
Note Laser State can be assigned to m ultiple (n) lines.
One (1) line is required to switch the autom atic m ode.
One (1) line is required to switch the laser.
AU X can be assigned to m ultiple (n) lines.
Autom atic m ode on/oﬀ and Laser on/oﬀ functions m ust be activated in the hardware conﬁguration.
The following applies to the output signals:
U se Function
Laser Activation This line is used when the laser is switched on/oﬀ.
Relevant for Laser W izard Page 06-10 Initialisation Settings.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Laser Wizard
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_LaserWizard.htm 10/15

--- Page 772 ---

Laser Wizard Page 07-10 Laser Status
Laser W izard Page 07-10
Prerequisite: The page is only shown, if the 'Checkbox 'U se IO Control Lines' is selected.
Purpose: To deﬁne the laser system  states and assign them  to status lines.
Each com bination of signal lines corresponds to its own state.
The m eaning determ ines the reaction of laserDESK :
M eaning Action
OK The laser system  is OK , but not turned on.
No job can be executed.
On The laser system  is switched on.
A job can be executed.
W arning The laser system  is functional, but in critical condition.
The deﬁned status m essage is displayed.
However, it is still possible to carry out the job.
Error The laser system  has a fault. If a job is executed, it is aborted, the
laser is switched oﬀ and the deﬁned status m essage is displayed. The
error will be reset the next tim e you turn it on in laserDESK  and the
status will be checked again.
Back to Overview.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Laser Wizard
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_LaserWizard.htm 11/15

--- Page 773 ---

Laser Wizard Page 08-10 Serial Control
Laser W izard Page 08-10
Prerequisite: The page is only shown, if the Checkbox 'U se Serial Connection' is selected.
Purpose: To set serial com m ands (if the laser system  to be used requires them )
Serial Com m and Function
Laser Status Querying the status of the laser
Laser One Status Query whether the laser is ready-for-m ission and a job can be executed.
Open Shutter Com m and to open the shutter
Close Shutter Com m and to close the shutter
Shutter Status Querying Shutter Status
The 'U se Com m and' checkbox and the 'Error Handling' checkbox can be used to specify whether the serial com m and should be
used and
whether it involves error handling.
Checkbox 'U se Com m and' is selected: The serial com m and takes precedence over an IO action.
If the 'Error handling' checkbox is selected at the sam e tim e: The properties 'W ait for Response', 'Response on Success' and
'Response on Failure' are evaluated (analogous to the serial control nodes).
'W aiting for response' = 'No' => response is not evaluated (but a response m ust com e)
'W ait for response' = 'Yes' => answer is evaluated. If no response is received, the function has failed (com m unication m ay be
interrupted).
'Response on success' is an em pty string => response is not evaluated.
'Response on error' is an em pty string => response is not evaluated.
Otherwise, the response is evaluated according to the following schem e:
01. If 'Response on Success' is deﬁned and the response contains the deﬁned text, the function is successfully executed.
Otherwise, an error will be detected. If 'Response on Success' is an em pty string, (2) is executed.
02. If 'Response on Error' is deﬁned and the response contains the deﬁned text, the function has failed. Otherwise, it will be
considered successful.
If there are several possible answers, an em pty string "" allows the acceptance of all answers without error evaluation.
Note The serial interface used is deﬁned in the hardware conﬁguration > processing laser > list box 'Serial connection'.

Laser Wizard Page 09-10 Laser Switching
Laser W izard Page 09-10
Prerequisite: The page is only shown, if the 'U se Checkbox 'U se IO Control Lines' and/or the 'Checkbox 'U se Serial Connection' is
selected.
Purpose: To specify the switch-on and switch-oﬀ operations of the laser system  (triggered by the Laser button, the rem ote
com m and, or the laser switching signal).
It can:
Deﬁned laser signals (signal out) can be output
W aiting for Input Signals (Signal In)
Serial com m ands (Serial Connection) are sent
M aintain for a deﬁned period of tim e (delay)
The order in the G U I and the sequence of execution correspond to each other.
The W indow "Properties" sets the param eters.
In the case of IO signals, only a deﬁned signal line or a speciﬁc laser status can be selected.
In a serial com m and, the serial port of the laser is always used. It is therefore suﬃcient to set the sending/receiving dates.
To insert an action into the switching sequence, drag and drop the corresponding button into the desired list box.
To delete an action, select it and press the 'Rem ove' button.
CAU TION!
W hen laserDESK  is started, all output signal lines are set to 0 V (low).
W hen closing laserDESK  or releasing the RTC, all lines are also reset to 0 V.
Therefore, m ake sure that the laser is connected in such a way that resetting the signals does not generate laser
power!

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 About Laser Definition Files
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_Laser_Definition_Files.htm 1/2

--- Page 778 ---

About Laser Deﬁnition Files
In laserDESK, each processing laser requires a dedicated laser deﬁnition ﬁle (see also Setting the Param eters 'Processing
Laser')
Is needed for RTC board initialization
File extension *.sll
XM L form at (laserDESK ≥V1.6.19: with <ApplicationVersion> tag)
Contains param eter values to adapt RTC output signals to this very laser system
File location C:\Program Data\Scanlab\SLlaserDESK\Laserdeﬁnitions
After laserDESK installation, there are a set of laser deﬁnition ﬁles for a num ber of com m on laser types
These can be changed by the Laser Deﬁnition W izard (laserDESK ≥V1.6.19).
Im portant: This renders the supplied wiring diagram , see Connecting Lasers to RTC Boards, invalid!
These are used as entries in listbox 'Laser Type' (Dialog "Hardware Settings" > 'Hardware Settings' > 'Processing Laser')
Note After installation, the laser deﬁnition ﬁles are copied to C:\Program Data\Scanlab\SLlaserDESK\Laserdeﬁnitions if they
are not present yet. (For this reason, the adm inistrator m ust have write perm ission for the corresponding directory -
see Installing laserDESK). The laser deﬁnition ﬁles are not changed by a new installation. W hen you create a new laser
deﬁnition ﬁle, the existing GeneralType.sll laser deﬁnition ﬁle autom atically gets overwritten and the new ﬁle will
persist and used even after a new installation.
If you want to save the existing laser deﬁnition ﬁle, then renam e or copy it before creating the new laser deﬁnition ﬁle.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Hardware Control
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_HardwareControl_Overview.htm 1/3

--- Page 780 ---

Hardware Control
M enu Hardware lets perform  several tasks for controlling and testing the hardware, such as setting laser control param eters,
calibrating the scan system , checking signal transfer or querying system  inform ation. Independently from  jobs, alm ost all
hardware control functions can be executed alongside an opened job, the 'M arking Library' or "Hardware Conﬁguration".
Note "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" vs. "Laser of Type 'G eneral'"
A "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" needs an additional special wiring to the RTC5 board | RTC6 board (see
Proposals for Connecting Lasers in Connecting Lasers and RTC Boards).
A "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" m ust be switched on in the window "Laser Control" in the Production Proﬁle
before starting any tasks.
W ith a "Laser of Type 'G eneral'", laserDESK  and the RTC control only the laser beam  synchronized with the scan
head positioning.
W ith a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'", the laser system  itself is switched on and is error checked. In case of an
error, job execution is canceled, all signals to switch the laser beam  on are switched oﬀ and in the W indow Output
an error m essage is displayed.
Norm ally, the security m echanism  of the laser system  will switch oﬀ the laser. Switching the laser system  on again
resets the error, if it is not present anym ore.
See also:
Direct M anual Control of the Laser System  and Scan Head
Laser W izard
Determ ining the Scan System  Calibration Factor
M anually Restarting the Laser
Testing the Signal Transfer by the RTC IOs
Testing the M otor Controllers
Setting the Position of the intelliW ELD II Zoom  Axis
Calibrating the Processing-on-the-Fly M echanism
Creating the Variable Polygon Delay Calibration Curve
Evaluating the Scan Head Tracking Error
Evaluating the Best Param eter Set
Viewing System  Inform ation - Dialog "Inform ation"
Viewing System  Inform ation - Dialog "Runtim e Inform ation"
Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System  ‒ in Dem o M ode
Releasing/Regaining the RTC Connection
Initializing the Rem ote Control
Reference Run for all Deﬁned Axes
Selecting an RTC Board

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Direct Manual Control of the Laser System and Scan Head
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_ManualLaserControl.htm 1/3

Direct M anual Control of the Laser System  and Scan Head
Dialog "M anual Laser Control" lets you test the laser or scan head. Independently of m arking procedures, you can switch the laser
on. This allows you to test the perform ance of your laser or scan head during m aintenance work or com pare diﬀerent laser
param eter settings, etc.
WARNING !
During testing the harm ful laser radiation can be em itted, particularly with an opened shutter and a dem ounted scan
system .
This presents a risk of injury when the laser radiation contacts parts of the body.
Therefore, all relevant laser safety regulations m ust be known and adhered to before testing the laser (see Laser
Safety).
Dialog "M anual Laser Control"
Controls Description
Laser Switches on the laser beam  for the duration deﬁned in 'Switch on tim e [m s]' (see above).
If the laser is switched on, it can be switched oﬀ by clicking the button again.
The operating state (on/oﬀ) is indicated.
Note
W ith a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'", it has to be switched on by the laser button prior
to open the m anual laser control.
Shutter Opens or closes the shutter (if this function is supported by the laser system ).
The shutter position (opened/closed) is indicated.
WARNING !
W ith an opened shutter, laser radiation can be em itted! See safety note above.
Laser Param eter
   Power Deﬁnes the laser power (in [%]).
   Frequency [kHz] Deﬁnes the laser frequency (in [kHz]).
   Pulse W idth [µs] Deﬁnes the pulse width (in [µs]).
For CO2 lasers the power will deﬁne this value and it cannot be edited directly.
   Switch On Tim e [m s] Deﬁnes the tim e (in [m s]) the laser is switched on.
If set to the value "-1" the laser will rem ain continuously on until switched oﬀ.
Beam  Position [m m ] x/y Deﬁnes the beam  position within the work area in the x and y directions (in [m m ]).
G o Sets the scan system 's m irrors to the deﬁned x/y beam  position (see above)
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Direct Manual Control of the Laser System and Scan Head
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_ManualLaserControl.htm 2/3

--- Page 784 ---

Controls Description
Z Position [m m ] Deﬁnes the focus position in the z direction (in [m m ]) by adjusting the varioSCAN.
Only available, if Option "3D" is enabled on the RTC5 board | RTC6 board.
G o Sets the scan system 's focusing lens to the deﬁned z position (see above)
To test the laser:
01. Choose 'Laser Control > Laser System ' from  the M enu Hardware.
The Dialog "M anual Laser Control" opens.
02. Enter the laser param eter's values.
03. Enter the x and y values for the beam  position.
Click the G o button to set the scan system  to the deﬁned beam  position
04. If available, enter the z value for the z position.
Click the G o button to set the scan system  to the deﬁned z position.
05. Click the Shutter button to open or close the shutter.
The shutter position indicator will change accordingly.
06. Click the Laser button to switch on the laser.
The deﬁned laser param eters are applied.
The operating state indicator displays a beam  and the laser is switched on as long as deﬁned in the 'Laser param eter'.
To abort running the laser, click the Laser button again.
07. If necessary, repeat the procedure with changed param eters.
08. W hen you have ﬁnished testing, click the X button to close the Dialog "M anual Laser Control".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Determining the Scan System Calibration Factor
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_CalibrationFactorWizard.htm 1/3

--- Page 786 ---

Determ ining the Scan System  Calibration Factor
To determine exact calibration factors for the work area, a calibration must be performed. This calibration is executed within the
'Calibration Factor Wizard' dialog. The calibration comprises a test marking, measurement of the marking and input of the test
results. The scan system will then calculate and apply the determined calibration factors.
Note This Calibration Factor Wizard leads you through a short calibration. The calculated calibration factors are the result of
a linear scaling.
If you want a more precise but also a more complex calibration, you should use the 3D-Calibration Wizard (see How to
Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System).
Note For calibrating the system the correction ﬁle's values should be used. Therefore, the 'use pure correction ﬁle factors'
checkbox should be activated.
If the checkbox is not activated, the current values from the "Hardware Conﬁguration" (see 'Δ Calibration Factor X/Y
[‰]' in 'Optics' parameters) are applied for the calibration. That's ﬁne if you want to check the values.
Note You can start the scan system's calibration factor routine only if "Hardware Conﬁguration" has already been opened.
To calibrate the scan system:
01. Choose M enu File > "Open" > "Hardware Conﬁguration".
"Hardware Conﬁguration" opens and the Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
02. Click the button OK to close the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
Using a "Laser of Type 'General'", continue with step 4.
03. If you use a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" select
‒ M enu View > "Production Proﬁle"
‒ or "Production Proﬁle" in the Toolbar File.
When the Laser Control Window for 'Production Proﬁle' opens, click the Laser button to switch the laser on.
The output window shows "Laser on".
04. In the M enu Hardware, select 'Laser Control\Calibration Factor Wizard'.
The 'Calibration Factor Wizard' dialog opens.
Note When the calibration proceeds, the existing calibration factors get overwritten.
05. Click the Next button to start the calibration procedure.
The page for deﬁning the rectangle for the test marking opens.
06. Enter the values for the width and height (in [mm]) in the corresponding input ﬁelds.
The larger the size, the more accurate the calibration.
Click the Next button to continue.
The page for starting the marking opens.
07. If you have not already done so, then position an appropriate test material at the laser focus and close all shields.
08. If you do not want to use pure correction ﬁle factors, deactivate the corresponding check box (see note above).
Start marking by the Start M arking button.
Click the Next button to continue.
The page for entering the measured values opens.
09. M easure the sides of the marked rectangle.
10. Enter the measured values (in [mm]) in the corresponding 'M easured Width' and 'M easured Height' input ﬁelds.
Click the Next button to continue.
The page with the calculated calibration factors opens.
11. Click the Finish button to take over the new calibration factors.
The 'Calibration Factor Wizard' dialog closes automatically.
The new factors are transferred into the 'Optics' parameters 'Δ Calibration Factor X/Y [‰]'.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Determining the Scan System Calibration Factor
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_CalibrationFactorWizard.htm 2/3

--- Page 787 ---

12. If you use a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" switch oﬀ the laser by clicking the Laser button in the Laser Control Window.
The output window shows "Laser oﬀ".
13. To check the parameters, click the 'Hardware Settings' button in the Toolbar "File" to open the corresponding dialog and
select the 'Optics' parameters.
14. Click the button OK to close the Dialog "Hardware Settings".
Note If you close "Hardware Conﬁguration" subsequently without saving, the changes are not applied!
To save the settings permanently, choose M enu File > 'Save' or click the Save button in the Toolbar File.
15. Choose the 'File\Save' menu or click the Save button to save the settings permanently.
or
When you close "Hardware Conﬁguration" (by opening a job or 'M arking Library' or when exiting laserDESK), the query "Do
you want to save your changes?" appears in the 'Save changes?' dialog.
Click the button OK.
