26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Testing the Signal Transfer by the RTC IOs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_RTCIODesk.htm 1/5

--- Page 791 ---

Testing the Signal Transfer by the RTC IOs
To test whether signals from  the RTC5 board | RTC6 board are applied correctly, input bits can be m onitored and output bits can
be set in an RTC IO desk. The bits are represented by radio buttons and m atch the pins of the RTC board EXTENSION 1 socket
connector and the LASER D-SU B connector.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Testing the Signal Transfer by the RTC IOs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_RTCIODesk.htm 2/5

--- Page 792 ---

Extension 1 IOs
Because several pins are predeﬁned for speciﬁc use, only 'Bit 7 ...0' input and output pins (see ﬁgure above) are available during a
m arking process. But here all pins are supported.
The 'Input' ﬁeld serves as a "live m onitor". Incom ing signals are indicated as activated radio buttons (dots inside). The refresh tim e
is 1 s, thus shorter signals m ay not be detected.
Bits 15 ... 0 of the 'Output' ﬁeld' can be set. But bits 15 ... 8 is autom atically refreshed by the program  and override the user values.
Thus, bits 7 …  0 should actually be used. The states of the output bits 15 ... 8 is refreshed together with the input.
The 'Autom atic U pdate' checkbox is only related to Output Bits 15 ... 8. If activated, they are updated autom atically overwriting
any user input. If not activated, the user input is not overwritten and can be applied by clicking the Set button. But it is possible
that the program  m ay change that setting due to program  actions. In this state the G U I and the real output lines m ay diﬀer. The
Read button shows the real output line state again.
To set output bits, activate the corresponding radio buttons and apply the setting by clicking the Set button. The Read button
serves to check whether the selected radio buttons are set. If not, these buttons are deselected.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Testing the Signal Transfer by the RTC IOs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_RTCIODesk.htm 3/5

--- Page 793 ---

Connector LASER IOs
The 'Input' lines serves as a "live m onitor". Incom ing signals are indicated as activated radio buttons (dots inside). The refresh
tim e is 1 s, thus shorter signals m ay not be detected.
Because the output lines can not be read back the radio buttons are only for user input. The state is not displayed!
To set output lines, activate the corresponding radio buttons and apply the setting by clicking the Set button.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Testing the Signal Transfer by the RTC IOs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_RTCIODesk.htm 4/5

--- Page 794 ---

Signal Testing
To test the signal transfer:
01. Choose M enu Hardware > M anual Control > RTC IO-Control.
The dialog 'RTC IO Desk' opens.
02. To check incom ing signals, just m onitor 'Bit 15...0' and 'Line 1-0' in the 'Input' ﬁelds.
If the signals transfer correctly, the appropriate radio buttons are tem porarily activated.
03. To check outgoing signals activate the appropriate radio button(s) in the 'Extension 1 Output' ﬁeld.
Select a bit pattern in 'Bit 15 ...0'.
04. If desired, activate the 'Autom atic U pdate' checkbox.
05. If desired, use 'Line1-0' in the 'Connector LASER Output' ﬁeld for user input.
06. Click the Set button/s to set the selection.
The corresponding RTC com m and is executed and output of the actual indicated states of bits 15...0 and lines 1-0 occurs.
07. If you want to check, whether all activated radio buttons are set, click the Read button.
Radio buttons which are not set is deactivated.
08. If necessary, select and set the appropriate radio button(s) again.
09. W hen you have ﬁnished testing signal transfer, close the 'RTC IO Desk' dialog by the Close button.
(original text - this is not a translation)

   actual Position Shows the actual m otor position.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Calibrating the Processing-On-the-Fly Mechanism
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_POFCalibrationWizard.htm 1/3

--- Page 799 ---

Calibrating the Processing-on-the-Fly M echanism
When using a transportation system with the RTC5 board | RTC6 board's POF option enabled, you need to determine exact
calibration factors for the processing-on-the-ﬂy mechanism. For that purpose, a calibration must be performed within the
'Processing on the Fly Calibration Wizard' dialog.
Note You can start the scan system's calibration only if "Hardware Conﬁguration" has already been opened.
Note If using two encoders (one for the x and one for the y direction), you must perform the calibration twice: the ﬁrst time
for calibrating in the x direction, the second time for calibrating in the y direction.
To calibrate the processing-on-the-ﬂy mechanism:
01. Choose M enu File > "Open" > "Hardware Conﬁguration".
"Hardware Conﬁguration" opens and the Dialog "Hardware Settings" opens.
02. Close the Dialog "Hardware Settings" by the button OK.
03. Choose M enu Hardware > "Adjustment" > "POF Calibration Wizard".
The dialog "Processing on the Fly Calibration Wizard" opens.
Note When the calibration proceeds, the existing calibration factors get overwritten.
04. Click the Next button to start the calibration procedure.
The page for selecting the used encoder type opens.
05. Click the radio button for the used encoder:
‒ 'Encoder in x'
‒ or 'Encoder in y'
‒ or 'Encoder for Rotation'.
Click the Next button to continue.
The page with the request for labeling a ﬁxed reference position opens.
06. Label the start position of your transportation system.
Therefore, mark a position on your transportation system and reference it to marked ﬁxed point of the machine.
Click the Next button to continue.
The page with the request for moving the transportation system opens.
07. M ove the transportation system in the positive direction, then stop it.
The longer the distance the more precise the calibration result.
08. Click the Next button to continue.
The page for entering the measured value opens.
09. M easure the distance between the marked ﬁxed point of the machine (start point) and the mark on your transportation
system (end point).
10. Enter the measured value in the 'M easured distance in [mm]' input ﬁeld.
Click the Next button to continue.
The page with the calculated calibration factor opens.
11. Click the Finish button to take over the new calibration factor.
The 'Processing on the Fly Calibration Wizard' dialog closes automatically.
The new factor is transferred into the 'Processing-On-The-Fly' parameter 'Calibration Factors'.
12. If you use a transportation system in the x and y directions, repeat the calibration procedure for the remaining direction.
13. To check the 'Calibration Factors',
‒ open the Dialog "Hardware Settings" by the corresponding button in the Toolbar File,
‒ then select 'Processing-On-The-Fly' in the left ﬁeld of the dialog'.
14. Click the button OK to close the Dialog "Hardware Settings".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Creating/Calibrating the Variable Polygon Delay Curve
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_PolygonDelay.htm 1/2

--- Page 802 ---

Creating the Variable Polygon Delay Calibration Curve
A variable polygon delay curve allows varying the length of the polygon delay depending on the angle between the two successive
m arking vectors (see 'Variable Polygon Delay' in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual). The standard curve of the RTC5 board | RTC6
board can be replaced by a custom ized curve. To do so, you m ust perform  a calibration by the 'Variable Polygon Calibration
W izard'.
Note The variable polygon delay curve can only be created with a connected iDRIVE scan system .
The created variable polygon delay curve is saved as a variable polygon delay ﬁle (CalVarPolDelay.txt) in the directory
C:\Programs\Scanlab\laserDESK or overwrites an existing ﬁle.
Depending on your application (with diﬀerent process requirem ents), it m ight be useful to create several curves/ﬁles by entering
diﬀerent param eters (m ax. polygon delay, m ax. position deviation, m arking speed). The ﬁles can be renam ed and collected in
their own directory. To use the variable polygon delay ﬁle, it m ust be activated and selected within the Dialog "Hardware
Settings".
To create/calibrate the variable polygon delay curve:
01. In the M enu Hardware select 'Adjustm ent > Polygon Delay'.
The 'Variable Polygon Delay Calibration' dialog opens.
Note W hen the calibration proceeds, any already-existing variable polygon delay ﬁle gets overwritten.
02. Click the Forward button to start the calibration procedure.
The page for entering the polygon delay opens.
03. Enter the value for the m axim um  polygon delay (in [μs]) in the corresponding input ﬁeld.
Click the Forward button to continue.
The page for entering the position deviation opens.
04. Enter the value for the m axim um  position deviation (in [μm ]) in the corresponding input ﬁeld.
Click the Forward button to continue.
The page for entering the m arking speed opens.
05. Enter the value for the m arking speed (in [m /s]) in the corresponding input ﬁeld.
Click the Forward button to continue.
The page for starting the evaluation opens.
06. Click the Start evaluation button to start creation of a new variable polygon delay curve.
W hen the evaluation procedure is ﬁnished, the Apply button is shown.
07. Click Apply to create a new variable polygon delay ﬁle or to overwrite the existing ﬁle.
The M S W indows-standard dialog 'Save As...' opens.
08. Deﬁne the ﬁle nam e and path and click Save.
The page for ﬁnishing the evaluation procedure opens.
09. Click the Finish button.
The 'Variable Polygon Delay Calibration' dialog closes autom atically.
10. Activate and select the ﬁle in the 'Optics' Param eters (inside the Dialog "Hardware Settings").
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:04 Evaluating the Scan Head Tracking Error
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_Tracking%20Error.htm 1/2

--- Page 804 ---

Evaluating the Scan Head Tracking Error
G alvanom eter scanner m otions do not occur instantaneously with respect to vector control, but rather after a certain tim e lag, the
tracking error. The tracking error characterizes the reaction properties of the galvanom eter scanners and is speciﬁed as another
key dynam ic param eter for each scan head. The tracking error is denoted in the related scan head m anual. The laser and scanner
delays settings in the W indow "Properties" should be m odiﬁed depending on the scan head tracking error.
For this purpose, in the m arking param eters, the com m and "Calculate Delays" is available from  the Context M enu (Right M ouse
Button) (see Laser Delays und Scanner Delays).
For further inform ation about tracking error, refer to the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual and the scan head m anual.
The Tracking Error W izard dialog serves to easily valuate the tracking error of your scan head. The evaluation is based on
averaging. Archive the evaluated value ‒ then you can calculate the Laser Delays and Scanner Delays in the W indow "Properties"
(see Calculate Delays).
Additionally to the tracking error, the m axim um  speed of the scan system  and the evaluation speed are indicated.
Note The scan head tracking error can only be evaluated with a connected iDRIVE scan system .
The Tracking Error W izard cannot be applied with an SCANahead system .
To evaluate the tracking error:
01. Choose M enu Hardware > Adjustm ent > Tracking Error.
The Dialog "Tracking Error W izard" opens.
02. Click the Next button to start the evaluation procedure.
The page for starting the m easurem ent opens.
03. Click the Start M easurement button.
W hile m easuring, a Stop button is displayed, which lets you abort the m easurem ent if desired.
W hen the m easuring is ﬁnished, the m essage "Evaluation successful." opens.
04. Click the Next button.
The page with the m easurem ent results opens:
‒ Tracking Error (in [m s])
‒ M axim um  Speed of System  (in [m /s])
‒ Evaluation Speed (in [m /s])
05. Archive the displayed values to use them  when setting the laser and scanner delays.
06. Click the Finish button.
The dialog "Tracking Error W izard" closes autom atically.

Evaluating the Best Param eter Set
To ﬁnd the m ost suited param eter set for your application, an evaluation routine can be perform ed within the 'Param eter W izard'
dialog. This routine com prises the selection of a param eter set, the setting of param eter values, the execution of the test pattern
m arking, and ﬁnally the selection of the m ost suited param eters.
Note The m arking param eter set need to be deﬁned in the job and is used as the tem plate or starting param eter set. If the
test pattern should be ﬁlled, the job Fill Param eter set is used. Therefore, you should deﬁne these param eters prior to
running the Param eter W izard.
(Inform ation on editing individual param eters and m anaging the param eter set is described in W indow "Properties".)
Note U sing a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" you can start the evaluating routine only if the laser has already been
switched on in the 'Production Proﬁle'.
To evaluate the best param eter set:
01. If not yet done, open the desired job.
02. If not yet done, deﬁne the job's m arking and ﬁll param eters.
U sing a "Laser of Type 'G eneral'", continue with step 4.
03. If you use a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'" select
‒ M enu View > "Production Proﬁle"
‒ or "Production Proﬁle" in the Toolbar File.
   or
open the Laser Control W indow from  the M enu "View".
W hen the window opens, click the Laser button to switch the laser on.
The output window shows "Laser on".
04. In the M enu Hardware, select 'Adjustm ent\Param eter W izard'.
The 'Param eter W izard' dialog opens.
05. Click the Next button to start the evaluation routine.
The 'Sym bol Selection' page for selecting and deﬁning the object for the test m arking pattern opens.
06. Select the test pattern in the 'Object' listbox (predeﬁned rectangle, circle, triangle, letter A, or a user deﬁned sym bol of the
M arking Library).
Enter the desired values to deﬁne the object's size and start position in the corresponding input ﬁelds. Note that the value for
'Spacing' deﬁnes the em pty space between two objects.
Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Additional Settings' page opens.
07. If you want the test pattern to be ﬁlled, activate the corresponding check box.
Select the desired m arking param eter set in the corresponding listbox.
Only the m arking param eter sets of the opened job are available.
Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Param eter Selection' page opens.
08. Select the param eters which should be variable.
Two diﬀerent param eters for x and y direction can be selected in the corresponding listboxes.
Possible param eters are: Laser Power, M arking Speed, J um p Speed, Frequency, Pulse Length, all kind of delays, (Filling) Line
Distance, and (if activated) the sky writing param eters Tim elag and LaserOn-Shift.
To deﬁne the start value, the increm ent and (up to 10) steps, enter the desired values in the corresponding input ﬁelds.
Be careful, that all patterns are in the m arking area.
Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Test Pattern M arking' page opens.
09. If you want the x and y axes to be labeled with the applied variable param eters, activate the 'Add Param eter Labels' check
box.
Then click the Start M arking button to execute the test m arking.
During execution, the Start M arking button is deactivated.
If a param eter set is not allowed, for exam ple, one param eter is out of range, this pattern is not m arked and m issing. An

appropriate m essage opens.
Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Result' page opens.
10. For each m arked pattern a button is shown. The lower left A1 button has the deﬁned starting param eter set (it is not
displayed, if one param eter is out of range). The positions of all other buttons correspond to the increm ents respective to A1.
Select the m ost suited m arking by clicking on the corresponding button.
‒ If you want to reﬁne the selected param eters, continue with step 11.
‒ If you want to store this param eter set, continue with step 12.

The Param eter W izard jum ps back to the 'Param eter Selection' page.
The selected param eter set is kept and used as the new starting param eters.
Repeat steps 8 to 10 to deﬁne two new (or the sam e) param eters for variation.
12. Enter a nam e into the input ﬁeld.
If you want to keep the test pattern, activate the 'K eep Pattern as a new J ob' check box. (Else the pattern is discarded and
your old job is reloaded again.)

param eters are lost.)

W indow.
The output window shows "Laser oﬀ".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Setting the Position of the intelliWELD II's Zoom Axis
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_intelliWELDIIControl.htm 1/3

--- Page 809 ---

Setting the Position of the intelliW ELD II Zoom  Axis
The 'Scanlab intelliW ELD Control' dialog lets you enlarge the spot size by setting the position of the intelliW ELD II's zoom  axis.
Note The intelliW ELD II scan system  needs an additional zoom  correction ﬁle besides the standard 3D correction ﬁle. This
ﬁle has the nam e extension "_zoom" added to the ﬁlenam e xxx.ct5 of the corresponding 3D correction ﬁle. Both ﬁles
(xxx.ct5 and xxx_zoom.ct5) need to be present to be loaded to the RTC board. The zoom  correction ﬁle m ust be
stored in the sam e directory as the standard 3D correction ﬁle.
If the zoom  correction ﬁle is m issing, the intelliW ELD II support is deactivated.
W hen the standard 3D correction ﬁle is selected in the hardware conﬁguration on the 'Scan Head' page, laserDESK  autom atically
loads the additional zoom  ﬁle, provided that the intelliWELD II support is activated. The activation is done by selecting the
appropriate checkbox in the hardware conﬁguration on the 'Optics' page.
If the intelliW ELD II support is activated, the m enu com m and 'Hardware\M anual Control\intelliW ELD II' is available which opens
the 'Scanlab intelliW ELD II Control' dialog.
Here you can set the position of the intelliWELD II's zoom  axis as an unsigned 32-bit value. The allowed range for the zoom  factor is
0‒1023. Values outside this range are clipped.
After the m otion of the zoom  axis the actual zoom  factor (returned from  the intelliWELD II's zoom  axis) is displayed.
This m anual setting rem ains valid until another zoom  factor is set or the RTC board is initialized again. It is not changed by or
during a job execution.
Note How to use the zoom  functionality of the intelliWELD II is described in detail in the intelliWELD II M anual, Part 1 -
G eneral Description, chapter '3D Zoom  Setting'.
  To check or change the zoom  axis' position:
01. Choose 'M anual Control > intelliW ELD II' in the M enu Hardware.
The 'Scanlab intelliW ELD II Control' dialog opens.
The actual zoom  factor is shown.
02. If you want to change the zoom  factor,
‒ ﬁrst enter the 32-bit value in the 'Zoom  Factor' input ﬁeld or click the arrow buttons (up/down),
‒ then click the Set Zoom  button.
The zoom  axis m oves to the new position and the displayed actual zoom  factor will change accordingly.
03. To close the dialog, click the Close button.
(original text - this is not a translation)

Tab 'Sensors'
Tab 'Sensors' is only available , if the connected iDRIVE scan system  is equipped with sensors for extended m onitoring (and
therefore, as well as with type "ISB3" servo boards).
To query inform ation about sensors, laserDESK  oﬀers the Tab 'Sensors' in the Dialog "Inform ation".
Note Som e iDRIVE scan system s are equipped with sensors for extended m onitoring (and therefore, as well as with type
"ISB3" servo boards). laserDESK  detects these sensors autom atically. However, the corresponding query needs the
scan system  return channel (DATA OU T±). Therefore, it m ust not be occupied due to job execution or other
functionalities (such as a laserDESK  control elem ent Protocol Start). laserDESK  only then displays Tab 'Sensors' and
continues updating the colum n 'Value' (= current values of the sensors) about every second (com pare to: Tab
'Extended Head Status').
Tab 'Sensors'
Tab 'Sensors'
Control Description
Table with sensor data sets DRIVE scan system  sensor values (read-only): current, error range, warning range.
Colors in colum n 'Value' (as with Tab 'Head Status'):
 (white)   No evaluation as sensor has not been activated
 (red)     Sensor value is outside the error range
 (green)   Sensor value is within the error limits
 (orange)  Sensor value is outside the warning range
Note that checkbox 'Alarm  activated' and checkbox 'W arning activated' each shows
the current sensor m onitoring setting on the DRIVE scan system  at ﬁrst:
Checkbox 'Alarm  activated' = selected m eans:
The iDRIVE scan system  m onitors the sensor state. As soon as the sensor value is
outside the error range, PowerOK  = 0 is set.
Checkbox 'W arning activated' = selected m eans:
The iDRIVE scan system  m onitors the sensor state. As soon as the sensor value is
outside the warning range, Tem pOK  = 0 is set.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Tab 'Sensors'
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/DLG_Information_5_Sensors.htm 2/3

--- Page 824 ---

Control Description
However, it is possible to change them  (that is, selecting|clearing) m ust be applied
m anually by clicking the Button Activate Sensor.
Button Start Logging | Stop Logging After clicking the button Start Logging its label changes to Stop Logging. The data set
(= all current sensor values =colum n 'Value') is added to the ﬁle SensorData.csv which
is located in the deﬁned log directory, see Section 'Directories'. Further data records
are added every second until the button Stop Logging is clicked or Dialog "Runtim e
Inform ation" is closed. Clicking the button Start Logging again adds further data
records to the ﬁle SensorData.csv.
Button Clear Error If you have activated a sensor and the iDRIVE-Scan system  switches oﬀ during a job
execution due to an error status | warning status: As soon as the cause no longer exists,
you can click on the Button Clear Error. The iDRIVE-Scan system  is then ready for
operation again.
Background: an error state | warning state persists after it has been detected by the
scan head-internal control. It can be cancelled by the Button Clear Error.
Button Activate Sensor Changes the sensor-related behavior of the iDRIVE-Scan system  according to all
currently selected | cleared checkboxes ʻAlarm  activated’ | checkboxes ʻW arning
activated’.
To show sensor inform ation:
Click Dialog "Inform ation" > Tab 'Sensors'.
To change sensor-related behavior of the iDRIVE Scan System :
01. Click Dialog "Inform ation" > Tab 'Sensors'.
02. Select (clear) the desired checkboxes 'Alarm  activated'.
03. Select (clear) the desired checkboxes 'W arning activated'.
04. Click Button Activate Sensor.

certain prerequisites

The Hardware State is displayed as a hexadecim al num ber. Each Bit represents an individual state:
Bit M eaning (if bit is set)
  0x0000 0001  program  runs
  0x0000 0002  RTC board initialized
  0x0000 0004  laser system  is deﬁned correct and initialized
  0x0000 0008  all external controls are initialized
  0x0000 000F  all hardware com ponents are initialized (sum  of Bit 0-3)
  0x0000 0010  get_head_status returns 'Power OK '
  0x0000 0020  hardware initialization running
  0x0000 0040  switching Autom atic M ode oﬀ
  0x0000 0080  unused
  0x0000 0100  RTCHandler worker thread is running (O-Pin 12)
  0x0000 0200  list is in execution (O-Pin 14)
  0x0000 0400  another part of list m ust be loaded yet
  0x0000 0800  job list has variable parts
  0x0000 1000  list execution is stopped by em ergency button
  0x0000 2000  external stop signal
  0x0000 4000  system  in Autom atic M ode (O-Pin 13)
  0x0000 8000  list calculation and execution in progress (M anual M ode execution)
  0x0001 0000  execution error occured (O-Pin 15)
  0x0002 0000  list is calculated
  0x0004 0000  set, when execution is ﬁnished, reset by start com m and
  0x0008 0000  set, when pilot laser dialog is the active window (pilot laser m ode)
  0x0010 0000  auxiliary error ﬂag, used tem porarily

--- Page 838 ---

Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System
Note To use the 3D-Calibration W izard optim ally, it is strongly recom m ended to read the How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis
Scan System topic before.
This topic gives you an overview of the com plete calibration process.
Further detailed inform ation on calibration can be found in the application note 'Calibrating a 3-Axis Laser Scan
System '. Contact Scanlab to obtain the application note.
Note If you want to proceed an xy-plane correction (2D) without correction ﬁle and system -speciﬁc xm l-conﬁguration ﬁle,
refer to the How to Calibrate an U nknown Optical System  topic.
Note U nless otherwise indicated, varioSCAN stands for all types of varioSCAN and varioSCAN FLEX.
Note Before you start to perform  the calibration ...
... the following points m ust be checked in order to avoid errors:
‒ Your scan system  m ust correspond to one of the following system  conﬁgurations:
    - System  for 2D processing with F-Theta lens or
    - System  for 2D processing in a plane by using a varioSCAN for pre-focusing, without F-Theta lens or
    - System  for 3D processing with varioSCAN, without F-Theta lens or
    - System  for 3D processing with varioSCAN and F-Theta lens.
‒ The laser scan system  is adjustable to diﬀerent heights in order to m ove the m arking substrates to the diﬀerent z-
levels (z <0, z=0, z>0). (Not for pure 2D calibration)
‒ The correct Hardware Conﬁguration has been selected. Coordinate transform ations (for exam ple, oﬀset or rotation)
is set to zero while the wizard is running in order to avoid errors. The 'Tim elag Com pensation' has been set according
to the tracking error of the varioSCAN. If a SCANahead system  is used, the 'SCANahead Control Support' has been
activated. (Both, see Setting 'Optics' Param eters).
‒ Adapted M arking Param eters (especially Laser Param eters and Laser Delays) used for the m arking on the test
m aterial, have been selected for the job node of the Hardware Conﬁguration. If a SCANahead system  is used, the
M arking Param eters ‒ SCANahead Param eters have been set to 100%.
‒ The laser scan system  has been warm ed-up for m inim um  30 m inutes.
‒ U sing a varioSCAN FLEX, a reference run has been perform ed (refer to Testing the M otor Controllers). The dialog
"M otor Control" m ust then rem ain open until the calibration procedure has been com pleted.
‒ A suitable ﬂat m arking substrate (for exam ple, anodized alum inum  or steel plate) is available.
‒ Suitable m easuring tools (digital m icroscope, coordinate m easuring m achine) are available.

System  Settings
At ﬁrst, the conﬁguration ﬁle (in xm l form at) and the initial correction ﬁle (in ct5 form at), both provided by SCANLAB, m ust be
loaded.
After the conﬁguration ﬁle has been read, several properties are displayed. Only the power is taken from  the actual default
param eter set, because the power is independent of the optical system .
Note For further inform ation, see Step 1: System  Settings in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System .
The value in the 'Laser Frequency [kHz]' ﬁeld is aﬀected by the other param eters. It will change autom atically when m odifying
other param eters. Nevertheless, this ﬁeld can be edited to achieve better m arking results.
Adjust the setup dependent param eters as follows:
01. Check whether the selected *.ct5 correction ﬁle and *.xml conﬁguration ﬁle m atch each other and the system  to be
calibrated.
02. To load the conﬁguration ﬁle, click the corresponding Button 
  and select the required *.xmlﬁle.
03. If not already selected in the Hardware Conﬁguration, load the initial correction ﬁle.
Click the corresponding Button 
  and select the required *.ct5 ﬁle.
04. If necessary, adapt the conﬁguration ﬁle:
‒ Click the Adapt Conﬁguration File button to open a dialog (description, see Adapting the XM L Data File).
‒ M odify the param eters/values to your requirem ents.
Note The values given by the lim itations of the optical system  should not be increased to avoid overﬂow errors.
05. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'System  Adjustm ent' page opens.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 System Adjustment
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_3.htm 1/2

--- Page 843 ---

System  Adjustm ent
If not already done, the com ponents of the laser scan system  have to be adjusted corresponding to the schem atic diagram . The
working distance A (m echanical distance between the laser input axis and the z=0 working plane) and the distance B (m echanical
distance between the exit of the varioSCAN and the entrance of the xy scan system ) is read from  the loaded conﬁguration ﬁle and
are shown below the schem atic diagram  (values in [m m ]). U sing a varioSCAN FLEX, the distance B need not be adjusted.
Note A precise system  adjustm ent is the prerequisite for a successful calibration process.
Note How to adjust the com ponents is described in detail in the m anuals of the varioSCAN or the varioSCAN FLEX.
Refer to the 'Adjustm ent and Alignm ent' chapter.
Depending on the conﬁguration, carefully adjust the laser scan system  as follows:
01. Suitable arrange all com ponents of the system : laser, beam  expander, adjustable m irrors, varioSCANand scan head or
varioSCAN FLEX with scan head.
02. Check working distance A and distance B.
If necessary correct the varioSCAN's or scan head position.
03. Align the beam  expander (and the m irrors if necessary).
04. Align the varioSCAN.
05. Align the working plane, which m ust be parallel in x and y direction to the scan system 's bottom  side.
06. If necessary readjust individual com ponents.
07. W hen the adjustm ent has been com pleted, click the Next button to continue.
The 'Adjust the Focus' page opens.
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Adjust the Focus
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_4.htm 1/2

--- Page 845 ---

Adjust the Focus
By an iterative procedure - the aim  is to get the m arked center line as the sm allest.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 3: Adjust the Focus in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System .
Note If you need to widen the z range, it is recom m ended to select the 'non linear z distances' checkbox.
Note If you use a varioSCAN FLEX and if your Hardware Conﬁguration is setup with several m otor control axes, select the
radio button of the axis which is connected to the varioSCAN FLEX.
To determ ine the focus distance exactly:
01. Select a m ethod by the corresponding radio button. Depending on the choice, the interface changes and shows only the
required controls.
02. If necessary: Adjust the line spacing. The z-distance in the line pattern is preset to half the Rayleigh length.
03. Start the m arking. Im portant: M arking is done in the reference point for option (1) and (2), in the origin for option (3)!
04. Specify the best focused line. If the m iddle line (num ber 0) is the best focused, the focus distance is correct and this
calibration step is com plete. G o to step 6.
05. Optional: Repeat steps 2...4 with sm aller z distances to obtain an even m ore precise determ ination of the focus position.
Note After this step the focus ring or stepper m otor position should not be changed anym ore. The resulting calibration
is only valid for this very position.
Note If you use a focusing unit (for exam ple, varioSCAN FLEX) with stepper m otor you should note down this axis
position. The absolute position is displayed in the 'M otor Control' dialog. This position is strongly related to the
correction ﬁle. A diﬀerent position needs a diﬀerent correction ﬁle!
06. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Tilt Correction' page opens.
Note If the focus position in the real system  diﬀers strongly from  the theoretical position, you should select the 'non linear z
distances' checkbox and/or increase the z distance.
Note If the system  cannot reach the z-distance because it has been speciﬁed too large: only a warning is issued but nothing
is executed.
Note If several lines are focused approxim ately the sam e: select the m iddle one, create a new correction ﬁle with it, reduce
the z-distance and repeat the procedure.
Note Applicable for option 3 (= A value) only:
After the correction ﬁle has been created, the new A value is shown. This can change the z-range. Therefore, you
should check experim entally whether the z-range required by the application is actually available.
The newly created correction ﬁle gets "_A" as nam e suﬃx. This nam e is used for each iteration. That is, the
correction ﬁle of the previous iteration is overwritten. Once you proceed to the next calibration step: the nam e is
perm anently taken over and the ﬁle is not overwritten any m ore.
You can only create a new correction ﬁle after you have executed a m arking with the current setting! If you change a
setting, you m ust execute the m arking once again.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25
© SCANLAB G m bH 2025
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Adjust the Focus
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_4.htm 2/2

--- Page 846 ---

You are here: Hardware Control > Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System  > Tilt Correction
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Tilt Correction
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_5.htm 1/2

--- Page 847 ---

Tilt Correction
Note Tilt correction is not suitable for use with telecentric lenses.
In two consecutive steps two concentric circles are m arked to adjust the beam  direction: one bigger circle at or near z = zm ax and
one sm aller circle at or near z = zm in. The circles are m arked on the sam e m arking substrate to be able to m easure the relative
shift.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 4: Tilt Correction in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System .
To com pensate the residual tilt:
01. M ark two circles, one at z = zm ax and one at z = zm in.
Be careful not to m ove the m arking substrate in x,y between the two consecutive starts.
02. M easure the shift by using a coordinate m easuring m achine.
Note Because the relative shift is evaluated, absolute positions in the coordinate system  are not necessary.
W hen m easuring/evaluating the test pattern, m ake sure to align the m arking substrate correctly in xy direction.
The crosshair axes are in x,y direction.
03. Enter the results into the 'Δx' and 'Δy' input ﬁeld.
04. Click the Calculate Correction button to calculate a new correction ﬁle.
The new correction ﬁle with the corrected beam  tilt is calculated.
The Calculate Correction button changes to Repeat.
Note As a secondary eﬀect of the tilt correction, there m ay be a shift of the origin. Therefore, the scan head origin in the
m achine coordinate system  should be checked after the calibration procedure and adapted in the Hardware
Conﬁguration if necessary.
05. If you want to repeat the procedure for reﬁnem ent, click the Repeat button. (Otherwise, continue with step 7.)
The new calculated correction ﬁle (see step 4) is loaded.
The actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_OFFSET".
06. Repeat the tilt correction procedure by perform ing steps 1 - 4 again.
If desired, this can also be done several tim es.
Note The new correction ﬁle is loaded to and used by the RTC board either by clicking Repeat or Next.
07. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'XY-Plane Correction' page opens.
Depending on how m any tim es the procedure was repeated, the correction ﬁle is displayed with the appendix "_OFFSET"
accordingly m any tim es.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25
© SCANLAB G m bH 2025
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Tilt Correction
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_5.htm 2/2

--- Page 848 ---

You are here: Hardware Control > Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System  > XY-Plane Calibration
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 XY-Plane Calibration
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_6.htm 1/2

--- Page 849 ---

XY-Plane Correction
The m arking will create a test pattern at z = 0 consistent of 11 x 11 sym bols. The sym bol type (cross +, x-cross x, ﬁlled circle ●) can
be selected for best results of your coordinate m easuring m achine.
Note The origin is deﬁned by the center sym bol. This sym bol is m arked with (0,0) values for the galvanom eter scanner and
corresponds to the origin of the scan head.
If your m easurem ent system  evaluates non-zero values for the origin, these values serve as an oﬀset for all positions.
Therefore, you do not need to place the test pattern exactly in your m easurem ent system  that the center point m atches
the coordinate origin.
If you need to shift the origin and adapt it to your m achine coordinate system , you can set appropriate values in the
Hardware Conﬁguration after you have ﬁnished the wizard (see Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters).
To create a new xy calibration:
01. Select a suitable sym bol type (Cross, X-Cross or Filled Circle) in the 'Sym bol Selection' ﬁeld.
Note The ﬁlled circle sym bols always have a diam eter of 2 m m  - regardless of the size of the scan ﬁeld.
W ith a sm all scan ﬁeld size or a narrow test pattern this can lead to overlapping of the ﬁlled circles and thus m ake
the evaluation m ore diﬃcult or even im possible.
Therefore, select the sym bols cross or x-cross for sm all scan ﬁelds.
02. M ark the test pattern.
03. M easure the pattern by using a coordinate m easuring m achine.
04. Enter the m easured values in the table cells.
The m easured values can be im ported by Copy & Paste from  another program  (for exam ple, M S Excel).
05. Enter a tolerance value in the 'Tolerance [µm ]' input ﬁeld (larger than 0.0).
06. Click the Calculate Correction button to calculate a new correction ﬁle.
The calculation process m ay take som e tim e; m eanwhile a progress bar is displayed.
Note The new correction ﬁle is loaded to and used by the RTC board only, if you go forward to the next step, not if you
repeat this step.
07. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Stretch Correction' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_XY_PLANE".
(original text - this is not a translation)

Stretch Correction
In two consecutive steps two test patterns, each consisting of 3 x 3 sym bols, are m arked at two diﬀerent z-levels (z > 0 and z < 0).
Each test pattern is m arked on an own m arking substrate.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 6: Stretch Correction in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan
System .
Note If your m easurem ent system  evaluates non-zero values for the origin, these values serve as an oﬀset for all positions of
this layer. Therefore, you do not need to place the test pattern exactly in your m easurem ent system  that the center
point m atches the coordinate origin.
To evaluate the correction of the stretch factors:
01. Select a suitable sym bol type (Cross, X-Cross or Filled Circle) in the 'Sym bol Selection' ﬁeld.
02. M ark two test patterns, each on an own m arking substrate and at a diﬀerent z-level (z > 0 or z < 0) .
03. M easure the pattern by using a coordinate m easuring m achine.
04. Enter the m easured values into the table cells.
The m easured values can be im ported by Copy & Paste from  another program  (for exam ple, M S Excel).
05. Click the Calculate Correction button to calculate a new correction ﬁle.
Note The new correction ﬁle is loaded to and used by the RTC board only, if you go forward to the next step, not if you
repeat this step.
06. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Calibration of the ABC Values' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_STRETCH".
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Calibrating ABC Values
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationWizard_8.htm 1/3

--- Page 853 ---

Calibrating the ABC Values
A seven line pattern is m arked at diﬀerent z-positions according to your system  conﬁguration.
The procedure is perform ed in two runs: in a ﬁrst run for coarse tuning, in a second run for ﬁne-tuning.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 7: Calibration of the ABC Values in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis
Scan System .
Note To avoid confusion, it is recom m ended to execute the m arking as a step by step procedure in the order of the table.
To calibrate the ABC values:
01. If necessary, m odify the distance for the z steps in the line pattern according to your requirem ents.
The value, initial shown in this ﬁeld, corresponds to half the value shown in the 'Rayleigh Length [m m ]' ﬁeld in the system
settings (see Step 1: System  Settings), but only if the z range allows it.
If the z-range is too sm all, the distance for the z steps are adjusted autom atically.
02. If not yet highlighted, select the 'x coordinate' cell in the ﬁrst row.
03. Start the m arking of the pattern.
The selected/highlighted line shows the m arking position.
04. Evaluate the best/sm allest line by using a digital m icroscope.
05. Enter the num ber of the best line in the highlighted table cell.
06. M ark the second line test pattern.
The z-output value in the top row is entered autom atically.
The cell in the line below is highlighted.
07. Evaluate the best/sm allest line by using a digital m icroscope.
08. Enter the num ber of the best line in the highlighted table cell.
09. Repeat the procedure row by row for every position/coordinate.
10. After com pleting the table, click the Calculate ABC button to calculate new ABC values.
These is updated in the 'A', 'B' and 'C' ﬁelds (m essage: "New ABC values ﬁtted").
Sim ultaneously, the values are written into a new correction ﬁle.
Note The new correction ﬁle is loaded to and used by the RTC board only, if you go forward to the next page, not if you
repeat this step.
11. Click the Next button to continue.
The page for the ﬁne tuning opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_ABC".
12. Repeat the procedure described in steps 1 - 10, now for ﬁne tuning.
Note The new correction ﬁle is loaded to and used by the RTC board only, if you go forward to the next page, not if you
repeat this step.
Note As a secondary eﬀect of calibrating the ABC values, the z range m ay be restricted.
In this case, a m essage is shown in the following 'Calibration Results' page.
13. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Calibration Results' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with a further appendix "_ABC".
(original text - this is not a translation)

Calibrating a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System  ‒ in Dem o M ode
You are using the 3D-Calibration W izard in laserDESK  Dem o M ode. This m eans that the 3D-Calibration W izard calculates an
im proved *.ct5 correction ﬁle based on the speciﬁed target values and your m easured values.
In Dem o M ode, neither an RTC5 board | RTC6 board nor a laserDESK  U SB dongle is required. Thus, the 3D-Calibration W izard
cannot control the laser scan system  and not execute the m arking of the test patterns. Therefore, you need your own laser
processing software to control the laser scan system . Furtherm ore, all test patterns m ust be created by yourself.
Note To m ake the m ost out of 3D-Calibration W izard, read How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System  in advance as it
explains the com plete calibration. Pay particular attention to the instructions for perform ing the calibration in Dem o
M ode.
Note Further detailed inform ation on calibration can be found in the M anual "Calibrating a 3-Axis Laser Scan System ".
Contact SCANLAB, to obtain a copy.
Note If you want to proceed an xy-plane correction (2D) without correction ﬁle and system -speciﬁc xm l-conﬁguration ﬁle,
refer to the How to Calibrate an U nknown Optical System .
Note U nless otherwise indicated, varioSCAN stands for all types of varioSCAN and varioSCAN FLEX.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Calibrating a 2-Axis or 3-Axis Scan System – in Demo Mode
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_1.htm 2/3

--- Page 857 ---

Starting the Calibration
Prerequisites
Before you start the calibration, the following points m ust be checked in order to avoid errors:
‒ In dem o m ode, all m arking patterns required within the calibration m ust be created and executed externally. For this purpose, a
proprietary laser processing software for your laser scan system  is available and ready for use. If you use a varioSCAN FLEX, its
stepper m otor is also controllable externally.
‒ Your scan system  m ust correspond to one of the following system  conﬁgurations:
    - System  for 2D processing with F-Theta lens or
    - System  for 2D processing in a plane by using a varioSCAN for pre-focusing, without F-Theta lens or
    - System  for 3D processing with varioSCAN, without F-Theta lens or
    - System  for 3D processing with varioSCAN and F-Theta lens.
‒ The laser scan system  is adjustable to diﬀerent heights in order to m ove the m arking substrates to the diﬀerent z-levels (z <0,
z=0, z>0). (Not for pure 2D calibration)
‒ M ake sure that no coordinate transform ations are applied to the test patterns.
‒ The laser scan system  has been warm ed-up for m inim um  30 m inutes.
‒ A suitable ﬂat m arking substrate (for exam ple, anodized alum inum  or steel plate) is available.
‒ Suitable m easuring tools (digital m icroscope, coordinate m easuring m achine) are available.
Procedure
Click the Next button to continue.
The 'System  Settings' page opens.

loaded.
After the conﬁguration ﬁle has been read, several properties are displayed.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 1: System  Settings in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System .
Adjust the setup dependent param eters as follows:
01. Check whether the selected *.ct5 correction ﬁle and *.xml conﬁguration ﬁle m atch each other and the system  to be
calibrated.
02. To load the conﬁguration ﬁle, click the corresponding Button 
  and select the required *.xml ﬁle.
The 3D-Calibration W izard autom atically recognizes the system  conﬁguration on the basis of the param eters in the
conﬁguration ﬁle.

  and select the required *.ct5 ﬁle.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 System Adjustment
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_3.htm 1/4

--- Page 861 ---

System  Adjustm ent
If not already done, the ﬁrst step is to align the com ponents of the laser scan system  m echanically according to the schem atic
diagram . The working distance A (m echanical distance between the laser input axis and the z=0 working plane) and, if a varioSCAN
is used, the distance B (m echanical distance between the exit of the varioSCAN and the entrance of the xy scan system ) is read
from  the loaded conﬁguration ﬁle and are shown below the schem atic diagram  (values in [m m ]). U sing a varioSCAN FLEX, the
distance B need not be adjusted.
Note A precise system  alignm ent is the prerequisite for a successful calibration process.
Check in a second step whether the laser is focused accurately. The aim  of this step is to focus the laser so that the laser spot is as
sm all as possible when m arking in the reference point. If you are using a varioSCAN, you m ay adjust the laser by turning the focus
ring on the varioSCAN. If you are using a varioSCAN FLEX you m ay adjust the laser by m oving the stepper m otor of the varioSCAN
FLEX. This m ust be don com pletely in your own m arking software and, if necessary, with external control of the varioSCAN FLEX.
Note The procedure of the second step is not possible with 2D system s with f-Theta and without a varioSCAN.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 System Adjustment
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_3.htm 2/4

--- Page 862 ---

M echanical Alignm ent
Depending on the conﬁguration, carefully align the com ponents of the laser scan system  as follows:
Note How to adjust the com ponents is described in detail in the m anuals of the varioSCAN or the varioSCAN FLEX.
Refer to the 'Adjustm ent and Alignm ent' chapter.
01. Suitable arrange all com ponents of the system : laser, beam  expander, adjustable m irrors, varioSCANand scan head or
varioSCAN FLEX with scan head.
02. Check working distance A and distance B.
If necessary correct the varioSCAN's or scan head position.
03. Align the beam  expander (and the m irrors if necessary).
04. Align the varioSCAN.
05. Align the working plane, which m ust be parallel in x and y direction to the scan system 's bottom  side.
06. If necessary, readjust individual com ponents.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 System Adjustment
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_3.htm 3/4

--- Page 863 ---

Adjustment of the Laser Focus
To check and adjust the laser focus:
01. Create a test pattern of 7 parallel lines.
02. Bring/m ove the varioSCAN / varioSCAN FLEX to its neutral position.
03. M ark the test pattern at diﬀerent defocus values in the speciﬁed reference point:
The central line has the defocus value d = 0 m m . A defocus is set for m arking the upper and lower lines. The defocus value
between two neighboring lines should diﬀer by at least half a Rayleigh length. For a test pattern with 7 lines this m eans
d = -3×Δd ... +3×Δd
with Δd ≥ zR/2 and zR =Rayleigh length.
04. Evaluate the laser spot quality by using digital m icroscope.
If the line with the sm allest spot was m arked with a positive defocus value, then proceed as follows to shift the focus position
in the negative z direction:
‒ On the varioSCAN, turn the focus ring clockwise.
‒ On the varioSCAN FLEX, m ove the stepper m otor to sm aller control values (in the direction of the lower lim it switch).
If the line with the sm allest spot has been m arked with a negative defocus value, then proceed as follows to shift the focus
position in the positive z direction:
‒ On the varioSCAN, turn the focus ring counterclockwise.
‒ On the varioSCAN FLEX, m ove the stepper m otor to larger control values (in the direction of the upper lim it switch).
05. Repeat steps 2 and 3 until the central line is best focused.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25
© SCANLAB G m bH 2025

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Tilt Correction
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_4.htm 1/2

--- Page 865 ---

Tilt Correction
Note Tilt correction is not suitable for use with telecentric lenses.
If you are using a telecentric lense, skip this step.
In two consecutive steps two concentric circles m ust be m arked to adjust the beam  direction: one bigger circle at or near z = zm ax
and one sm aller circle at or near z = zm in. The circles m ust be m arked on the sam e m arking substrate to be able to m easure the
relative shift.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 4: Tilt Correction in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan System .
To com pensate the residual tilt:
01. Create two test patterns:
‒ one sm aller circle with cross-hair in the m iddle,
‒ one bigger circle, with cross-hair in the m iddle.
02. M ark the two circles in or near the m iddle of the im age ﬁeld by using your laser processing software:
‒ m ark the bigger circle at z = zm ax,
‒ change the z-position to z = zm in,
‒ m ark the sm aller circle at z = zm in.
Be careful not to m ove the m arking substrate in xy between the two consecutive starts.
03. M easure the shift by using a coordinate m easuring m achine. The circle at z = zm in is the reference.
Note Because the relative shift is evaluated, absolute positions in the coordinate system  are not necessary.
W hen m easuring/evaluating the test pattern, m ake sure to align the m arking substrate correctly in xy direction.
The crosshair axes are in x,y direction.
04. Enter the results into the 'Δx' and 'Δy' input ﬁelds.
05. Click the Calculate Correction button to calculate a new correction ﬁle.
The new correction ﬁle with the corrected beam  tilt is calculated.
Note As a secondary eﬀect of the tilt correction, there m ay be a shift of the origin. Therefore, the scan head origin in the
m achine coordinate system  should be checked after the calibration procedure and adapted in the Hardware
Conﬁguration if necessary.
Note The new correction ﬁle is saved and applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next.
06. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'XY-Plane Correction' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_OFFSET".
07. If you want to repeat the procedure for reﬁnem ent,
‒ click the Back button to return to the 'Tilt Correction' page and
‒ repeat the tilt correction procedure by perform ing steps 1 - 5 again.
If desired, this can also be done several tim es.
The correction ﬁle is then displayed accordingly often with the appendix "_OFFSET".
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25
© SCANLAB G m bH 2025

file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_4.htm 2/2

XY-Plane Correction
A test pattern consistent of 11 x 11 sym bols m ust be m arked at z = 0. Depending on the im age ﬁled size, the grid size and the
sym bol center distance is speciﬁed on this page.
Note It is advantageous if the origin (0,0) is m arked by a center sym bol.
If your m easuring system  then evaluates non-zero values for the origin, these values can serve as an oﬀset for all
positions.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 5: XY-Plane Correction in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan
System .
To create a new xy calibration:
01. Create a test pattern with the grid size and sym bol center distance given in the instructions on this page.
The sym bols you want to m ark can have diﬀerent shapes: for exam ple, cross +, x-cross x or ﬁlled circle ●. It is recom m ended
to choose a shape that provides the best results for your coordinate m easuring m achine. It is also recom m ended to add axis
m arkers in the test patterns to indicate the x and y orientation of the SCANLAB Reference Coordinate System .
02. M ark the test pattern at z = 0 by using your laser processing software.
03. M easure the pattern by using a coordinate m easuring m achine.
04. Enter the m easured values in the table cells.
The m easured values can be im ported by Copy & Paste from  another program  (for exam ple, M S Excel).
05. Enter a tolerance value in the 'Tolerance [µm ]' input ﬁeld (larger than 0.0).
06. Click the Calculate Correction button to calculate a new correction ﬁle.
The calculation process m ay take som e tim e; m eanwhile a progress bar is displayed.
Note The new correction ﬁle is saved and applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next.
07. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Stretch Correction' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_XY_PLANE".

Stretch Correction
In two consecutive steps, a test pattern, consisting of 3 x 3 sym bols, m ust be m arked at two diﬀerent z-levels (z > 0 and z < 0).
Depending on the im age ﬁeld size, the grid size and the sym bol center distance is speciﬁed on this page. The test pattern m ust be
m arked on separate m arking substrates for each m arking.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 6: Stretch Correction in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis Scan
System .
Note If your m easurem ent system  evaluates non-zero values for the origin, these values serve as an oﬀset for all positions of
this layer. Therefore, you do not need to place the test pattern exactly in your m easurem ent system  that the center
point m atches the coordinate origin.
To evaluate the correction of the stretch factors:
01. Create a test pattern with the grid size and sym bol center distance given in the instructions on this page.
The sym bols you want to m ark can have diﬀerent shapes: for exam ple, cross +, x-cross x or ﬁlled circle ●. It is recom m ended
to choose a shape that provides the best results for your coordinate m easuring m achine. It is also recom m ended to add axis
m arkers in the test patterns to indicate the x and y orientation of the SCANLAB Reference Coordinate System .
02. M ark the test pattern twice by using your laser processing software:
‒ ﬁrst m ark the test pattern on an own m arking substrate at the upper z-position indicated on this page,
‒ then m ark the test pattern on an own m arking substrate at the lower z-position indicated on this page.
03. M easure the two patterns by using a coordinate m easuring m achine.
04. Enter the m easured values into the table cells.
The m easured values can be im ported by Copy & Paste from  another program  (for exam ple, M S Excel).
05. Click the Calculate Correction button to calculate a new correction ﬁle.
Note The new correction ﬁle is saved and applied only, if you go forward to the next step with Next.
06. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Calibration of the ABC Values' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_STRETCH".
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Calibration of the ABC Values
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/G_Wizards/HardwareControl_3DCalibrationOfflineWizard_7.htm 1/2

--- Page 871 ---

Calibration of the ABC Values
A seven-line pattern m ust be m arked at diﬀerent z-positions according to your system  conﬁguration. Thereby, each line m ust be
m arked with a diﬀerent defocus. The procedure is perform ed in two runs: in a ﬁrst run for coarse tuning, in a second run for ﬁne-
tuning.
Note For further inform ation, see Step 7: Calibration of the ABC Values in the overview topic How to Calibrate a 2- or 3-Axis
Scan System .
Note To avoid confusion, it is recom m ended to execute the m arking as a step by step procedure in the order of the table.
To calibrate the ABC values:
01. Create a test pattern consisting of 7 parallel lines.
02. If necessary, m odify the value in the 'Distance for the z steps in the line pattern [m m ]' ﬁeld.
This is the defocus value Δd by which the defocus changes from  line to line in the test pattern.
If the z-range is too sm all, the distance for the z steps are adjusted autom atically.
03. M ark the line pattern with diﬀerent defocus for each line at the z-position given in the top row (see colum n 'z coordinate').
04. Evaluate the best/sm allest line by using a digital m icroscope.
05. Enter the num ber of the best line in the 'Best line num ber' colum n in the top row and quit with <Return>.
The z-output value in the top row is entered autom atically.
The 'z coordinate' cell in the line below is highlighted.
06. Repeat the procedure (step 3 to step 5) row by row for every z-position (given in the colum n 'z coordinate').
07. After com pleting the table, click the Calculate ABC button to calculate new ABC values.
These is updated in the 'A', 'B' and 'C' ﬁelds (m essage: "New ABC values written in correction ﬁle").
Sim ultaneously, the values are written into a new correction ﬁle.
Note The new correction ﬁle is saved and applied only, if you go forward to the next page with Next.
08. Click the Next button to continue.
The page for the ﬁne tuning opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with the appendix "_ABC".
09. Repeat the procedure described in steps 2 - 7 now for ﬁne tuning.
Note The new correction ﬁle is saved and applied only, if you go forward to the next page with Next.
Note As a secondary eﬀect of calibrating the ABC values, the z range m ay be reduced.
In this case, a m essage is shown in the following 'Calibration Results' page.
10. Click the Next button to continue.
The 'Calibration Results' page opens.
There, the actual correction ﬁle is shown with another appendix "_ABC".
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK  V1.6.25

Releasing/Regaining the RTC Connection
To allow other program s to access the RTC5 board | RTC6 board, it m ight be necessary to disconnect laserDESK from  the RTC
board. To avoid closing laserDESK, the RTC connection can be released (and regained). The RTC connection can be released in two
ways: by the M enu M enu Hardware or by the status bar.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:05 Releasing/Regaining the RTC Connection

Releasing/Regaining the RTC Connection by the M enu M enu Hardware
To release the RTC connection:
01. Choose (the active) 'RTC Connection' in the M enu Hardware.
The m enu com m and 'RTC Connection' changes to the inactive state:
The connection is released.
To regain the RTC connection:
01. Choose (the inactive) 'RTC Connection' in the M enu Hardware.
The m enu com m and 'RTC Connection' changes to the active state:
The connection is regained.

Initializing the Rem ote Control
W hen using the rem ote control, laserDESK 'serves' the client's queries and executes the desired actions. Thus, the PC running
laserDESK is the server and the custom er program  is the client.
If the custom er's client program  hangs or the connection shuts down, 'Initialize Rem ote Control' lets you reset the server
connection point to m ake the rem ote control interface available again.
Note An active connection is interrupted by this com m and!
Note The rem ote control, and especially its param eters, telegram  syntax and com m ands, are described in Rem ote Control
M anual.
To initialize the rem ote control:
01. Choose 'Initialize Rem ote Control' in the M enu Hardware.
The rem ote control connection is initialized again.

Reference Run for all Deﬁned Axes
laserDESK lets you execute a reference run of all deﬁned axes with one click.
To do so, the following conditions m ust be m et:

(see Setting 'Hardware Devices' Param eters\M otor Control Axes')
The 'Reference Run M ode' of the axes predeﬁned in the Hardware Conﬁguration (see 'M otor Control Axes') is used for these
reference m otions.
To execute a reference run for all deﬁned axes, you can start it with one click by the m enu com m and 'Hardware\Reference Run for
Axis' or by the Reference Run button in the status bar.
Note W hen starting the reference run for all deﬁned axes, the dialog "M otor Control" is opened autom atically.

em ergency stop.

   or

The reference run starts im m ediately and the dialog "M otor Control" opens.
The controller state is indicated in the corresponding output ﬁeld of the dialog.

file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_RTCBoardSelection.htm 1/3

Selecting an RTC Board
If several RTC boards (RTC5 and/or RTC6) are build-in or connected to the PC on which laserDESK  is installed, you can switch to
another board during runtim e by opening the Dialog "Select Board". In the Dialog "Select Board", all RTC boards found are listed.
Only one board can be selected and used at a tim e.
Dialog "Select Board"
PCI boards (RTC5) and PCIe boards (RTC5 and RTC6) are autom atically detected. If no PCI/PCIe board of the speciﬁc type (RTC5 or
RTC6) is found, a dum m y board (serial num ber 0) is inserted, to allow the user to create jobs for that board in 'oﬄine m ode'.
Ethernet boards (RTC6 only) m ust be searched. The local IP address of the PC is evaluated and displayed. If necessary, you m ay
change this address. You have to click the Search button to ﬁnd all RTC6 Ethernet boards in this subnet. They are displayed in a
com bo box for selection.
If an RTC6 Ethernet board was previously selected, its IP address are displayed, too.
Additional, there are two ways of searching:
  1. Searching the subnet: You need to deﬁne a valid subnet IP address and the subnet m ask of this subnet.
  2. Searching an IP range: You need to deﬁne the ﬁrst and last IP address of this range.
W hen the search is ﬁnished, all found RTC6 Ethernet boards are additionally listed.
Note Because the RTC6 Ethernet board sequencing m ay change for each search, the serial num ber is used to ﬁnd the last
board. It m ay not appear at the sam e list place and m ay have a diﬀerent board num ber assigned in the board
adm inistration.
To use RTC6 Ethernet boards, there is no further action necessary in com parison to PCIe boards. If an RTC6 Ethernet board is
selected and the connection is interrupted, an appropriate error m essage is displayed. If the connection is re-established, you can
acquire this board again using the correspondent functions (dialog "Select Board" or Releasing/Regaining the RTC Connection).
Note U nder "Hardware" > "System  Inform ation" > "Scan System " > Ethernet Boards you get inform ation about the RTC6
Ethernet boards.
You can search for m ore boards in diﬀerent LAN areas without acquiring them  or release the current acquired board.
Note In the 'RTC Board' page in the Hardware Conﬁguration, the acquired board and all found RTC boards are listed.
This page is for inform ation only ‒ a diﬀerent selection and a further search are not possible here.
To change the RTC board while using laserDESK :
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Selecting an RTC Board
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/HardwareControl_RTCBoardSelection.htm 2/3

--- Page 882 ---

01. Select 'RTC Board Selection' from  the M enu Hardware.
The Dialog "Select Board" opens.
02. If you want to acquire an RTC5 | RTC6 PCI/PCIe board, continue with step 6.
If you want to acquire an RTC6 Ethernet board, continue with step 3.
03. Click the appropriate radio button (Subnet or IP Range) for the kind of search.
04. Deﬁne LAN IP Address and Subnet M ask or the IP Range Start and End Address in the corresponding input ﬁeld / com bo box.
05. Click the Search button to start the searching.
W hen the search is ﬁnished, all found RTC6 Ethernet boards are listed additionally.
If no board is found, a corresponding m essage is displayed.
06. Select the required RTC board by activating the corresponding check box in the 'Select' colum n.
Note If you want to use the full functionality of a connected SCANahead system , you m ust select the RTC6 board.
07. Click the button OK.
The Dialog "Select Board" is closed and the selected board is initialized and acquired.
All other boards are released and can be used by other program s.
Depending on the used RTC board, the laserDESK  output window shows (i.a.) "U sed DLL: .\RTC5DLL.DLL" or "U sed DLL:
.\RTC6DLL.DLL".
Note If you have connected an SCANahead system  and have selected an RTC6 board, you have to select SCANahead Control
Support under Hardware Settings > Optics in order to take full advantage of SCANahead system  functionality.
The laserDESK  output window will than show "SCANahead system  detected.".

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Using the Pilot Laser for Alignment
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/Pilot_Laser.htm 1/6

U sing the Pilot Laser for Alignm ent
If your laser scan system  is equipped with a pilot laser, this can be used as an easy to handle tool for aligning the job's m arking
objects to the m arking surface (or just for m arking sim ulation). Pilot laser m arking is started continuously. Sim ultaneously the
job's m arking objects can be m oved by buttons or keys until the objects are aligned correctly to the m arking surface. After the
pilot laser m arking has been stopped, the transform ation values can be applied to the job.
Pilot laser alignm ent can only be executed in the Pilot Laser Proﬁle. In this proﬁle, no job or hardware settings can be changed.
The RTC5 board | RTC6 board is initialized for the pilot laser and the processing laser is switched oﬀ.
Prerequisites for a pilot laser alignm ent:
The job m ust com prise a m arking object
The M anual M ode m ust be set, that is, the Autom atic M ode is switched oﬀ
(If the Autom atic M ode is switched on, see G U I Proﬁles / Control W indows, the pilot laser proﬁle cannot be selected)
The rem ote control is blocked
All control elem ents (nodes) is skipped, except protocol nodes, tim er nodes and z-focus control
The execution is carried out with the jum p delay of the job
Note The pilot laser feature executes only two-dim ensional processing in a plane but does not support 3D processing.
Only the z focus control nodes are applied to adjust the focus plane.
W hen selecting the pilot laser proﬁle, the corresponding window "Laser Control" opens, which provides the controls for pilot laser
alignm ent:
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Using the Pilot Laser for Alignment

Pilot Laser Lets you start the pilot laser for m arking.
Note

this laser is switched oﬀ, the Pilot Laser button is deactivated.
   Starts Lets you deﬁne the num ber of m arkings.
W e recom m end a high value (> 10,000) to ensure that the adjustm ent procedure is ﬁnished before the
pilot laser stops execution.
Enter the required value into the input ﬁeld or use the arrow buttons.
   Sel. Lets you select a variant to be executed within the job, if the job com prises two or m ore variants.
A variant is selected by the nam e displayed in J ob Explorer.
‒ If two or m ore variants are created, the selection ﬁeld's drop-down list shows all variants.
   The selected variant and all other m arking objects, which are not included in a variant, is executed.
‒ If only one variant has been created, its nam e is displayed.
   This variant and all other m arking objects, which are not included in a variant, is executed.
‒ If no variant has been created, the listbox shows "All".
   All m arking objects are executed.

variants' graphics param eters m ust be set. Otherwise, variants cannot be executed.

Hardware Conﬁguration (see Setting 'Scan Head' Param eters). In this case scan head [1], scan head [2]
or scan heads [1]+[2] can be selected.
If scan head [2] has not been activated, "1" for scan head [1] is displayed, but the listbox is disabled.
Break
STOP M arking W hen you have ﬁnished the alignm ent procedure, this button lets you stop the pilot laser m arking.
Or you can click the button as an em ergency stop.
Pilot Laser Settings
   Listbox Scope of execution. Available entries:
‒ Com plete J ob: all objects inclusive ﬁlling is executed; this setting can cause a long m arking tim e for
one execution;
‒ Outlines Only: only the object outlines are executed. This setting is recom m ended for the m ost
applications;
‒ J ob Bounding Box: only the job bounding box (and not the single objects) is executed. This setting is
helpful if the job includes a huge num ber of m arking objects, but it can lead to restrictions regarding
accuracy;
   Speed m /s Lets you deﬁne the execution speed (in [m /s]).
Enter the required value or click the arrow buttons (up/down).
Pilot Laser Transform ation
   X Lets you deﬁne the transform ation values (depending on the increm ent settings, see 'M ove - Rotate' in
the 'Edit' Param eters).
Click the arrow buttons (up/down) ‒ the m arking objects is m oved in x or y dim ension or rotated
sim ultaneously. Or enter a desired value and press <Enter>.
The rotation axis is always the origin of the work area's coordinate system  (z axis).
   Y
   Rotation:
Apply To J ob Data Applies the transform ation values to the job data (see param eters 'M ark Rotation' and 'M ark
Translation' in the G raphics Param eters ‒ J ob). Only then, the transform ation values are applied to
subsequent m arking with the processing laser. The transform ation values are applied for the com plete
job and thus for all variants, regardless of which variant has been used for transform ation.
Note
The transform ation values are applied only, if you click this button before leaving the pilot laser proﬁle.
Changing to another proﬁle (design, test, production) before, the values get lost.
The U ndo button lets you reset the transform ation as long as this is tem porary. I.e.:
‒ Any transform ation which is not applied to the job data (see above) can be reset.
‒ The transform ation applied to the job data at last can be reset.
‒ The transform ation applied to the job data penultim ate cannot be reset.
Note
After changing to another proﬁle (design, test, production), the transform ation cannot be reset by this
function.
Pilot Laser Transform ation
Lets you deﬁne the transform ation values (depending on the increm ent settings, see 'M ove - Rotate' in
the 'Edit' Param eters).
Click the buttons (left-turning/right-turning or up/down/left/right) ‒ the m arking objects are m oved or
rotated sim ultaneously.
The rotation axis is always the origin of the work area's coordinate system  (z axis).
Note Even though the pilot laser proﬁle's window "Laser Control" com prises controls for alignm ent, the alignm ent is
operated norm ally by keyboard. W hile m onitoring the pilot laser's beam  at the m arking surface, you can align the job
(m arking objects) easier by pressing the following keys (to use the keys, the graphic view m ust be the active):
‒ arrow keys (<←> <→> <↑> <↓>) for x/y directions,
‒ plus and m inus key (<+> <->) for rotation.
Pressing the <Shift> key sim ultaneously, the increm ent increases by a factor of ten.

Note Before starting the pilot laser transform ation,
‒ the 'Pilot Laser' param eters m ust be set in "Hardware Conﬁguration" (see Setting 'Pilot Laser' Param eters),
‒ the increm ent settings m ust be set in the G U I Settings (see 'M ove - Rotate' in the 'Edit' Param eters).
To transform  a laser job:
01. Ensure, that the Autom atic M ode is switched oﬀ.
If opened, the output window shows "M anual M ode".
02. Choose M enu View > Pilot Laser Proﬁle or Toolbar File > Pilot Laser Proﬁle.
The corresponding Laser Control W indow opens.
The m arking objects' m arking vectors are displayed autom atically in the work area.
03. Enter the required num ber of executions into the 'Starts' input ﬁeld.
04. If the job com prises two or m ore variants, select the variant to be executed in the 'Sel.' listbox.
05. If a second scan head has been activated, select the required scan head(s) in the 'Head' listbox.
06. Deﬁne the scope of execution: select 'Com plete J ob', 'Outlines Only' or 'J ob Bounding Box'.
07. Deﬁne the execution speed (in [m /s]).
08. Click the M anual M arking button.
The m arking procedure starts.
During m arking, the button shows a stylized laser beam :
If opened, the output window shows "Laser m arking started" and "Repetitions: 1 ...".
The pilot laser m arking can be observed at the laser scan system 's processing area.
09. To use the keyboard keys, place the cursor in the work area and click the m ouse button.
10. W hile m onitoring the pilot laser m arking at the laser scan system  processing area, press the plus, m inus and arrow keys to
rotate or m ove the job (objects or bounding box) and thus to adjust it to the m arking surface.
Note If you cannot adjust the job exactly, because the increm ent values are to rough, stop the m arking.
M odify the increm ent values in the G U I settings (see 'M ove - Rotate' in the 'Edit' Param eters) and start the pilot
laser transform ation again.
11. W hen the job is adjusted exactly to the m arking surface, click the STOP M arking button to stop the pilot laser m arking.
On the screen, the transform ation is indicated by the m arking vectors of the job or the selected variant.
The exam ple below shows two variants. Only the (red) m arking vectors of the selected variant (2 rectangles) is shown.
12. Click the Apply To J ob Data button.
W hen executing the m arking subsequently with the processing laser, this values are applied.
13. In som e cases, for exam ple, when using a positioning device, it m ay be advantageous to save the transform ation values
together with the job perm anently.
To do so, save the job after the pilot laser alignm ent. Otherwise, the transform ation values get lost.

RTC6 Ethernet Boards with Standalone Option
Note This feature (including the boot im age m echanism ) is only applicable using an RTC6 Ethernet board with the
Standalone option enabled.
If this option is not yet enabled on your RTC6 Ethernet board, contact SCANLAB.
W indow "Standalone Control"
G eneral Inform ation
Standalone Handling
Error Num bers
Rem arks on Standalone Function in laserDESK
Window "Standalone Control"
To support an RTC6 Ethernet board, the W indow "Standalone Control" allows to execute all necessary actions for the standalone
handling.
W indow "Standalone Control"
Note The buttons Download Data, Reset to PC M ode, Write Boot Im age to PC and Write Boot Im age to RTC are disabled,
if no RTC6 Ethernet board with Standalone option is acquired.
Button Description
Check J ob Lets you check the current job for standalone capability. The Check J ob action will not display
hardware dependent errors to allow checking without the ﬁnally used hardware.
For the 'Check J ob' action it is not necessary to have an RTC acquired.
During the checking a blocking window is displayed, which will close autom atically. For very com plex
jobs, the check m ay take a while.
W hen the check is ﬁnished and the job is ﬁne for standalone application the button is m arked with a
green checkm ark.
W hen the check is ﬁnished and problem s are present the button is m arked with a red X and unusable
elem ents are listed in a window:
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 RTC6 Ethernet Boards w. Standalone Option
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/RTC6EthStandaloneSupport.htm 2/10

--- Page 891 ---

Button Description
Additionally, not usable elem ents of the job (for exam ple, variable objects and som e control nodes) are
displayed in a selectable color (default red *) in J ob Explorer:
To get m ore detailed inform ation, you can click the Show all Hints button (see below).
* The color for the unusable elem ents can be selected in the 'View' Param eters, see
'G raphics\Standalone Error'.
Show all Hints This button is only enabled if a checking has already occurred and problem s are present (see 'Check
J ob' above).
Opens an inform ation window with details on the problem s:
For error num bers and their m eaning see Error Num bers.
Download Data Lets you write the data into the NAND m em ory of the RTC6 Ethernet board (non-volatile m em ory, see
RTC6 M anual).
The RTC6 Ethernet board is put into Standalone-Full-M ode. The current Hardware Conﬁguration and
the opened job is used.
First the boot sequence is created. Next the job is checked and the RTC list is calculated and
downloaded to the RTC6 Ethernet board. Finally, the Standalone-Full-M ode is activated on the selected
RTC6 Ethernet board.
