Button Description
This action will check the hardware needed for the job execution, too.
Clear Data Lets you delete the contents of the NAND m em ory.
The RTC6 Ethernet board is put into PC m ode.
The NAND m em ory is cleared and autom atic booting is disabled.
Write Boot Im age to PC Lets you write the NAND m em ory of the RTC6 Ethernet board on the PC as a boot im age.
This im age m ay be transferred to an RTC6 Ethernet board again (as a backup or to a diﬀerent board).
Click the button to select a path in a M S W indows-standard dialog and to save the boot im age.
Write Boot Im age on
RTC
Lets you transfer a previously written boot im age back into the NAND m em ory of an RTC6 Ethernet
board.
Afterward, the Standalone-Full-M ode is activated.
Click the button to select the boot im age in a M S W indows-standard dialog and to load it.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 RTC6 Ethernet Boards w. Standalone Option
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/RTC6EthStandaloneSupport.htm 4/10

--- Page 893 ---

G eneral Inform ation
If an RTC6 Ethernet board is set into Standalone-Full-M ode, it m ay be reconnected to laserDESK  and can be used as a norm al RTC6
Ethernet board again. New lists can be loaded and executed.
Nevertheless, the Standalone-Full-M ode is not changed unless a new download is executed or the Clear Data button is clicked.
Especially if the board is disconnected and a power cycle is executed, the current list is cleared and the data of the last download
is used again.
The write and read access of the NAND m em ory of the RTC are long running tasks which can not be interrupted and no further RTC
access is possible. Therefore, laserDESK  blocks all actions during this tim e.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 RTC6 Ethernet Boards w. Standalone Option
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/RTC6EthStandaloneSupport.htm 5/10

Standalone Handling
For standalone handling:
01. Open an existing job, for exam ple, in the Design Proﬁle.
02. Choose M enu View > Standalone Proﬁle or Toolbar File > Standalone Proﬁle.
The W indow "Standalone Control" opens. 
Note A job can be edited in the Standalone Proﬁle too. But not usable m arking objects and control elem ents are set to
inactive in the toolbar (see Toolbars) or the M enu Tools.
03. Click the Check J ob button.
A blocking window is displayed, which will close autom atically. For very com plex jobs, the check m ay take a while.
‒ If the job is ﬁne for standalone application, the Check J ob button is m arked with a green hook.
‒ If problem s are present, the Check J ob button is m arked with a red X.
04. If problem s are present, click the Show all Hints button.
A window "Inform ation" opens and shows notes regarding the problem s.
05. If you want to write data into the NAND m em ory of the RTC6 Ethernet board, click the Download Data button.
The RTC list is calculated and downloaded to the RTC6 Ethernet board.
06. If you want to reset the RTC board into PC m ode, click the Clear Data button.
The contents of the NAND m em ory is deleted and autom atic booting is disabled.
07. If you want to write the NAND m em ory of the RTC6 Ethernet board on the PC as a boot im age, click the Write Boot Im age to
PC button.
A M S W indows-standard dialog opens.
08. Select a path and click the button OK to save the boot im age.
09. If you want to transfer a previously written boot im age back into the NAND m em ory of an RTC6 Ethernet board, click the
Write Boot Im age on RTC button.
A M S W indows-standard dialog opens.
10. Select the boot im age and click the button OK to load it back.
Subsequently, the Standalone-Full-M ode is activated.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 RTC6 Ethernet Boards w. Standalone Option
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/RTC6EthStandaloneSupport.htm 9/10

--- Page 898 ---

Rem arks on Standalone Function in laserDESK
In standalone m ode, there is no connection between the PC and laserDESK . Therefore, only those laserDESK  functions are
possible that the RTC6 Ethernet board can execute on its own, that is, that do not require any interaction with laserDESK .
First, you m ust deﬁne the entire job execution and all settings in laserDESK  in advance. These are then downloaded once to the
RTC6 Ethernet board, which uses them  from  then on.
The following laserDESK functions are not possible or only possible to a lim ited extent:
No support of variable data (serial num bers, tim e, variable text)
No use of laserDESK  rem ote control
No support for m otor axes
No support for serial com m unication
No full support of the laser system  (no activation sequence and error check possible)
No extended scan system  error check
No recording of data by protocol nodes
No tim e m easurem ent
No handling of the pilot laser
Lim ited shutter handling (see below)
No status signals at EXTENSION 1 to indicate the current status of laserDESK . Instead, you can use the BU SYOU T pin of the
LASER connector.
Com m ents
In standalone m ode, the RTC6 Ethernet board is always in Autom atic M ode, that is, the /START input is activated and the
variant selection by IOs is set up.
The shutter is opened at the ﬁrst start in standalone m ode using the pin deﬁned in Laser W izard, but only if the shutter is not
to be switched at every start (deﬁned in Dialog "Hardware Settings" > page 'Processing laser', see Setting the Param eters
'Processing Laser'.
Otherwise, no other shutter function is supported.
There is a separate proﬁle for the standalone functions. Here, standalone m ode can be activated (deactivated), the boot
im age can be saved and loaded and the job can be checked for use in standalone m ode.
To obtain further control options, you can activate the rem ote interface, see How to W ork with Rem ote Interface M ode.
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Executing Laser Jobs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/JobExecution.htm 1/6

--- Page 900 ---

Executing Laser J obs
Two m odes are available for executing jobs:
In M anual M ode, you start a job by laserDESK  user interface. This is useful for test purposes during your application's
developm ent phase, for exam ple when you want to iteratively determ ine optim al m arking param eters. In M anual M ode, you
can select a speciﬁc variant or objects for individual execution.
In Autom atic M ode, the job is started and controlled exclusively by external input signals. The RTC5 | RTC6 start input is the
only input line to start a m arking, avoiding a delay by the M S W indows cycle tim e. The Autom atic M ode supports laserDESK
variant concept (see How to U se Variants) for fast switching between diﬀerent m arking processes The input lines of the RTC
Connector EXTENSION 1 are used to select variants. Thus, Autom atic M ode is for using the job in actual production.
Note
If variants are deﬁned, an input signal pattern m ust m atch a deﬁned bit pattern of one variant when the start signal is sent.
Only then this variant is executed. The input signals are the digital input lines 0-7 of the 'Extension 1' connector of the RTC
board:
‒ pin 3 of the connector LASER is the Autom atic M ode start input,
‒ pin 11 of the connector LASER is em ergency stop input.
(also see Connecting Lasers and RTC Boards and chapter 'Connector LASER' in the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual).
Starting a job m anually or switching it to Autom atic M ode is only possible in the Laser Control W indow.
A job can only be started under the following conditions:
The supplied dongle m ust be inserted into any U SB port of the PC.
The job m ust be opened in laserDESK .
The PC m ust contain an SCANLAB RTC5 board | RTC6 board.
Som e functions (for exam ple, Processing-On-the-Fly and 3D operation) require activation on the RTC board.
If m otors for m oving or rotating parts are used in the job, a reference run m ust be executed before m anually (see Testing the
M otor Controllers).
Note For all iDRIVE system s an autom atic power control and m onitoring is applied.
During job execution it is checked, whether an iDRIVE system  is connected and the power state is checked.
If a power failure is detected, the job execution is aborted and an error is signaled.
This feature is applied even if no power state checking is activated in the Hardware Conﬁguration.
To start a job m anually:
01. Ensure, that the Autom atic M ode is switched oﬀ.
If opened, the output window shows "M anual M ode".
02. Choose M enu View > "Test Proﬁle" or Toolbar File > "Test Proﬁle".
The corresponding Laser Control W indow opens.
Note U sing a "Laser of Type 'Fully Supported'", you need to have switched on the laser beforehand.
If not, instead of the M anual M arking button the blue Sim ulate Execution button is displayed.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Executing Laser Jobs
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/D_Anwendungen/JobExecution.htm 2/6

--- Page 901 ---

Select the 'Production Proﬁle' and switch on the laser (see steps 1 and 2 in the next section "To switch to
Autom atic M ode ..." below).
03. Enter the required num ber of executions into the 'Starts' input ﬁeld or use the arrow buttons.
04. Select the desired variant or m arking object to be executed within the job in the 'Sel.' listbox:
‒ Select a desired "VariantNam e" for the variant to be executed. All other m arking objects, which are not included in a
variant, will also be executed.
    or
‒ Select "Selection" and subsequently select one or m ore desired m arking objects in the J ob Explorer or work area (see
Selecting M arking Objects for Editing). Additional, the protocol nodes can be selected. Only the m arking object(s) and no
variant is executed.
    or (but only if no variant has been created)
‒ Select "All". All m arking objects are executed.
05. If the second scan head is activated (and thus the 'Head' listbox is available), select scan head(s) "1", "2" or "1+2".
06. Click the M anual M arking button.
The m arking procedure starts.
During m arking, the button shows a stylized laser beam :
If opened, the output window shows "Laser m arking started" and "Repetitions: 1 ...".
If param eters m ust be adapted to the current used hardware, the output window shows a m essage like "The frequency is out
of range and has been adapted".
If signiﬁcant deviations occur, the job is canceled - a respective m essage is shown in the output window.
If the expected execution tim e will last for longer than 10 seconds, the status bar displays a progress bar.
W hen m arking has ﬁnished, the output window will show "Laser m arking ﬁnished" and the status bar displays the actual
m arking tim e (in [s]).
07. To execute an em ergency stop, click the STOP M arking button.
To switch to Autom atic M ode:
01. Choose M enu View > "Production Proﬁle" or Toolbar File > "Production Proﬁle".
The corresponding Laser Control W indow for 'Production Proﬁle' opens.

--- Page 904 ---

Status Signals
At the RTC5 board | RTC6 board Connector EXTENSION 1 various status signals are provided during job execution, see:
DIG ITAL OU T 8
DIG ITAL OU T 12
DIG ITAL OU T 13
DIG ITAL OU T 14
DIG ITAL OU T 15
At DIGITAL OU T 8 on pin 17 the status signal 'J ob execution not aborted' is given.
This signal is reset when a job is started and set again when the job has successfully ﬁnished.
It is not set when the job ﬁnishes under following conditions:
a. The job is aborted by an external stop signal
b. The user clicks the STOP button in laserDESK
c. The job is aborted by a cancel condition of a serial com m unication
Note This signal is only valid if the system  is fully operational but an event has aborted the job. You can im m ediately restart
the job.
The Autom atic M ode is not switched oﬀ if it is active.
The signal is set if a running job is aborted because a system  or hardware error occurred. In that case the error output is set and
this signal should be ignored.
This state is also available in the rem ote m ode status. There the status ﬂag RM _STATE_J OB_ABORTED = 0x00800000 is deﬁned and
returned by the status query (com m and #4). This ﬂag should only be evaluated when the job has ﬁnished (RM _STATE_EXEC_DONE
is set). This is the inverted status of the output line.
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Upgrading laserDesk
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Upgrade.htm 1/4

--- Page 906 ---

U pgrading laserDESK
Upgrading laserDESK can be necessary in the following cases:
You want to use laserDESK features which are not yet activated on the appropriate dongle (see Editions / Functional Range).
The dongle must be upgraded.
SCANLAB releases a new laserDESK version (update ‒ indicated by an increase of the third digit of the version number:
"Version Nr. n.n.n"). An update does not require a dongle upgrade.
If you want to use such a laserDESK software release, this merely needs to be installed.
SCANLAB oﬀers a new laserDESK version with new options (upgrade ‒ indicated by an increase of the ﬁrst or second digit of
the version number: "Version Nr. n.n.n "). A software upgrade requires a dongle upgrade too.
If you want to use the new laserDESK software, this must be installed and the dongle must be upgraded.
Note From version 1.6 laserDESK is a 64-bit program. This means laserDESK uses the entire RAM  of the PC, not just the 4 GB
that a 32-bit program can access. This allows to use bigger jobs and reduces a lot of Out-of-M emory exceptions.
If you have an older version and want to take advantage of this beneﬁt, the new laserDESK software version must be
installed and the dongle upgraded.
Note When laserDESK is updated by a new version, the current user settings stored as parameters in the user.conﬁg and
Application.conﬁg conﬁguration ﬁles are applied.
Upgrading the Dongle
Installing the newer laserDESK Version
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Upgrading laserDesk
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Upgrade.htm 2/4

--- Page 907 ---

U pgrading the Dongle
Upgrading the appropriate dongle is very easy by the dialog "Licensing". The Store U pgrade Request File button lets you create a
license upgrade request ﬁle (xml-ﬁle) with all important data of your software edition (application data, dongle data, options).
Together with your update query, you need to send this ﬁle to SCANLAB. Then you will receive a license upgrade ﬁle in return. The
Load U pgrade License File button in the dialog "Licensing" serves to execute the upgrade using the returned ﬁle. Then the
appropriate dongle is actualized to enable laserDESK to use the new features.
Note An upgrade is speciﬁc to one dongle only. The creation of the license upgrade request ﬁle and the upgrade execution
must be performed with the same dongle.
The upgrade can only be performed by users with laserDESK "Administrator" access authorization.
Creating the U pgrade Request File
To create the request ﬁle:
01. Insert the dongle in any USB connector of your PC.
02. Start laserDESK (see Starting laserDESK).
03. Choose M enu Help > "Licensing".
The dialog "Licensing" opens.
04. Click the Store U pgrade Request File button.
The M S Windows-standard dialog 'Save As...' opens.
05. Deﬁne the ﬁle name ([Name].xml) and path.
06. Click on Save.
The ﬁle is created immediately.
A success message with the ﬁle path opens.
07. Send the license upgrade request ﬁle to SCANLAB.
U pgrading the Dongle
To upgrade the dongle:
01. Insert the dongle in any USB connector of your PC.
02. Start laserDESK (see Starting laserDESK).
03. Copy the upgrade license ﬁle you got from SCANLAB into any directory of your PC or network.
04. Choose M enu Help > "Licensing".
The dialog "Licensing" opens.
05. Click the Load U pgrade License File button.
The M S Windows-standard dialog "Open" opens.
06. Select the upgrade license ﬁle and click Open.
The dongle is written immediately.
A success message with the request to restart the application opens.
07. Click the Yes button to restart laserDESK.
After restart, laserDESK gives access to the unlocked options.
26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Upgrading laserDesk
file:///C:/Users/82108/Downloads/scanlab_support/Content/E_Dialoge/Upgrade.htm 3/4

--- Page 908 ---

Installing the newer laserDESK Version
The newer laserDESK version (update or upgrade) can be downloaded and installed from the SCANLAB website (see
www.scanlab.de).
Note If your current laserDESK version is 0.n.n (Beta release), you must uninstall it before downloading and installing the
new laserDESK software.
If your current laserDESK version is 1.n.n or higher, during installation the query "Delete old version?" opens.
Accept the query to get the old version uninstalled.
If you have installed a software upgrade, the appropriate dongle too must be upgraded, to let you use the new laserDESK version
(see Upgrading the Dongle).
(original text - this is not a translation)

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Connecting Lasers to RTC Boards

Connecting Lasers to RTC Boards
For proper function, the used laser m ust be connected correctly to the RTC board.
Note Diﬀerent RTC board variants have diﬀerent connectors (ports). The only authoritative docum entation for these is the
RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Note In principle, laserDESK  can address all (connectors) ports. However, as som e connections are pre-deﬁned for special
use, not all connections are free to use. Therefore, only certain connections in the control nodes (see Inserting Control
Elem ents) are available to choose from  .

connector EXTENSION 1 and/or connector EXTENSION 2.

the job.

Proposal for Connecting an IPG Laser with Type B or Type B2 Interface

Refer to the m anual of the laser m anufacturer for all further necessary connections.

connection interface m ay have changed.
Note The following jum pers m ust be closed on the RTC5/6 PCI board:
- J P3: Pin 15 = Data7 (Digital Out)
- J P8: Pin 17 = Latch Signal
Refer to the RTC5 M anual | RTC6 M anual.
Note The following jum pers m ust be closed on the RTC6 Ethernet board:
- Solder J um per Field B: Pin17 = Latch
- Solder J um per Field C: Pin15 = Data7
Refer to the RTC6 M anual.

board described here, the serial connection param eters m ust be deﬁned in laserDESK 'Hardware Settings' and related
to the Hippo laser:
- previously deﬁne the serial connection in the 'PC Interfaces' Param eters,
- then select the deﬁned serial connection in the 'Processing Laser' Param eters.

26. 3. 27. 오후  12:06 Notes on Earlier laserDESK Versions

Notes on Earlier laserDESK Versions
laserDESK V1.6.19.0
Note The SPI and Trum pf TruPulse nano lasers require a signal to switch to cw m ode. U ntil laserDESK ≤V1.6.18, this was
perm anently program m ed in the corresponding laser classes. From  laserDESK ≥V1.6.19, this function can be freely
deﬁned for each laser type, see Laser W izard Page 05-10 Signal Assignm ent).
Note U p to laserDESK ≤V1.6.18, the cw signal was on the LASER connection and this cannot be m asked. As the cw switch
can take place within a list execution, the pin status is not known during execution! laserDESK therefore uses the status
when the list is created. The non-CW  pin m ust therefore be constant during a list execution. As of laserDESK ≥V1.6.19,
it is possible to assign the cw switch pin to a diﬀerent port to avoid this problem . Due to the ﬁxed pin assignm ent in
laserDESK ≤V1.6.18, this problem  did not exist.
(original text - this is not a translation)
Doc.Rev. 2.38.0 en-U S [2025-07-21 14:21] for laserDESK V1.6.25
